DIA_KN_IPRUP.name: Intraperitoneal Bladder Rupture
DIA_KN_IPRUP.description: Intraperitoneal Bladder Rupture: If the tear is on the top of the bladder, the hole will usually open to the part of the abdomen that holds the liver, spleen, and bowel. Urine leaking into the abdomen is a serious problem. This tear can be sewn closed with surgery. A catheter is left in the bladder for up to 2 weeks after surgery to allow the bladder to rest. The tube will either come out through the urethra or out through the skin below the belly button.

DIA_KN_EXRUP_1.name: Extraperitoneal Bladder Rupture
DIA_KN_EXRUP_1.description: Extraperitoneal Bladder Rupture: If the tear is at the bottom or side of the bladder, the urine will leak into the tissues around the bladder instead of the abdominal cavity. Complex injuries of this type should be repaired with surgery. But often it can be treated by simply placing a wide catheter into the bladder to keep it empty. The urine and blood drain into a collection bag. It usually takes at least 10 days for the bladder to heal. The catheter is left in the bladder until an X-ray shows that the leak has sealed. If the catheter doesn’t drain properly, surgery is needed.

DIA_KN_EXRUP_2.name: Extraperitoneal Bladder Rupture
DIA_KN_EXRUP_2.description: Extraperitoneal Bladder Rupture: If the tear is at the bottom or side of the bladder, the urine will leak into the tissues around the bladder instead of the abdominal cavity. Complex injuries of this type should be repaired with surgery. But often it can be treated by simply placing a wide catheter into the bladder to keep it empty. The urine and blood drain into a collection bag. It usually takes at least 10 days for the bladder to heal. The catheter is left in the bladder until an X-ray shows that the leak has sealed. The catheter doesn’t drain properly, surgery is needed!

DIA_KN_PENRUP_1.name: Penetrating Bladder Rupture
DIA_KN_PENRUP_1.description: Penetrating Bladder Rupture: Injury to the bladder from a bullet or other penetrating object is usually fixed with surgery. Most of the time, other organs in the area will be injured and need repair as well. After surgery, a catheter is left in the bladder to drain the urine and blood until the bladder heals.

DIA_KN_WDIA.name: Weekly Dialysis Apointment
DIA_KN_WDIA.description: The patient has chronic kidney failure and has to come in every week for dialysis.

DIA_SH_PRO.name: Prostatitis
DIA_SH_PRO.description: Prostatitis is inflammation (swelling) of the prostate gland.

SYM_CRP_SEVERE_PAIN.name: Severe Pain
SYM_CRP_SEVERE_PAIN.description: Patient is experiencing intense amount of pain.

SYM_CRP_ENCEPHALITIS.name: Encephalitis
SYM_CRP_ENCEPHALITIS.description: Encephalitis (en-sef-uh-LIE-tis) is inflammation of the brain. There are several causes, but the most common is a viral infection. Encephalitis often causes only mild flu-like signs and symptoms — such as a fever or headache — or no symptoms at all. Sometimes the flu-like symptoms are more severe. Encephalitis can also cause confused thinking, seizures, or problems with movement or with senses such as sight or hearing. In some cases, encephalitis can be life-threatening. Timely diagnosis and treatment are important because it's difficult to predict how encephalitis will affect each individual.

SYM_CRP_SEIZURES.name: Seizures
SYM_CRP_SEIZURES.description: A seizure is a sudden, uncontrolled electrical disturbance in the brain. It can cause changes in your behaviour, movements or feelings, and in levels of consciousness.

SYM_CRP_DYSPHASIA.name: Dysphasia
SYM_CRP_DYSPHASIA.description: Dysphasia is a language disorder marked by deficiency in the generation of speech, and sometimes also in its comprehension, due to brain disease or damage.

SYM_CRP_FND.name: Focal Neurologic Deficit
SYM_CRP_FND.description: A focal neurologic deficit is a problem with nerve, spinal cord, or brain function. It affects a specific location, such as the left side of the face, right arm, or even a small area such as the tongue. Speech, vision, and hearing problems are also considered focal neurological deficits.

SYM_CRP_BRAIN_ABW.name: Abnormal Brain Wave Patterns
SYM_CRP_BRAIN_ABW.description: EEG has detected abnormal brain wave patterns. Spikes, sharp waves, or spike-wave complexes occurring with consistent localization are observed sometimes early in the course of brain tumours. However, they are more common either as early findings of slowly growing neoplasms associated with seizures or later after focal slowing has developed.

SYM_CRP_BREAST_LUMP.name: Breast Lump
SYM_CRP_BREAST_LUMP.description: A breast lump is a localized swelling, protuberance, bulge, or bump in the breast that feels different from the breast tissue around it.

SYM_CRP_BREAST_DISCH.name: Breast Discharge
SYM_CRP_BREAST_DISCH.description: Nipple discharge is the release of fluid from the nipple. It is a very common breast symptom and in most cases is part of the normal function of the breast rather than being caused by a problem.

SYM_CRP_BREAST_PAIN.name: Breast Pain
SYM_CRP_BREAST_PAIN.description: Tightness, tenderness or sharp pain in one or both breasts that occurs commonly and usually doesn't mean anything is seriously wrong.

SYM_CRP_BREAST_SCHANGE.name: Breast Shape Changes
SYM_CRP_BREAST_SCHANGE.description: The patient has experienced a change in the shape or size of their breasts.

SYM_CRP_BREAST_DIMP.name: Breast Dimpling
SYM_CRP_BREAST_DIMP.description: Also known as peau d'orange, dimpling of the breast causes the skin to look like the pitting and uneven skin of an orange. Sometimes, the skin can also be red and inflamed.

SYM_CRP_SUS_CALC.name: Suspicious Calcifications
SYM_CRP_SUS_CALC.description: Breast calcifications are small calcium deposits that develop in a woman's breast tissue. They are very common and are usually benign (noncancerous). Breast calcifications are more common in women, but can also be found in men. In some instances, certain types of breast calcifications may suggest early breast cancer.

SYM_CRP_IRR_BMASS.name: Irregular Breast Density
SYM_CRP_IRR_BMASS.description: A potential abnormality on a mammogram might be called a nodule, mass, lump, density, or distortion. A mass (lump) that has an irregular border or a starburst appearance (spiculated) might be cancerous.

SYM_CRP_SPEC_MARG.name: Spiculated Margins
SYM_CRP_SPEC_MARG.description: Spiculated margins are a true diagnostic feature of malignancy. Strands of tissue are seen radiating out from an ill-defined mass, producing a stellate appearance.

SYM_CRP_MASSE_HOMOGENE.name: Homogenous Mass
SYM_CRP_MASSE_HOMOGENE.description: Homogenous mass shown on an ultrasound image.

SYM_CRP_GYNECOMASTIA.name: Gynecomastia
SYM_CRP_GYNECOMASTIA.description: Abnormal increase in breast volume in men.

SYM_CRP_POLYPS.name: Colorectal Polyp
SYM_CRP_POLYPS.description: A polyp is a growth of tissue on the lining of the colon and rectum that projects into the intestines. People with colon polyps usually have no symptoms. Most colon polyps are harmless, though some types can become cancerous.

SYM_CRP_WHITE_STOOLS.name: White Stools
SYM_CRP_WHITE_STOOLS.description: A white or clay-like stool is caused by a lack of bile, which may indicate a serious underlying problem. Bile is a digestive fluid produced by the liver and stored in the gallbladder. A stool gets its normal brownish color from bile, which is excreted into the small intestine during the digestive process.

SYM_CRP_ED.name: Erectile Dysfunction
SYM_CRP_ED.description: Erectile dysfunction (ED) is the inability to get or keep an erection firm enough to have sexual intercourse. It's sometimes referred to as impotence, although this term is now used less often. Occasional ED isn't uncommon. Many men experience it during times of stress. Erectile dysfunction following radical prostatectomy for clinically localized prostate cancer is a known potential complication of the surgery.

SYM_CRP_IRR_MNS.name: Irregular Menstruation
SYM_CRP_IRR_MNS.description: Missed, delayed or erratic periods or abnormal bleeding patterns. Irregular menstruation can have causes that aren't due to underlying disease. Examples include family history of irregular menstruation, menopause or menarche, physical stress, psychological stress, smoking or medication side effects.

SYM_CRP_DYSPAREUNIA.name: Dyspareunia (Female)
SYM_CRP_DYSPAREUNIA.description: Painful intercourse can occur for reasons that range from structural problems to psychological concerns. Many women have painful intercourse at some point in their lives. The medical term for painful intercourse is dyspareunia, defined as persistent or recurrent genital pain that occurs just before, during or after intercourse.

SYM_CRP_APM.name: Abnormal Pelvic Mass Detected
SYM_CRP_APM.description: A pelvic mass is an enlargement or swelling in the pelvic region. Most pelvic masses are discovered during routine gynaecologic or physical examinations. Pelvic masses may originate from either the gynaecologic organs, such as the cervix, uterus, uterine adnexa, or from other pelvic organs, such as the intestines, bladder, ureters, and renal organs. There are many gynecologic problems that can result in a pelvic mass. Additionally, there are other diseases that may also result in a pelvic mass. Most are benign conditions, such as an Ovarian Cyst, while others can be related to gynecologic cancer.

SYM_CRP_OVAR_CYST.name: Ovarian Cysts
SYM_CRP_OVAR_CYST.description: Ovarian cysts are fluid-filled sacs or pockets in an ovary or on its surface. Most ovarian cysts don't cause symptoms. In some cases, menstrual irregularities, pain during intercourse or irregular bowel movements can occur. Most ovarian cysts are harmless and often clear up on their own without treatment.

SYM_CRP_APM1.name: Abnormal Prostate Findings
SYM_CRP_APM1.description: Testing has found abnormalities with the patient's prostate.

SYM_CRP_HEMATOSPERMIA.name: Hematospermia
SYM_CRP_HEMATOSPERMIA.description: Hematospermia is defined as blood in the semen. While often perceived as having little significance, blood in the ejaculate can cause great concern to the men who experience it. The condition is common, with many episodes going unnoticed; therefore, the prevalence of hematospermia remains unknown.

SYM_CRP_METRORRAGIE.name: Metrorrhagia
SYM_CRP_METRORRAGIE.description: Blood loss occurring outside menstrual periods.

SYM_CRP_AMENORRHEE.name: Amenorrhea
SYM_CRP_AMENORRHEE.description: Abnormal absence or suppression of menses.

SYM_CRP_SECHERESSE_VAGIN.name: Vaginal Dryness
SYM_CRP_SECHERESSE_VAGIN.description: Vaginal dryness is the result of decreased levels of estrogen.

SYM_CRP_LEUCORRHEE.name: Leukorrhea
SYM_CRP_LEUCORRHEE.description: A whitish viscid discharge from the vagina resulting from inflammation or congestion of the mucous membrane.

SYM_CRP_CONTRACTION_UTERINE.name: Contracted Pelvis
SYM_CRP_CONTRACTION_UTERINE.description: It is a pelvis in which one or more of its diameters is reduced so that it interferes with the normal mechanism of labour.

SYM_CRP_PREMATURE.name: Preterm
SYM_CRP_PREMATURE.description: Of, relating to, being, or brought forth by premature birth.

SYM_CRP_POST_PARTUM.name: Post-Partum
SYM_CRP_POST_PARTUM.description: Following childbirth or the birth of young.

SYM_CRP_ULCERATION_GENITAL.name: Genital Ulcer
SYM_CRP_ULCERATION_GENITAL.description: Genital ulcers, defined as single or multiple vesicular, ulcerative or erosive lesions of the genital tract, with or without inguinal lymphadenopathy, should lead to consideration of sexually transmitted infection.

SYM_CRP_ACTINIC_KERATOSIS.name: Actinic Keratosis
SYM_CRP_ACTINIC_KERATOSIS.description: Actinic keratosis is a rough, scaly patch on the skin that develops from years of exposure to the sun.

SYM_CRP_NODULE_SKIN.name: Nodule on the Skin
SYM_CRP_NODULE_SKIN.description: A small, shiny bump or nodule on the skin

SYM_CRP_ABNORMAL_MOLE.name: Abnormal Mole
SYM_CRP_ABNORMAL_MOLE.description: A mole with an uneven border, unusual color, or change in growth.

SYM_CRP_ABNORMAL_NODULE.name: Abnormally Coloured Nodule
SYM_CRP_ABNORMAL_NODULE.description: The patient has a nodule (a lump) on the skin that has a strange red/violet colour.

SYM_CRP_SEBORRHEE.name: Seborrhea
SYM_CRP_SEBORRHEE.description: Abnormally increased secretion and discharge of sebum.

SYM_CRP_HIRSUTISME.name: Hirsutism
SYM_CRP_HIRSUTISME.description: Excessive body hair in women on parts of the body where hair is normally absent or minimal.

SYM_CRP_ACANTHOSIS_NIGRICANS.name: Acanthosis Nigricans
SYM_CRP_ACANTHOSIS_NIGRICANS.description: A skin condition that is marked by dark discoloration and velvety thickening of the skin especially of body folds or creases (as of the armpit, groin, or neck) and that is often associated with obesity, insulin resistance, hormonal irregularity, or medication use but may rarely occur as a sign of cancer (as of the stomach or liver).

SYM_CRP_BOUFFEE_VASOMOTRICE.name: Flushing
SYM_CRP_BOUFFEE_VASOMOTRICE.description: Flushing is to become markedly red in the face and often other areas of the skin, from various physiological conditions.

SYM_CRP_DRY_SKIN.name: Xerosis
SYM_CRP_DRY_SKIN.description: Dry skin is skin that has lost its protective fatty layer and becomes dehydrated.

SYM_CRP_TELANGIECTASIS.name: Telangiectasia
SYM_CRP_TELANGIECTASIS.description: Telangiectasias, also known as spider veins, are small dilated blood vessels that can occur near the surface of the skin or mucous membranes, measuring between 0.5 and 1 millimeter in diameter. These dilated blood vessels can develop anywhere on the body but are commonly seen on the face around the nose, cheeks and chin.

SYM_CRP_EAR_PRESSURE.name: Pressure in the Ears
SYM_CRP_EAR_PRESSURE.description: The patient feels like they have pressure in their ears or their ears feel full.

SYM_CRP_EAR_DAMAGE.name: Pressure in the Ears
SYM_CRP_EAR_DAMAGE.description: The patient feels like they have pressure in their ears or their ears feel full.

SYM_CRP_SNORE.name: Snore
SYM_CRP_SNORE.description: Snoring is the action or fact of making a snorting or grunting sound while asleep. It may indicate Obstructive Sleep Apnea

SYM_CRP_INFLAMMED_SINUSES.name: Inflamed Sinuses
SYM_CRP_INFLAMMED_SINUSES.description: Inflamed mucous tissue lining that covers the sinuses.

SYM_CRP_NASAL_DRIP.name: Post Nasal Drip
SYM_CRP_NASAL_DRIP.description: Post nasal drip is mucus from your nose and it drips harmlessly down the back of your throat, and you swallow it.

SYM_CRP_INFLAMMED_RH.name: Inflammed Nasal Cavities
SYM_CRP_INFLAMMED_RH.description: The patient's nasal cavity is is inflammed.

SYM_CRP_SINUS_FINDINGS.name: Nasal Findings
SYM_CRP_SINUS_FINDINGS.description: Nasal findings has been found by a CT.

SYM_CRP_NASAL_DRAINAGE.name: Nasal Discharge
SYM_CRP_NASAL_DRAINAGE.description: The patient has unidentified fluids coming from the nose.

SYM_CRP_IRREGULAR_NASAL_CYCLE.name: Irregular Nasal Cycle
SYM_CRP_IRREGULAR_NASAL_CYCLE.description: The nasal cycle is the often unnoticed alternating partial congestion and decongestion of the nasal cavities in humans and other animals. It is a physiological congestion of the nasal conchae, also called the nasal turbinates, due to selective activation of one half of the autonomic nervous system by the hypothalamus. It should not be confused with pathological nasal congestion. In this case the nasal cycle is irregular and this might be a sign of something wrong with the nose or the nerves connecting to the nose.

SYM_CRP_DIFFICULTY_BREATHING_THROUGH_NOS.name: Difficulty Breathing Through the Nose
SYM_CRP_DIFFICULTY_BREATHING_THROUGH_NOS.description: The patient is having difficulties breathing through their nose.

SYM_CRP_LOSS_OF_SMELL_OR_TASTE.name: Loss of Smell and/or Taste
SYM_CRP_LOSS_OF_SMELL_OR_TASTE.description: The patient has a loss of smell and/or taste.

SYM_CRP_FACIAL_PAIN.name: Facial Pain
SYM_CRP_FACIAL_PAIN.description: The patient feels pain on their face.

SYM_CRP_FACIAL_PRESSURE.name: Facial Pressure
SYM_CRP_FACIAL_PRESSURE.description: The patient feels pressure on their face.

SYM_CRP_FACIAL_WEAKNESS.name: Facial Weakness
SYM_CRP_FACIAL_WEAKNESS.description: The patient's is weak due to some sort of trauma or disease of the facial nerve.

SYM_CRP_HEMOPTYSIS1.name: Mild Hemoptysis
SYM_CRP_HEMOPTYSIS1.description: Hemoptysis is the coughing up of blood or blood-stained mucus from the bronchi, larynx, trachea, or lungs. In other words, it is the airway bleeding. This can occur with lung cancer, infections such as tuberculosis, bronchitis, or pneumonia, and certain cardiovascular conditions.

SYM_CRP_SWOLLEN_NECK.name: Swollen Neck
SYM_CRP_SWOLLEN_NECK.description: The patient has a swollen neck.

SYM_CRP_NECK_INJURY.name: Neck Injury
SYM_CRP_NECK_INJURY.description: The patient experienced a neck injury.

SYM_CRP_HYPOSALIVATION.name: Hyposalivation
SYM_CRP_HYPOSALIVATION.description: The patient's mouth isn't producing saliva that much, causing the patient's mouth to be dry.

SYM_CRP_DRY_MOUTH.name: Xerostomia
SYM_CRP_DRY_MOUTH.description: Xerostomia means dry mouth, the patient's mouth is dry.

SYM_CRP_MOUTH_DRAINAGE.name: Mouth Drainage
SYM_CRP_MOUTH_DRAINAGE.description: The patient has unidentified fluids coming from the mouth.

SYM_CRP_LUMP_IN_THROAT.name: Lump in Throat
SYM_CRP_LUMP_IN_THROAT.description: A lump in the throat has been found in the patient

SYM_CRP_PNEUMONIA.name: Pneumonia
SYM_CRP_PNEUMONIA.description: Pneumonia is an infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs. The air sacs may fill with fluid or pus, causing cough with phlegm or pus, fever, chills, and difficulty breathing. A variety of organisms, including bacteria, viruses and fungi, can cause pneumonia.Without treating bacterial pneumonia it can lead to complications like respiratory failure.

SYM_CRP_ARDS.name: Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome (ARDS)
SYM_CRP_ARDS.description: Acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) is a type of respiratory failure characterized by rapid onset of widespread inflammation in the lungs. Symptoms include shortness of breath, rapid breathing, and bluish skin coloration.

SYM_CRP_STRIDOR.name: Stridor
SYM_CRP_STRIDOR.description: Stridor is a high-pitched, wheezing sound caused by disrupted airflow. Stridor may also be called musical breathing or extrathoracic airway obstruction. Airflow is usually disrupted by a blockage in the larynx (voice box) or trachea (windpipe). Stridor affects children more often than adults.

SYM_CRP_DECREASED_PITCH_AND_LOUDNESS.name: Decreased Pitch and Loudness
SYM_CRP_DECREASED_PITCH_AND_LOUDNESS.description: The patient has decreased pitch range and loudness

SYM_CRP_CHRONIC_WOUND.name: Chronic Wound
SYM_CRP_CHRONIC_WOUND.description: Chronic wounds are sores that don’t heal within about three months. They can start small and might scab over again and again, but they don’t get better.

SYM_CRP_HIGH_EPLVL.name: High Epinephrine Levels
SYM_CRP_HIGH_EPLVL.description: The patient has elevated blood levels of epinephrine (adrenaline). Adrenaline is normally produced by both the adrenal glands and a small number of neurons in the medulla oblongata.

SYM_CRP_HIGH_NEPLVL.name: High Norepinephrine Levels
SYM_CRP_HIGH_NEPLVL.description: The patient has elevated blood levels of norepinephrine (noradrenaline). Norepinephrine is a naturally occurring chemical in the body that acts as both a stress hormone and neurotransmitter. It's released into the blood as a stress hormone when the brain perceives that a stressful event has occurred.

SYM_CRP_HIGH_CEALVL.name: High Carcinoembryonic Antigen Levels
SYM_CRP_HIGH_CEALVL.description: The patient has elevated blood levels of carcinoembryonic antigen. A high level of CEA can be a sign of certain types of cancers. These include cancers of the colon and rectum, prostate, ovary, lung, thyroid, or liver. High CEA levels may also be a sign of some noncancerous conditions, such as cirrhosis, noncancerous breast disease, and emphysema.

SYM_CRP_HYPONATREMIA.name: Hyponatremia
SYM_CRP_HYPONATREMIA.description: Hyponatremia occurs when the concentration of sodium in your blood is abnormally low. Sodium is an electrolyte, and it helps regulate the amount of water that's in and around your cells.

SYM_CRP_HYPERGLYCEMIA.name: Hyperglycemia
SYM_CRP_HYPERGLYCEMIA.description: Hyperglycemia is the technical term for high blood glucose (blood sugar). High blood sugar happens when the body has too little insulin or when the body can't use insulin properly.

SYM_CRP_HYPERCORTISOL.name: Hypercortisolism
SYM_CRP_HYPERCORTISOL.description: Cushing's syndrome, also called hypercortisolism, is a rare endocrine disorder caused by chronic exposure of the body's tissues to excess levels of cortisol - a hormone naturally produced by the adrenal gland.

SYM_CRP_HYPERALDOSTERONISM.name: Hyperaldosteronism
SYM_CRP_HYPERALDOSTERONISM.description: Hyperaldosteronism is a disease in which the adrenal gland(s) make too much aldosterone which leads to hypertension (high blood pressure) and low blood potassium levels. Primary hyperaldosteronism can be caused by either hyperactivity in one adrenal gland (unilateral disease) or both (bilateral disease).

SYM_CRP_THROMBOCYTOPENIA.name: Thrombocytopenia
SYM_CRP_THROMBOCYTOPENIA.description: Thrombocytopenia is a condition in which you have a low blood platelet count. Platelets (thrombocytes) are colourless blood cells that help blood clot. Platelets stop bleeding by clumping and forming plugs in blood vessel injuries.

SYM_CRP_RC_APLASIA.name: Red Cell Aplasia
SYM_CRP_RC_APLASIA.description: Pure red cell aplasia (PRCA) or erythroblastopenia refers to a type of anemia affecting the precursors to red blood cells but not to white blood cells. In PRCA, the bone marrow ceases to produce red blood cells. There are multiple etiologies that can cause PRCA.

SYM_CRP_HRM_01.name: Estrogen Receptor Positive
SYM_CRP_HRM_01.description: A cancer is called estrogen-receptor-positive (or ER+) if it has receptors for estrogen. This suggests that the cancer cells, like normal breast cells, may receive signals from estrogen that could promote their growth.

SYM_CRP_HRM_02.name: Progesterone Receptor Positive
SYM_CRP_HRM_02.description: The cancer is progesterone-receptor-positive (PR+) if it has progesterone receptors. Again, this means that the cancer cells may receive signals from progesterone that could promote their growth.

SYM_CRP_PANCYTOPENIA.name: Pancytopenia
SYM_CRP_PANCYTOPENIA.description: Pancytopenia is a condition that occurs when a person has low counts for all three types of blood cells: red blood cells, white blood cells, and platelets. Pancytopenia is usually due to a problem with the bone marrow that produces the blood cells. However, there can be several different underlying causes.

SYM_CRP_RH-.name: Anti-Erythrocyte Alloimmunization
SYM_CRP_RH-.description: Alloimmunization is defined as an immune response to foreign antigens after exposure to genetically different cells or tissues.

SYM_CRP_HYPERANDROGEN.name: Biological Hyperandrogenism
SYM_CRP_HYPERANDROGEN.description: High levels of androgens in females.

SYM_CRP_HYPOCALCEMIA.name: Hypocalcemia
SYM_CRP_HYPOCALCEMIA.description: Hypocalcemia is an abnormal drop in the level of calcium in the blood.

SYM_CRP_HYPERKALIEMIE.name: Hyperkalemia
SYM_CRP_HYPERKALIEMIE.description: Hyperkalemia is an elevated level of potassium (K+) in the blood.

SYM_CRP_HYPOKALIEMIE.name: Hypokalemia
SYM_CRP_HYPOKALIEMIE.description: Hypokalemia is a low level of potassium (K+) in the blood serum.

SYM_CRP_THROMBOCYTOSE.name: Thrombocytosis
SYM_CRP_THROMBOCYTOSE.description: Thrombocytosis is a condition in which there is an excessive number of platelets in the blood.

SYM_CRP_HYPERPRL.name: Hyperprolactinemia
SYM_CRP_HYPERPRL.description: Abnormal increase in plasma prolactin concentration.

SYM_CRP_HEMOLYSE.name: Haptoglobin - Low Levels
SYM_CRP_HEMOLYSE.description: Lab tests discovered descrese in level of Haptoglobin.

SYM_CRP_MED_APLASIA.name: Bone Aplasia
SYM_CRP_MED_APLASIA.description: Bone marrow aplasia refers to those hematologic conditions that are caused by a marked reduction and/or defect in the pluripotent or committed stem cells, or the failure of the bone marrow microenvironment to support hematopoiesis.

SYM_CRP_SURCHARGE_FER.name: Iron Overload
SYM_CRP_SURCHARGE_FER.description: Iron overload or hemochromatosis indicates accumulation of iron in the body from any cause.

SYM_CRP_HYPERAMMONEMIA.name: Hyperammonemia
SYM_CRP_HYPERAMMONEMIA.description: Hyperammonemia is a metabolic disturbance characterised by an excess of ammonia in the blood. It is a dangerous condition that may lead to brain injury and death.

SYM_CRP_AB_LFT.name: Abnormal LFT Results
SYM_CRP_AB_LFT.description: The patient's Liver Function Test have returned abnormal results, further testing is needed to help determine the possible cause. Abnormal liver function test results don't always indicate liver disease and may instead be a result of a number of circumstances.

SYM_CRP_NERVE_DAMAGE.name: Postoperative Neuropathic Pain
SYM_CRP_NERVE_DAMAGE.description: Postoperative neuropathic pain (PONP) can develop if nerves were damaged during a surgery. Damaged nerves cannot correctly transmit signals from various parts of the body to the brain. Instead, these signals become exaggerated, causing chronic pain that may persist for months or even years.

SYM_CRP_ANAPHYLACTIC_SHOCK.name: Anaphylactic Shock
SYM_CRP_ANAPHYLACTIC_SHOCK.description: Anaphylactic shock is a rare but severe allergic reaction that can be deadly if you don't treat it right away. It's most often caused by an allergy to food, insect bites, or certain medications. A shot of a drug called epinephrine is needed immediately.

SYM_CRP_CARCINOID_CRISIS.name: Carcinoid Crisis
SYM_CRP_CARCINOID_CRISIS.description: Carcinoid crisis is a life-threatening form of carcinoid syndrome that results from the release of an overwhelming amount of biologically active compounds from the tumour that may be triggered by extreme stress, tumour manipulation (biopsy or surgery) or by anesthesia.

SYM_CRP_SEROMA.name: Seroma
SYM_CRP_SEROMA.description: A seroma is a collection of fluid that builds up under the surface of your skin. Seromas may develop after a surgical procedure, most often at the site of the surgical incision or where tissue was removed. The seroma may take a few weeks to absorb on its own. Letting a seroma absorb on its own is the best way to heal naturally as long as no complications arise.

SYM_CRP_MILD_AR.name: Mild Allergic Reaction
SYM_CRP_MILD_AR.description: The patient has had a mild allergic reaction during or after the surgery. This may be a reaction to certain anaesthetic drugs used, environmental conditions, other types of drugs or materials used during surgery or even the wound dressings.

SYM_CRP_POSTOP_PAIN.name: Post-Operative Pain
SYM_CRP_POSTOP_PAIN.description: Postoperative pain is an anticipated and temporary increase in background pain that usually occurs following surgery. Postoperative pain remains a significant problem for patients and a challenge for anesthesiologists and acute pain medicine specialists. Despite advancements in the last two decades with increased use of multimodal analgesic regimens, advancements in regional anesthesia, and use of continuous analgesic infusions postoperatively, certain subgroups of patients will go on to develop chronic postoperative pain. Poorly managed postoperative pain can lead to complications and prolonged rehabilitation.

SYM_CRP_M_SCAR_TISSUE.name: Minor Scar Tissue
SYM_CRP_M_SCAR_TISSUE.description: A scar is a mark that is left on the skin after a wound or an injury to the surface of the skin has healed. The formation of scars or scar tissue is a normal part of the healing process, and occurs when the skin is cut due to injury or surgery. The growth of scar tissue is influenced by many factors, these include: The severity of the original injury. The size, depth and location of the wound on the body.

SYM_CRP_MRSA_INFECTION.name: Resistant S. Aureus Infection
SYM_CRP_MRSA_INFECTION.description: Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA) infection is caused by a type of staph bacteria that's become resistant to many of the antibiotics used to treat ordinary staph infections. These can quickly turn into deep, painful abscesses that require surgical draining. Sometimes the bacteria remain confined to the skin. But they can also burrow deep into the body, causing potentially life-threatening infections in bones, joints, surgical wounds, the bloodstream, heart valves and lungs.

SYM_CRP_LI_LOCALPOISIONING.name: Local Anaesthetic Poisoning
SYM_CRP_LI_LOCALPOISIONING.description: Local anaesthetic toxicity generally occurs as a result of therapeutic error. Situations leading to toxicity include inadvertent venous or arterial injection as well as too high a dose of ingested or topically administered local anaesthetic-containing preparations. The CNS is more sensitive to the effects of local anesthetics than the cardiac system and will generally manifest signs/symptoms of toxicity first.

SYM_CRP_WOUND_INFECTION2.name: Wound Infection
SYM_CRP_WOUND_INFECTION2.description: A wound infection occurs when germs, such as bacteria, grow within the damaged skin of a wound.

SYM_CRP_LI_BAGGY_SKIN.name: Asymmetric or ‘Baggy’ Skin Surface
SYM_CRP_LI_BAGGY_SKIN.description: When fat is removed from under the skin, some patients believe that their skin will shrink to fit their smoother, more toned body. However, this is not always the case, especially in the areas where the skin is significantly damaged. This is most common in the stomach and gluteal area, though it can occur all over the body. If skin has been stretched over fat deposits for a long period of time, it will may not tighten or shrink back when the fat deposits are no longer there. Any area that has stretch marks, for example, is unlikely to quickly tighten after liposuction. Excess and sagging skin is the most common cause for dimpling after liposuction.

SYM_CRP_PULMONARY_EMBOLISM.name: Pulmonary Embolism
SYM_CRP_PULMONARY_EMBOLISM.description: Pulmonary embolism (PE) is a blockage of an artery in the lungs by a substance that has moved from elsewhere in the body through the bloodstream (embolism). Most times, a pulmonary embolism is caused by blood clots that travel from the legs, or rarely, other parts of the body (deep vein thrombosis or DVT). Surgery increases a patient's risk for pulmonary embolism. \n\nTreatment Requirements: Cardiology or ICU dpt.

SYM_CRP_EYE_DIPLOPIA.name: Diplopia (Double Vision)
SYM_CRP_EYE_DIPLOPIA.description: Diplopia, also known as Double Vision, is the simultaneous perception of two images of a single object that may be displaced horizontally, vertically, diagonally (i.e., both vertically and horizontally), or rotationally in relation to each other.

SYM_CRP_EYE_CONJU_SCAR.name: Conjunctival Scarring
SYM_CRP_EYE_CONJU_SCAR.description: Conjunctival scaring can be a persistent problem after surgery. Instead of returning to the typical translucent white appearance, the conjunctiva can remain chronically hyperemic and pink. Many conditions can cause the conjunctiva, the thin transparent membrane that covers the white of the eye and the underside of the eyelids, to become scarred. Scarring damages the conjunctiva and prevents it retaining the protective mucous layer, which helps tears adhere to the surface.

SYM_CRP_FOLLICULITIS.name: Folliculitis
SYM_CRP_FOLLICULITIS.description: Folliculitis is a common skin condition in which hair follicles become inflamed. It's usually caused by a bacterial or fungal infection. At first it may look like small red bumps or white-headed pimples around hair follicles — the tiny pockets from which each hair grows.

SYM_CRP_EARLY_SAT.name: Early Satiety
SYM_CRP_EARLY_SAT.description: Early satiety is the inability to eat a full meal or feeling full after only a small amount of food.

SYM_CRP_HEPATOMEGALY.name: Hepatomegaly
SYM_CRP_HEPATOMEGALY.description: Hepatomegaly is when the patient has an abnormally large liver. It's often a sign of a serious health concern.

SYM_CRP_TURJESCENCE_JUGULAIRE.name: JVD
SYM_CRP_TURJESCENCE_JUGULAIRE.description: Jugular vein distention (JVD) occurs when the pressure inside the vena cava increases and appears as a bulge running down the right side of a person’s neck.

SYM_CRP_REFLUX_HJ.name: AJR
SYM_CRP_REFLUX_HJ.description: The abdominojugular reflux (AJR) sign is useful in diagnosing right ventricular failure, but is often performed and interpreted incorrectly.

SYM_CRP_PERINEURAL_INVASION.name: Perineural Invasion
SYM_CRP_PERINEURAL_INVASION.description: Perineural invasion, abbreviated PNI, refers to the invasion of cancer to the space surrounding a nerve.

SYM_CRP_METASTASIZED_CANCER.name: Metastatic Cancer
SYM_CRP_METASTASIZED_CANCER.description: Metastatic cancer is cancer that spreads from its site of origin to another part of the body.

SYM_CRP_MCP_VIR.name: Merkel Cell Polyomavirus Findings
SYM_CRP_MCP_VIR.description: Merkel cell polyomavirus (MCV or MCPyV) was first described in January 2008 in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania. It was the first example of a human viral pathogen discovered using unbiased metagenomic next-generation sequencing with a technique called digital transcriptome subtraction. MCV is one of seven currently known human oncoviruses. Detection of the virus is still at a research phase and is generally not available as a clinical test. Detection of viral DNA is performed by PCR or FISH.

SYM_CRP_MCP_VIR_EL.name: Electron Microscope MCV Findings
SYM_CRP_MCP_VIR_EL.description: Transmission electron microscopy of MCV VLP at ×50,000 magnification shows characteristic polyomaviral icosahedral capsid structures. This evidence points towards Merkel cell polyomavirus or MCV, which is one of seven currently known human oncoviruses.

SYM_CRP_FISH_POSLESION.name: FISH-Positive Lesion
SYM_CRP_FISH_POSLESION.description: The patient's lesion has been tested and FISH examinations have confirmed it is malignant.

SYM_CRP_BL_SPC.name: Salt-and-Pepper Chromatin
SYM_CRP_BL_SPC.description: In pathology, salt-and-pepper chromatin, also salt-and-pepper nuclei and stippled chromatin, refers to cell nuclei that demonstrate granular chromatin (on light microscopy). Salt-and-pepper chromatin is typically seen in endocrine tumours such as medullary thyroid carcinoma, neuroendocrine tumours and pheochromocytoma.

SYM_CRP_BL_MMCC.name: Mixed Mucinous Cells
SYM_CRP_BL_MMCC.description: Mucinous carcinoma can form along side of other types cancer cells, when this happens, the cancer is referred to as mixed mucinous carcinoma.

SYM_CRP_BL_HISRC.name: Signet Ring Cell Morphology
SYM_CRP_BL_HISRC.description: In histology, a signet ring cell is a cell with a large vacuole, the malignant type is seen predominantly in carcinomas. Signet ring cells are most frequently associated with various forms of cancer. By definition, a 50% or greater (High) signet-ring-cell component is required for a colorectal carcinoma to be designated as a signet-ring-cell colorectal carcinoma.

SYM_CRP_CDF_T_08.name: BRAF V600E Mutation Detected
SYM_CRP_CDF_T_08.description: The B-Raf proto-oncogene (BRAF) encodes a cytoplasmic serine/threonine kinase with a key role in regulating the mitogen-activated protein kinase signal transduction pathway. An activating missense mutation in codon 600 of exon 15 (V600E) of BRAF gene has been identified in multiple neoplasms including melanoma, colorectal carcinoma, papillary thyroid carcinoma, hairy cell leukemia, and Langerhans cell histiocytosis.

SYM_CRP_CDF_T_18.name: BRAF V599E Mutation Detected
SYM_CRP_CDF_T_18.description: The B-Raf proto-oncogene (BRAF) encodes a cytoplasmic serine/threonine kinase with a key role in regulating the mitogen-activated protein kinase signal transduction pathway. Mutations in the BRAF-gene are found in benign and malignant melanocytic lesions, >90% being a V599E mutation. This mutation results in constitutively active kinase function and increased colony formation in vitro.

SYM_CRP_CDF_T_11.name: BRCA1 Faulty Gene Findings
SYM_CRP_CDF_T_11.description: BRCA1/2 is a human tumour suppressor gene (also known as a caretaker gene) and is responsible for repairing DNA. BRCA1 and BRCA2 are unrelated proteins, but both are normally expressed in the cells of breast and other tissue, where they help repair damaged DNA, or destroy cells if DNA cannot be repaired. Women who carry a fault in BRCA1 or BRCA2 have a high lifetime risk of breast cancer, estimated to be in the range of 30-60%, and a lifetime ovarian cancer risk of about 20%. They can benefit from measures for prevention and early detection of cancers. Men who carry a fault in BRCA1 or BRCA2 may be at some increased risk of prostate cancer and male breast cancer.

SYM_CRP_CDF_T_12.name: GATA3 Mutation Findings
SYM_CRP_CDF_T_12.description: GATA3 is a transcription factor that in humans is encoded by the GATA3 gene. The GATA3 transcription factor is critical for the embryonic development of various tissues as well as for inflammatory and humoral immune responses and the proper functioning of the endothelium of blood vessels. Immuocytochemical analysis of GATA3 protein in breast cells is a valuable marker for diagnosing primary breast cancer, being tested as positive in up to 94% of cases.

SYM_CRP_CDF_T_13.name: Neurofibromatosis Type I (NF-1) Findings
SYM_CRP_CDF_T_13.description: Neurofibromatosis type I (NF-1) is a complex multi-system human disorder caused by the mutation of a gene on chromosome 17 that is responsible for production of a protein called neurofibromin which is needed for normal function in many human cell types. Approximately 10% of patients with neurofibromatosis I (NFI) patients will have central nervous system (CNS) tumours. The most common of these are hypothalamic-optic gliomas, followed by brainstem and cerebellar pilocytic astrocytomas.

SYM_CRP_CDF_T_14.name: Li-Fraumeni Syndrome (LFS)
SYM_CRP_CDF_T_14.description: Li-Fraumeni syndrome (LFS) is an inherited familial predisposition to a wide range of certain, often rare, cancers. This is due to a change (mutation) in a tumour suppressor gene known as TP53.

SYM_CRP_CDF_T_15.name: Loss of Heterozygosity
SYM_CRP_CDF_T_15.description: Loss of heterozygosity on chromosome arm 10q is the most frequent gene alteration for both primary and secondary glioblastomas; it occurs in 60-90% of cases. This mutation appears to be specific for glioblastoma multiforme and is found rarely in other tumour grades. This mutation is associated with poor survival. LOH at 10q plus 1 or 2 of the additional gene mutations appear to be frequent alterations and are most likely major players in the development of glioblastomas.

SYM_CRP_CDF_T_16.name: Overexpression of PDGFR
SYM_CRP_CDF_T_16.description: Platelet-derived growth factor (PDGF) is one among numerous growth factors that regulate cell growth and division. In particular, PDGF plays a significant role in blood vessel formation, the growth of blood vessels from already-existing blood vessel tissue, mitogenesis, i.e. proliferation, of mesenchymal cells. In Oncology, the PDGF gene acts as a major mitogen for glial cells by binding to the PDGF receptor (PDGFR). Amplification or overexpression of PDGFR is typical in the pathway leading to secondary glioblastomas.

SYM_CRP_CDF_T_17.name: IDH1/IDH2 Negative Findings
SYM_CRP_CDF_T_17.description: Isocitrate dehydrogenase (IDH) is a key enzyme involved in the conversion of isocitrate to α-ketoglutarate (α-KG) in the tricarboxylic acid (TCA) cycle. IDH mutation produces a neomorphic enzyme, which can lead to the abnormal accumulation of R-2-HG and promotes leukemogenesis. IDH mutation occurs in 20% of acute myeloid leukemia (AML) patients, mainly including IDH1 R132, IDH2 R140, and IDH2 R172.

SYM_CRP_CM_CD340_POS.name: HER2/neu (CD340) Positive Mutation
SYM_CRP_CM_CD340_POS.description: Receptor tyrosine-protein kinase erbB-2, also known as CD340, proto-oncogene Neu, Erbb2, or ERBB2, is a protein that in humans is encoded by the ERBB2 gene. ERBB is abbreviated from erythroblastic oncogene B, a gene isolated from avian genome. It is also frequently called HER2 or HER2/neu. HER2 is a member of the human epidermal growth factor receptor (HER/EGFR/ERBB) family. Amplification or over-expression of this oncogene has been shown to play an important role in the development and progression of certain aggressive types of breast cancer. In recent years the protein has become an important biomarker and target of therapy for approximately 30% of breast cancer patients.

SYM_CRP_CDF_T_22.name: EGFR Mutation Over-Expression
SYM_CRP_CDF_T_22.description: The epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR; ErbB-1; HER1 in humans) is a transmembrane protein that is a receptor for members of the epidermal growth factor family (EGF family) of extracellular protein ligands. Mutations that lead to EGFR overexpression (known as upregulation or amplification) have been associated with a number of cancers, including adenocarcinoma of the lung (40% of cases), anal cancers, glioblastoma (50%) and epithelian tumours of the head and neck (80-100%). These somatic mutations involving EGFR lead to its constant activation, which produces uncontrolled cell division.

SYM_CRP_CDF_T_23.name: High-Degree Microsatellite Instability
SYM_CRP_CDF_T_23.description: Microsatellite instability (MSI) is the condition of genetic hypermutability (predisposition to mutation) that results from impaired DNA mismatch repair (MMR). The presence of MSI represents phenotypic evidence that MMR is not functioning normally. MMR corrects errors that spontaneously occur during DNA replication, such as single base mismatches or short insertions and deletions. The proteins involved in MMR correct polymerase errors by forming a complex that binds to the mismatched section of DNA, excises the error, and inserts the correct sequence in its place. Cells with abnormally functioning MMR are unable to correct errors that occur during DNA replication and consequently accumulate errors. Microsatellite instability is associated with many forms of cancer but is most prevalent in associations with colon cancers.

SYM_CRP_CDF_T_24.name: CDH1 (CD324) Mutation Findings
SYM_CRP_CDF_T_24.description: Cadherin-1 also known as CAM 120/80 or epithelial cadherin (E-cadherin) or uvomorulin is a protein that in humans is encoded by the CDH1 gene. CDH1 has also been designated as CD324 (cluster of differentiation 324). It is a tumour suppressor gene. Loss of E-cadherin function or expression has been implicated in cancer progression and metastasis. E-cadherin downregulation decreases the strength of cellular adhesion within a tissue, resulting in an increase in cellular motility. This in turn may allow cancer cells to cross the basement membrane and invade surrounding tissues.

SYM_CRP_SQLL_CARCINOMA_BLOOD_MARKERS.name: Squamous Cell Carcinoma Blood Markers
SYM_CRP_SQLL_CARCINOMA_BLOOD_MARKERS.description: A blood test reveals blood markers like CA-125, CA-19-9 and SCC antigens are elevated, pointing towards a Squamous Cell Carcinoma tumour somewhere.

SYM_CRP_ADENOCARCINOMA_BLOOD_MARKERS.name: Adenocarcinoma Blood Markers
SYM_CRP_ADENOCARCINOMA_BLOOD_MARKERS.description: A blood test reveals certain blood markers are elevated, pointing towards a Adenocarcinoma tumour somewhere.

SYM_CRP_MELANOMA_BLOOD_MARKERS.name: Melanoma Blood Markers
SYM_CRP_MELANOMA_BLOOD_MARKERS.description: A blood test reveals certain blood markers antigens are elevated pointing towards a Melanoma tumour somewhere.

SYM_CRP_SM_P_004.name: Micropapillary Invasion Pattern
SYM_CRP_SM_P_004.description: A micropapillary pattern is defined as a pattern showing micropapillary structures without a fibrovascular core. It is well known that the presence of a micropapillary pattern leads to a poorer prognosis in ovary, breast and bladder cancer.

SYM_CRP_SM_P_005.name: Psammoma Body
SYM_CRP_SM_P_005.description: Psammoma bodies are round microscopic calcific collections. Psammoma bodies are a common finding in well-differentiated serous carcinoma and they may be found in association with malignant glandular cells detected with cervicovaginal cytology.

SYM_CRP_SM_P_007.name: Thyroid Transcription Factor 1 Positive
SYM_CRP_SM_P_007.description: NK2 homeobox 1 (NKX2-1), also known as thyroid transcription factor 1 (TTF-1), is a protein which in humans is encoded by the NKX2-1 gene. TTF-1 positive cells are found in the lung as type II pneumocytes and club cells. In the thyroid, follicular and parafollicular cells are also positive for TTF-1. For lung cancers, adenocarcinomas are usually positive, while squamous cell carcinomas and large cell carcinomas are rarely positive. Small cell carcinomas (of any primary site) are usually positive. TTF1 is more than merely a clinical marker of lung adenocarcinoma. It plays an active role in sustaining lung cancer cells in view of the experimental observation that it is mutated in lung cancer.

SYM_CRP_SM_P_008.name: Mucin 4 Highly Expressed
SYM_CRP_SM_P_008.description: Mucin 4 (MUC 4) is a mucin protein that in humans is encoded by the MUC4 gene. Like other mucins, MUC-4 is a high-molecular weight glycoprotein. The major constituents of mucus, the viscous secretion that covers epithelial surfaces such as those in the trachea, colon, and cervix, are highly glycosylated proteins called mucins. These glycoproteins play important roles in the protection of the epithelial cells and have been implicated in epithelial renewal and differentiation. MUC-4 has been found to play various roles in the progression of cancer, particularly due to its signaling and anti-adhesive properties which contribute to tumour development and metastasis.

SYM_CRP_IS_001.name: Tumour Protein P63 Bright Expression
SYM_CRP_IS_001.description: Tumour protein p63, typically referred to as p63, also known as transformation-related protein 63 is a protein that in humans is encoded by the TP63 (also known as the p63) gene. P63 immunostaining has utility for head and neck squamous cell carcinomas, differentiating prostatic adenocarcinoma (the most common type of prostate cancer) and benign prostatic tissue.

SYM_CRP_IS_002.name: Tumour Protein P53 Abnormal Expression
SYM_CRP_IS_002.description: Tumour protein p53, also known as p53, cellular tumour antigen p53 (UniProt name), tumour suppressor p53 is any isoform of a protein encoded by homologous genes in various organisms, such as TP53 (humans) and Trp53 (mice). This homolog (originally thought to be, and often spoken of as, a single protein) is crucial in multicellular organisms, where it prevents cancer formation, and thus functions as a tumour suppressor. The TP53 gene can also be modified by mutagens (chemicals, radiation, or viruses), increasing the likelihood for uncontrolled cell division.

SYM_CRP_SM_P_010.name: Mucin 2 Highly Expressed
SYM_CRP_SM_P_010.description: Mucins are a family of high molecular weight, heavily glycosylated proteins (glycoconjugates) produced by epithelial tissues in most animals. Mucin 2, oligomeric mucus gel-forming, also known as MUC2, is a protein that in humans is encoded by the MUC2 gene. Mucin 2 is particularly prominent in the gut where it is secreted from goblet cells in the epithelial lining into the lumen of the large intestine. In Oncology, overexpression of MUC-2 might protect against antitumour immune effectors by forming a mucous layer, thus promoting tumour development.

SYM_CRP_SM_P_011.name: Mucin 5AC Highly Expressed
SYM_CRP_SM_P_011.description: Heavily glycosylated secreted mucin MUC5AC, by the virtue of its cysteine-rich repeats, can form inter- and intramolecular disulfide linkages resulting in complex polymers, which in turn craft the framework of the polymeric mucus gel on epithelial cell surfaces. MUC5AC is a molecule with versatile functional implications including barrier functions to epithelial cells, host–pathogen interaction, immune cell attraction to sites of premalignant or malignant lesions and tumor progression in a context-dependent manner. Differential expression, glycosylation and localization of MUC5AC have been associated with a plethora of benign and malignant pathologies.

SYM_CRP_SM_P_012.name: Mucin 1 Highly Expressed
SYM_CRP_SM_P_012.description: Mucin 1, cell surface associated (MUC1), also called polymorphic epithelial mucin (PEM) or epithelial membrane antigen or EMA, is a mucin encoded by the MUC1 gene in humans. MUC1 is a glycoprotein with extensive O-linked glycosylation of its extracellular domain. Mucins protect the body from infection by pathogen binding to oligosaccharides in the extracellular domain, preventing the pathogen from reaching the cell surface. Overexpression of MUC1 is often associated with colon, breast, ovarian, lung and pancreatic cancers.

SYM_CRP_SM_P_013.name: Napsin A Highly Expressed
SYM_CRP_SM_P_013.description: Napsin A is a functional aspartic proteinase, expressed in the cytoplasm of healthy lung parenchyma. It is homologous with the polypeptide TAO2 and involved in maturation of the biologically active surfactant protein B. It also consists of a 38-kDa protein, a single-chain protein expressed in type II pneumocytes, alveolar macrophages, renal tubules, and exocrine glands and ducts in the pancreas. Napsin A may be an alternative marker for primary lung cancer.

SYM_CRP_IS_003.name: Alpha-Fetoprotein Elevated Levels
SYM_CRP_IS_003.description: Alpha-fetoprotein is a protein that in humans is encoded by the AFP gene. The AFP gene is located on the q arm of chromosome 4. AFP is a major plasma protein produced by the yolk sac and the fetal liver during fetal development. AFP is found mainly in liver cancer and nonseminomatous germ cell tumors, which are rare. These are found in the pineal gland in the brain. Some people with cirrhosis or chronic active hepatitis also have higher blood levels of AFP. Like any elevated tumor marker, elevated AFP by itself is not diagnostic, only suggestive.

SYM_CRP_CM_CD10_BRIGHT.name: Neprilysin (CD10) Bright Expression
SYM_CRP_CM_CD10_BRIGHT.description: Neprilysin, also known as membrane metallo-endopeptidase, neutral endopeptidase, cluster of differentiation 10, and common acute lymphoblastic leukemia antigen is an enzyme that in humans is encoded by the MME gene. Identified in common acute lymphoblastic leukemia as a cancer specific antigen, CD10 is a cell surface ectoenzyme widely expressed on different types of cells.

SYM_CRP_CM_CD10_NEG.name: Neprilysin (CD10) Negative Expression
SYM_CRP_CM_CD10_NEG.description: Neprilysin, also known as membrane metallo-endopeptidase, neutral endopeptidase, cluster of differentiation 10, and common acute lymphoblastic leukemia antigen is an enzyme that in humans is encoded by the MME gene. Identified in common acute lymphoblastic leukemia as a cancer specific antigen, CD10 is a cell surface ectoenzyme widely expressed on different types of cells.

SYM_CRP_CM_CD13_NEG.name: Aminopeptidase N (CD13) Bright Expression
SYM_CRP_CM_CD13_NEG.description: Membrane alanyl aminopeptidase (EC 3.4.11.2) also known as alanyl aminopeptidase (AAP) or aminopeptidase N (AP-N) is an enzyme that in humans is encoded by the ANPEP gene. Aminopeptidase N is located in the small-intestinal and renal microvillar membrane, and also in other plasma membranes. In the small intestine aminopeptidase N plays a role in the final digestion of peptides generated from hydrolysis of proteins by gastric and pancreatic proteases. Its function in proximal tubular epithelial cells and other cell types is less clear. Defects in this gene appear to be a cause of various types of leukemia or lymphoma.

SYM_CRP_CM_CD56_BRIGHT.name: NC Adhesion Molecule (CD56) Bright Expression
SYM_CRP_CM_CD56_BRIGHT.description: Neural cell adhesion molecule (NCAM), also called CD56, is a homophilic binding glycoprotein expressed on the surface of neurons, glia and skeletal muscle. Although CD56 is often considered a marker of neural lineage commitment due to its discovery site, CD56 expression is also found in, among others, the hematopoietic system. Here, the expression of CD56 is most stringently associated with, but certainly not limited to, natural killer cells. In anatomic pathology, pathologists make use of CD56 immunohistochemistry to recognize certain tumours. tumours that are CD56-positive are myeloma, myeloid leukemia, neuroendocrine tumours, Wilms' tumour, neuroblastoma, extranodal NK/T-cell lymphoma, nasal type, pancreatic acinar cell carcinoma, pheochromocytoma, paraganglioma, small cell lung carcinoma, and the Ewing's sarcoma family of tumours.

SYM_CRP_CM_CD44_BRIGHT.name: CD44 Antigen Bright Expression
SYM_CRP_CM_CD44_BRIGHT.description: The CD44 antigen is a cell-surface glycoprotein involved in cell–cell interactions, cell adhesion and migration. In humans, the CD44 antigen is encoded by the CD44 gene on Chromosome 11. CD44 is a cell surface adhesion receptor that is highly expressed in many cancers and regulates metastasis via recruitment of CD44 to the cell surface. Its interaction with appropriate extracellular matrix ligands promotes the migration and invasion processes involved in metastases.

SYM_CRP_CM_CD44v6_BRIGHT.name: CD44v6 Antigen Bright Expression
SYM_CRP_CM_CD44v6_BRIGHT.description: CD44v6 is an isoform of the CD44 family of transmembrane glycoproteins. High CD44v6 expression in epithelial cancer correlates with tumour growth, invasion, metastasis, and recurrence. CD44v6 is a co-receptor of the receptor tyrosine kinases c-Met, RON and VEGFR-2.

SYM_CRP_CM_KI67_LLI.name: Antigen KI-67 Low Labeling Index
SYM_CRP_CM_KI67_LLI.description: Antigen KI-67 also known as Ki-67 or MKI67 is a protein that in humans is encoded by the MKI67 gene (antigen identified by monoclonal antibody Ki-67). Ki-67 is an excellent marker to determine the growth fraction of a given cell population. The fraction of Ki-67-positive tumour cells (the Ki-67 labeling index) is often correlated with the clinical course of cancer.

SYM_CRP_CM_KI67_BLI.name: Antigen KI-67 Low Labeling Index
SYM_CRP_CM_KI67_BLI.description: Antigen KI-67 also known as Ki-67 or MKI67 is a protein that in humans is encoded by the MKI67 gene (antigen identified by monoclonal antibody Ki-67). Ki-67 is an excellent marker to determine the growth fraction of a given cell population. The fraction of Ki-67-positive tumour cells (the Ki-67 labeling index) is often correlated with the clinical course of cancer.

SYM_CRP_CM_CD146_BRIGHT.name: MCAM (CD146) Bright Expression
SYM_CRP_CM_CD146_BRIGHT.description: Melanoma cell adhesion molecule (MCAM) is a cell adhesion molecule that is abnormally expressed in a variety of tumours and is closely associated with tumour metastasis.

SYM_CRP_CM_CK7_BRIGHT.name: Keratin 7 (CK7) Bright Expression
SYM_CRP_CM_CK7_BRIGHT.description: Keratin, type II cytoskeletal 7 also known as cytokeratin-7 (CK-7) or keratin-7 (K7) or sarcolectin (SCL) is a protein that in humans is encoded by the KRT7 gene. Keratin-7 is found in simple glandular epithelia, and in transitional epithelium. Epithelial cells of the lung and breast both contain keratin-7, but some other glandular epithelia, such as those of the colon and prostate, do not. Because the keratin-7 antigen is found in both healthy and neoplastic cells, antibodies to CK7 can be used in immunohistochemistry to distinguish ovarian and transitional cell carcinomas from colonic and prostate cancers, respectively. It is commonly used together with CK20 when making such diagnoses.

SYM_CRP_CM_CK7_NEG.name: Keratin 7 (CK7) Negative Expression
SYM_CRP_CM_CK7_NEG.description: Keratin, type II cytoskeletal 7 also known as cytokeratin-7 (CK-7) or keratin-7 (K7) or sarcolectin (SCL) is a protein that in humans is encoded by the KRT7 gene. Keratin-7 is found in simple glandular epithelia, and in transitional epithelium. Epithelial cells of the lung and breast both contain keratin-7, but some other glandular epithelia, such as those of the colon and prostate, do not. Because the keratin-7 antigen is found in both healthy and neoplastic cells, antibodies to CK7 can be used in immunohistochemistry to distinguish ovarian and transitional cell carcinomas from colonic and prostate cancers, respectively. It is commonly used together with CK20 when making such diagnoses.

SYM_CRP_CM_CK20_BRIGHT.name: Keratin 20 (CK20) Bright Expression
SYM_CRP_CM_CK20_BRIGHT.description: Keratin 20, often abbreviated CK20, is a protein that in humans is encoded by the KRT20 gene. In immunohistochemistry, antibodies to CK20 can be used to identify a range of adenocarcinoma arising from epithelia that normally contain the CK20 protein. For example, the protein is commonly found in colorectal cancer, transitional cell carcinomas and in Merkel cell carcinoma, but is absent in lung cancer, prostate cancer, and non-mucinous ovarian cancer. It is often used in combination with antibodies to CK7 to distinguish different types of glandular tumour.

SYM_CRP_CM_CK20_NEG.name: Keratin 20 (CK20) Negative Expression
SYM_CRP_CM_CK20_NEG.description: Keratin 20, often abbreviated CK20, is a protein that in humans is encoded by the KRT20 gene. In immunohistochemistry, antibodies to CK20 can be used to identify a range of adenocarcinoma arising from epithelia that normally contain the CK20 protein. For example, the protein is commonly found in colorectal cancer, transitional cell carcinomas and in Merkel cell carcinoma, but is absent in lung cancer, prostate cancer, and non-mucinous ovarian cancer. It is often used in combination with antibodies to CK7 to distinguish different types of glandular tumour.

SYM_CRP_CM_CD274_BRIGHT.name: PD-L1 (CD274) Bright Expression
SYM_CRP_CM_CD274_BRIGHT.description: Programmed death-ligand 1 (PD-L1) also known as cluster of differentiation 274 (CD274) or B7 homolog 1 (B7-H1) is a protein that in humans is encoded by the CD274 gene. Programmed death-ligand 1 (PD-L1) is a 40kDa type 1 transmembrane protein that has been speculated to play a major role in suppressing the adaptive arm of immune system during particular events such as pregnancy, tissue allografts, autoimmune disease and other disease states such as hepatitis.

SYM_CRP_CMA_CA153.name: Cancer Antigen 15-3 High Blood Levels
SYM_CRP_CMA_CA153.description: Cancer antigen 15-3 (CA15-3) is a protein made by a variety of cells, particularly breast cancer cells. The protein moves into the blood, where it can be measured. CA15-3 levels are higher than normal in most women with breast cancer that has spread to other parts of the body (called metastatic breast cancer). Not all types of breast cancer will cause CA 15-3 levels to rise, as some types of cancer cells don’t over-produce the antigen.

SYM_CRP_CMA_CA2729.name: Cancer Antigen 27-29 High Blood Levels
SYM_CRP_CMA_CA2729.description: Cancer Antigen 27-29 is one of several antigens that doctors use as tumour markers for breast cancer. These markers tend to increase whenever cancer is present. The CA 27.29 test is primarily used to monitor metastatic breast cancer and less commonly to detect recurrence in people previously treated for early-stage breast cancer.

SYM_CRP_ELAVATED_5HIAA.name: 5-HIAA High Urine Levels
SYM_CRP_ELAVATED_5HIAA.description: The patient has elevated levels of 5-HIAA (5-hydroxyindoleacetic acid) in their urine. Serotonin, measured in urine as 5 hydroxyindoleacetic acid (5HIAA). Elevated levels of 5HIAA is correlated with carcinoid syndrome and carcinoid crises. Prolonged exposure of the heart valves to high levels of serotonin is associated with complications of the disease.

SYM_CRP_CGA_BL_DETECTED.name: Chromogranin-A High Blood Levels
SYM_CRP_CGA_BL_DETECTED.description: The patient has elevated levels of the tumour marker Chromogranin-A in their blood. Chromogranin A (CgA) is a general marker associated with all carcinoid tumours. It is a protein produced by tumour cells and released. Chromogranin A is associated with other hormones and may be involved in their regulation. There is a normal level of Chromogranin A in the blood, and the range currently is roughly less than 39ng/L.

SYM_CRP_CMA_CA125.name: Cancer Antigen 125 High Blood Levels
SYM_CRP_CMA_CA125.description: CA-125 also known as mucin 16 or MUC16 is a protein that in humans is encoded by the MUC16 gene. MUC16 is a member of the mucin family glycoproteins. A CA 125 test measures the amount of the protein CA 125 (cancer antigen 125) in your blood. A CA 125 test may be used to monitor certain cancers during and after treatment. In some cases, a CA 125 test may be used to look for early signs of ovarian cancer in people with a very high risk of the disease. The amount of CA-125 is higher with ovarian cancer. In premenopausal women, many other illnesses or diseases besides cancer can cause higher levels of CA-125.

SYM_CRP_PSA_BL_DETECTED.name: Prostate Specific Antigen High Levels
SYM_CRP_PSA_BL_DETECTED.description: Prostate specific antigen (PSA) is a glycoprotein produced solely by the prostate. Its function is to liquefy semen. Small amounts leak into the bloodstream, where it can be measured. Prostate specific antigen is tissue-specific but not cancer-specific. Elevated levels can occur in men with benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH), prostatitis, urinary tract infection or prostatic infarction.

SYM_CRP_UAS_PCA3.name: Prostate Cancer Antigen 3 High Levels
SYM_CRP_UAS_PCA3.description: Prostate cancer antigen 3 (PCA3) is highly expressed in prostatic tumours. Testing has returned high urine levels of PCA3.

SYM_CRP_HPV_HR.name: High-Risk HPV
SYM_CRP_HPV_HR.description: High-risk HPV can cause normal cells to become abnormal. These abnormal cells can lead to cancer over time. High-risk HPV most often affects cells in the cervix, but it can also cause cancer in the vagina, vulva, anus, penis, mouth, and throat.

SYM_CRP_CMA_CAHP1.name: Hepatocyte Paraffin 1 Antigen Detected
SYM_CRP_CMA_CAHP1.description: Hepatocyte paraffin 1 is a monoclonal antibody that has been developed specifically to react with hepatocytes in routine formalin-fixed and paraffin-embedded surgical pathology tissues. It results in a distinct, granular cytoplasmic staining of hepatocytes but fails to react with bile ducts and nonparenchymal liver cells. The antibody decorates a majority of hepatocellular carcinomas, including fibrolamellar variants. It fails to react with a wide variety of other adult malignancies, with the exception of focal staining in a few gastrointestinal malignancies, including a subpopulation of gastric carcinomas.

SYM_CRP_EGB.name: Eosinophilic Granular Bodies
SYM_CRP_EGB.description: The frequent presence of eosinophilic granular bodies is often a useful clue in deterring a higher grade neoplasm toward a lower grade tumour, such as a pleomorphic xanthoastrocytoma (PXA) or a pilocytic astrocytoma.

SYM_CRP_ASCITES.name: Ascites
SYM_CRP_ASCITES.description: Abdominal swelling caused by accumulation of fluid, most often related to liver disease.

SYM_CRP_PLEURAL_EFFUSION.name: Pleural Effusion
SYM_CRP_PLEURAL_EFFUSION.description: Pleural effusion, sometimes referred to as “water on the lungs,” is the build-up of excess fluid between the layers of the pleura outside the lungs. The pleura are thin membranes that line the lungs and the inside of the chest cavity and act to lubricate and facilitate breathing.

SYM_CRP_DIFF_URINE.name: Urinary Retention
SYM_CRP_DIFF_URINE.description: Difficulty urinating and completely emptying the bladder. Urinary retention can have causes that aren't due to underlying disease. Examples include holding in urine intentionally or medication side effects.

SYM_CRP_ABSCESS.name: Abscess
SYM_CRP_ABSCESS.description: Buildup of pus within the tissue surrounding a surgical wound.

SYM_CRP_OAP.name: Pulmonary Edema
SYM_CRP_OAP.description: Pulmonary edema is fluid accumulation in the tissue and air spaces of the lungs.

SYM_CRP_MARBRURE.name: Livedo Reticularis
SYM_CRP_MARBRURE.description: Livedo reticularis is a common skin finding consisting of a mottled reticulated vascular pattern that appears as a lace-like purplish discoloration of the skin. The discoloration is caused by swelling of the venules owing to obstruction of capillaries by small blood clots.

SYM_CRP_PERITONITE.name: Peritoneal Fluid Shows Infection
SYM_CRP_PERITONITE.description: Peritoneal fluid analysis has shown a result that is suggestive of an intraperitoneal infection.

SYM_CRP_ENDOCARDITIS.name: Endocarditis Detected
SYM_CRP_ENDOCARDITIS.description: Echocardiography has discovered a vegatative growth on a valve in the heart, strongly suggestive of endocarditis.

SYM_CRP_DIABETES_SECONDARY.name: Diabetes
SYM_CRP_DIABETES_SECONDARY.description: Diabetes mellitus refers to a group of diseases that affect the metabolism of glucose. There are many types of diabetes, but generally there are too high levels of glucose in the blood.

SYM_CRP_CARPAL_TUNNEL.name: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome
SYM_CRP_CARPAL_TUNNEL.description: A condition where the median nerve in the wrist is compressed by the surrounding structures when passing through the tunnel near the base of the wrist. Pain and a sleeping sensation of the hand are the main symptoms.

SYM_CRP_ARF_FONCTION.name: Acute Renal Failure
SYM_CRP_ARF_FONCTION.description: Acute renal failure refers to an abrupt loss of kidney function, effectively seizing the ability to filter the blood and produce urine. The causes can be many, but are usually due to extensive tissue damage or decreased kidney blood flow.

SYM_CRP_ARF_ORGANIC.name: Acute Renal Failure
SYM_CRP_ARF_ORGANIC.description: Acute renal failure refers to an abrupt loss of kidney function, effectively seizing the ability to filter the blood and produce urine. The causes can be many, but are usually due to extensive tissue damage or decreased kidney blood flow.

SYM_CRP_HEART_ATTACK.name: Heart Attack
SYM_CRP_HEART_ATTACK.description: Electrocardiography has detected an ongoing heart attack.

SYM_CRP_STK.name: Brain Arteries Blocked
SYM_CRP_STK.description: Echocardiography has revealed the blockage of multiple arteries in the brain due to blood clots.

SYM_CRP_TAR.name: Traumatic Aortic Rupture
SYM_CRP_TAR.description: A patient suffered a traumatic rupture of the aorta, resulting in a massive internal hemorrhage.

SYM_CRP_THROMBOSIS.name: Superficial Thrombosis
SYM_CRP_THROMBOSIS.description: Ultrasonography has discovered superficial vein thrombosis.

SYM_CRP_INSUFFISANCE_SR_AIGUE.name: Acute Adrenal Insufficiency
SYM_CRP_INSUFFISANCE_SR_AIGUE.description: A primary adrenal insufficiency caused by a sudden defective production of adrenal steroids.

SYM_CRP_GALLSTONES.name: Gallstones
SYM_CRP_GALLSTONES.description: Gallstones, also called cholelithiasis, are concretions of  usually made from bilirubin, bile acids and calcium formed in the gall bladder. Gallstones usually do not cause symptoms, however, if they block the bile duct they can cause a variety of serious conditions.

SYM_CRP_HTAP.name: Pulmonary Hypertension Detected
SYM_CRP_HTAP.description: Echocardiography has revealed pulmonary hypertension (hypertension in the arteries of the lungs).

SYM_CRP_HEMORRAGIE_DIG_HAUTE.name: UDH
SYM_CRP_HEMORRAGIE_DIG_HAUTE.description: During an upper digestive hemorrhage (UDH), the bleeding occurs upstream of the Treitz ligament (oesophagus, stomach and duodenum).

SYM_CRP_HEMORRAGIE_DIG_BASSE.name: LDH
SYM_CRP_HEMORRAGIE_DIG_BASSE.description: In a lower digestive hemorrhage (LDH), blood loss originates downstream from the Treitz ligament (small intestine, colon, rectum and anus).

SYM_CRP_REJET_GREFFE.name: Transplant Rejection
SYM_CRP_REJET_GREFFE.description: The patient is in the process of rejection of his transplant, which corresponds to the destruction of the graft transplanted into an organism, due to an immune response directed against him.

SYM_CRP_CELL_REJECTION.name: Cellular Rejection
SYM_CRP_CELL_REJECTION.description: Cellular Rejection occurs when the body's blood cells identify a transplanted organ as foreign object and begin mounting an army of cells to attack it. Having rejection does not mean that the patient will lose their transplanted organ, but it is extremely important that rejection is diagnosed and treated as soon as possible.

SYM_CRP_HEPATIC_ENCE.name: Hepatic Encephalopathy
SYM_CRP_HEPATIC_ENCE.description: Hepatic encephalopathy is a decline in brain function that occurs as a result of severe liver disease. In this condition, the liver can't adequately remove toxins. This causes a buildup of toxins in the bloodstream, which can lead to brain damage.

SYM_CRP_SUPRACLAVICULAR_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_CRP_SUPRACLAVICULAR_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_CRP_AXILLARY_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_CRP_AXILLARY_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_CRP_INGUINAL_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_CRP_INGUINAL_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_CRP_SOUFFLE_ABDOMINAL.name: Abdominal Murmur
SYM_CRP_SOUFFLE_ABDOMINAL.description: The presence of an abdominal murmur may reflect the existence of an aneurysm of the abdominal aorta or a narrowing of the renal arteries.

SYM_CRP_POUL_HYPERPULSE.name: Bounding Pulse
SYM_CRP_POUL_HYPERPULSE.description: A bounding pulse signifies high pulse pressure. It may be due to low peripheral resistance, increased cardiac output, increased stroke volume or decreased distensibility of the arterial system.

SYM_CRP_ABOLITION_POULS.name: No Pulse
SYM_CRP_ABOLITION_POULS.description: There is no perceived pulse to a member.

SYM_CRP_HEMO_INSTA.name: Hemodynamic Instability
SYM_CRP_HEMO_INSTA.description: Hemodynamic instability occurs when there's abnormal or unstable blood pressure, which can cause inadequate blood flow. This symptom can be life-threatening and may require ICU intervention.

SYM_CRP_H_BENT_DIGIT.name: Bent Digit of the Hand
SYM_CRP_H_BENT_DIGIT.description: The patient has presented with a finger that is bent inwards on their hand and while they still have motor control of the finger the patient is unable to correct the bending themselves.

SYM_CRP_H_THICKCORD.name: Prominent Cord
SYM_CRP_H_THICKCORD.description: The patient has a hardened and thick muscle/tissue cord on their hand.

SYM_CRP_THPAFA.name: Thickening of the Palmar Fascia
SYM_CRP_THPAFA.description: The palmar fascia is a triangular structure in the palm of the hand based distally. When thickened it becomes tight and shortened, contracting the fingers inwards toward the palm.

SYM_CRP_DIMPLES.name: Dimpling of the Skin
SYM_CRP_DIMPLES.description: The patient's skin has a dimpled appearance, similar to the surface structure of an orange peel or golf ball.

SYM_CRP_PSEUDOMEMBRANE.name: Pseudomembrane
SYM_CRP_PSEUDOMEMBRANE.description: A pseudomembrane is a false membrane in the eye or throat that sometimes develops during infections and inflammations. It typically covers the conjunctiva, or the white part of the eyes, and can be very uncomfortable, and a pseudomembrane also develops when the patient has Diphtheria.

SYM_CRP_NECROSIS.name: Necrosis
SYM_CRP_NECROSIS.description: Necrosis is a form of cell injury which results in the premature death of cells in living tissue by autolysis.

SYM_CRP_APTHOUS_ULCERS.name: Apthous Ulcers
SYM_CRP_APTHOUS_ULCERS.description: Oral examination has revealed the presence of apthous ulcers. These are recurrent round or oval sores inside the mouth on an area where the skin is not tightly bound to the underlying bone, such as on the inside of the lips and cheeks or underneath the tongue.

SYM_CRP_LPT1.name: Large Primary Tumour Detected
SYM_CRP_LPT1.description: Testing has detected a Large Primary Tumour within the patient, this can sometimes be sign that the cancer is in a more advanced state.

SYM_CRP_LPT2.name: Abnormal Mass Detected
SYM_CRP_LPT2.description: Testing has detected an abnormal mass within the patient, further testing is required to identify malignancy, type or progression.

SYM_CRP_MCRO_CYSTS.name: Microcysts
SYM_CRP_MCRO_CYSTS.description: A microcyst is simply a very small cyst. Cysts are an abnormal, usually noncancerous growth filled with liquid or a semisolid substance, sometimes causing pain.

SYM_CRP_NIGHT_URGE.name: Frequent Urination at Night
SYM_CRP_NIGHT_URGE.description: A frequent need to get up and go to the bathroom to urinate at night is called nocturia. It differs from enuresis, or bedwetting, in which the person does not arouse from sleep, but the bladder empties anyway. Nocturia is a common cause of sleep loss, especially among older adults.

SYM_CRP_BL_LSAC.name: Large Anaplastic Cells
SYM_CRP_BL_LSAC.description: Anaplastic cells display marked pleomorphism (variability). The nuclei are characteristically extremely hyperchromatic (darkly stained) and large. The nuclear-cytoplasmic ratio may approach 1:1 instead of the normal 1:4 or 1:6. Giant cells that are considerably larger than their neighbors may be formed and possess either one enormous nucleus or several nuclei (syncytia). Anaplastic nuclei are variable and bizarre in size and shape.

SYM_CRP_HYPERTROPHIC_PO.name: Hypertrophic Pulmonary Osteoarthropathy
SYM_CRP_HYPERTROPHIC_PO.description: Hypertrophic pulmonary osteoarthropathy (HPO) is a rare paraneoplastic syndrome that is frequently associated with lung cancer. Hypertrophic osteoarthropathy (HOA) is a clinical syndrome of clubbing of the fingers and toes, enlargement of the extremities, and painful, swollen joints. This condition is characterized by symmetric periostitis involving the radius and fibula and, to a lesser extent, the femur, humerus, metacarpals, and metatarsals.

SYM_CRP_COLONOFIND.name: Colonoscopic Findings
SYM_CRP_COLONOFIND.description: Testing has reported unusual colon findings. Further tests or results from the biopsy will help determine if the findings are cause for concern.

SYM_CRP_PROPTOSIS.name: Proptosis
SYM_CRP_PROPTOSIS.description: The patient has abnormal protrusion or displacement of the eyes.

SYM_CRP_FACIAL_NERVE_PARA.name: Facial Nerve Paralysis
SYM_CRP_FACIAL_NERVE_PARA.description: Facial nerve paralysis is characterised by facial weakness, usually only in one side of the face, with other symptoms possibly including loss of taste, hyperacusis and decreased salivation and tear secretion.

SYM_CRP_DYSPLASIA.name: Dysplasia
SYM_CRP_DYSPLASIA.description: Dysplasia (Cytologic Atypia) the presence of cells of an abnormal type within a tissue, which may signify a stage preceding the development of cancer.

SYM_CRP_EPI_HIGH1.name: Elevated Epidermoid Cell Count
SYM_CRP_EPI_HIGH1.description: Testing has detected an abnormally high level of epidermoid cells. This can indicate a worse prognosis or more developed cancer.

SYM_CRP_OOST.name: Overgrowth of Scar Tissue
SYM_CRP_OOST.description: Overgrowth of scar tissue occurs when too much collagen is produced at the site of the wound. The scar keeps growing, even after the wound has healed.

SYM_CRP_BLEEDING2.name: Light Bleeding
SYM_CRP_BLEEDING2.description: The patient is bleeding lightly, there is typically no change in vital signs and fluid resuscitation is not usually necessary. Stopping the bleeding may be necessary to prevent further complications.

SYM_CRP_BLEEDING3.name: Moderate Bleeding
SYM_CRP_BLEEDING3.description: The patient is bleeding somewhat heavily, invention is necessary to prevent hypovolemic shock.

SYM_CRP_RAPID_BLINKING.name: Excessive Blinking
SYM_CRP_RAPID_BLINKING.description: Blinking is a reflex, which means your body does it automatically. You can also make yourself blink when you want to. Excessive blinking is when you blink more than you want to. Excessive blinking is caused by over-stimulation of the blinking reflex. This is most commonly due to a foreign body in the eye. This can be as simple as an ingrown eyelash rubbing against the front of the eyeball. Thus anything which causes a corneal abrasion causes excessive blinking and watering in an attempt to wash out the foreign body. Excessive blinking may also be caused by allergic reactions causing watering, itching and roughening of the conjunctiva. There are other also many other disease states associated with excessive blinking. Treatment for excessive blinking depends on the underlying cause.

SYM_CRP_LAZY_EYE.name: Amblyopia (Lazy Eye)
SYM_CRP_LAZY_EYE.description: Amblyopia is a vision development disorder in which an eye fails to achieve normal visual acuity, even with prescription eyeglasses or contact lenses. Also called lazy eye, amblyopia begins during infancy and early childhood. In most cases, only one eye is affected.

SYM_SLP_NERVE_DAMAGE.name: Postoperative Neuropathic Pain
SYM_SLP_NERVE_DAMAGE.description: Postoperative neuropathic pain (PONP) can develop if nerves were damaged during a surgery. Damaged nerves cannot correctly transmit signals from various parts of the body to the brain. Instead, these signals become exaggerated, causing chronic pain that may persist for months or even years.

SYM_SLP_LES_RUPTURE.name: Rupture of a Lesion
SYM_SLP_LES_RUPTURE.description: A lesion or tumour inside the patient has ruptured. This is associated with life-threatening internal bleeding.

SYM_SLP_HEMOPTYSIS2.name: Severe Hemoptysis
SYM_SLP_HEMOPTYSIS2.description: Hemoptysis is the coughing up of blood or blood-stained mucus from the bronchi, larynx, trachea, or lungs. In other words, it is the airway bleeding. This can occur with lung cancer, infections such as tuberculosis, bronchitis, or pneumonia, and certain cardiovascular conditions.

SYM_SLP_HYPERCALCEMIA.name: Hypercalcemia
SYM_SLP_HYPERCALCEMIA.description: Hypercalcemia is a condition in which the calcium level in your blood is above normal. Hypercalcaemia is most often caused by overactivity in the four tiny glands in the neck (parathyroid glands) or from cancer. Extra calcium in the blood affects many bodily systems.

SYM_SLP_SEVERE_HYPERCALCEMIA.name: Severe Hypercalcemia
SYM_SLP_SEVERE_HYPERCALCEMIA.description: Severe hypercalcemia is a medical emergency requiring urgent treatment. Hypercalcemia is a condition in which the calcium level in your blood is above normal. Rarely, severe hypercalcemia can interfere with your heart function, causing palpitations and fainting, indications of cardiac arrhythmia, and other heart problems.

SYM_SLP_ACUTE_LIVER_FAILURE.name: Acute Liver Failure
SYM_SLP_ACUTE_LIVER_FAILURE.description: Acute liver failure (ALF) is a rare syndrome defined by a rapid decline in hepatic function.

SYM_CRP_BL_PMCC.name: Pure Mucinous Cells
SYM_CRP_BL_PMCC.description: Mucinous carcinoma can form along side of other types cancer cells but when it doesn't the cancer is considered 'pure'. Pure mucinous carcinoma is composed of 90–100% mucinous cells.

SYM_CRP_CDF_T_25.name: P16INK4a Overexpression
SYM_CRP_CDF_T_25.description: p16 (also known as p16INK4a, cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor 2A, CDKN2A, multiple tumor suppressor 1 and numerous other synonyms), is a protein that slows cell division by slowing the progression of the cell cycle from the G1 phase to the S phase, thereby acting as a tumor suppressor. p16 is a widely used immunohistochemical marker in gynecologic pathology.
