Strong and diffuse cytoplasmic and nuclear expression of p16 in squamous cell carcinomas (SCC) of the female genital tract is strongly associated with high-risk human papilloma virus (HPV) infection and neoplasms of cervical origin. The majority of SCCs of uterine cervix express p16. However, p16 can be expressed in other neoplasms and in several normal human tissues.

SYM_CRP_CM_CK19_BRIGHT.name: Cytokeratin 19 (CK19) Brightly Expressed
SYM_CRP_CM_CK19_BRIGHT.description: Keratin, type I cytoskeletal 19 also known as cytokeratin-19 (CK-19) or keratin-19 (K19) is a 40 kDa protein that in humans is encoded by the KRT19 gene. Expression in certain cancers allows CK19 to help act as a marker and help determine what type of cancer the patient may have.

SYM_ONCO_CRP_SC1_1_DETECTED.description: Testing has detected Basal Cell Carcinoma with no signs of metastasis.

SYM_ONCO_CRP_SC1_2_DETECTED.description: Testing has detected Basal Cell Carcinoma with no signs of metastasis.

SYM_ONCO_CRP_SC3_1_DETECTED.name: Melanoma Detected
SYM_ONCO_CRP_SC3_1_DETECTED.description: Testing has detected Melanoma with no evidence that it has spread to the lymph tissues, lymph nodes, or body organs. It appears locally confined without ulceration.

SYM_ONCO_CRP_SC3_2_DETECTED.name: Melanoma Detected
SYM_ONCO_CRP_SC3_2_DETECTED.description: Testing has detected Melanoma with evidence it extends beyond the epidermis (very outer layer of skin) into the thicker dermis layer of the skin. There are no signs that it is metastatic.

SYM_OL_LIP_EDEMA.description: The patient has swollen lips due to Angioedema.

SYM_OL_LARYNGEAL_EDEMA.name: Laryngeal Angioedema
SYM_OL_LARYNGEAL_EDEMA.description: The patient has Angioedema in their Larynx leading to difficulty breathing.

SYM_OL_INTESTINAL_EDEMA.name: Intestinal Angioedema
SYM_OL_INTESTINAL_EDEMA.description: The patient has Angioedema in their Intestines causing intense abdomen pain.

SYM_OL_KFR.name: Kayser-Fleischer Rings
SYM_OL_KFR.description: Kayser-Fleischer rings are dark rings that appear to encircle the cornea of the eye. They are due to copper deposition in the Descemet's membrane as a result of particular liver diseases.

SYM_OL_SPLENOMEGALY.name: Splenomegaly
SYM_OL_SPLENOMEGALY.description: An enlarged spleen is also known as splenomegaly.

SYM_OL_RUP_SPLENOMEGALY.name: Ruptured Spleen
SYM_OL_RUP_SPLENOMEGALY.description: A ruptured spleen is an emergency medical condition that occurs when the capsule-like covering of the spleen breaks open, pouring blood into your abdominal area. Depending on the size of the rupture, a large amount of internal bleeding can occur.

SYM_OL_HEPATOMEGALY.name: Hepatomegaly
SYM_OL_HEPATOMEGALY.description: Hepatomegaly is enlargement of the liver beyond its normal size.

SYM_OL_RENAL_FAILURE.name: Renal Failure
SYM_OL_RENAL_FAILURE.description: Renal Failure means one or both of your kidneys no longer function well on their own.

SYM_OL_PULSELESS.name: Pulseless
SYM_OL_PULSELESS.description: Absent pulse is a late sign and indicates tissue death.

SYM_OL_KIDNEY_CYSTS.name: Kidney Cysts
SYM_OL_KIDNEY_CYSTS.description: Imaging has revealed multiple cysts on the patients kidneys.

SYM_DIAG_C1INH.name: Abnormal C1 Esterase Inhibitor
SYM_DIAG_C1INH.description: C1 inhibitor levels are reduced in cases of hereditary angioedema. In this condition, 85% of patients will have decreased antigenic and functional C1 inhibitor levels, whereas 15% will have a dysfunctional protein.

SYM_DIAG_C1Q.name: Abnormal C1Q and C1 Esterase Inhibitor
SYM_DIAG_C1Q.description: Blood test showed low C1Q and low or dysfunctional C1 Inhibitor.

SYM_DIAG_ACE_INH.name: ACE Inhibitor Detected in Blood Test
SYM_DIAG_ACE_INH.description: Blood test has revealed ACE Inhibitors in the blood and a normal C1 Inhibitor level.

SYM_DIAG_AIP.name: Abnormal PBG in Urine
SYM_DIAG_AIP.description: A raised urine porphobilinogen (PBG) is suggestive of an acute attack of porphyria.

SYM_DIAG_CLARKSONS.name: Capillary Leakage Findings
SYM_DIAG_CLARKSONS.description: Evaluation of symtoms including Arterial hypotension, hemoconcentration and hypoalbuminemia in absence of secondary causes of shock and infection indicates Clarksons Disease (Idiopathic Capillary Leak Syndrome).

SYM_DIAG_WILSONS.name: Wilsons Disease Findings
SYM_DIAG_WILSONS.description: Liver biopsy showing more than 250 ug/g of dried liver tissue confirms wilsons disease.

SYM_DIAG_COMPARTMENT.name: High Muscle Compartment Pressure
SYM_DIAG_COMPARTMENT.description: Manometer readings of >30 mmHg in the muscle compartment is consistant with compartment syndrome and is at risk of ischemic necrosis.

SYM_DIAG_NPT3.name: Gene Identified
SYM_DIAG_NPT3.description: Genetic testing reveals a mutation in the npc1/npc2 gene.

SYM_DIAG_ZES.name: Multiple Gastrinoma
SYM_DIAG_ZES.description: MRI has revealed multiple gastrinoma in the pancreas

SYM_DIAG_MENETRIERS.name: Menetriers Biopsy Findings
SYM_DIAG_MENETRIERS.description: Mucosal biopsy taken during endoscopy is consistant with menetriers disease.

SYM_DIAG_ACHALASIA.name: Abnormal Esophageal Motility
SYM_DIAG_ACHALASIA.description: Esophageal manometry shows abnormal motility consistant with achalasia.

SYM_DIAG_MALS.name: MALS Findings in Echocardiograph
SYM_DIAG_MALS.description: Doppler ultrasonography measuring peak systolic velocities greater than 200cm/s in the celiac artery are suggestive of celiac artery stenosis associated with MALS.

SYM_DIAG_BOUVERET.name: Bouveret Syndrome Findings on CT
SYM_DIAG_BOUVERET.description: CT scan has revealed presence of a bilioduodenal fistula that has gastric outlet obstruction.

SYM_DIAG_POTS.name: Excessive Heart Rate Increase
SYM_DIAG_POTS.description: An active stand test found an increase in heart rate greater than 30 beats per minute.

SYM_DIAG_BCS.name: Abnormal Liver Ultrasound
SYM_DIAG_BCS.description: Liver ultrasound reveals significant damage to hepatic veins with signs of thrombosis and/or stenosis.

SYM_DIAG_BSS.name: Gene Identified
SYM_DIAG_BSS.description: Genetic testing has revealed abnormalities in GP1BA, GP1BB and GP9 genes.

SYM_DIAG_EVANS.name: Evans Syndrome Findings
SYM_DIAG_EVANS.description: CBC testing revealed the presence of both hemolytic anemia and immune thrombocytopenic purpura with a positive coombs test suggestive of Evans syndrome.

SYM_DIAG_ADEM.name: ADEM Findings on MRI
SYM_DIAG_ADEM.description: MRI revealed multifocal demyelinating lesions in the brain suggestive of ADEM.

SYM_DIAG_ARPKD.name: Gene Identified
SYM_DIAG_ARPKD.description: Genetic testing reveals abnormalities in the PKHD1 gene.

SYM_DIAG_BEHCETS.name: Rucurrent Ulcers
SYM_DIAG_BEHCETS.description: Clinical evaluation of recurrent ulcers on mouth and genitals along with an elevated crp suggests behcets disease.

SYM_DIAG_BIOTIN.name: Gene Identified
SYM_DIAG_BIOTIN.description: Genetic testing revealed abnormalities in the BTD gene.

SYM_DIAG_ABDOEPILEPSY.name: Abdominal Epilepsy Findings
SYM_DIAG_ABDOEPILEPSY.description: Abnormal EEG shows characteristics of Abdominal Epilepsy.

SYM_DIAG_ABDOMIGRAINE.name: Signs of Abdominal Migraines
SYM_DIAG_ABDOMIGRAINE.description: Diagnostic evaluation of patients symptoms and exclusion of other diagnoses indicates a diagnosis of Abdominal Migraines.

SYM_DIAG_DJS.name: Dubin-Johnson Syndrome Findings
SYM_DIAG_DJS.description: In patients with Dubin–Johnson syndrome coproporphyrin I is 3–4 times higher than coproporphyrin III. Analysis of urine porphyrins shows a normal level of coproporphyrin, but the I isomer accounts for 80% of the total (normally 25%).

SYM_DIAG_BDBS.name: Wyburn-Mason Syndrome Findings
SYM_DIAG_BDBS.description: Fluorescent Angiogram has revealed AVMs (Arteriovenous Malformations) in the brain and retina.

SYM_DIAG_UPTE.name: Epileptic TBI Findings
SYM_DIAG_UPTE.description: MRI Brain scan has findings consistant with a TBI causing epilepsy.

SYM_JM_RINGING_IN_THE_EARS.name: Tinnitus
SYM_JM_RINGING_IN_THE_EARS.description: Also called ringing in the ears, is the sensation of hearing ringing or other sounds without any external source.

SYM_JM_EAR_FLUID_DRAINAGE.name: Ear Discharge
SYM_JM_EAR_FLUID_DRAINAGE.description: The patient has unidentified fluids coming from the ear.

SYM_JM_SWOLLEN_EAR_CANAL.name: Swollen Ear Canal
SYM_JM_SWOLLEN_EAR_CANAL.description: Constriction of the ear canal due to the swelling of the lining and tissues.

SYM_JM_HEARING_DIFFICULTIES.name: Hearing Problems
SYM_JM_HEARING_DIFFICULTIES.description: Difficulties with hearing perception like diminished hearing or ringing in the ears.

SYM_JM_DIMINISHED_HEARING.name: Diminished Hearing
SYM_JM_DIMINISHED_HEARING.description: The patient does not hear well.

SYM_JM_HYPERACIDITY.name: Hyperacidity
SYM_JM_HYPERACIDITY.description: Hyperacidity is a condition where the stomach secretes an excess of gastric acids.

SYM_JM_LARGE_ARTERIES_DAMAGED.name: Large Arteries Damaged
SYM_JM_LARGE_ARTERIES_DAMAGED.description: Some larger arteries are damaged and may cause life-threatening complications.

SYM_JM_NECROSIS.name: Necrosis
SYM_JM_NECROSIS.description: Premature death of a tissue caused by its own enzymes. It occurs when too little blood flows to the tissue. This can be cause by an injury, radiation, or chemicals. Necrosis cannot be reversed.

SYM_JM_PENETRATING_WOUND.name: Penetrating Wound
SYM_JM_PENETRATING_WOUND.description: Wound caused by penetration of a foreign object.

SYM_JM_LUNGS_PERFORATED.description: Perforation of the lung membranes and tissue.

SYM_CRP_JM_S_RUPTURED_EAR_DRUM.description: The patients eardrum ruptured and hasn't healed.

SYM_CRP_JM_EAR_INFECTED.description: A ear infection was found in the patient.

SYM_CRP_JM_FLUID_IN_MIDDLE_EAR.description: Fluid in the middle ear has been found.

SYM_CRP_JM_BAROTRAUMA.description: The doctor confirms that the patient has Barotrauma.

SYM_CRP_JM_SEVERED_EAR.description: The patient's ear has been severed from trauma.

SYM_CRP_JM_FOREIGN_OBJECT_IN_EAR.description: The patient's ear has a object lodged in it.

SYM_CRP_JM_VESTIBULAR_NEURITIS.description: The patient's vestibular nerve is inflammed.

SYM_CRP_JM_VESTIBULAR_TUMOR.description: The patient's vestibular nerve has a tumor growing on it.

SYM_CRP_JM_EAR_WAX_BLOCKAGE.description: The patient's ear is blocked by ear wax.

SYM_CRP_JM_EAR_WAX_REMOVAL.name: Ear Wax Removal
SYM_CRP_JM_EAR_WAX_REMOVAL.description: The patient wants their ear wax removed.

SYM_CRP_JM_BPPV.description: The doctor has ruled out all possible diseases related to BPPV, the doctor suspects the patient has BPPV.

SYM_CRP_JM_PERICHONDRIAL_HEMATOMA.name: Blood Clot in Ear
SYM_CRP_JM_PERICHONDRIAL_HEMATOMA.description: The patient's ear has a blood clot.

SYM_CRP_JM_AUDITORY_NEUROPATHY_M.name: Absent/Abnormal ABR W/ Absent Acoustic Reflexes (Mild)
SYM_CRP_JM_AUDITORY_NEUROPATHY_M.description: The doctor has performed a Otoauditory Emissions Exam and it has shown a normal outer hair cell function, absent or abnormal Auditory Brainstem Response (ABR) with presence of the cochlear microphonic, and absent acoustic reflexes, and it is fortunately mild.

SYM_CRP_JM_AUDITORY_NEUROPATHY_S.description: The doctor has performed a Otoauditory Emissions Exam and it has shown a normal outer hair cell function, absent or abnormal Auditory Brainstem Response (ABR) with presence of the cochlear microphonic, and absent acoustic reflexes, and it is unfortunately severe.

SYM_CRP_JM_PRESBYCUSIS_M.description: The doctor has performed a evaluation and audiometry and has concluded that the patient is suffering from Presbycusis (Age Related Hearing Loss), and it is fortunately mild.

SYM_CRP_JM_PRESBYCUSIS_S.name: Age Related Hearing Loss (Severe)
SYM_CRP_JM_PRESBYCUSIS_S.description: The doctor has performed a evaluation and audiometry and has concluded that the patient is suffering from Presbycusis (Age Related Hearing Loss), and it is unfortunately severe.

SYM_CRP_JM_LABYRINTHITIS.name: Inflammed Vestibular and Cochlea Nerve
SYM_CRP_JM_LABYRINTHITIS.description: The doctor has performed a MRI or a CT and it has shown inflammation in both the vestibular and cochlea nerve, which indicates Labyrinthitis.

SYM_CRP_JM_OTOPHYMA.name: Swelling of the Ears
SYM_CRP_JM_OTOPHYMA.description: The doctor has performed a ear examination and it seems that the patient's ears are swollen, the doctor concludes that the patient has a very rare affliction called Otophyma.

SYM_CRP_JM_ADENOCARCINOMA_EAR.name: Adenocarcinoma Found in Ear Canal
SYM_CRP_JM_ADENOCARCINOMA_EAR.description: A tumour has with the cancer Adenocarcinoma has been found in the Ear Canal by a Biopsy.

SYM_CRP_JM_SQUAMOUS_CELL_EAR.name: Squamous Cell Carcinoma Found in Ear Canal
SYM_CRP_JM_SQUAMOUS_CELL_EAR.description: A tumour has with the cancer Squamous Cell Carcinoma has been found in the Ear Canal by a Biopsy.

SYM_CRP_JM_MELANOMA_EAR.name: Melanoma Found in Ear Canal
SYM_CRP_JM_MELANOMA_EAR.description: A tumour has with the cancer Melanoma has been found in the Ear Canal by a Biopsy.

SYM_CRP_JM_MALIGNANT_EAR_INFECTION_E.name: Malignant Ear Infection (Externa)
SYM_CRP_JM_MALIGNANT_EAR_INFECTION_E.description: A malignant infection caused by Pseudomonas Aeruginosa has been found causing Otitis Externa in the patient.

SYM_CRP_JM_MALIGNANT_EAR_INFECTION_M.name: Malignant Ear Infection (Media)
SYM_CRP_JM_MALIGNANT_EAR_INFECTION_M.description: A malignant infection caused by Pseudomonas Aeruginosa has been found causing Otitis Media in the patient.

SYM_CRP_JM_NASAL_POLYPS.name: Ethmoidal Nasal Polyps
SYM_CRP_JM_NASAL_POLYPS.description: Nasal polyps are soft, painless, noncancerous growths on the lining of your nasal passages or sinuses. They hang down like teardrops or grapes. They result from chronic inflammation. There are two primary types of nasal polyps: ethmoidal and antrochoanal. Ethmoidal polyps arise from the ethmoid sinuses and extend through the middle meatus into the nasal cavity. Antrochoanal polyps usually arise in the maxillary sinus and extend into the nasopharynx and represent only 4–6% of all nasal polyps. They either can be treated with saline spray or surgery depending on how big the nasal polyp is.

SYM_CRP_JM_NA_RH.name: Non-Allergic Rhinitis Comfirmed
SYM_CRP_JM_NA_RH.description: A case of non-allergic Rhinitis has been comfirmed.

SYM_CRP_JM_ASN_CONFIRM.name: Allergic Sinusitis Confirmed
SYM_CRP_JM_ASN_CONFIRM.description: A case of Allergic Sinusitis has been confirmed.

SYM_CRP_JM_ENS_FINDINGS.name: ENS Findings
SYM_CRP_JM_ENS_FINDINGS.description: Empty Nose Syndrome findings has been detected by a CT scan.

SYM_CRP_JM_FUNGAL_INFECTION_OF_SINUS.name: Aspergillus Found in Sinus
SYM_CRP_JM_FUNGAL_INFECTION_OF_SINUS.description: Aspergillus Fungus has been found in the sinuses by a sputum culture.

SYM_CRP_JM_DEVIATED_NASAL_SEPTUM.name: The Nasal Septum Is Deviated
SYM_CRP_JM_DEVIATED_NASAL_SEPTUM.description: A Nasal Cavity Inspection revealed a deviated nasal septum in the patient.

SYM_CRP_JM_SWOLLEN_NASAL_TURBINATE.name: Swollen Nasal Turbinates
SYM_CRP_JM_SWOLLEN_NASAL_TURBINATE.description: The patient's nasal turbinates are swollen.

SYM_CRP_JM_DYSOSMIA_CONFIRMED.description: The doctor confirms that the patient has dysosmia.

SYM_CRP_JM_SQUAMOUS_CELL_NOSE.name: Squamous Cell Carcinoma Found in Nasal Cavity
SYM_CRP_JM_SQUAMOUS_CELL_NOSE.description: A tumour has with the cancer Squamous Cell Carcinoma has been found in the Nasal Cavities by a Biopsy.

SYM_CRP_JM_ADENOCARCINOMA_NOSE.name: Adenocarcinoma Found in Nasal Cavity
SYM_CRP_JM_ADENOCARCINOMA_NOSE.description: A tumour has with the cancer Adenocarcinoma has been found in the Nasal Cavities by a Biopsy.

SYM_CRP_JM_MELANOMA_NOSE.name: Melanoma Found in Nasal Cavity
SYM_CRP_JM_MELANOMA_NOSE.description: A tumour has with the cancer Melanoma has been found in the Nasal Cavities by a Biopsy.

SYM_CRP_JM_FOREIGN_OBJECT_IN_NOSE.name: Foreign Object in Nose
SYM_CRP_JM_FOREIGN_OBJECT_IN_NOSE.description: The patient's nose has a object lodged in it.

SYM_CRP_JM_COLLAPSED_SINUS.description: The patient has a collapsed sinus causing facial deformity.

SYM_CRP_JM_ACUTE_RHINOSINUSITIS.name: Acute Infection of the Nose and Sinuses
SYM_CRP_JM_ACUTE_RHINOSINUSITIS.description: The patient has a acute infection of the nose and sinuses causing acute rhinosinuitis.

SYM_CRP_JM_CHRONIC_RHINOSINUSITIS.name: Chronic Inflammation of the Nose and Sinuses
SYM_CRP_JM_CHRONIC_RHINOSINUSITIS.description: The patient has chronic inflammation of the nose and sinuses causing chronic rhinosinusitis.

SYM_CRP_JM_RHINOPHYMA.name: Rhinophyma Confirmed
SYM_CRP_JM_RHINOPHYMA.description: The doctor has found a thick and excessive amount of Rhinophyma-like mass on the nose/has found excess tissue, oil, bacteria and sebaceous glands using a nose biopsy.

SYM_CRP_JM_CLEFT_LIP_PALATE.name: Cleft Lip and Palate
SYM_CRP_JM_CLEFT_LIP_PALATE.description: The patient has informed the doctor about their cleft lip and palate/the doctor has confirmed that the patient has a cleft lip and palate.

SYM_CRP_JM_PROBLEMATIC_BLOOD_VESSELS_N.name: Problematic Blood Vessel
SYM_CRP_JM_PROBLEMATIC_BLOOD_VESSELS_N.description: A Rhinoscopy has found a problematic blood vessel in the patients nose.

SYM_CRP_JM_INVERTED_PAPILLOMA_N.name: HPV Tumour Found in Nose
SYM_CRP_JM_INVERTED_PAPILLOMA_N.description: A biopsy has found a benign tumour in the nose caused by the Human Papillomavirus (HPV).

SYM_CRP_JM_NASAL_POLYPS_S.name: Antrochoanal Nasal Polyps
SYM_CRP_JM_NASAL_POLYPS_S.description: Nasal polyps are soft, painless, noncancerous growths on the lining of your nasal passages or sinuses. They hang down like teardrops or grapes. They result from chronic inflammation. There are two primary types of nasal polyps: ethmoidal and antrochoanal. Ethmoidal polyps arise from the ethmoid sinuses and extend through the middle meatus into the nasal cavity. Antrochoanal polyps usually arise in the maxillary sinus and extend into the nasopharynx and represent only 4–6% of all nasal polyps. They either can be treated with saline spray or surgery depending on how big the nasal polyp is..

SYM_CRP_JM_SADDLE_NOSE.name: Nasal Trauma
SYM_CRP_JM_SADDLE_NOSE.description: The patient has experienced nasal trauma causing a squashed nose.

SYM_CRP_JM_PERFORATED_SEPTUM.name: Perforated Nasal Septum
SYM_CRP_JM_PERFORATED_SEPTUM.description: The patient has perforation of the nasal septum.

SYM_CRP_JM_SEPTAL_CLOT.name: Septal Blood Clot
SYM_CRP_JM_SEPTAL_CLOT.description: The patient has a blood clot in their nasal septum after experiencing nasal trauma.

SYM_CRP_JM_MENINGES_DEFORMITY.name: Abnormal Sac of Fluid and Brain Tissue
SYM_CRP_JM_MENINGES_DEFORMITY.description: A CT or MRI has found a abnormal sac of fluid, brain tissue and meninges because of a skull deformity.

SYM_CRP_JM_STAPHY.name: Streptococcus Pyogenes Present
SYM_CRP_JM_STAPHY.description: Streptococcus pyogenes, or Group A streptococcus (GAS), is a facultative, Gram-positive coccus which grows in chains and causes numerous infections in humans including pharyngitis, tonsillitis, scarlet fever, cellulitis, erysipelas, rheumatic fever, post-streptococcal glomerulonephritis, necrotizing fasciitis, myonecrosis and lymphangitis. The only known reservoirs for GAS in nature are the skin and mucous membranes of the human host. The clinical diseases produced by GAS have been well described, however, the pathogenic mechanisms underlying them are poorly understood, largely because each is the culmination of highly complex interactions between the human host defense mechanisms and specific virulence factors of the streptococcus.

SYM_CRP_JM_COXSACK.name: Coxsackievirus A16 Present
SYM_CRP_JM_COXSACK.description: Coxsackieviruses are a few related enteroviruses that belong to the Picornaviridae family of nonenveloped, linear, positive-sense single-stranded RNA viruses.They usually cause Hand, Foot and Mouth Disease.There is no treatment for this virus, or either wait until the infection is over.

SYM_CRP_JM_GERD_SUSPECTED.name: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease Suspected
SYM_CRP_JM_GERD_SUSPECTED.description: The doctor's has evaluated all the symptoms of the patient, and has concluded the patient has Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease.

SYM_CRP_JM_BLUNT_LARYNX_CONTUSION.description: A case of Laryngeal Trauma has been comfirmed.

SYM_CRP_JM_BLUNT_PHARYNX_LACERATION.description: A case of Pharyngeal Penetration has been comfirmed.

SYM_TORSION.name: Ovarian Ascended
SYM_TORSION.description: Ovarian ascended on an ultrasound image.

SYM_ABCES.name: Abscess
SYM_ABCES.description: A localized collection of pus surrounded by inflamed tissue.

SYM_SORTIE_ORGANE.name: Prolapse
SYM_SORTIE_ORGANE.description: The falling down or slipping of a body part from its usual position or relations.

SYM_CANDIDA_ALBICANS.name: Candida Albicans
SYM_CANDIDA_ALBICANS.description: Candida albicans (sometimes referred to as monilia) is a opportunistic fungus that is normally present on the skin and in mucous membranes such as the vagina, mouth, or rectum..

SYM_ATTEINTE_TUBAIRE.name: Tube Damage
SYM_ATTEINTE_TUBAIRE.description: Tube damage on an ultrasound image.

SYM_BHCG.name: B-HCG
SYM_BHCG.description: Beta human chorionic gonadotropin (HCG) is a hormone produced by the placenta during pregnancy, and is typically detected in the blood.

SYM_LIEC.name: Cervical Intraepithelial Lesions
SYM_LIEC.description: Cervical intraepithelial lesions at pap smear.

SYM_LEL_RU.name: Free Fluid in the Recto Uterine Pouch
SYM_LEL_RU.description: The cul-de-sac, also known as the pouch of Douglas or rectouterine pouch, is an extension of the postero-inferior reflection of the peritoneal fold between the uterus (anteriorly) and rectum (posteriorly). It is the most inferior aspect of the peritoneal cavity and therefore the first location where free fluid accumulates.

SYM_CYSTADENOCARCINOME.name: Cystadenocarcinoma
SYM_CYSTADENOCARCINOME.description: Cystadenocarcinoma is a malignant form of a cystadenoma and is a cancer derived from glandular epithelium, in which cystic accumulations of retained secretions are formed.

SYM_ADENOFIBROME.name: Adenofibroma
SYM_ADENOFIBROME.description: Adenoma with a large amount of fibrous tissue.

SYM_HYPERPLASIE_EP.name: Epithelial Hyperplasia
SYM_HYPERPLASIE_EP.description: Epithelial hyperplasia is benign proliferative breast lesion which arising as an unexpected new growth of epithelial cells.

SYM_CARCINOME_CANALAIRE.name: Ductal Carcinoma
SYM_CARCINOME_CANALAIRE.description: Ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) is the presence of abnormal cells inside a milk duct in the breast. DCIS is considered the earliest form of breast cancer.

SYM_HTA_G.name: Gestational Hypertension
SYM_HTA_G.description: Gestational hypertension is defined as consistently elevation of systemic blood pressure above or equal to 140/90mmHg in a previously normotensive woman.

SYM_INSULINO_RPR.name: Insulin Resistance
SYM_INSULINO_RPR.description: Purpose of insulin resistance (IR) adapted by mother is to deliver enough quantity of nutrients to the growing fetus.

SYM_CLUE_CELLS.name: Clue Cells
SYM_CLUE_CELLS.description: Clue cells are epithelial cells of the vagina that get their distinctive stippled appearance by being covered with bacteria.

SYM_LOCHIE.name: Lochia
SYM_LOCHIE.description: The normal discharge from the uterus after childbirth.

SYM_ENGORGEMENT_M.name: Milk Stasis
SYM_ENGORGEMENT_M.description: Milk stasis is the buildup of milk within the breast tissue of lactating women.

SYM_LYMPHANGITE_M.name: Lymphangitis
SYM_LYMPHANGITE_M.description: Inflammation of the walls of the lymphatic vessels.

SYM_SIGNE_BUDIN.name: Budin's Sign
SYM_SIGNE_BUDIN.description: Budin's sign is positive if the milk is mixed with pus.

SYM_ABCES_M.name: Breast Abscess.
SYM_ABCES_M.description: A local accumulation of pus within the breast due to infection.

SYM_TVP_PP.name: DVT Detected
SYM_TVP_PP.description: Deep vein thrombosis detected with USG.

SYM_TVPELV.name: PVT Detected
SYM_TVPELV.description: Pelvic vein thrombosis detected with USG.

SYM_HYPOG_HYPOGT.name: Hypogonadotropic Hypogonadism
SYM_HYPOG_HYPOGT.description: Hypogonadotropic hypogonadism (HH) is a form of hypogonadism that is due to a problem with the pituitary gland or hypothalamus.

SYM_IMAGE_COCARDE.name: Image in Roundel
SYM_IMAGE_COCARDE.description: Image in roundel with a central hyperechogenicity found on imagery.

SYM_IMAGE_DIVERTICULAIRE.name: Diverticular Image
SYM_IMAGE_DIVERTICULAIRE.description: "Mistletoe ball" or "Bilboquet ball" diverticular images found in imagery.

SYM_NECROSE_ISCHEMIQUE_HYPOPHYSE.name: Anterior Pituitary Ischemic Necrosis
SYM_NECROSE_ISCHEMIQUE_HYPOPHYSE.description: Intrasellar arachnoidocele with pituitary gland pressed against the sellar floor found on imaging.

SYM_ELARGISSEMENT_HYPOPHYSE.name: Pituitary Enlargement
SYM_ELARGISSEMENT_HYPOPHYSE.description: Symmetrical enlargement of the pituitary gland, thinning of the pituitary stem without affecting the sellar floor and clear and early hypersignal of the anterior lobe of the pituitary gland are found on imaging.

SYM_TSST1.name: TSST-1
SYM_TSST1.description: Toxic shock syndrome toxin 1 (TSST-1), a prototype superantigen secreted by a Staphylococcus aureus bacterium.

SYM_OBGYN_HYPERMOBILITE_URETRALE.name: Urethral Hypermobility
SYM_OBGYN_HYPERMOBILITE_URETRALE.description: Loss of tone of tissue in the proximal urethra.

SYM_OBGYN_ATTEINTE_TROMPES.name: Tubal Involvement
SYM_OBGYN_ATTEINTE_TROMPES.description: Inflammation of a fallopian tube on ultrasound.

SYM_OBGYN_LIG_TROMPES.name: Tubal Ligation
SYM_OBGYN_LIG_TROMPES.description: Patient asks to have fallopian tubes tied.

SYM_OBGYN_ABCES_BARTHOLIN.name: Bartholin's Abscess
SYM_OBGYN_ABCES_BARTHOLIN.description: The patient has an Bartholin abscess.

SYM_OBGYN_FISTULE_BARTHOLIN.name: Fistula
SYM_OBGYN_FISTULE_BARTHOLIN.description: Bartholinitis is fistulated on the skin.

SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_BARTHOLIN.name: Bartholin's Cyst
SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_BARTHOLIN.description: The patient has an Bartholin cyst.

SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_PILONIDAL.name: Pilonidal Cyst
SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_PILONIDAL.description: The patient has an pilonidal cyst.

SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_EPIDERMIQUE_VULVE.name: Epidermoid Cyst
SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_EPIDERMIQUE_VULVE.description: The patient has an epidermoid cyst.

SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_VULVE_INCLUSION.name: Inclusion Cyst
SYM_OBGYN_KYSTE_VULVE_INCLUSION.description: The patient has an inclusion cyst.

SYM_OBGYN_HEMATOCOLPOS.name: Hematocolpos
SYM_OBGYN_HEMATOCOLPOS.description: A hematocolpos was found on imagery.

SYM_HEMORRAGIE_GEN.name: Gynecologic Hemorrhage
SYM_HEMORRAGIE_GEN.description: Gynecologic hemorrhage represents excessive bleeding of the female reproductive system.

SYM_PLACENTA_BAS.name: Low-Lying Placenta
SYM_PLACENTA_BAS.description: A low-lying placenta shown on an ultrasound image.

SYM_HEMATOME_RETROPLACENTAIRE.name: Retroplacental Hematomas
SYM_HEMATOME_RETROPLACENTAIRE.description: A retroplacental hematomas shown on an ultrasound image.

SYM_MAP.name: PROM
SYM_MAP.description: Premature rupture of membranes (PROM) is a rupture (breaking open) of the membranes (amniotic sac) before labor begins.

SYM_PRE_E.name: Placental Insufficiency
SYM_PRE_E.description: Placental insufficiency (also called placental dysfunction or uteroplacental vascular insufficiency) occurs when the placenta does not develop properly, or is damaged.

SYM_HEMATOME_DECIDUAL.name: Chorionic Hematoma
SYM_HEMATOME_DECIDUAL.description: Chorionic hematoma (also chorionic hemorrhage) is the pooling of blood (hematoma) between the chorion, a membrane surrounding the embryo, and the uterine wall.

SYM_ATONIE_UTERINE.name: Uterine Atony
SYM_ATONIE_UTERINE.description: Uterine atony is a loss of tone in the uterine musculature.

SYM_PLAS_ABSCESS.name: Abscess
SYM_PLAS_ABSCESS.description: Buildup of pus within the tissue surrounding a surgical wound.

SYM_SLP_UNDESIRED_RESULTS_V2.name: Undesired Results
SYM_SLP_UNDESIRED_RESULTS_V2.description: Unfavourable outcomes are part and parcel of performing surgeries of any kind. Post-treatment undesired results occur after a surgery or treatment has been completed and the result differs from what was expected. This may lead to further complications and increased stress for the patient.

SYM_SLP_S_SCAR_TISSUE_V2.name: S Scar Tissue
SYM_SLP_S_SCAR_TISSUE_V2.description: A scar is a mark that is left on the skin after a wound or an injury to the surface of the skin has healed. The formation of scars or scar tissue is a normal part of the healing process, and occurs when the skin is cut due to injury or surgery. The growth of scar tissue is influenced by many factors, these include: The severity of the original injury. The size, depth and location of the wound on the body. Excess scar tissue, layers deep, can significantly reduce function and movement months after surgery. This can have negative effects and decrease patient satisfaction.

SYM_SLP_PLAS_NERVE_DAMAGE_V2.name: Plas Nerve Damage
SYM_SLP_PLAS_NERVE_DAMAGE_V2.description: Postoperative neuropathic pain (PONP) can develop if nerves were damaged during a surgery. Damaged nerves cannot correctly transmit signals from various parts of the body to the brain. Instead, these signals become exaggerated, causing chronic pain that may persist for months or even years.

SYM_SLP_B_CAPSULAR_V2.name: B Capsular
SYM_SLP_B_CAPSULAR_V2.description: Capsular contracture is a breast augmentation complication that develops when internal scar tissue forms a tight or constricting capsule around a breast implant, contracting it until it becomes misshapen and hard.

SYM_SLP_BARSA_INFECTION_V2.name: Barsa Infection
SYM_SLP_BARSA_INFECTION_V2.description: At face value, bacterial infection is a problem for any implantable medical device, including breast implants. Symptoms of an infection can include a high fever and breast pain, redness and/or swelling. If it appears that antibiotics alone won't clear the infection, then the surgeon may need to clean out the infected tissue and either replace or completely remove the implant.

SYM_SLP_NOSE_SEPTALPERF_V2.name: Nose Septalperf
SYM_SLP_NOSE_SEPTALPERF_V2.description: A nasal septum perforation is a medical condition in which the nasal septum, the bony/cartilaginous wall dividing the nasal cavities, develops a hole or fissure. Some perforations can cause bleeding, pain, and a whistling sound when inhaling. If dried blood or scabs build up, you could also have trouble breathing through your nose. Many perforations do not need to be closed but serious or complicated ones can lead to infections.

SYM_SLP_EYE_GLOBE_PERFORATION_V2.name: Eye Globe Perforation
SYM_SLP_EYE_GLOBE_PERFORATION_V2.description: Globe rupture occurs when the integrity of the outer membranes of the eye is disrupted by blunt or penetrating trauma. Any full-thickness injury to the cornea, sclera, or both is considered an open globe injury and is approached in the same manner in the acute setting. Globe rupture is an ophthalmologic emergency and prompt action is critical to maximizing functional outcome. Although the globe's position within the orbit protects it from injury in many situations, damage to the posterior segment of the eye is associated with a very high frequency of permanent visual loss.

SYM_SLP_EYE_UNDESIRED_RESULTS_V2.name: Eye Undesired Results
SYM_SLP_EYE_UNDESIRED_RESULTS_V2.description: Unsatisfactory eye alignment after surgery is the most common complication when correcting Strabismus and other eye misalignment issues. Despite careful pre-operative measurements and utilization of common surgical dosage tables, a certain percentage of patients will be overcorrected or undercorrected after surgery requiring corrective surgical procedures to alleviate the issue.

SYM_SLP_EYE_CIC_V2.name: Eye Cic
SYM_SLP_EYE_CIC_V2.description: Conjunctival inclusion cysts occur when conjunctival epithelial cells are buried beneath the conjunctival surface during surgery. These cells can multiply over time to create a subconjunctival cyst days to years after the original surgery. Conjunctival scaring is more common after a re-operation and also after a surgical resection, when advancement of the muscle belly may advance the thicker posterior Tenon’s capsule closer to the limbus where it is more visible.

SYM_SLP_EYE_SLIPPED_MUSCLE_V2.name: Eye Slipped Muscle
SYM_SLP_EYE_SLIPPED_MUSCLE_V2.description: Slipped muscles develop when the muscular capsule is imbricated without including the muscle or muscle tendon during strabismus surgery. When the capsule is reattached to the sclera, the tendon and muscle are then free to slip posteriorally from the site of attachment.

SYM_CRP_NASAL_HEMATOMA.name: Nasal Hematoma
SYM_CRP_NASAL_HEMATOMA.description: A nasal septal hematoma is a collection of blood within the septum of the nose. The septum is the part of the nose between the nostrils. An injury disrupts the blood vessels so that fluid and blood may collect under the lining.

SYM_CRP_BLEPHAROPTOSIS.name: Blepharoptosis
SYM_CRP_BLEPHAROPTOSIS.description: Blepharoptosis, also known as ptosis, is a drooping of the upper eyelid that may affect one or both eyes. The eyelid may droop only slightly or may droop enough to cover the pupil and block vision.

SYM_SLP_IMPLANT_INFECTION01.name: Cheek Implant Infection
SYM_SLP_IMPLANT_INFECTION01.description: The patient has developed an infection around a recent cheek implant. Such an infection is serious and the implant will need to be removed and the wound cleaned.

SYM_CRP_CSFRHINORRHEA.name: Cerebrospinal Fluid (CSF) Rhinorrhea
SYM_CRP_CSFRHINORRHEA.description: Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) rhinorrhea is a condition where the fluid that surrounds the brain leaks into the nose and sinuses. Head trauma, surgery, or even birth defects can make a hole in the membranes that hold this fluid. It then leaks into the nose or ear, causing a watery, runny nose.

SYM_CRP_LOW_NOSEBRIDGE.name: Very Low Nose Bridge
SYM_CRP_LOW_NOSEBRIDGE.description: The patient has an exceptionally low nasal bridge. This can cause issues concerning the functionality of the nose.

SYM_CRP_DISPROPORT_APPEAR.name: Disproportionate Appearance (Nose)
SYM_CRP_DISPROPORT_APPEAR.description: The appearance and size of the patient's nose is noticeably difference from what would be considered normal.

SYM_CRP_PINCHED_NOSTRILS.name: Pinched Nostrils
SYM_CRP_PINCHED_NOSTRILS.description: A pinched nasal tip refers to a nose that is excessively narrowed in the region of the tip. This type of nasal abnormality can arise as a congenital problem, meaning the patient was born with this or as a result of having had prior unsuccessful rhinoplasty surgery.

SYM_CRP_NOSETOOHIGH.name: High Tip of the Nose
SYM_CRP_NOSETOOHIGH.description: The patient's nose is pointed unusually high at the tip.

SYM_CRP_UNEVEN_NOSTRILS.name: Uneven Nostrils
SYM_CRP_UNEVEN_NOSTRILS.description: One of the patient's nostrils is of a noticeably different size compared to the other.

SYM_CRP_OCULAR_EXTRUDE.name: Ocular Contents Extruding From Globe
SYM_CRP_OCULAR_EXTRUDE.description: The patient's eye is extruding the internal contents of the globe of the eye.

SYM_CRP_OCULAR_MOTILITY.name: Loss of Ocular Motility
SYM_CRP_OCULAR_MOTILITY.description: Loss of Ocular Motility, also know as Oculomotor Dysfunction, occurs when there is the absence or defect of controlled, voluntary, and purposeful eye movement.

SYM_CRP_TISSUE_SHRAPNEL.name: Tissue Shrapnel Damage
SYM_CRP_TISSUE_SHRAPNEL.description: The patient has shallow tissue damage from shrapnel.

SYM_CRP_FACE_HEMATOMA.name: Facial Hematoma
SYM_CRP_FACE_HEMATOMA.description: A facial hematoma is a collection of blood within the face. An injury disrupts the blood vessels so that fluid and blood may collect under the lining.

SYM_CRP_LOSS_OF_UMBILICUS.name: Loss of Umbilicus
SYM_CRP_LOSS_OF_UMBILICUS.description: Loss of the umbilicus or naval results in the loss of a person's belly button. While this will cause no health issues it might make a person embarrassed especially if it was a complication of abdominal surgery.

SYM_CRP_BURNING_SENSE.name: Burning Sensation
SYM_CRP_BURNING_SENSE.description: The patient is experiencing a painful burning sensation.

SYM_CRP_SKIN_DISCOLOURATION.name: Skin Discolouration
SYM_CRP_SKIN_DISCOLOURATION.description: The patient has patches of skin that noticeably differ from their normal skin tone.

SYM_CRP_CVI.name: Chronic Venous Insufficiency
SYM_CRP_CVI.description: Chronic venous insufficiency occurs when the leg veins don't allow blood to flow back up to the heart. Normally, the valves in veins make sure that blood flows toward the heart. But when these valves don't work well, blood can also flow backwards. This can cause blood to collect (pool) in the legs.

SYM_CRP_ECCHYMOSIS.name: Ecchymosis
SYM_CRP_ECCHYMOSIS.description: Ecchymosis is a skin discoloration that results from bleeding underneath the skin. Unlike a bruise, Ecchymosis isn't caused by a blow or impact that ruptured blood vessels.

SYM_CRP_SF_THROMBOPHLEBITIS.name: Superficial Thrombophlebitis
SYM_CRP_SF_THROMBOPHLEBITIS.description: Superficial thrombophlebitis (STP) refers to inflammation and clot formation in the superficial vein which runs under the skin. It commonly occurs in the varicose veins of the leg, but it can also occur in any superficial vein after an injury.

SYM_CRP_SKINBURN_MILD.name: First Degree Burn
SYM_CRP_SKINBURN_MILD.description: A first-degree burn is also called a superficial burn or wound. It's an injury that affects the first layer of the skin.

SYM_PLAS_C001_V2.name: C001
SYM_PLAS_C001_V2.description: The patient has expressed that they want a Cosmetic Breast Augmentation to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C002_V2.name: C002
SYM_PLAS_C002_V2.description: The patient has expressed that they want Liposuction to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C003_V2.name: C003
SYM_PLAS_C003_V2.description: The patient has expressed that they want Augmentation Rhinoplasty to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C004_V2.name: C004
SYM_PLAS_C004_V2.description: The patient has expressed that they want a Hair Transplant to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C005.name: Patient Request (Chemical Peel)
SYM_PLAS_C005.description: The patient has expressed that they want a Chemical Peel to enhance their appearance. The patient wishes to only have a light peel, so nothing too taxing on the skin or requiring long term treatment.

SYM_PLAS_C006.name: Patient Request (Spider Veins)
SYM_PLAS_C006.description: The patient has expressed that they want a procedure to remove spider veins (Telangiectasias) from a part or parts of their body to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C007.name: Patient Request (Skin Conditioning)
SYM_PLAS_C007.description: The patient has expressed that they want botox injections to help condition their skin and enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C008.name: Patient Request (Skin Conditioning)
SYM_PLAS_C008.description: The patient has expressed that they want dermal filler injections to help condition their skin and enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C009.name: Patient Request (Skin Rejuvenation)
SYM_PLAS_C009.description: The patient has expressed that they want a laser resurfacing procedure to help rejuvenation their skin and enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_R001.name: Blepharospam (Evaluation)
SYM_PLAS_R001.description: There is no particular test for blepharospasm. Diagnosis relies on physical examination, medical history and ruling out other possible causes. The doctor has evaluated the patient's symptoms and determined the diagnosis is indeed blepharospasm.

SYM_PLAS_R002_V2.name: R002
SYM_PLAS_R002_V2.description: The patient has been confirmed to have misaligned eyes.

SYM_PLAS_R003.name: Simple Varicose Veins
SYM_PLAS_R003.description: The patient has varicose veins but fortunately they appear to be rather simple and lack the signs or symptoms of a more serious diagnosis. Varicose veins that cause symptoms should not be ignored and treated promptly to prevent patient discomfort and to help prevent future issues.

SYM_PLAS_R004_M.name: R004
SYM_PLAS_R004_M.description: The patient's finger is permanently bent inwards towards the palm. The bending is a result of thick tissue forcing the finger into a contractured position. Evaluation suggests this is the cause of a Dupuytren’s Contracture.

SYM_PLAS_R004_F.name: R004
SYM_PLAS_R004_F.description: The patient's finger is permanently bent inwards towards the palm. The bending is a result of thick tissue forcing the finger into a contractured position. Evaluation suggests this is the cause of a Dupuytren’s Contracture.

SYM_PLAS_R005.name: Hypertrophic Scarring
SYM_PLAS_R005.description: The patient has presented with Hypertrophic Scarring that is giving them some degree of discomfort. While these scars aren’t dangerous or life-threatening correcting the discomfort will help enhance the patient's quality of life.

SYM_PLAS_R006.name: Keloid Scarring
SYM_PLAS_R006.description: The patient has presented with Keloid Scarring that is giving them some degree of discomfort. While these scars aren’t dangerous or life-threatening correcting the discomfort will help enhance the patient's quality of life.

SYM_PLAS_RT001_V2.name: Rt001
SYM_PLAS_RT001_V2.description: One of the patient's digits has been amputated due to an accident or injury.

SYM_PLAS_C010.name: Patient Request (Mastopexy)
SYM_PLAS_C010.description: The patient has expressed that they want a Mastopexy to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C011.name: Patient Request (Septoplasty)
SYM_PLAS_C011.description: The patient has expressed that they want a Septoplasty to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_R008.name: Nasal Deformities
SYM_PLAS_R008.description: The patient has nasal deformities that effect the aesthetics and functions of the nose.

SYM_PLAS_RT002.name: Amputated Toe
SYM_PLAS_RT002.description: One of the patient's toes has been amputated due to an accident or injury.

SYM_PLAS_RT003.name: Irreparable Ocular Trauma
SYM_PLAS_RT003.description: One of the patient's eyes has been all but obliterated and will not be able to be recovered.

SYM_PLAS_CKU001.name: Checkup (Breast Augmentation)
SYM_PLAS_CKU001.description: The patient has a scheduled appointment with their doctor to follow up their recent Breast Augmentation surgery.

SYM_PLAS_CKU002.name: Checkup (Augmentation Rhinoplasty)
SYM_PLAS_CKU002.description: The patient has a scheduled appointment with their doctor to follow up their recent Augmentation Rhinoplasty surgery.

SYM_PLAS_CKU003.name: Checkup (Liposuction)
SYM_PLAS_CKU003.description: The patient has a scheduled appointment with their doctor to follow up their recent Liposuction surgery.

SYM_PLAS_CKU004.name: Checkup (Mastopexy)
SYM_PLAS_CKU004.description: The patient has a scheduled appointment with their doctor to follow up their recent Mastopexy surgery.

SYM_PLAS_CKU005.name: Checkup (Septoplasty)
SYM_PLAS_CKU005.description: The patient has a scheduled appointment with their doctor to follow up their recent Septoplasty surgery.

SYM_PLAS_C012.name: Patient Request (Cheek Augmentation)
SYM_PLAS_C012.description: The patient has expressed that they want a Cheek Augmentation to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_C013.name: Patient Request (Abdominoplasty)
SYM_PLAS_C013.description: The patient has expressed that they want a Abdominoplasty to enhance their appearance.

SYM_PLAS_R010.name: Venous Ulceration
SYM_PLAS_R010.description: The patient has an abnormal wound caused by damaged veins.

SYM_PLAS_CKU006.name: Checkup (Cheek Augmentation)
SYM_PLAS_CKU006.description: The patient has a scheduled appointment with their doctor to follow up their recent Cheek Augmentation surgery.

SYM_PLAS_CKU007.name: Checkup (Abdominoplasty)
SYM_PLAS_CKU007.description: The patient has a scheduled appointment with their doctor to follow up their recent Abdominoplasty surgery.

SYM_SH_GENITAL_DISCHARGE.name: Genital Discharge
SYM_SH_GENITAL_DISCHARGE.description: Unusual discharge from the vagina, penis or bottom.

SYM_SH_GENITAL_SORES.name: Genital Sores
SYM_SH_GENITAL_SORES.description: Sores, warts or ulcers that typically appear on the penis, scrotum, vagina, vulva or around the anus.

SYM_SH_EXCESS_SEMEN.name: High Semen Volume
SYM_SH_EXCESS_SEMEN.description: The patient reports an abnormally large ejaculate (or semen) volume.

SYM_SH_LOW_SEMEN.name: Low Semen Volume
SYM_SH_LOW_SEMEN.description: The patient reports an unusually low ejaculate (or semen) volume.

SYM_SH_PHIMOSIS.name: Phimosis
SYM_SH_PHIMOSIS.description: Phimosis is a condition where the foreskin is too tight to be pulled back over the head of the penis (glans).

SYM_SH_NEISSERIA_GONORRHOEAE.name: Neisseria Gonorrhoeae Detected
SYM_SH_NEISSERIA_GONORRHOEAE.description: Tests have shown Neisseria Gonorrhoeae bacteria.

SYM_SH_PUBIC_LICE.name: Pubic Lice Present
SYM_SH_PUBIC_LICE.description: Crab (pubic) lice present in pubic hair.

SYM_SH_HIV.name: HIV Detected
SYM_SH_HIV.description: Tests have shown Human Immunodeficiency Virus.

SYM_SH_HSV.name: HSV Antibodies Detected
SYM_SH_HSV.description: Tests have shown the presence of antibodies to the Herpes Simplex Virus.

SYM_SH_SCABIES.name: Scabies Detected
SYM_SH_SCABIES.description: Mites, eggs or burrow lines have indicated the presence of Scabies.

SYM_SH_HPV.name: HPV Detected
SYM_SH_HPV.description: Genital screening has shown the presence of HPV.

SYM_SH_MCI.name: MC Detected
SYM_SH_MCI.description: Physical examination has shown the presence of MC.

SYM_SH_TV.name: TV Detected
SYM_SH_TV.description: Genital swab analysis has shown the presence of the Trichomoniasis virus.

SYM_SH_BV.name: BV Detected
SYM_SH_BV.description: Genital swab analysis has shown the presence of bacterial vaginosis.

SYM_SH_MGEN_MALE.name: Mgen Detected
SYM_SH_MGEN_MALE.description: Tests have shown the presence of mycoplasma genitalium.

SYM_SH_PREP.name: At Risk
SYM_SH_PREP.description: The patient is at high risk of HIV. Reasons can include sexuality, drug use or serodiscordant relationships.

SYM_SH_ED.name: Erectile Dysfunction
SYM_SH_ED.description: The patient struggles to achieve or maintain an erection.

SYM_SH_HYPERS.name: Hyperspermia
SYM_SH_HYPERS.description: Tests have shown the patient has hyperspermia.

SYM_SH_HYPOS.name: Hypospermia
SYM_SH_HYPOS.description: Tests have shown the patient has hypospermia.

SYM_SH_BAL.name: Balanitis
SYM_SH_BAL.description: A groin examination has shown the patient has balanitis.

SYM_SH_PRO.name: Prostatitis
SYM_SH_PRO.description: Tests have shown the patient has prostatitis.

SYM_KN_SWOLLENTESTIS_WP.name: Painful Swollen Testicle
SYM_KN_SWOLLENTESTIS_WP.description: The testicle on one side is swollen and painful

SYM_KN_SWOLLENTESTIS_WPT.name: Swollen Testicle
SYM_KN_SWOLLENTESTIS_WPT.description: The testicle on one side is swollen, but not painful

SYM_KN_TESTICULARINFARCTION.name: Testicular Infarction
SYM_KN_TESTICULARINFARCTION.description: Testicular damage occurs as a consequence of decreased blood flow, and therefore decreased oxygen and nutrient supply, to the testicle. If the testicle is not viable during surgical exploration, it must be removed to prevent further necrosis, or tissue death.

SYM_KN_HIGHRIDINGTESTIS.name: High-Riding Testicle
SYM_KN_HIGHRIDINGTESTIS.description: The testicle appears to be higher in the scrotum than normal

SYM_KN_ABNORMALLIETESTIS.name: Abnormal Lie of the Testicle
SYM_KN_ABNORMALLIETESTIS.description: The testicle appears to be in an abnormal position

SYM_KN_SUDDENPAINSCROTUM.name: Sudden Pain in Scrotum
SYM_KN_SUDDENPAINSCROTUM.description: The patient experienced a sudden pain in the scrotum

SYM_KN_ABCREMASCTICREFLEX.name: Absence of the Cremasteric Reflex
SYM_KN_ABCREMASCTICREFLEX.description: The cremasteric reflex may be absent with testicular torsion, upper and lower motor neuron disorders, as well as a spine injury of L1-L2. It can also occur if the ilioinguinal nerve has accidentally been cut during a hernia repair.

SYM_KN_CREMASCTICREFLEX.name: The Cremasteric Reflex Is Still Present
SYM_KN_CREMASCTICREFLEX.description: This reflex is elicited by lightly stroking or poking the superior and medial inner part of the thigh regardless of the direction of stroke. The normal response is an immediate contraction of the cremaster muscle that pulls up the testis ipsilaterally on the same side of the body.

SYM_KN_HARD_TESTIS.name: Hardend Testicle
SYM_KN_HARD_TESTIS.description: The testicle is harder than normal

SYM_KN_LUMPTS.name: A Lump or Mass Inside the Testis
SYM_KN_LUMPTS.description: A lump or mass inside the testis has been noticed during physical examination.

SYM_KN_ALFAPRO.name: Tumor Marker Alpha-Fetoprotein
SYM_KN_ALFAPRO.description: Tumor marker alpha-fetoprotein has been noticed in the bloodstream

SYM_KN_BETAHCG.name: Tumor Marker Bèta-HCG
SYM_KN_BETAHCG.description: Tumor marker bèta-HCG has been noticed in the bloodstream

SYM_KN_TSTM.name: Testicular Cancer Is Detected Through Ultrasonography.
SYM_KN_TSTM.description: Testicular cancer is detected through ultrasonography. The differential diagnosis of testicular cancer requires examining the histology of tissue obtained from an inguinal orchiectomy. A biopsy should not be performed, as it raises the risk of spreading cancer cells into the scrotum.

SYM_KN_HVSC.name: A Feeling Often Described as "heaviness" in the Scrotum
SYM_KN_HVSC.description: A feeling often described as "heaviness" in the scrotum

SYM_KN_ENLSM_PRO_1.name: An Enlarged Prostate Gland That Is Symmetric and Smooth.
SYM_KN_ENLSM_PRO_1.description: An enlarged prostate gland on rectal examination that is symmetric and smooth supports a diagnosis of BPH.

SYM_KN_DHT.name: High Levels of Dihydrotestosterone
SYM_KN_DHT.description: High levels of Dihydrotestosterone was detected in the bloodstream

SYM_KN_ENLSM_PRO_2.name: An Enlarged Prostate Gland That Is Symmetric and Smooth.
SYM_KN_ENLSM_PRO_2.description: An enlarged prostate gland on rectal examination that is symmetric and smooth supports a diagnosis of BPH.

SYM_KN_RETRO_EJAC.name: Retrograde Ejaculation
SYM_KN_RETRO_EJAC.description: Retrograde ejaculation occurs when semen which would be ejaculated via the urethra is redirected to the urinary bladder. Normally, the sphincter of the bladder contracts before ejaculation, sealing the bladder which besides inhibiting the release of urine also prevents a reflux of seminal fluids into the male bladder during ejaculation. The semen is forced to exit via the urethra, the path of least resistance. When the bladder sphincter does not function properly, retrograde ejaculation may occur.

SYM_KN_URIN_INCO.name: Urinary Incontinence
SYM_KN_URIN_INCO.description: Urinary incontinence, also known as involuntary urination, is any uncontrolled leakage of urine.

SYM_KN_SWOLLENEPIDIDYMIS.name: Swollen and Tender Epididymis
SYM_KN_SWOLLENEPIDIDYMIS.description: The Epididymis, the tube that connects a testicle to a vas deferens in the male reproductive system, is swollen and is tender uppon touching.

SYM_KN_INGUINAL_LYMPH_NODES.name: Swollen Lymph Nodes
SYM_KN_INGUINAL_LYMPH_NODES.description: Enlarged/swollen lymph nodes are a common sign of infectious, autoimmune, and malignant diseases.

SYM_KN_IMMUN_REACT.name: The Immunesystem Reacts to the New Organ
SYM_KN_IMMUN_REACT.description: The immunesystem reacts to the new organ

SYM_KN_ACUTE_ORG_REJEC.name: The Body Is Rejecting the New Organ
SYM_KN_ACUTE_ORG_REJEC.description: The body is rejecting the new organ. If not acted uppon, the rejection will be fatal

SYM_KN_UNPL_ODOR.name: Unpleasant Odor
SYM_KN_UNPL_ODOR.description: An unpleasant odor can be smelled around the patient

SYM_KN_RCC_7.name: Renal Cell Cancer Smaller Than 7 Centimeters Is Detected on a CT-Scan.
SYM_KN_RCC_7.description: Renal cell cancer is detected on a CT-scan. The tumor appears to be is smaller than 7 centimeters.

SYM_KN_RCC_8.name: Renal Cell Cancer Larger Than 7 Centimeters Is Detected on a CT-Scan.
SYM_KN_RCC_8.description: Renal cell cancer is detected on a CT-scan. The tumor appears to be is larger than 7 centimeters.

SYM_KN_FLANK_PAIN.name: Flank Pain
SYM_KN_FLANK_PAIN.description: Flank pain: pain on the side of the body between the hip and ribs.

SYM_KN_ABDOMINAL_MASS.name: Abdominal Mass
SYM_KN_ABDOMINAL_MASS.description: A mass can be felt in the abdomen

SYM_KN_DEPR_ERYTHR.name: Depression of Erythropoietin
SYM_KN_DEPR_ERYTHR.description: Depression of erythropoietin. Erythropoietin  stimulates red blood cell production (erythropoiesis) in the bone marrow. A depression of erythropoietin gives anemia.

SYM_UR_HYPERCALCEMIA.name: Hypercalcemia
SYM_UR_HYPERCALCEMIA.description: Hypercalcemia is a condition in which the calcium level in your blood is above normal. Hypercalcaemia is most often caused by overactivity in the four tiny glands in the neck (parathyroid glands) or from cancer. Extra calcium in the blood affects many bodily systems.

SYM_KN_RCC_INF_VENACAVA.name: Metastised Tumor in the Inferior Vena Cava. The Inferior Vena Cava (or IVC) Is a Large Vein That Carries the Deoxygenated Blood From the Lower and Middle Body Into the Right Atrium of the Heart. It Is Formed by the Joining of the Right and the Left Common Iliac Veins, Usually at the Level of the Fifth Lumbar Vertebra.
SYM_KN_RCC_INF_VENACAVA.description: Metastised tumor in the Inferior Vena Cava

SYM_KN_BLOODY_URINE.name: Blood Clots in Urine
SYM_KN_BLOODY_URINE.description: If sufficient blood is present in the urine, the blood may form a clot. The clot can completely block the flow of urine, causing sudden extreme pain and inability to urinate. Bleeding severe enough to cause such a clot is usually caused by an injury to the urinary tract.

SYM_KN_TESTISBlOODFLOW.name: Lack of Blood Flow to the Testicle
SYM_KN_TESTISBlOODFLOW.description: Lack of blood flow to the testicle

SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_1.name: Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_1.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Seminoma means it's only one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_2.name: Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_2.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Seminoma means it's only one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_3.name: Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_3.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Seminoma means it's only one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_4.name: Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_SM_TSTM_4.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Seminoma means it's only one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_1.name: Non-Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_1.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Non-seminoma means it's more than one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_2.name: Non-Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_2.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Non-seminoma means it's more than one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_3.name: Non-Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_3.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Non-seminoma means it's more than one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_4.name: Non-Seminoma Testicular Cancer
SYM_KN_NSM_TSTM_4.description: After orchiectomy was performed, the type of cancer has been differentiated. Non-seminoma means it's more than one type of cancercell.

SYM_KN_BPH_DETC_1.name: Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia
SYM_KN_BPH_DETC_1.description: Benign prostatic hyperplasia was detected through a biopsy

SYM_KN_BPH_DETC_2.name: Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia
SYM_KN_BPH_DETC_2.description: Benign prostatic hyperplasia was detected through a biopsy. However the enlarged prostate didn't react to selective α1-blockers so the minimal invasive surgery is necessary.

SYM_KN_BACSCRO.name: Bacteria Have Been Found in the Scrotum
SYM_KN_BACSCRO.description: Bacteria have been found in the scrotum

SYM_KN_CCRCC_1.name: Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma Was Detected
SYM_KN_CCRCC_1.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma was detected

SYM_KN_CCRCC_21.name: Ccrcc 2
SYM_KN_CCRCC_21.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma was detected

SYM_KN_CCRCC_22.name: Ccrcc 2
SYM_KN_CCRCC_22.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma was detected

SYM_KN_CCRCC_31.name: Ccrcc 3
SYM_KN_CCRCC_31.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma was detected

SYM_KN_CCRCC_32.name: Ccrcc 3
SYM_KN_CCRCC_32.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma was detected

SYM_KN_CCRCC_4.name: Clear Cell Renal Cell Carcinoma Was Detected
SYM_KN_CCRCC_4.description: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma was detected

SYM_KN_VESCI_FIST.name: Vesicovaginal Fistula
SYM_KN_VESCI_FIST.description: Vesicovaginal fistula, is an abnormal fistulous tract extending between the bladder (vesica) and the vagina that allows the continuous involuntary discharge of urine into the vaginal vault.

SYM_KN_URETHRAL_STONES_DETECTED.name: Ureter Stone Detected
SYM_KN_URETHRAL_STONES_DETECTED.description: Ultrasound examination has shown a large stone obstructing one of the ureters (the tubes connecting kidneys and the bladder).

SYM_KN_BLADDER_STONES_DETECTED.name: Bladder Stones Detected
SYM_KN_BLADDER_STONES_DETECTED.description: Ultrasonography has shown multiple stones in the patient's bladder.

SYM_KN_KIDNEY_STONES_DISCOVERED.name: Kidney Stones Discovered
SYM_KN_KIDNEY_STONES_DISCOVERED.description: CT examination discovered large stones inside a kidney.

SYM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOTIC_SYMPTOMS.name: Psychotic Symptoms
SYM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOTIC_SYMPTOMS.description: Patients exhibit significant and persistent abnormalities in cognition, emotional regulation, or behavior, with a disruption in the integrity and coherence of mental activities. Their reality testing and social functioning are severely impaired, leading to an inability to adapt to normal life, accompanied by a lack of insight.

SYM_NEW_PSY_PSG_PLMI.name: PSG: Repeated Periodic Limb Movements
SYM_NEW_PSY_PSG_PLMI.description: The patient exhibits ≥5 periodic limb movements per hour during sleep, which supports the diagnosis of restless legs syndrome (RLS).

SYM_NEW_PSY_RESTLESS_LEGS.name: Restless Legs Syndrome
SYM_NEW_PSY_RESTLESS_LEGS.description: Restless legs syndrome (RLS) is characterized by an intense discomfort in the lower limbs, primarily occurring during nighttime sleep or when at rest. Patients frequently describe this abnormal sensation as crawling, tingling, itching, or aching.

SYM_NEW_PSY_ACUTE_AND_TRANSIENT_PSYCHOTI.name: BPD: Brief Psychotic Disorder
SYM_NEW_PSY_ACUTE_AND_TRANSIENT_PSYCHOTI.description: Acute Brief Psychotic Disorder is a psychiatric condition characterized by the sudden onset of psychotic symptoms that last for a relatively short duration, typically less than one month.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DEPERSONALIZATION_DIA.name: Depersonalization
SYM_NEW_PSY_DEPERSONALIZATION_DIA.description: Depersonalization refers to a dissociative symptom where individuals experience a sense of detachment or estrangement from their own thoughts, feelings, or sense of self.

SYM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOGENIC_EXCITEMENT.name: Psychogenic Excitement
SYM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOGENIC_EXCITEMENT.description: Psychogenic excitement refers to a state of agitation and heightened arousal that occurs suddenly following intense psychological stimuli, characterized by extreme agitation, shouting, emotional outbursts, and even convulsive episodes. The episode may last for several hours or days before subsiding.

SYM_NEW_PSY_CATATONIC_EXCITEMENT.name: Catatonic Excitement
SYM_NEW_PSY_CATATONIC_EXCITEMENT.description: Catatonic excitement is an uncoordinated state of motor agitation that is typically unpredictable, lacking clear motivation or purpose, and often appears suddenly and lasts for a short duration, characterized by impulsive behaviors such as sudden acts of violence or destruction, but rarely associated with speech disorganization.

SYM_NEW_PSY_HEBEPHRENIC_EXCITEMENT.name: Hebephrenic Excitement
SYM_NEW_PSY_HEBEPHRENIC_EXCITEMENT.description: Hebephrenic excitement is a form of uncoordinated psychomotor agitation characterized by increased speech and motor activity that is aimless and disorganized. It is markedly incongruent with the individual's thoughts and emotions.

SYM_NEW_PSY_JAMAIS_VU.name: Déjà Vu
SYM_NEW_PSY_JAMAIS_VU.description: Déjà vu is characterized by the feeling that previously familiar situations, people, or places are being encountered as if they are new and unfamiliar.

SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPOESTHESIA.name: Hypoesthesia
SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPOESTHESIA.description: Hypoesthesia is defined as a reduction in sensory perception, characterized by an increased threshold for detecting external stimuli. Patients with hypoesthesia may experience a diminished sensitivity to strong stimuli or may be unable to perceive sensations altogether (sensory loss).

SYM_NEW_PSY_AFFECTIVE_PARALYSIS.name: Affective Paralysis
SYM_NEW_PSY_AFFECTIVE_PARALYSIS.description: Affective paralysis, also known as affective shock, refers to a temporary suppression of emotional responses caused by intense psychological stress. Patients in this state may experience extreme sorrow or terror but exhibit a lack of internal emotional experience and facial expression, appearing as if they are in a daze, sometimes even unresponsive to painful stimuli or showing signs of stupor.

SYM_NEW_PSY_NARROWING_OF_ATTENTION.name: Narrowed Attention
SYM_NEW_PSY_NARROWING_OF_ATTENTION.description: Narrowed attention refers to a significant constriction in the range of attention, with a marked reduction in active attention. The patient is able to perceive stimuli normally within this narrowed range, but stimuli outside of this limited focus are difficult to notice or respond to.

SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPOPROSEXIA.name: Hypoprosexia
SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPOPROSEXIA.description: Hypoprosexia refers to a decline in an individual's ability to focus on external stimuli. This condition involves a reduction in both voluntary and involuntary attention. It is characterized by difficulty in maintaining concentration, decreased stability of attention, and a narrowing of the attentional focus.

SYM_NEW_PSY_NIGHT-EATING.name: NES: Night Eating Syndrome
SYM_NEW_PSY_NIGHT-EATING.description: Night eating involves excessive eating between dinner and bedtime or refers to eating large amounts of food after falling asleep, typically awakening during the night to consume foods high in carbohydrates.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SHIFT_WORK.name: Shift Work
SYM_NEW_PSY_SHIFT_WORK.description: The patient’s work schedule is inconsistent with conventional social work hours, often involving work during times typically reserved for sleep (work hours outside the standard period of 8 AM to 6 PM), especially including night shifts.

SYM_NEW_PSY_POSSIBLE_MENTAL_DISORDERS.name: Suspicious Mental Disorders
SYM_NEW_PSY_POSSIBLE_MENTAL_DISORDERS.description: The patient exhibits signs suggestive of a psychiatric disorder or mental health condition. It is advisable to refer them for evaluation by a psychiatrist or request a psychiatric consultation.。

SYM_NEW_PSY_TOBACCO_WITHDRAWAL.name: Tobacco Withdrawal
SYM_NEW_PSY_TOBACCO_WITHDRAWAL.description: Tobacco withdrawal refers to the physiological and psychological symptoms experienced when an individual who has been using tobacco products.

SYM_NEW_PSY_INCREASED_APPETITE.name: Hyperphagia
SYM_NEW_PSY_INCREASED_APPETITE.description: Increased appetite, also known as hyperphagia, refers to a heightened or excessive desire to eat.

SYM_NEW_PSY_TIC_DISORDERS.name: Tic Disorders
SYM_NEW_PSY_TIC_DISORDERS.description: Tic Disorder is a neuropsychiatric condition characterized by the presence of multiple motor and vocal tics over a period of time.

SYM_NEW_PSY_STEROTYPE_OF_SPEECH.name: Stereotyped Speech
SYM_NEW_PSY_STEROTYPE_OF_SPEECH.description: Stereotyped speech refers to a condition where a patient exhibits repetitive and rigid verbal behavior, often due to difficulties in cognitive flexibility and limited conceptual thinking.

SYM_NEW_PSY_COPROLALIA.name: Coprolalia
SYM_NEW_PSY_COPROLALIA.description: Coprolalia is defined as the involuntary and repetitive utterance of obscene, vulgar, or derogatory language, including offensive remarks about ethnicity, race, or religion.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SENSORY_TICS.name: Sensory Tics
SYM_NEW_PSY_SENSORY_TICS.description: Sensory tics refer to a type of tic experienced by individuals with Tourette Syndrome or other tic disorders, where the individual has a heightened or unusual sensory perception that precedes or accompanies the tic。

SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPLEX_VOCAL_TICS.name: Complex Vocal Tics
SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPLEX_VOCAL_TICS.description: Complex vocal tics involve sudden, meaningful vocalizations

SYM_NEW_PSY_SIMPLE_VOCAL_TICS.name: Simple Vocal Tics
SYM_NEW_PSY_SIMPLE_VOCAL_TICS.description: Simple vocal tics are characterized by sudden, meaningless vocalizations

SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPLEX_MOTOR_TICS.name: Complex Motor Tics
SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPLEX_MOTOR_TICS.description: Complex motor tics are characterized by movements that are more elaborate and prolonged than simple motor tics. These tics often involve multiple muscle groups and may appear more purposeful, though they are still involuntary.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SIMPLE_MOTOR_TICS.name: Simple Motor Tics
SYM_NEW_PSY_SIMPLE_MOTOR_TICS.description: Simple motor tics are characterized by sudden, involuntary, and purposeless movements involving one or a few muscle groups. These tics are typically non-rhythmic and may occur rapidly and repeatedly.

SYM_NEW_PSY_INVOLUNTARY_MOVEMENT.name: Involuntary Movements
SYM_NEW_PSY_INVOLUNTARY_MOVEMENT.description: Involuntary movements refer to abnormal motor activities that occur in patients who are fully conscious but cannot consciously control or direct these movements.

SYM_NEW_PSY_OVERPRICED_FEAR_OF_OBESITY.name: Obesophobia
SYM_NEW_PSY_OVERPRICED_FEAR_OF_OBESITY.description: Obesophobia refers to a persistent and uncontrollable fear of becoming overweight, which leads individuals to adopt extreme measures to prevent weight gain. Patients often engage in behaviors such as restricting food intake, excessive physical exercise, abusing laxatives or diet pills, and inducing vomiting.

SYM_NEW_PSY_BODY_IMAGE_DISORDER.name: Body Image Disorder
SYM_NEW_PSY_BODY_IMAGE_DISORDER.description: The patient exhibits distorted body perception. One manifestation involves the belief of being overweight despite being underweight, with such perceptions often focused on specific body parts like the thighs or abdomen. Additionally, some patients experience a sensation of their body expanding immediately after eating, or even feel their body becomes fat as soon as food is placed in their mouth.

SYM_NEW_PSY_ANOREXIA_NERVOSA.name: Anorexia Nervosa
SYM_NEW_PSY_ANOREXIA_NERVOSA.description: Anorexia nervosa is an eating disorder characterized by an intense fear of gaining weight, a distorted body image, and a severe restriction of food intake leading to significant weight loss.

SYM_NEW_PSY_LOW_LEVELS_OF_OREXIN_IN_CERE.name: Low Cerebrospinal Fluid (CSF) Orexin Levels
SYM_NEW_PSY_LOW_LEVELS_OF_OREXIN_IN_CERE.description: The patient has low levels of orexin in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF).

SYM_NEW_PSY_PSG_BARCOLEPSY.name: PSG: Narcolepsy
SYM_NEW_PSY_PSG_BARCOLEPSY.description: Presence of REM sleep within a short period after sleep onset (typically within 15 minutes), which is unusual in healthy individuals.

SYM_NEW_PSY_MSLT_BARCOLEPSY.name: MSLT: Narcolepsy
SYM_NEW_PSY_MSLT_BARCOLEPSY.description: The Multiple Sleep Latency Test (MSLT) shows a shortened average sleep latency and the presence of two or more sleep-onset REM periods (SOREMPs).

SYM_NEW_PSY_CATAPLEXY.name: Cataplexy
SYM_NEW_PSY_CATAPLEXY.description: Cataplexy refers to a sudden and brief episode of muscle weakness or paralysis caused by a complete or partial loss of bilateral muscle tone, resulting in generalized or localized flaccid attacks. These episodes can manifest as falls, head droop, eyelid droop, mouth opening, or tongue protrusion.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SLEEP_PARALYSIS.name: Sleep Paralysis
SYM_NEW_PSY_SLEEP_PARALYSIS.description: Sleep paralysis is a transient condition that occurs during the transition between sleep and wakefulness. It is characterized by a temporary inability to move or speak, despite being fully conscious. Episodes typically last for a few seconds to a few minutes.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SLEEP_RELATED_HALLUCINARION.name: Sleep Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_SLEEP_RELATED_HALLUCINARION.description: Sleep hallucinations encompass a range of phenomena, including hypnagogic (occurring while falling asleep) and hypnopompic (occurring upon waking) hallucinations. These experiences involve vivid sensory perceptions, which can be visual, auditory, or tactile.

SYM_NEW_PSY_LIFE_EVENT.name: Life Event
SYM_NEW_PSY_LIFE_EVENT.description: Patients were experiencing significant life events prior to the onset of their condition, such as bereavement, divorce or career changes.

SYM_NEW_PSY_LEARNING_DIFFICULTY.name: Learning Difficulties
SYM_NEW_PSY_LEARNING_DIFFICULTY.description: The patient may experience learning difficulties and poor academic performance due to intellectual disability, attention problems, or cognitive impairments.

SYM_NEW_PSY_ADHD.name: ADHD
SYM_NEW_PSY_ADHD.description: Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) is a common neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by persistent patterns of inattention, hyperactivity, and impulsivity.

SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPERACTIVITY.name: Hyperactivity
SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPERACTIVITY.description: Hyperactivity is characterized by excessive activity, heightened excitement, and increased restlessness.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DIVERGENCE_OF_ATTENTION.name: Attention Dispersion
SYM_NEW_PSY_DIVERGENCE_OF_ATTENTION.description: Divergence of Attention refers to a marked reduction in the ability to focus and sustain attention on a specific object or task.

SYM_NEW_PSY_CEREBRAL_ATROPHY.name: Cerebral Atrophy
SYM_NEW_PSY_CEREBRAL_ATROPHY.description: Cerebral Atrophy refers to the imaging manifestation of abnormal reduction in brain cells and the shrinkage of brain tissue. It can be a sign of normal aging or result from various causes such as brain injury, neurological diseases, or infections.

SYM_NEW_PSY_HIPPOCAMPAL_ATROPHY.name: Hippocampal Atrophy
SYM_NEW_PSY_HIPPOCAMPAL_ATROPHY.description: Selective atrophy of the temporal lobe, particularly the hippocampal structures, is considered a hallmark pathological feature of Alzheimer's disease.

SYM_NEW_PSY_ORGANIC_PERSONALITY_CHANGE.name: Organic Personality Disorder
SYM_NEW_PSY_ORGANIC_PERSONALITY_CHANGE.description: Organic Personality Change specifically results from organic brain pathology and is considered part of the primary disease symptoms.

SYM_NEW_PSY_APRAXIA.name: Apraxia
SYM_NEW_PSY_APRAXIA.description: Apraxia is a condition in which an individual, despite having normal sensory function, muscle strength, and coordination, is unable to perform purposeful movements.

SYM_NEW_PSY_AGNOSIA.name: Agnosia
SYM_NEW_PSY_AGNOSIA.description: Agnosia is a neurological disorder characterized by the inability to recognize or identify objects, sounds, or other sensory stimuli despite having intact sensory function. This impairment occurs at the cortical level of the brain, and is not due to damage to peripheral sensory organs such as hearing loss.

SYM_NEW_PSY_APHASIA.name: Aphasia
SYM_NEW_PSY_APHASIA.description: Aphasia refers to the loss of the ability to understand or express language due to localized damage in certain areas of the brain. Patients with aphasia have clear consciousness and do not suffer from sensory deficits or paralysis of the speech muscles.

SYM_NEW_PSY_PERFORMANCE_ANXIETY.name: Performance Anxiety
SYM_NEW_PSY_PERFORMANCE_ANXIETY.description: Performance anxiety refers to the excessive fear and anxiety experienced by an individual when speaking in public or performing in front of an audience.

SYM_NEW_PSY_AGRO_PHOBIA.name: Agoraphobia
SYM_NEW_PSY_AGRO_PHOBIA.description: Agoraphobia is characterized by a fear of being alone or leaving the house, as well as an intense fear of specific situations or places.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SPECIFIC_PHOBIA.name: Specific Phobia
SYM_NEW_PSY_SPECIFIC_PHOBIA.description: Specific phobia refers to an intense, irrational fear of a particular object, situation, or activity.

SYM_NEW_PSY_CLAUSTROPHOBIA.name: Claustrophobia
SYM_NEW_PSY_CLAUSTROPHOBIA.description: Claustrophobia is an intense and irrational fear of enclosed or confined spaces, such as airplanes, elevators, or other small or enclosed areas.

SYM_NEW_PSY_FEAR_OF_BLOOD_INJECTIONS_AND.name: Blood-Injection-Injury Phobia
SYM_NEW_PSY_FEAR_OF_BLOOD_INJECTIONS_AND.description: Blood-injection-injury phobia is characterized by an intense and irrational fear of situations involving blood, trauma, injections, tooth extraction, or surgery.

SYM_NEW_PSY_FEAR_OF_NATURAL_ENVIRONMENT.name: Natural Environment Phobia
SYM_NEW_PSY_FEAR_OF_NATURAL_ENVIRONMENT.description: Natural environment phobia is characterized by an intense and irrational fear of natural environments or phenomena, such as heights (acrophobia), darkness (nyctophobia), thunderstorms (astraphobia), strong winds, rain, or flowing water.

SYM_NEW_PSY_ZOOPHOBIA.name: Zoophobia
SYM_NEW_PSY_ZOOPHOBIA.description: Animal phobia is characterized by an intense and irrational fear of animals or insects, such as spiders, mice, cats, dogs, or pigs.

SYM_NEW_PSY_EYE_PHOBIA.name: Eye Phobia
SYM_NEW_PSY_EYE_PHOBIA.description: Eye phobia refers to the fear of making eye contact with others.

SYM_NEW_PSY_BLUSH_PHOBIA.name: Erythrophobia
SYM_NEW_PSY_BLUSH_PHOBIA.description: Erythrophobia refers to the fear of blushing in social situations or when interacting with others in public.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SOCIAL_PHOBIA.name: Social Phobia
SYM_NEW_PSY_SOCIAL_PHOBIA.description: In social situations, the fear of interacting with others is referred to as social phobia or social anxiety.

SYM_NEW_PSY_PSG_INSOMNIA.name: PSG：Insomnia
SYM_NEW_PSY_PSG_INSOMNIA.description: Polysomnography (PSG) revealed that the patient had prolonged sleep latency, extended wake after sleep onset, reduced sleep efficiency, and shortened objective sleep time. These findings are consistent with a diagnosis of insomnia. No evidence of sleep-disordered breathing was observed.

SYM_NEW_PSY_ALCOHOL_WITHDRAWAL.name: Alcohol Withdrawal
SYM_NEW_PSY_ALCOHOL_WITHDRAWAL.description: Alcohol withdrawal refers to a range of symptoms that occur when an individual who has been consuming alcohol regularly and in significant amounts suddenly reduces or stops alcohol intake.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SCHIZOPHRENIA.name: Schizophrenia
SYM_NEW_PSY_SCHIZOPHRENIA.description: Schizophrenia is a chronic and severe mental disorder characterized by a profound disruption in thought processes, perceptions, emotional responsiveness, and social functioning.

SYM_NEW_PSY_CHRONIC_INSOMNIA.name: Chronic Insomnia Disorder
SYM_NEW_PSY_CHRONIC_INSOMNIA.description: Chronic insomnia disorder is a sleep disorder characterized by persistent difficulties with sleep initiation, maintenance, or early morning awakening despite adequate opportunities for sleep, leading to daytime impairment or distress.

SYM_NEW_PSY_BIPOLAR_DISORDER.name: Bipolar Disorder
SYM_NEW_PSY_BIPOLAR_DISORDER.description: Bipolar disorder is a mental health condition characterized by significant and severe mood swings, which include emotional highs (mania or hypomania) and lows (depression).

SYM_NEW_PSY_ACUTE_STRESS_DISORDER.name: Acute Stress Disorder
SYM_NEW_PSY_ACUTE_STRESS_DISORDER.description: Acute stress disorder is a mental health condition that can occur after an individual experiences a traumatic event. It is characterized by severe anxiety, dissociation, and other symptoms that occur within three days to one month following the trauma.

SYM_NEW_PSY_PTSD.name: PTSD
SYM_NEW_PSY_PTSD.description: Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) is a mental health condition triggered by experiencing or witnessing a traumatic event.

SYM_NEW_PSY_OBSESSIVE-COMPULSIVE_DISORDE.name: OCD
SYM_NEW_PSY_OBSESSIVE-COMPULSIVE_DISORDE.description: Obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD) is a chronic mental health condition characterized by the presence of obsessions (recurrent, intrusive thoughts, images, or urges) and/or compulsions (repetitive behaviors or mental acts performed to reduce the anxiety caused by obsessions).

SYM_NEW_PSY_GENERALIZED_ANXIETY_DISORDER.name: Generalized Anxiety Disorder
SYM_NEW_PSY_GENERALIZED_ANXIETY_DISORDER.description: Generalized anxiety disorder is a chronic mental health condition characterized by excessive, uncontrollable, and often irrational worry about various aspects of life, including personal health, work, social interactions, and everyday routines.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SOMATIC_SYMPTOM_DISORDER.name: Somatic Symptom Disorder
SYM_NEW_PSY_SOMATIC_SYMPTOM_DISORDER.description: Somatic symptom disorder is a mental health condition characterized by a preoccupation with physical symptoms that cause significant distress or impairment. The symptoms are often distressing and may or may not be associated with a known medical condition, but the focus is on the excessive thoughts, feelings, or behaviors related to these symptoms.

SYM_NEW_PSY_MAJOR_DEPRESSIVE_DISORDER.name: Depressive Disorder
SYM_NEW_PSY_MAJOR_DEPRESSIVE_DISORDER.description: Depressive disorder is a mental health condition characterized by persistent feelings of sadness, hopelessness, and a lack of interest or pleasure in daily activities.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DEPERSONALIZATION.name: Depersonalization
SYM_NEW_PSY_DEPERSONALIZATION.description: Derealization, also known as derealization or reality detachment, refers to a condition where patients experience a diminished clarity in the perception of their surroundings.

SYM_NEW_PSY_PANIC_ATTACK.name: Panic Attack
SYM_NEW_PSY_PANIC_ATTACK.description: A panic attack is a sudden and intense episode of overwhelming fear or discomfort that reaches its peak within minutes and is characterized by a variety of physical and cognitive symptoms.

SYM_NEW_PSY_VERBAL_AUDITORY_HALLUCINATIO.name: Auditory Verbal Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_VERBAL_AUDITORY_HALLUCINATIO.description: Auditory verbal hallucinations specifically involve hearing voices or sounds that resemble human speech.

SYM_NEW_PSY_COMMENT_HALLUCINATION.name: Commentary Auditory Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_COMMENT_HALLUCINATION.description: Commentary auditory hallucinations specifically refer to auditory hallucinations where the content involves judgments or criticisms about the individual's moral character, behavior, or actions. These hallucinations often include reprimands, ridicule, or sarcasm.

SYM_NEW_PSY_ARGUMENT_HALLUCINATION.name: Argumentative Auditory Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_ARGUMENT_HALLUCINATION.description: Argumentative auditory hallucinations refer to a type of auditory hallucination where the individual hears voices engaged in arguments or debates. These voices may be perceived as arguing with each other or with the person experiencing the hallucinations.

SYM_NEW_PSY_COMMAND_HALLUCINATION.name: Command Auditory Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_COMMAND_HALLUCINATION.description: Command auditory hallucinations can indeed lead to severe outcomes, including self-harm and violence toward others.

SYM_NEW_PSY_VISUAL_HALLUCINATION.name: Visual Hallucinations
