SYM_NEW_PSY_VISUAL_HALLUCINATION.description: Visual hallucinations refer to the experience of seeing visual images or scenes that are not actually present in the external environment. These hallucinations can vary greatly in their content and detail.

SYM_NEW_PSY_OLFACTORY_HALLUCINATION.name: Olfactory Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_OLFACTORY_HALLUCINATION.description: Olfactory hallucinations refer to the experience of smelling odors that are not present in the environment. These hallucinations can involve a variety of smells, often leading to distressing emotional responses.

SYM_NEW_PSY_GUSTATORY_HALLUCINATION.name: Gustatory Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_GUSTATORY_HALLUCINATION.description: Gustatory hallucinations refer to the experience of tasting flavors or sensations that are not actually present in the food or environment. These hallucinations can lead to the perception of abnormal or unpleasant tastes, which can significantly impact an individual's eating behavior and overall quality of life.

SYM_NEW_PSY_TACTILE_HALLUCINATION.name: Tactile Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_TACTILE_HALLUCINATION.description: Tactile hallucinations refer to the experience of feeling abnormal sensations on the skin or mucous membranes without any actual external stimulus. These hallucinations can involve a wide range of tactile experiences that are not grounded in reality.

SYM_NEW_PSY_FUNCTIONAL_HALLUCINATION.name: Functional Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_FUNCTIONAL_HALLUCINATION.description: Functional hallucinations refer to a phenomenon where, in the presence of objective stimuli, the individual experiences an additional, unrelated hallucination in the same sensory modality. This type of hallucination involves the simultaneous presence of both genuine perception and hallucination, with each experience occurring independently and without blending together.

SYM_NEW_PSY_REFLEX_HALLUCINATION.name: Reflex Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_REFLEX_HALLUCINATION.description: Reflexive hallucinations refer to a phenomenon where an objective sensory stimulus triggers the simultaneous occurrence of a hallucination in a different sensory modality.

SYM_NEW_PSY_POVERTY_OF_THOUGHT.name: Thought Poverty
SYM_NEW_PSY_POVERTY_OF_THOUGHT.description: Thought poverty refers to a type of thought disorder characterized by a decrease in the quantity and richness of thoughts, leading to reduced verbal output and limited cognitive processing.

SYM_NEW_PSY_LOOSENESS_OF_THOUGHT.name: Thought Loosening
SYM_NEW_PSY_LOOSENESS_OF_THOUGHT.description: Thought loosening refers to a type of thought disorder characterized by a lack of coherence and logical connection between thoughts. This symptom is often observed in various psychiatric conditions and impacts the clarity and organization of an individual's thinking.

SYM_NEW_PSY_BLOCKING_OF_THOUGHT.name: Thought Blocking
SYM_NEW_PSY_BLOCKING_OF_THOUGHT.description: Thought blocking is a cognitive symptom characterized by a sudden and involuntary interruption in the flow of thought. This interruption can cause the individual to experience a blank mind and disrupt their ability to continue or complete their line of reasoning. Thought blocking is not due to conscious distraction, forgetfulness, or attention deficit.

SYM_NEW_PSY_PRESSURE_OF_THOUGHT.name: Thought Flooding
SYM_NEW_PSY_PRESSURE_OF_THOUGHT.description: Thought flooding is a type of thought disorder characterized by the overwhelming and involuntary emergence of a large number of thoughts or associations in the mind.

SYM_NEW_PSY_NEOLOGISM.name: Neologisms
SYM_NEW_PSY_NEOLOGISM.description: Neologisms are a type of cognitive logic disorder characterized by the fusion, condensation, and assembly of unrelated concepts. Patients create new symbols, words, graphics, or language to replace specific concepts, with meanings that are comprehensible only to themselves and appear absurd or bizarre to others.

SYM_NEW_PSY_PARALOGIC_THINKING.name: Paralogical Thinking
SYM_NEW_PSY_PARALOGIC_THINKING.description: Paralogical Thinking is characterized by a lack of logical reasoning in thought processes. It manifests as conclusions that lack appropriate premises or involve inverted causal relationships.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SOPHISTIC_THINKING.name: Sophistic Thinking
SYM_NEW_PSY_SOPHISTIC_THINKING.description: Sophistic thinking refers to a condition where patients engage in lengthy, verbose discourse on trivial or meaningless topics from everyday life, often lacking practical significance or concrete goals.

SYM_NEW_PSY_THOUGHT_AMBIVALENCE.name: Thought Ambivalence
SYM_NEW_PSY_THOUGHT_AMBIVALENCE.description: Thought ambivalence refers to a cognitive condition in which an individual simultaneously holds two opposing and contradictory concepts or beliefs. These conflicting ideas are in a state of balance or opposition, making it difficult for the individual to make a decision or choose between them.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DELUSION_OF_PERSEECUTION.name: Paranoid Delusion
SYM_NEW_PSY_DELUSION_OF_PERSEECUTION.description: Paranoid Delusion is a type of thought disorder characterized by a fixed, unfounded belief that others are persecuting or conspiring against the individual or their loved ones.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DELUSION_OF_REFERECNE.name: Delusion of Reference
SYM_NEW_PSY_DELUSION_OF_REFERECNE.description: Delusion of Reference is a type of psychotic belief where individuals are convinced that events, conversations, or stimuli in their environment that are objectively unrelated to them have a special significance or are directly connected to them. This delusion involves interpreting external events or everyday occurrences as having personal relevance or as being specifically directed towards oneself.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DELUSION_OF_INFLUENCE.name: Delusion of Influence
SYM_NEW_PSY_DELUSION_OF_INFLUENCE.description: Delusion of Influence is a type of delusion in which the patient firmly believes that their mental processes or bodily functions are being interfered with or controlled by some external entity, mysterious force, or device.

SYM_NEW_PSY_EXPERIENCE_OF_BEING_REVALED.name: 被洞悉感
SYM_NEW_PSY_EXPERIENCE_OF_BEING_REVALED.description: 被洞悉感是一种其它妄想，患者坚信自己的思想未经过言语或者其他方式表达出来就被别人知道了，甚至人尽皆知，自己成了一个透明人，毫无隐私可言。但是别人究竟是如何知道的，患者却不一定能描述得清楚。思维扩散更强调患者感到其思想在向周围传播，基本与被洞悉感属同一概念。如患者感到自己的思维显声，在向周围扩散，使尽人皆知，则称思维被广播。上述三者均系思维自主性障碍。

SYM_NEW_PSY_PARATHYMIA.name: Parathymia
SYM_NEW_PSY_PARATHYMIA.description: Parathymia refers to an emotional reaction that is contrary to the nature of the external stimulus.

SYM_NEW_PSY_APATHY.name: Apathy
SYM_NEW_PSY_APATHY.description: Apathy refers to a reduction in emotional responsiveness to external stimuli, characterized by a diminished emotional reaction. This condition manifests as a notable decrease in emotional engagement and expression.

SYM_NEW_PSY_AMBIVALENT_FEELINGS.name: Ambivalent Feelings
SYM_NEW_PSY_AMBIVALENT_FEELINGS.description: Ambivalent Feelings refer to the simultaneous experience of two entirely opposing and mutually contradictory emotional states. This phenomenon involves the presence of conflicting emotions at the same time, creating an internal emotional dissonance.

SYM_NEW_PSY_PARABULIA.name: Parabulia
SYM_NEW_PSY_PARABULIA.description: Parabulia refers to a condition where intentional behaviors or actions are contrary to common sense or social norms, making them difficult to understand.

SYM_NEW_PSY_CATATONIC_STUPOR.name: Catatonic Stupor
SYM_NEW_PSY_CATATONIC_STUPOR.description: Catatonic stupor is characterized by a state of severe motor inhibition with prominent skeletal muscle rigidity. Patients remain immobile, either sitting, standing, or lying in bed for extended periods, with fixed facial expressions and neglected autonomic functions. They exhibit no voluntary movement or verbal communication and show resistance to limb movement. Awareness of surroundings remains intact, and symptoms can persist from days to years.

SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPULSIVE_THOUGHT.name: Compulsive Thought
SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPULSIVE_THOUGHT.description: Compulsive thought refers to the persistent, intrusive, and distressing thoughts, images, or impulses that repeatedly invade a person's mind. While individuals recognize these thoughts as irrational or irrelevant, they find themselves unable to stop or control them, leading to significant anxiety and distress.

SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPULSIVE_INTENTION.name: Compulsive Intention
SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPULSIVE_INTENTION.description: Compulsive intention refers to an intrusive and uncontrollable urge to act in a way that conflicts with the individual's own desires or values. Despite recognizing the irrationality of these impulses, the individual is unable to control or suppress them, leading to significant distress.

SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPULSIVE_AFFECT.name: Compulsive Affect
SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPULSIVE_AFFECT.description: Compulsive affect refers to an unnecessary and persistent concern or fear related to the loss of control over one’s own emotions. This type of affective experience involves excessive worry and anxiety about the possibility of uncontrollable emotional responses, such as the fear of going insane or acting in ways that are illegal or immoral.

SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPULSIVE_HALLUCINATION.name: Obsessive Hallucinations
SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPULSIVE_HALLUCINATION.description: Obsessive hallucinations are defined by the persistent and repetitive experience of hallucinations with consistent content, which the individual cannot escape or dismiss. This type of hallucination leads to significant distress and is considered relatively rare.

SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPULSIVE_ACT.name: Compulsive Behavior
SYM_NEW_PSY_COMPULSIVE_ACT.description: Compulsive behavior refers to actions or mental activities that a patient feels compelled to repeat, often to alleviate or counteract the discomfort and anxiety caused by obsessive thoughts. These repetitive behaviors are driven by the need to prevent or neutralize perceived threats or fears associated with obsessive ideas.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DULLNESS.name: Dullness
SYM_NEW_PSY_DULLNESS.description: Dullness is generally considered milder than emotional apathy, with emotional apathy being milder than emotional deterioration. Patients with emotional blunting exhibit a delayed response to typical emotional stimuli, and even in the presence of intense emotional factors, their reactions remain subdued, with a lack of deep inner experience.

SYM_NEW_PSY_FLASH_BACK.name: Re-Experiencing Traumatic Events
SYM_NEW_PSY_FLASH_BACK.description: Re-experiencing traumatic events involves patients reliving the traumatic situation in various forms. This can include involuntarily recalling the distressing experience, vivid traumatic images, perceptions, and imaginations, or frequently experiencing nightmares related to the traumatic event.

SYM_NEW_PSY_AVOIDANCE.name: Avoidance
SYM_NEW_PSY_AVOIDANCE.description: Avoidance symptoms refer to the patient's persistent efforts to avoid situations, people, objects, thoughts, feelings, and conversations related to the traumatic event.

SYM_NEW_PSY_ELATION.name: Elation
SYM_NEW_PSY_ELATION.description: Elation in clinical terms refers to a marked enhancement in emotional activity, characterized by persistent feelings of joy, cheerfulness, and an exaggerated sense of well-being. Patients exhibit an unusually high mood, with behaviors reflecting a state of being excessively pleased, optimistic, and confident.

SYM_NEW_PSY_GRANDIOSE_DELUSION.name: Grandiose Delusions
SYM_NEW_PSY_GRANDIOSE_DELUSION.description: Delusions of grandeur refer to the patient's belief that they hold a high position of power or authority; claim extraordinary talents or numerous groundbreaking inventions; assert they possess immense wealth and spend it extravagantly; or believe they belong to a prestigious lineage or noble heritage.

SYM_NEW_PSY_FLIGHT_OF_THOUGHT.name: Thought Racing
SYM_NEW_PSY_FLIGHT_OF_THOUGHT.description: Thought racing is a type of manic thought disorder characterized by accelerated associative processes. The individual experiences a torrent of ideas and concepts that come in rapid succession.

SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPERBULIA.name: Hyperbulia
SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPERBULIA.description: Hyperbulia refers to an abnormal increase in volitional activity, characterized by excessive confidence and persistent behavior.

SYM_NEW_PSY_TRANSFERENCE_OF_ATTENTION.name: Attention Shifting
SYM_NEW_PSY_TRANSFERENCE_OF_ATTENTION.description: Attention shifting primarily refers to the increased excitability and decreased stability of passive attention. Patients exhibit a heightened responsiveness to external stimuli, causing their focus to shift frequently and easily from one object or task to another.

SYM_NEW_PSY_TEILIGHT_STATE.name: Teilight State
SYM_NEW_PSY_TEILIGHT_STATE.description: Teilight State refers to a condition characterized by a narrowing of consciousness with a concomitant decrease in the clarity of awareness. Within this restricted field of consciousness, patients can perceive external stimuli and respond normally. However, they may have difficulty recognizing or understanding things beyond this limited range.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DISRIENTATION.name: Disorientation
SYM_NEW_PSY_DISRIENTATION.description: Orientation disorder is characterized by a loss or error in the ability to accurately recognize and understand one's environment and personal status.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DELIRIUM.name: Delirium
SYM_NEW_PSY_DELIRIUM.description: Delirium, also known as acute brain syndrome or acute organic syndrome, is characterized by a fundamental disturbance in consciousness and overall mental activity. It manifests with a decreased clarity of consciousness and is accompanied by various cognitive impairments.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DEPRESSIVE_SYNDROME.name: Depressive Syndrome
SYM_NEW_PSY_DEPRESSIVE_SYNDROME.description: Patients with depressive syndrome experience a persistent state of low mood, characterized by a significant decrease in interest and energy, and a notable lack of pleasure.

SYM_NEW_PSY_ANXIETY_TENSION_SYNDROME.name: Anxiety Syndrome
SYM_NEW_PSY_ANXIETY_TENSION_SYNDROME.description: Anxiety syndrome, also known as generalized anxiety disorder (GAD), is characterized by a predominant cluster of anxiety symptoms.

SYM_NEW_PSY_INSOMNIA.name: Athens Insomnia Scale
SYM_NEW_PSY_INSOMNIA.description: The patient had clinically significant insomnia as measured by the Athens Insomnia Scale.。

SYM_NEW_PSY_IMPAIRED_COGNITIVE_FUNCTION.name: Cognitive Impairment
SYM_NEW_PSY_IMPAIRED_COGNITIVE_FUNCTION.description: Cognitive impairment refers to a decline in one or more cognitive domains, such as memory, attention, executive function, or problem-solving skills.

SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPONESIA.name: Hyponesia
SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPONESIA.description: Hyponesia refers to a generalized decline in the functioning of all basic memory processes, characterized by a reduction in recent memory. In more severe cases, remote memory is also affected.

SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPERMNESIA.name: Hypermnesia
SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPERMNESIA.description: Hypermnesia refers to an enhanced ability to recall past events and experiences that would typically have been forgotten under normal circumstances.

SYM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOGENIC_AMNESIA.name: Psychogenic Amnesia
SYM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOGENIC_AMNESIA.description: Psychogenic amnesia refers to the inability to recall specific periods or events from the past, typically related to particularly distressing or traumatic experiences. This condition may last for days, months, or even longer.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DIFFICULTY_FALLING_ASLEEP.name: Difficulty Falling Asleep
SYM_NEW_PSY_DIFFICULTY_FALLING_ASLEEP.description: Difficulty Falling Asleep refers to the difficulty or prolonged effort required to initiate sleep. Individuals experiencing this symptom may struggle to fall asleep despite feeling tired or having an adequate sleep opportunity.

SYM_NEW_PSY_NIGHTMARE.name: Nightmare
SYM_NEW_PSY_NIGHTMARE.description: Nightmare is a common parasomnia characterized by the repeated awakening from sleep, accompanied by agitation and distress. Upon awakening, individuals can quickly become alert and often recall vivid and highly realistic dreams, typically involving imminent physical danger to the individual.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SLEEP_MAINTENANCE_DISORDER.name: Sleep Maintenance Disorder
SYM_NEW_PSY_SLEEP_MAINTENANCE_DISORDER.description: Patients have difficulty maintaining sleep and often have difficulty falling asleep again after awakening in the middle of the night.

SYM_NEW_PSY_EARLY_AWAKENING.name: Early Awakening
SYM_NEW_PSY_EARLY_AWAKENING.description: Early Awakening refers to the condition where a person wakes up earlier than desired or expected, often significantly earlier than their intended wake-up time, and is unable to return to sleep.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DAYTIME_SLEEPINESS.name: Daytime Sleepiness
SYM_NEW_PSY_DAYTIME_SLEEPINESS.description: Daytime Sleepiness refers to a condition where patients struggle to maintain alertness and wakefulness during the daytime, despite needing to stay awake and attentive.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DEPRESSION.name: Depression
SYM_NEW_PSY_DEPRESSION.description: Depression refers to a sustained state of emotional distress characterized by feelings of sadness, melancholy, and dejection over a period of time. It is associated with an enhancement of negative emotions, in contrast to elevated mood.

SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPOBULIA.name: Hypobulia
SYM_NEW_PSY_HYPOBULIA.description: Hypobulia, also known as reduced volition, refers to a significant reduction in volitional activity, where the patient exhibits a pathological lack of initiative and ambition. They demonstrate diminished resolve and strength to overcome difficulties, showing a pervasive disinterest in surrounding activities and a lack of motivation.

SYM_NEW_PSY_INHIBITION_OF_THOUGHT.name: Psychomotor Retardation
SYM_NEW_PSY_INHIBITION_OF_THOUGHT.description: Psychomotor retardation refers to a significant slowing of associative thinking, a decrease in the quantity of thoughts, and difficulties in generating associations. Patients often perceive their thinking as dull, with slow reactions, difficulties in problem-solving, and impaired memory. In conversation, they may exhibit reduced speech, slow verbal response, a low tone of voice, and simple expressions.

SYM_NEW_PSY_BLUNTING_OF_ATTENTION.name: Attention Blunting
SYM_NEW_PSY_BLUNTING_OF_ATTENTION.description: Attention blunting refers to the slow concentration and difficulty in shifting attention.

SYM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOGENIC_STUPOR.name: Psychogenic Stupor
SYM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOGENIC_STUPOR.description: Psychogenic stupor is a condition characterized by a marked suppression of behavior and speech following acute, intense psychological trauma.

SYM_NEW_PSY_DEPRESSIVE_STUPOR.name: Depressive Stupor
SYM_NEW_PSY_DEPRESSIVE_STUPOR.description: Depressive stupor is one of the most severe manifestations of a depressive episode. Along with profound mood depression, patients exhibit a marked reduction in speech and behavior, sometimes to the point of having no spontaneous actions or speech.

SYM_NEW_PSY_SUICIDE.name: Suicide
SYM_NEW_PSY_SUICIDE.description: Suicide refers to the intentional and premeditated act of self-harm or self-destruction, often driven by severe feelings of hopelessness, hallucinations, delusions, or other pathological psychological conditions.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_HEART.name: Healthy Heart
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_HEART.description: The patient has a healthy heart shape and function.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_LUNGS.name: Healthy Lungs
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_LUNGS.description: The patient has a pair of healthy lungs.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_GASTROINTESTINAL_TRACT.name: Healthy Gastrointestinal Tract
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_GASTROINTESTINAL_TRACT.description: The patient has a healthy GI tract.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_KIDNEYS.name: Healthy Renal System
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_KIDNEYS.description: The patient has a healthy renal system.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_NERVES.name: Healthy Nerves
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_NERVES.description: The patient has healthy nerves with healthy sensations.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_PANCREAS.name: Healthy Pancreas
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_PANCREAS.description: The patient has a healthy pancreas with healthy insulin levels.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_REFLEX.name: Healthy Reflex
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_REFLEX.description: The patient has a healthy reflex when the doctor performs a physical exam.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_HEART_RHYTHM.name: Normal Heart Rhythm
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_HEART_RHYTHM.description: The patient has a normal heart rhythm when the patient's heart is attached to a ECG or being heard by using a stethescope.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BREATHING.name: Normal Breathing Patterns
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BREATHING.description: The patient has a normal breathing pattern.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BLOOD_PRESSURE.name: Normal Blood Pressure
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BLOOD_PRESSURE.description: The patient has a normal blood pressure which is between 90/60mmHg and 120/80mmHg.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_NEUROLOGICAL_FUNCTION.name: Healthy Neurological Function
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_NEUROLOGICAL_FUNCTION.description: The patient has a healthy neurological function.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_EYESIGHT.name: Normal Eyesight
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_EYESIGHT.description: The patient has a normal eyesight.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BLOOD_CELL_COUNT.name: Healthy Blood Cell Count
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BLOOD_CELL_COUNT.description: The patient has a healthy blood cell count.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BLOOD_CONTENT.name: Healthy Amount of Proteins and Glucose
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BLOOD_CONTENT.description: The patient has a healthy amount of Proteins and Glucose in their blood.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_VITAMIN_LEVELS.name: Healthy Vitamin Levels
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_VITAMIN_LEVELS.description: The patient has a healthy amount of vitamins in his/her blood.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_SMELL.name: Healthy Smell and Taste
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_SMELL.description: The patient has a healthy sense of smell and taste.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_STOOLS.name: Healthy Stools
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_STOOLS.description: The patient has healthy stool shape and colour.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_URINE.name: Healthy Urine
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_URINE.description: The patient has healthy urine colour and urine content.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_LIFESTYLE.name: Healthy Lifestyle
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_LIFESTYLE.description: The patient is exercising and having a balanced schedule.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_DIET.name: Healthy Diet
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_DIET.description: The patient is having healthy and balanced meals.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_WEIGHT_AND_HEIGHT.name: Normal Body Mass Index
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_WEIGHT_AND_HEIGHT.description: The patient has a healthy Body Mass Index. Body Mass Index (BMI) is a person’s weight in kilograms divided by the square of height in meters. A high BMI can be an indicator of high body fatness. BMI can be used to screen for weight categories that may lead to health problems but it is not diagnostic of the body fatness or health of an individual.

SYM_JM_ASTHMA.name: Asthma
SYM_JM_ASTHMA.description: The patient has a history or currently has Asthma and they require a inhaler.

SYM_JM_DIABETES.name: Diabetes
SYM_JM_DIABETES.description: The patient has a history of Diabetes or currently has Diabetes and they require insulin injections and diet changes.

SYM_JM_DRUG_ABUSE.name: Drug Abuse
SYM_JM_DRUG_ABUSE.description: The patient has a history of drug abuse or currently has a drug addiction and they require counseling and lifestyle changes.

SYM_JM_GERD.name: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease
SYM_JM_GERD.description: The patient has a history or currently has Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease and they require Antacids and Diet Changes.

SYM_JM_OVERWEIGHT.name: Overweight/Obese
SYM_JM_OVERWEIGHT.description: The patient has a history or currently is overweight/has obesity and they require diet and lifetyle changes.

SYM_JM_SMOKING.name: Smoking
SYM_JM_SMOKING.description: The patient has a history or currently has a smoking addiction and they require counseling and lifestyle changes.

SYM_JM_MALNUTRITION.name: Malnutrition
SYM_JM_MALNUTRITION.description: The patient is currently malnourished and requires immediate care with a food pump.

SYM_JM_MENTAL_HEALTH_ISSUES.name: Mental Health Issues
SYM_JM_MENTAL_HEALTH_ISSUES.description: The patient has a history or currently is suffering from Depression or Anxiety and required counseling.

SYM_JM_SEDITARY_LIFESTYLE.name: Sedentary Lifestyle
SYM_JM_SEDITARY_LIFESTYLE.description: The patient has a sedentary lifestyle which can cause health problems and they need lifestyle changes.

SYM_JM_HIGH_CHOLESTEROL.name: High Cholesterol
SYM_JM_HIGH_CHOLESTEROL.description: The patient has high cholesterol which increases the patients risk of stroke and heart attack.

SYM_JM_HYPERTENSION.name: Hypertension
SYM_JM_HYPERTENSION.description: The patient has a blood pressure higher than 120/80mmHg, which increases the risk of stroke and hert attack and they require hypertensive medications.

SYM_JM_ARRHYTHMIA.name: Arrhythmia
SYM_JM_ARRHYTHMIA.description: An arrhythmia is a problem with the rate or rhythm of the heartbeat. During an arrhythmia, the heart can beat too fast, too slowly, or with an irregular rhythm. When a heart beats too fast, the condition is called tachycardia. When a heart beats too slowly, the condition is called bradycardia. The patient requires antiarrhythmics.

SYM_JM_CARDITIS.name: Myocarditis
SYM_JM_CARDITIS.description: Myocarditis, also known as inflammatory cardiomyopathy, is inflammation of the heart muscle. Symptoms can include shortness of breath, chest pain, decreased ability to exercise, and an irregular heartbeat. The duration of problems can vary from hours to months. Complications may include heart failure due to dilated cardiomyopathy or cardiac arrest.

SYM_JM_VALVE_DISEASE.name: Valvular Heart Disease
SYM_JM_VALVE_DISEASE.description: Valvular heart disease is when any valve in the heart has damage or is diseased. There are several causes of valve disease. The normal heart has four chambers (right and left atria, and right and left ventricles) and four valves (Figure 1). Diseases include Stenosis and Regurgitation.

SYM_JM_SEVERE_HEART_DISEASE.name: Severe Heart Disease
SYM_JM_SEVERE_HEART_DISEASE.description: The patient has a very serious case of heart disease and you must treat it soon. The patient might be currently suffering from a heart attack, heart failure, severe coronary heart disease, severe angina and etc.

SYM_JM_CARDIAC_INFECTION.name: Endocarditis
SYM_JM_CARDIAC_INFECTION.description: Endocarditis is an infection that affects the inner membrane that separates the chambers and valves of the heart (endocardium). Heart infection symptoms can include: Fever, Shortness of breath, Weakness or fatigue, Swelling in your legs or abdomen, Changes in your heart rhythm, Dry or persistent cough, Skin rashes or unusual spots.

SYM_JM_SEVERE_LUNG_DISEASE.name: Severe Lung Disease
SYM_JM_SEVERE_LUNG_DISEASE.description: The patient has a very serious case of lung disease and you must treat it soon. The patient might be currently suffering from ARDS, Pulmonary Embolism, End-stage Lung Cancer, Severe Pneumonia and etc.

SYM_JM_PNEUMONITIS.name: Pneumonitis
SYM_JM_PNEUMONITIS.description: Pneumonitis (noo-moe-NIE-tis) is a general term that refers to inflammation of lung tissue. Technically, pneumonia is a type of pneumonitis because the infection causes inflammation. Pneumonitis, however, is usually used by doctors to refer to noninfectious causes of lung inflammation.

SYM_JM_LUNG_INFECTION.name: Pneumonia
SYM_JM_LUNG_INFECTION.description: Pneumonia is an infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs. The air sacs may fill with fluid or pus (purulent material), causing cough with phlegm or pus, fever, chills, and difficulty breathing. A variety of organisms, including bacteria, viruses and fungi, can cause pneumonia.

SYM_JM_DIVERTICULITIS.name: Diverticulitis
SYM_JM_DIVERTICULITIS.description: Diverticula are small, bulging pouches that can form in the lining of your digestive system. They are found most often in the lower part of the large intestine (colon). Diverticula are common, especially after age 40, and seldom cause problems. The presence of diverticula is known as diverticulosis (die-vur-tik-yoo-LOE-sis). When one or more of the pouches become inflamed, and in some cases infected, that condition is known as diverticulitis (die-vur-tik-yoo-LIE-tis). Diverticulitis can cause severe abdominal pain, fever, nausea and a marked change in your bowel habits.

SYM_JM_CROHNS.name: Crohn's Disease
SYM_JM_CROHNS.description: Crohn's disease is a type of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) that may affect any segment of the gastrointestinal tract from the mouth to the anus. Symptoms often include abdominal pain, diarrhea (which may be bloody if inflammation is severe), fever, abdominal distension, and weight loss.

SYM_JM_ULC.name: Ulcerative Colitis
SYM_JM_ULC.description: Ulcerative colitis (UC) is an inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). It causes irritation, inflammation, and ulcers in the lining of your large intestine (also called your colon). There's no cure, and people usually have symptoms off and on for life.

SYM_JM_HEMORRHOIDS.name: Small Hemorrhoids
SYM_JM_HEMORRHOIDS.description: Hemorrhoids (HEM-uh-roids), also called piles, are swollen veins in your anus and lower rectum, similar to varicose veins. Hemorrhoids can develop inside the rectum (internal hemorrhoids) or under the skin around the anus (external hemorrhoids). Nearly three out of four adults will have hemorrhoids from time to time. Hemorrhoids have a number of causes, but often the cause is unknown. Fortunately, effective options are available to treat hemorrhoids. Many people get relief with home treatments and lifestyle changes.

SYM_JM_HEMORRHOIDS1.name: Small Hemorrhoids
SYM_JM_HEMORRHOIDS1.description: Hemorrhoids (HEM-uh-roids), also called piles, are swollen veins in your anus and lower rectum, similar to varicose veins. Hemorrhoids can develop inside the rectum (internal hemorrhoids) or under the skin around the anus (external hemorrhoids). Nearly three out of four adults will have hemorrhoids from time to time. Hemorrhoids have a number of causes, but often the cause is unknown. Fortunately, effective options are available to treat hemorrhoids. Many people get relief with home treatments and lifestyle changes.

SYM_JM_CELIAC_DISEASE.name: Coeliac Disease
SYM_JM_CELIAC_DISEASE.description: Celiac disease, sometimes called celiac sprue or gluten-sensitive enteropathy, is an immune reaction to eating gluten, a protein found in wheat, barley and rye. If you have celiac disease, eating gluten triggers an immune response in your small intestine. Over time, this reaction damages your small intestine's lining and prevents it from absorbing some nutrients (malabsorption). The intestinal damage often causes diarrhea, fatigue, weight loss, bloating and anemia, and can lead to serious complications. In children, malabsorption can affect growth and development, besides causing the symptoms seen in adults.

SYM_JM_CKD.name: Chronic Kidney Disease
SYM_JM_CKD.description: Chronic kidney disease, also called chronic kidney failure, describes the gradual loss of kidney function. Your kidneys filter wastes and excess fluids from your blood, which are then excreted in your urine. When chronic kidney disease reaches an advanced stage, dangerous levels of fluid, electrolytes and wastes can build up in your body. In the early stages of chronic kidney disease, you may have few signs or symptoms. Chronic kidney disease may not become apparent until your kidney function is significantly impaired.

SYM_JM_END_CKD.name: Chronic Kidney Disease
SYM_JM_END_CKD.description: Chronic kidney disease, also called chronic kidney failure, describes the gradual loss of kidney function. Your kidneys filter wastes and excess fluids from your blood, which are then excreted in your urine. When chronic kidney disease reaches an advanced stage, dangerous levels of fluid, electrolytes and wastes can build up in your body. In the early stages of chronic kidney disease, you may have few signs or symptoms. Chronic kidney disease may not become apparent until your kidney function is significantly impaired.

SYM_JM_KIDNEY_STONES.name: Kidney Stones
SYM_JM_KIDNEY_STONES.description: Kidney stones (also called renal calculi, nephrolithiasis or urolithiasis) are hard deposits made of minerals and salts that form inside your kidneys. Diet, excess body weight, some medical conditions, and certain supplements and medications are among the many causes of kidney stones. Kidney stones can affect any part of your urinary tract — from your kidneys to your bladder. Often, stones form when the urine becomes concentrated, allowing minerals to crystallize and stick together.

SYM_JM_GLUMEROLUNEPHRITIS.name: Glumerolunephritis
SYM_JM_GLUMEROLUNEPHRITIS.description: Glomerulonephritis (gloe-mer-u-low-nuh-FRY-tis) is inflammation of the tiny filters in your kidneys (glomeruli). Glomeruli remove excess fluid, electrolytes and waste from your bloodstream and pass them into your urine. Glomerulonephritis can come on suddenly (acute) or gradually (chronic).

SYM_JM_POLYCYSTIC_KD.name: Polycystic Kidney Disease
SYM_JM_POLYCYSTIC_KD.description: Polycystic kidney disease (PKD) is an inherited disorder in which clusters of cysts develop primarily within your kidneys, causing your kidneys to enlarge and lose function over time. Cysts are noncancerous round sacs containing fluid. The cysts vary in size, and they can grow very large. Having many cysts or large cysts can damage your kidneys. Polycystic kidney disease can also cause cysts to develop in your liver and elsewhere in your body. The disease can cause serious complications, including high blood pressure and kidney failure.

SYM_JM_UTI.name: Urinary Tract Infection
SYM_JM_UTI.description: A urinary tract infection (UTI) is an infection in any part of your urinary system — your kidneys, ureters, bladder and urethra. Most infections involve the lower urinary tract — the bladder and the urethra. Women are at greater risk of developing a UTI than are men. Infection limited to your bladder can be painful and annoying. However, serious consequences can occur if a UTI spreads to your kidneys. Doctors typically treat urinary tract infections with antibiotics. But you can take steps to reduce your chances of getting a UTI in the first place.

SYM_JM_SEVERE_UT_DISEASE.name: Severe Renal Disease
SYM_JM_SEVERE_UT_DISEASE.description: The patient has a very serious case of renal disease and you must treat it soon. The patient might be currently suffering from Renal Failure, Chronic Kidney Disease - End Stage and etc.

SYM_JM_PARKINSONS.name: Parkinson's Disease
SYM_JM_PARKINSONS.description: Parkinson's disease is a progressive nervous system disorder that affects movement. Symptoms start gradually, sometimes starting with a barely noticeable tremor in just one hand. Tremors are common, but the disorder also commonly causes stiffness or slowing of movement. In the early stages of Parkinson's disease, your face may show little or no expression. Your arms may not swing when you walk. Your speech may become soft or slurred. Parkinson's disease symptoms worsen as your condition progresses over time.

SYM_JM_ALZHEIMERS.name: Alzheimer's Disease
SYM_JM_ALZHEIMERS.description: Alzheimer's disease is a progressive disorder that causes brain cells to waste away (degenerate) and die. Alzheimer's disease is the most common cause of dementia — a continuous decline in thinking, behavioral and social skills that disrupts a person's ability to function independently. The early signs of the disease may be forgetting recent events or conversations. As the disease progresses, a person with Alzheimer's disease will develop severe memory impairment and lose the ability to carry out everyday tasks. Current Alzheimer's disease medications may temporarily improve symptoms or slow the rate of decline. These treatments can sometimes help people with Alzheimer's disease maximize function and maintain independence for a time.

SYM_JM_DEMENTIA.name: Dementia
SYM_JM_DEMENTIA.description: Dementia describes a group of symptoms affecting memory, thinking and social abilities severely enough to interfere with your daily life. It isn't a specific disease, but several different diseases may cause dementia. Though dementia generally involves memory loss, memory loss has different causes. Having memory loss alone doesn't mean you have dementia. Alzheimer's disease is the most common cause of a progressive dementia in older adults, but there are a number of causes of dementia. Depending on the cause, some dementia symptoms may be reversible.

SYM_JM_SEVERE_NEURO_DISEASE.name: Severe Neurological Disease
SYM_JM_SEVERE_NEURO_DISEASE.description: The patient has a very serious case of neurological disease and you must treat it soon. The patient might be currently suffering from Encephalitis, Meningitis, Paralysis, Intercranial Pressure and etc.

SYM_JM_LACTOSE_INTOLERANCE.name: Lactose Intolerance
SYM_JM_LACTOSE_INTOLERANCE.description: The patient has lactose intolerance which causes bloating, diarrhea and flatulance, the patient requires diet changes.

SYM_JM_TAPEWORM_INFECTION.name: Tapeworm Infections
SYM_JM_TAPEWORM_INFECTION.description: The patient has a tapeworm in their intestines and requires anti-parasitics.

SYM_JM_B12_DEFICIENCY.name: Vitamin B12 Deficiency
SYM_JM_B12_DEFICIENCY.description: The patient has a Vitamin B12 Deficiency which can cause neuro degeneration and cognitive decline if untreated.

SYM_JM_MAGNESIUM_DEFICIENCY.name: Magnesium Deficiency
SYM_JM_MAGNESIUM_DEFICIENCY.description: The patient has a magnesium deficiency and they require magnesium supplements.

SYM_JM_IRON_DEFICIENCY.name: Iron Deficiency
SYM_JM_IRON_DEFICIENCY.description: The patient has a iron deficiency, which can cause anemia and they require iron supplements.

SYM_JM_IODINE_DEFICIENCY.name: Iodine Deficiency
SYM_JM_IODINE_DEFICIENCY.description: The patient has a iodine deficiency which can cause cognitive decline and they require supplements of iodine.

SYM_JM_SEIZURES.name: Post-Surgical Seizures
SYM_JM_SEIZURES.description: Short episodes of seizures caused by an abnormal neuronal activity after surgery.

SYM_JM_ANNUAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT2.name: Annual Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_ANNUAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT2.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her General Practitioner to get an annual checkup.

SYM_JM_CARDIOVASCULAR_CHECKUP_APPOINTMEN.name: Cardiovascular Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_CARDIOVASCULAR_CHECKUP_APPOINTMEN.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her Cardiologist to get an cardiovascular checkup.

SYM_JM_NEUROLOGICAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT2.name: Neurological Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_NEUROLOGICAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT2.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her Neurologist to get an neurological checkup.

SYM_JM_GASTROINTESTINAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTM.name: Gastrointestinal Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_GASTROINTESTINAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTM.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her Gastroenterologist to get an gastroesophageal checkup.

SYM_JM_KIDNEY_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT2.name: Renal Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_KIDNEY_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT2.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her Nephrologist to get an renal checkup.

SYM_JM_NUTRI_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT.name: Dietary Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_NUTRI_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her Nutritionist to get an dietary checkup.

SYM_JM_MMR_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: MMR Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_MMR_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Mumps, Measles and Rubella Vaccination.

SYM_JM_INFLU_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Influenza Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_INFLU_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Influenza Vaccination which protects the patient from H1N1, H3N1 and 2 different types of Influenza B infections.

SYM_JM_VARICL_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Varicella Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_VARICL_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Varicella Vaccination which protects the patient from Shingles and Chickenpox.

SYM_JM_DTAP_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: DTaP Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_DTAP_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Diptheria, Tetanus and Pertusis (Whooping Cough) Vaccination.

SYM_JM_ANTHRAX_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Anthrax Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_ANTHRAX_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Anthrax Vaccination.

SYM_JM_EBOLA_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Ebola Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_EBOLA_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Ebola Vaccination.

SYM_JM_DENGUE_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Dengue Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_DENGUE_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Dengue Vaccination.

SYM_JM_MALARIA_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Malaria Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_MALARIA_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Malaria Vaccination.

SYM_JM_TB_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Tuberculosis Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_TB_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Tuberculosis Vaccination.

SYM_MOREDG_HYPERCALCEMIA.name: Hypercalcemia
SYM_MOREDG_HYPERCALCEMIA.description: Hypercalcemia is a condition in which the calcium level in your blood is above normal. Hypercalcaemia is most often caused by overactivity in the four tiny glands in the neck (parathyroid glands) or from cancer. Extra calcium in the blood affects many bodily systems.

SYM_MOREDG_SEVERE_HYPERCALCEMIA.name: Severe Hypercalcemia
SYM_MOREDG_SEVERE_HYPERCALCEMIA.description: Severe hypercalcemia is a medical emergency requiring urgent treatment. Hypercalcemia is a condition in which the calcium level in your blood is above normal. Rarely, severe hypercalcemia can interfere with your heart function, causing palpitations and fainting, indications of cardiac arrhythmia, and other heart problems.

SYM_MOREDG_SPLENOMEGALY.name: Splenomegaly
SYM_MOREDG_SPLENOMEGALY.description: Splenomegaly is a condition that occurs when your spleen becomes enlarged. It’s also commonly referred to as enlarged spleen or spleen enlargement.

SYM_MOREDG_ABCES_CEREBRAL.name: Brain Abscess
SYM_MOREDG_ABCES_CEREBRAL.description: Brain abscess (or cerebral abscess) is an abscess caused by inflammation and collection of infected material, coming from local (ear infection, dental abscess, infection of paranasal sinuses, infection of the mastoid air cells of the temporal bone, epidural abscess) or remote (lung, heart, kidney etc.) infectious sources, within the brain tissue.

SYM_MOREDG_SOUFFLE_SYSTOLIQUE.name: Systolic Heart Murmurs
SYM_MOREDG_SOUFFLE_SYSTOLIQUE.description: Systolic heart murmurs are heart murmurs heard during systole. The systole is the part of the cardiac cycle during which some chambers of the heart muscle contract after refilling with blood.

SYM_MOREDG_HVG.name: LVH
SYM_MOREDG_HVG.description: Left ventricular hypertrophy (LVH) is thickening of the heart muscle of the left ventricle of the heart, that is, left-sided ventricular hypertrophy.

SYM_MOREDG_SOUFFLE_DIASTOLIQUE.name: Diastolic Heart Murmurs
SYM_MOREDG_SOUFFLE_DIASTOLIQUE.description: Diastolic heart murmurs are heart murmurs heard during diastole. Diastole is the part of the cardiac cycle during which the heart refills with blood after the emptying done during systole (contraction).

SYM_MOREDG_BNP.name: High BNP
SYM_MOREDG_BNP.description: BNP (Brain natriuretic peptide), is a neuro-hormone synthesized by heart cells called cardiomyocytes in response to an increase in blood pressure, especially during the development of heart failure.

SYM_MOREDG_MASSE_HYPERBATTANTE.name: Para-Umbilical Mass
SYM_MOREDG_MASSE_HYPERBATTANTE.description: Perception of a swinging and expansive para-umbilical mass.

SYM_MOREDG_SYNDROME_LOGE.name: Compartment Syndrome
SYM_MOREDG_SYNDROME_LOGE.description: Compartment syndrome is a condition in which increased pressure within one of the body's anatomical compartments results in insufficient blood supply to tissue within that space. There are two main types: acute and chronic. Compartments of the leg or arm are most commonly involved.

SYM_MOREDG_INFARCTUS_MESENTERIQUE.name: Mesenteric Ischemia
SYM_MOREDG_INFARCTUS_MESENTERIQUE.description: Mesenteric ischemia is a medical condition in which injury to the small intestine occurs due to not enough blood supply. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at General Surgery dpt + ICU

SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_HYPOPHYSE.name: Pituitary Adenoma
SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_HYPOPHYSE.description: Discovery of a pituitary adenoma on MRI. This doesn't provide information on the secreting nature or not of it.

SYM_MOREDG_RETRECISSEMENT_AORTIQUE.name: Aortic Stenosis
SYM_MOREDG_RETRECISSEMENT_AORTIQUE.description: Decrease in diameter of the aortic valve.

SYM_MOREDG_SYNCOPE_REFLEXE.name: Reflex Syncope
SYM_MOREDG_SYNCOPE_REFLEXE.description: It is a neurovegetative reflex of arterial hypotension with more or less marked bradycardia following a stimulus.

SYM_MOREDG_SYNCOPE_SA.name: Conduction Disorder
SYM_MOREDG_SYNCOPE_SA.description: Complete paroxysmal blockage of the intracardiac conduction.

SYM_MOREDG_LIPOTHIMIE.name: Prodromes
SYM_MOREDG_LIPOTHIMIE.description: The signs and symptoms that precede syncope are called prodromes. Lipothymia can be summed up as an imminent feeling of fainting.

SYM_MOREDG_FA_ECG.name: AF
SYM_MOREDG_FA_ECG.description: The diagnosis of AF can only be confirmed on an ECG trace. This shows an absence of visible P wave and the most often irregular QRS.

SYM_MOREDG_BSA_ECG.name: SAB
SYM_MOREDG_BSA_ECG.description: Sino-Atrial Block (SAB) is a complete interruption of intra-atrial conduction after certain sinus impulses.

SYM_MOREDG_BAV3_ECG.name: AVB3
SYM_MOREDG_BAV3_ECG.description: The 3rd degree Atrioventricular Block is a complete and permanent interruption of the transmission of atrial impulses to the ventricles..

SYM_MOREDG_BBG_ECG.name: LBBB
SYM_MOREDG_BBG_ECG.description: Typical appearance of LBBB at ECG.

SYM_MOREDG_TV_ECG.name: VT
SYM_MOREDG_TV_ECG.description: Typical appearance of VT at ECG.

SYM_MOREDG_QT_LONG.name: QT Extension
SYM_MOREDG_QT_LONG.description: Extension of the QT space at the ECG.

SYM_MOREDG_TCJ_ECG.name: JT
SYM_MOREDG_TCJ_ECG.description: Junctional Tachycardia (JT) is a heterogeneous group of tachycardias whose circuit begins or necessarily passes through the atrioventricular junction.

SYM_MOREDG_ONDE_DELTA.name: Delta Wave
SYM_MOREDG_ONDE_DELTA.description: Impasto and slowing of the initial part of the QRS complex in connection with pre-excitation of the ventricle by an accessory bundle.

SYM_MOREDG_INSUFFISANCE_CARDIAQUE_G.name: Altered LVEF
SYM_MOREDG_INSUFFISANCE_CARDIAQUE_G.description: The Left Ventricle Ejection Fraction (LVEF) is the percentage of blood ejected from the left ventricle.

SYM_MOREDG_INSUFFISANCE_CARDIAQUE_D.name: Altered RVEF
SYM_MOREDG_INSUFFISANCE_CARDIAQUE_D.description: The Right Ventricle Ejection Fraction (RVEF) is the percentage of blood ejected from the right ventricle.

SYM_MOREDG_IPS_BAS.name: Low SPI
SYM_MOREDG_IPS_BAS.description: Systolic Pressure Index (SPI) is less than 0.9. IPS is based on the collection of systolic pressures from the lower and humeral limbs and the establishment of an ankle pressure / humeral pressure ratio.

SYM_MOREDG_OCCLUSION_A_COELIAQUE.name: Celiac Occlusion
SYM_MOREDG_OCCLUSION_A_COELIAQUE.description: Occlusion of the celiac artery and mesenteric arteries.

SYM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MS.name: Upper Limb Tissue Ischemia
SYM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MS.description: Ischemia is the cessation or insufficiency of blood circulation in a part of the body or an organ, which deprives the cells of oxygen supply and causes their necrosis. Ischemia can be due to blockage of a vessel (thrombosis) or compression of an artery (stenosis).

SYM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MI.name: Lower Limb Tissue Ischemia
SYM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MI.description: Ischemia is the cessation or insufficiency of blood circulation in a part of the body or an organ, which deprives the cells of oxygen supply and causes their necrosis. Ischemia can be due to blockage of a vessel (thrombosis) or compression of an artery (stenosis).

SYM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MESENTERIQUE.name: Occlusion of Mesenteric Arteries
SYM_MOREDG_ISCHEMIE_MESENTERIQUE.description: It is an acute arterial occlusion or low flow.

SYM_MOREDG_RUPTURE_ANEVRISME_AIC.name: IAA Ruptured
SYM_MOREDG_RUPTURE_ANEVRISME_AIC.description: Rupture of a common iliac artery aneurysm.

SYM_MOREDG_TRANSPLANTATION_COEUR.name: Transplant
SYM_MOREDG_TRANSPLANTATION_COEUR.description: This patient urgently needs a heart transplant!

SYM_MOREDG_PROLACTINOME_F.name: Prolactinome
SYM_MOREDG_PROLACTINOME_F.description: Discovery of a prolactinoma on MRI.

SYM_MOREDG_PROLACTINOME_H.name: Prolactinome
SYM_MOREDG_PROLACTINOME_H.description: Discovery of a prolactinoma on MRI.

SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_SOMATOTROPE.name: Somatotropic Adenoma
SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_SOMATOTROPE.description: No braking during the "minute" brake test.

SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_CORTICOTROPE_F.name: Adenome Corticotrope
SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_CORTICOTROPE_F.description: The test is in favor of the diagnosis of non-curable hypercortisolism if the cortisolemia does not drop below the threshold of 18 ng / ml [50 nmol / l]

SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_CORTICOTROPE_H.name: Adenome Corticotrope
SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_CORTICOTROPE_H.description: The test is in favor of the diagnosis of non-curable hypercortisolism if the cortisolemia does not drop below the threshold of 18 ng / ml [50 nmol / l]

SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_THYREOTROPE.name: Thyrotropic Adenoma
SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_THYREOTROPE.description: Discovery of central hyperthyroidism at the blood test. This is characterized by a high concentration of thyroid hormones associated with a normal high or abnormal concentration of TSH.

SYM_MOREDG_PAN_HYPOPITUITARISME_F.name: Pan Hypopituitarisme
SYM_MOREDG_PAN_HYPOPITUITARISME_F.description: Discovery of a pituitary adenoma that prevents the proper functioning of the pineal gland on MRI.

SYM_MOREDG_PAN_HYPOPITUITARISME_H.name: Pan Hypopituitarisme
SYM_MOREDG_PAN_HYPOPITUITARISME_H.description: Discovery of a pituitary adenoma that prevents the proper functioning of the pineal gland on MRI.

SYM_MOREDG_ISRNP_F.name: Isrnp
SYM_MOREDG_ISRNP_F.description: Discovery of peripheral adrenal insufficiency during the Synacthene test. This is characterized by a low cortisol level associated with a high concentration of ACTH.

SYM_MOREDG_ISRNP_H.name: Isrnp
SYM_MOREDG_ISRNP_H.description: Discovery of peripheral adrenal insufficiency during the Synacthene test. This is characterized by a low cortisol level associated with a high concentration of ACTH.

SYM_MOREDG_21OH_F.name: 21oh
SYM_MOREDG_21OH_F.description: It is accepted by the entire endocrinologist community that the diagnosis of D21OH-NC is based on the dosage of 17OHP in the morning and at best in the first part of the cycle. The sample must be taken in the absence of or at a distance from treatment with glucocorticoids, since even "substitute" treatment and a fortiori pharmacological treatment introduces a risk of ignoring the diagnosis (false negative) by inhibiting the corticotropic axis. The sampling for the determination of 17OHP must also be done in the morning, because if it is done in the afternoon, the concentration of 17OHP can be found within the normal range in a patient with D21OH-NC simply because of fluctuations nycthemeral of adrenal cortical function.

SYM_MOREDG_21OH_H.name: 21oh
SYM_MOREDG_21OH_H.description: It is accepted by the entire endocrinologist community that the diagnosis of D21OH-NC is based on the dosage of 17OHP in the morning and at best in the first part of the cycle. The sample must be taken in the absence of or at a distance from treatment with glucocorticoids, since even "substitute" treatment and a fortiori pharmacological treatment introduces a risk of ignoring the diagnosis (false negative) by inhibiting the corticotropic axis. The sampling for the determination of 17OHP must also be done in the morning, because if it is done in the afternoon, the concentration of 17OHP can be found within the normal range in a patient with D21OH-NC simply because of fluctuations nycthemeral of adrenal cortical function.

SYM_MOREDG_ISF_CORTICOT.name: Central Adrenal Insufficiency
SYM_MOREDG_ISF_CORTICOT.description: Discovery of central adrenal insufficiency during the Synacthene test. This is characterized by low cortisol levels associated with a low or abnormally normal concentration of ACTH.

SYM_MOREDG_AC_ANTI_CASR.name: Anti-CASR Ab
SYM_MOREDG_AC_ANTI_CASR.description: Discovery of anti-CASR antibody during the ELISA test.

SYM_MOREDG_CONN.name: Adrenal Adenoma
SYM_MOREDG_CONN.description: Discovery of an adrenal adenoma secreting on CT.

SYM_MOREDG_HPBL_SRN.name: Adrenal Hypertrophy
SYM_MOREDG_HPBL_SRN.description: Increase in size of the adrenal gland on CT scan.

SYM_MOREDG_INSULINOME.name: Inappropriate Secretion
SYM_MOREDG_INSULINOME.description: Inappropriate (normal or high) insulin and peptide C secretion in hypoglycaemia.

SYM_MOREDG_SD_HGP.name: Pancreatic Hypoglycemia
SYM_MOREDG_SD_HGP.description: Hypoglycemia of pancreatic origin not linked to an insulin-secreting adenoma.

SYM_MOREDG_GOITRE_MULTINODULAIRE_TOXIQUE.name: "Checkerboard" Scintigraphy
SYM_MOREDG_GOITRE_MULTINODULAIRE_TOXIQUE.description: Alternating hot and cold beaches (checkerboard) with scintigraphy.

SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_TOXIQUE.name: Toxic Adenoma
SYM_MOREDG_ADENOME_TOXIQUE.description: Hyperfixation of the isotope at the level of the nodule while the rest of the parenchyma is hypofixant or cold or "extinct" (due to the decrease in TSH), on scintigraphy.

SYM_MOREDG_CORTISOL_EFF.name: Very Low Cortisol
SYM_MOREDG_CORTISOL_EFF.description: The cortisol level in the blood is very low.

SYM_MOREDG_CETONEMIE.name: Ketonemia
SYM_MOREDG_CETONEMIE.description: An excess of ketones in the blood.

SYM_MOREDG_SPHEROCYTE.name: Spherocyte
SYM_MOREDG_SPHEROCYTE.description: Discovery of spherocytes in the blood smear.

SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_GPI.name: Low GPI
SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_GPI.description: Glucose-6-phosphate isomerase (GPI), alternatively known as phosphoglucose isomerase/phosphoglucoisomerase (PGI) or phosphohexose isomerase (PHI), is an enzyme that in humans is encoded by the GPI gene on chromosome 19. In the cytoplasm, the gene product functions as a glycolytic enzyme (glucose-6-phosphate isomerase) that interconverts glucose-6-phosphate (G6P) and fructose-6-phosphate (F6P).

SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_G6PD.name: Low G6PD
SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_G6PD.description: Glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase (G6PD or G6PDH) is a cytosolic enzyme that helps protect the red blood cells against oxidative damage from compounds like hydrogen peroxide.

SYM_MOREDG_PK_BAS.name: Low PK
SYM_MOREDG_PK_BAS.description: Pyruvate kinase is the enzyme involved in the last step of glycolysis.

SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_ADAMTS13.name: Low ADAMTS13
SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_ADAMTS13.description: ADAMTS13 (a disintegrin and metalloproteinase with a thrombospondin type 1 motif, member 13)—also known as von Willebrand factor-cleaving protease (VWFCP)—is a zinc-containing metalloprotease enzyme that cleaves von Willebrand factor (vWf), a large protein involved in blood clotting.

SYM_MOREDG_DREPANOCYTE_A.name: Drepanocyte
SYM_MOREDG_DREPANOCYTE_A.description: Discovery of drepanocytes in the blood smear.

SYM_MOREDG_DREPANOCYTE_C.name: Drepanocyte
SYM_MOREDG_DREPANOCYTE_C.description: Discovery of drepanocytes in the blood smear.

SYM_MOREDG_NPC1_MUTE.name: NPC1 Muté
SYM_MOREDG_NPC1_MUTE.description: The proteins NPC1 and NPC2 seem to work at the level of the endo-lysosomal system to facilitate the intracellular transport of cholesterol and other molecules, but their precise function is not yet known.

SYM_MOREDG_PLAQUETTES_GEANTES.name: Macrothrombocytes
SYM_MOREDG_PLAQUETTES_GEANTES.description: More than 10% of the platelets studied are giant.

SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_AIIB_B3.name: Abnormal Integrin
SYM_MOREDG_DEFICIT_AIIB_B3.description: Quantitative and / or qualitative anomalies of the integrin alphaIIb beta3 are found.

SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_RP.name: RP Mutations
SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_RP.description: Ribosomal proteins (RPs) from either the small (RPS7, RPS17, RPS19, RPS24) or the large (RPL5, RPL11, RPL35a) ribosomal subunit are mutated.

SYM_MOREDG_GLUCOCEREBROSIDASE.name: Glucocerebrosidase Deficiency
SYM_MOREDG_GLUCOCEREBROSIDASE.description: Low glucocerebrosidase level leading to accumulation of glucosylceramide deposits.

SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_SH2D1A.name: SH2D1A Mutation
SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_SH2D1A.description: Mutation of the SH2D1A gene which is located on the X chromosome. This gene codes for a protein called SAP (signalling lymphocyte activation molecular [SLAM] associated protein).

SYM_MOREDG_SIDEROBLASTE.name: Sideroblast
SYM_MOREDG_SIDEROBLASTE.description: Sideroblasts have been found. These are iron-containing granulations stained with Perls's reagent, corresponding to aggregates of ribosomes and ferritin.

SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_C.name: Hemoglobin  C
SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_C.description: Discovery of hemoglobins type C with a blood smear.

SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_D.name: Hemoglobin  D
SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_D.description: Discovery of hemoglobins type D with a blood smear.

SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_E.name: Hemoglobin  E
SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_E.description: Discovery of hemoglobins type E with a blood smear.

SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_M.name: Hemoglobin  M
SYM_MOREDG_HÉMOGLOBINE_M.description: Discovery of hemoglobins type M with a blood smear.

SYM_MOREDG_TRIADE_PLUMMER_VINSON.name: Triad
SYM_MOREDG_TRIADE_PLUMMER_VINSON.description: Classical triad of dysphagia, iron-deficiency anemia and esophageal webs.

SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_ACVRL_ENG.name: Mutation
SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_ACVRL_ENG.description: A mutation in one of the two ACVRL or ENG genes involved in the TGF-beta signaling pathway has been found.

SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_HFE.name: HFE Mutation
SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_HFE.description: The HFE gene located on chromosome 6 is mutated. Two types of alterations exist: the C282Y mutation and the H63D mutation.

SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_HJV_OU_HAMP.name: Mutation
SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_HJV_OU_HAMP.description: Mutations in the gene coding for hemojuvelin (HJV) on chromosome 1 or mutations in the gene coding for hepcidin (HAMP) on chromosome 19 have been found.

SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_TFR2.name: TFR2 Mutation
SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_TFR2.description: Mutations in the transferrin receptor-2 (TFR2) gene on chromosome 7 have been found.

SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_SLC40A1.name: SLC40A1 Mutation
SYM_MOREDG_MUTATION_SLC40A1.description: Mutations in the SLC40A1 gene located on chromosome 2 which codes for ferroportin (FPN) have been found.

SYM_MOREDG_ANTICORPS_ANTI_F4P.name: Anti-PF4 Ab
SYM_MOREDG_ANTICORPS_ANTI_F4P.description: Anti-PF4 / heparin antibodies were detected by ELISA.

SYM_GALLSTONES_RCDI.name: Gallstones Discovered
SYM_GALLSTONES_RCDI.description: Abdominal ultrasound has revealed the presence of gallstones blocking the pancreatic duct.

SYM_PANCREATIC_ENZYMES_ELEVATED_RCDI.name: Elevated Pancreatic Enzymes
SYM_PANCREATIC_ENZYMES_ELEVATED_RCDI.description: The patient has elevated levels of pancreatic enzymes in the blood.

SYM_DER_NAIL_PITTING.name: Nail Pitting
SYM_DER_NAIL_PITTING.description: Nail pitting is the presence of pinpoint depressions on the nail surface, commonly seen in nail conditions associated with psoriasis or alopecia areata.

SYM_DER_PHOTOEXACERBATION.name: Photoexacerbation
SYM_DER_PHOTOEXACERBATION.description: Photoexacerbation is the worsening of skin lesions after sun exposure, suggesting a photosensitive/photoaggravated skin disease. Enhanced sun protection and avoidance of triggers help with control.

SYM_DER_SYMMETRIC_MALAR_MACULES.name: Symmetric Malar Macules
SYM_DER_SYMMETRIC_MALAR_MACULES.description: Symmetric malar macules are symmetrically distributed pigmented spots on the face, often associated with melasma; UV radiation and hormonal changes are common triggers.

SYM_DER_FOREHEAD_INVOLVEMENT.name: Forehead Involvement
SYM_DER_FOREHEAD_INVOLVEMENT.description: Forehead involvement with symmetrically distributed pigmented spots, often associated with melasma; UV radiation and hormonal changes are common triggers.

SYM_DER_UPPERLIP_PATCH.name: Upper Lip Patch
SYM_DER_UPPERLIP_PATCH.description: Pigmented patch on the upper lip, often associated with melasma; UV radiation and hormonal changes are common triggers.

SYM_DER_KOEBNER.name: Koebner Phenomenon
SYM_DER_KOEBNER.description: The Koebner phenomenon is the appearance of new skin lesions identical to the original disease at sites of trauma/scratching, seen in psoriasis, lichen planus, etc.

SYM_DER_COLLARETTE_SCALE.name: Collarette Scale
SYM_DER_COLLARETTE_SCALE.description: A collarette scale is a scale with an inwardly folded collar-like shape, commonly seen in the 'herald patch' and secondary rashes of pityriasis rosea.

SYM_DER_PIH.name: Post-Inflammatory Hyperpigmentation
SYM_DER_PIH.description: Post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation is a pigmentary change following inflammation, resulting from inflammation-mediated melanin metabolism disorder; controlling the primary disease and sun protection are key.

SYM_DER_PIHYPO.name: Post-Inflammatory Hypopigmentation
SYM_DER_PIHYPO.description: Post-inflammatory hypopigmentation is a pigmentary change following inflammation, resulting from inflammation-mediated melanin metabolism disorder; controlling the primary disease and sun protection are key.

SYM_DER_SEBUM_EXCESS.name: Increased Sebum Secretion
SYM_DER_SEBUM_EXCESS.description: Increased sebum secretion is excessive sebum production, often associated with acne, androgen levels, and genetics.

SYM_DER_SCALP_SCALE.name: Scalp Scaling
SYM_DER_SCALP_SCALE.description: Increased scaling on the scalp, seen in seborrheic dermatitis, tinea capitis, or psoriasis, requiring differentiation based on KOH test and clinical presentation.

SYM_DER_LICHENIFICATION.name: Lichenification
SYM_DER_LICHENIFICATION.description: Lichenification is a chronic change from scratching/rubbing, characterized by deepened skin lines and thickened skin, commonly seen in chronic eczema.

SYM_DER_EXCORIATIONS.name: Excoriations/scratch Marks
SYM_DER_EXCORIATIONS.description: Excoriations/scratch marks are linear crusts/erosions formed by scratching due to itching, often indicating active pruritic dermatosis.

SYM_DER_SATELLITE_PUSTULES.name: Satellite Papules/pustules
SYM_DER_SATELLITE_PUSTULES.description: Satellite papules/pustules are scattered small papules/pustules around a main lesion, commonly seen in cutaneous/mucosal candidiasis.

SYM_DER_ANNULAR_BORDER.name: Annular Raised Border
SYM_DER_ANNULAR_BORDER.description: An annular raised border is a sign of annular erythema with a raised periphery and relatively clear center, suggesting superficial fungal diseases like tinea corporis/cruris.

SYM_DER_CENTRAL_CLEARING.name: Central Clearing
SYM_DER_CENTRAL_CLEARING.description: A pattern where the center of a skin lesion improves or clears up while the outer edge continues to expand, often seen in fungal infections like ringworm.

SYM_DER_ROSACEA_TRIGGERS_HOT_ALCOHOL.name: Flushing Induced by Hot Drinks/alcohol
SYM_DER_ROSACEA_TRIGGERS_HOT_ALCOHOL.description: The patient reports episodes of facial redness and warmth that are triggered by consuming hot beverages, spicy foods, or alcoholic drinks, a common feature of rosacea.

SYM_DER_PAPULOPUSTULES.name: Papulopustules
SYM_DER_PAPULOPUSTULES.description: The presence of small, red, inflamed bumps (papules) and pus-filled lesions (pustules) on the skin, characteristic of conditions like acne or papulopustular rosacea.

SYM_DER_NAIL_ONYCHOLYSIS.name: Onycholysis
SYM_DER_NAIL_ONYCHOLYSIS.description: The painless separation of the nail from the nail bed, starting at the tip and progressing towards the base. It can be associated with psoriasis, fungal infections, or trauma.

SYM_SLP_INTERNAL_BLEEDING.name: Internal Bleeding
SYM_SLP_INTERNAL_BLEEDING.description: Bleeding inside the body, particularly in the chest cavity, stomach cavity or muscles, that may be caused by major trauma, surgery or some unknown complication.

SYM_JM_EAR_DAMAGE.name: Pressure in the Ears
SYM_JM_EAR_DAMAGE.description: The patient feels like they have pressure in their ears or their ears feel full.

SYM_JM_LOSS_OF_SMELL_OR_TASTE.name: Loss of Smell and/or Taste
SYM_JM_LOSS_OF_SMELL_OR_TASTE.description: The patient has a loss of smell and/or taste.

SYM_JM_SWOLLEN_NECK.name: Swollen Neck
SYM_JM_SWOLLEN_NECK.description: The patient has a swollen neck.

SYM_JM_DRY_MOUTH.name: Xerostomia
SYM_JM_DRY_MOUTH.description: Xerostomia means dry mouth, the patient's mouth is dry.

SYM_OPH_OCULAR_DRYNESS.name: Ocular Dryness
SYM_OPH_OCULAR_DRYNESS.description: A subjective sensation of dryness, lack of lubrication, or grittiness in the eyes.

SYM_OPH_FOREIGN_BODY_SENSATION.name: Foreign Body Sensation
SYM_OPH_FOREIGN_BODY_SENSATION.description: The patient reports discomfort as if there is sand or a foreign object scratching inside the eye.

SYM_OPH_BURNING_STINGING.name: Burning or Stinging Sensation
SYM_OPH_BURNING_STINGING.description: A burning or stinging pain caused by ocular surface irritation.

SYM_OPH_PHOTOPHOBIA.name: Photophobia
SYM_OPH_PHOTOPHOBIA.description: Discomfort or worsening of eye pain induced by light stimulation.

SYM_OPH_FLUCTUATING_VISION.name: Fluctuating Vision
SYM_OPH_FLUCTUATING_VISION.description: Vision that noticeably improves and worsens throughout the day or after eye use.

SYM_OPH_TBUT_POSITIVE.name: Reduced Tear Film Break-Up Time
SYM_OPH_TBUT_POSITIVE.description: Fluorescein test shows decreased tear film stability, suggestive of dry eye.

SYM_OPH_SUDDEN_FLOATERS.name: Sudden Increase in Floaters
SYM_OPH_SUDDEN_FLOATERS.description: Appearance or significant increase of floating dark spots in a short period.

SYM_OPH_PHOTOPSIA_FLASHES.name: Photopsia / Flashes
SYM_OPH_PHOTOPSIA_FLASHES.description: Sensation of lightning-like flashes in the field of vision, often related to vitreous traction.

SYM_OPH_CURTAIN_VISION_LOSS.name: Curtain-Like Shadow in Vision
SYM_OPH_CURTAIN_VISION_LOSS.description: A dark shadow advancing from the top, bottom, or side towards the center of the visual field.

SYM_OPH_ACUTE_DECREASED_VA.name: Acute Decrease in Visual Acuity
SYM_OPH_ACUTE_DECREASED_VA.description: A significant reduction in the level of vision over a short period.

SYM_OPH_PERIPHERAL_FIELD_DEFECT.name: Peripheral Field Defect
SYM_OPH_PERIPHERAL_FIELD_DEFECT.description: Visual field testing indicates decreased or absent sensitivity in the peripheral range.

SYM_OPH_EYE_PAIN.name: Eye Pain
SYM_OPH_EYE_PAIN.description: Persistent pain or aching discomfort in the eye.

SYM_OPH_CILIARY_FLUSH_RED_EYE.name: Ciliary Flush / Red Eye
SYM_OPH_CILIARY_FLUSH_RED_EYE.description: A ring of purplish-red congestion around the cornea, indicative of anterior segment inflammation.

SYM_OPH_BLURRED_VISION.name: Blurred Vision
SYM_OPH_BLURRED_VISION.description: Lack of sharpness in both distance and near vision, which may worsen with fatigue.

SYM_OPH_CONSENSUAL_PHOTOPHOBIA.name: Consensual Photophobia
SYM_OPH_CONSENSUAL_PHOTOPHOBIA.description: Pain or increased photophobia in the affected eye when the healthy eye is illuminated.

SYM_OPH_PAINLESS_BLURRING.name: Painless Blurring of Vision
SYM_OPH_PAINLESS_BLURRING.description: Slowly progressive blurring of vision without associated pain.

SYM_OPH_GLARE_HALOS_NIGHT.name: Glare / Night Halos
SYM_OPH_GLARE_HALOS_NIGHT.description: Experience of dazzling light and rings around bright lights or at night.

SYM_OPH_DECREASED_CONTRAST.name: Decreased Contrast Sensitivity
SYM_OPH_DECREASED_CONTRAST.description: Reduced ability to distinguish between an object and its background in grayscale.

SYM_OPH_MYOPIC_SHIFT.name: Myopic Shift
SYM_OPH_MYOPIC_SHIFT.description: A shift in refractive status towards myopia, resulting in clearer near vision.

SYM_OPH_MONOCULAR_DIPLOPIA.name: Monocular Diplopia
SYM_OPH_MONOCULAR_DIPLOPIA.description: Double vision persists after covering the other eye, often associated with lens opacities.

SYM_OPH_METAMORPHOPSIA.name: Metamorphopsia
SYM_OPH_METAMORPHOPSIA.description: Straight lines appear bent or distorted, often indicating macular involvement.

SYM_OPH_STRAIGHT_LINES_WAVY.name: Straight Lines Appear Wavy
SYM_OPH_STRAIGHT_LINES_WAVY.description: Distortion of lines observed during an Amsler grid test.

SYM_OPH_CENTRAL_SCOTOMA.name: Central Scotoma
SYM_OPH_CENTRAL_SCOTOMA.description: A blind spot or area of reduced vision in the central visual field.

SYM_OPH_READING_DIFFICULTY.name: Difficulty Reading
SYM_OPH_READING_DIFFICULTY.description: Requires brighter light or larger print for near reading.

SYM_OPH_EYELID_PAIN_TENDERNESS.name: Eyelid Pain/Tenderness
SYM_OPH_EYELID_PAIN_TENDERNESS.description: Significant pain upon touching the eyelid margin, usually due to local inflammation.

SYM_OPH_EYELID_ERYTHEMA.name: Eyelid Erythema
SYM_OPH_EYELID_ERYTHEMA.description: Localized flushing of the skin, indicating an inflammatory response.

SYM_OPH_TEARING.name: Tearing
SYM_OPH_TEARING.description: Increased tear secretion or reflex tearing.

SYM_OPH_FLOATERS_CHANGING.name: Fluctuating Floaters
SYM_OPH_FLOATERS_CHANGING.description: The number or shape of floaters changes over time or with blood glucose fluctuations.

SYM_OPH_NIGHT_VISION_WORSE.name: Poor Night Vision
SYM_OPH_NIGHT_VISION_WORSE.description: Difficulty seeing in low-light conditions, with loss of detail.

SYM_OPH_COLOR_VISION_REDUCED.name: Reduced Color Vision
SYM_OPH_COLOR_VISION_REDUCED.description: Weakened ability to discriminate colors or a sense of faded color.

SYM_OPH_OSCILLATING_VISION_WITH_GLUCOSE.name: Glucose-Related Vision Fluctuation
SYM_OPH_OSCILLATING_VISION_WITH_GLUCOSE.description: Vision fluctuates with changes in blood glucose levels.

SYM_OPH_TEARING_EPIPHORA.name: Epiphora (Tearing)
SYM_OPH_TEARING_EPIPHORA.description: Tears overflowing the eyelid margin, often due to corneal irritation or poor drainage.

SYM_OPH_CORNEAL_ULCER.name: Corneal Ulcer
SYM_OPH_CORNEAL_ULCER.description: A defect in the corneal epithelium or stroma, potentially caused by infection, trauma, or dry eye, presenting with pain, redness, tearing, and decreased vision.

SYM_OPH_VITREOUS_OPACITY.name: Vitreous Opacity
SYM_OPH_VITREOUS_OPACITY.description: Cloudiness in the vitreous humor, potentially affecting vision, common in the elderly or patients with eye diseases.

SYM_OPH_VITREOUS_HEMORRHAGE.name: Vitreous Hemorrhage
SYM_OPH_VITREOUS_HEMORRHAGE.description: Bleeding into the vitreous humor, which can be caused by retinal tears, diabetic retinopathy, or trauma, presenting as decreased vision, floaters, or visual field defects.

SYM_OPH_RETINAL_TEAR.name: Retinal Tear
SYM_OPH_RETINAL_TEAR.description: A partial-thickness break in the retina that can lead to retinal detachment; symptoms include flashes, floaters, and visual field defects, requiring prompt treatment.

SYM_OPH_SUBCONJUNCTIVAL_HEMORRHAGE.name: Subconjunctival Hemorrhage
SYM_OPH_SUBCONJUNCTIVAL_HEMORRHAGE.description: Bleeding from a ruptured blood vessel under the conjunctiva, typically appearing as a red spot or large patch on the white of the eye, usually painless.

SYM_OPH_POOR_STEREOPSIS.name: Poor Stereopsis
SYM_OPH_POOR_STEREOPSIS.description: Limited binocular fusion leads to reduced or absent stereoscopic vision, common in individuals with amblyopia or strabismus, affecting fine motor skills and distance judgment.

SYM_OPH_SUPPRESSION_SCOTOMA.name: Suppression Scotoma
SYM_OPH_SUPPRESSION_SCOTOMA.description: To avoid diplopia, the brain actively suppresses the image from one eye, creating a functional scotoma; commonly seen in patients with amblyopia/strabismus.

SYM_OPH_RETINAL_NEOVASCULARIZATION.name: Retinal Neovascularization
SYM_OPH_RETINAL_NEOVASCULARIZATION.description: Abnormal, fragile blood vessels formed in response to ischemia, prone to hemorrhage and traction; common in proliferative diabetic retinopathy.

SYM_OPH_PAIN_WITH_EYE_MOVEMENT.name: Pain on Eye Movement
SYM_OPH_PAIN_WITH_EYE_MOVEMENT.description: Retro-orbital or posterior ocular pain experienced when moving the eyes, often associated with inflammation of the optic nerve and its sheath.

SYM_OPH_DYSCHROMATOPSIA.name: Dyschromatopsia
SYM_OPH_DYSCHROMATOPSIA.description: Decreased color discrimination and saturation perception, often affecting the red-green spectrum, and may be accompanied by reduced contrast sensitivity.

SYM_HYPO_SWALLOW.name: Foreign Object Swallowed
SYM_HYPO_SWALLOW.description: The patient has swallowed an unknown foreign object.

SYM_HYPO_DEHYDRATION.name: Dehydration
SYM_HYPO_DEHYDRATION.description: The patient has insufficient water in their body.

SYM_DEN_OROFACIAL_PAIN.name: Orofacial Pain
SYM_DEN_OROFACIAL_PAIN.description: The patient feels pain or discomfort in the teeth, gums, jawbone, facial muscles, joints, etc.

SYM_DEN_OROFACIAL_SWELLING.name: Orofacial Swelling or Mass
SYM_DEN_OROFACIAL_SWELLING.description: There is visible swelling or a palpable abnormal mass inside the patient's mouth or on the face/neck.

SYM_DEN_ORAL_FUNCTION_DIFFICULTY.name: Oral Dysfunction
SYM_DEN_ORAL_FUNCTION_DIFFICULTY.description: The patient experiences difficulty or limitation in performing basic oral functions such as opening, closing, chewing, swallowing, or speaking.

SYM_DEN_ABNORMAL_TOOTH_CONDITION.name: Abnormal Tooth Condition
SYM_DEN_ABNORMAL_TOOTH_CONDITION.description: One or more teeth show sensitivity to cold, hot, sweet, or sour stimuli, loosening, color change, or loss of pulp vitality.

SYM_DEN_RADIOGRAPHIC_ABNORMALITY.name: Radiographic Abnormality of the Jaw
SYM_DEN_RADIOGRAPHIC_ABNORMALITY.description: Radiographic exams such as X-rays or CT scans show abnormal low-density, high-density, or mixed-density shadows in the jawbone area.

SYM_DEN_ABNORMAL_ORAL_SECRETION.name: Abnormal Oral Secretion or Pus Discharge
SYM_DEN_ABNORMAL_ORAL_SECRETION.description: There are abnormal secretions in the oral cavity, such as pus from the gums or mucosa, excessive salivation, or fluid with an unusual taste.

SYM_DEN_MALOCCLUSION_OR_ASYMMETRY.name: Malocclusion or Facial Asymmetry
SYM_DEN_MALOCCLUSION_OR_ASYMMETRY.description: The patient's upper and lower teeth do not fit together properly, or the facial appearance is asymmetrical.

SYM_DEN_TMJ_SYMPTOMS.name: TMJ Symptoms (Clicking/Pain/Locking)
SYM_DEN_TMJ_SYMPTOMS.description: The preauricular joint area experiences pain, clicking, crepitus, or gets stuck (locking) during movement.

SYM_DEN_MUCOSAL_LESION.name: Oral Mucosal Lesion
SYM_DEN_MUCOSAL_LESION.description: The oral mucosa (e.g., lips, cheeks, tongue, palate) shows ulcers, erosions, white or red patches, blisters, or abnormal coloration.

SYM_DEN_GINGIVAL_ABNORMALITY.name: Gingival Abnormality
SYM_DEN_GINGIVAL_ABNORMALITY.description: The gingival tissue bleeds during brushing or spontaneously, turns red, swells, or recedes towards the root of the tooth.

SYM_DEN_NEUROLOGICAL_SYMPTOM.name: Facial Neurological Symptoms
SYM_DEN_NEUROLOGICAL_SYMPTOM.description: The patient feels numbness, reduced sensation, tingling, or paroxysmal severe pain in the facial skin, lips, or tongue.

SYM_DEN_RELEVANT_HISTORY.name: Relevant Orofacial History
SYM_DEN_RELEVANT_HISTORY.description: The patient has a history of orofacial trauma, recurrent lesions, or habits such as lip biting or unilateral chewing.

SYM_DEN_TOOTH_CAVITY_DETECTED.name: Tooth Defect Detected on Probing
SYM_DEN_TOOTH_CAVITY_DETECTED.description: Instrumental or radiographic examination reveals demineralization or a substantive cavity in the hard tissues of the tooth.

SYM_DEN_RADIOGRAPHIC_IMPACTION.name: Radiograph Shows Impacted Wisdom Tooth
SYM_DEN_RADIOGRAPHIC_IMPACTION.description: X-ray or CT imaging shows that the third molar has failed to erupt normally, exhibiting an abnormal position, angle, or obstruction by bone or soft tissue.

SYM_DEN_OPERCULUM_INFLAMMATION.name: Pericoronal Flap Redness, Swelling, and Pus Discharge
SYM_DEN_OPERCULUM_INFLAMMATION.description: The gingival flap covering a partially erupted tooth shows significant redness, swelling, heat, and pain, with pus expressed upon pressure.

SYM_DEN_DIFFUSE_FACENECK_SWELLING.name: Diffuse, Indurated Swelling of Face and Neck
SYM_DEN_DIFFUSE_FACENECK_SWELLING.description: Rapidly spreading swelling with indistinct borders on the face or neck, feeling hard as a board to the touch, accompanied by severe pain.

SYM_DEN_JAW_BONE_DESTRUCTION.name: Radiograph Shows Jaw Bone Destruction
SYM_DEN_JAW_BONE_DESTRUCTION.description: X-ray or CT imaging shows irregular low-density areas, moth-eaten changes, or sequestrum formation in the jawbone.

SYM_DEN_PAINFUL_GLAND_SWELLING_WHILE_EAT.name: Painful Salivary Gland Swelling During Meals
SYM_DEN_PAINFUL_GLAND_SWELLING_WHILE_EAT.description: The patient experiences recurrent, painful swelling of the submandibular or parotid salivary glands upon eating or seeing food.

SYM_DEN_TRANSLUCENT_LOWER_LIP_CYST.name: Bluish Cyst on Lower Lip
SYM_DEN_TRANSLUCENT_LOWER_LIP_CYST.description: A smooth-surfaced, fluctuant, bluish, translucent cystic mass appears on the lower lip mucosa.

SYM_DEN_BLUISH_CYST_FLOOR_OF_MOUTH.name: Bluish Cyst on Floor of Mouth
SYM_DEN_BLUISH_CYST_FLOOR_OF_MOUTH.description: A large, fluctuant, bluish cystic mass, resembling a frog's belly, appears on one side of the lingual frenulum on the floor of the mouth.

SYM_DEN_CYST_AT_NONVITAL_TOOTH_APEX.name: Periapical Radiolucency on Non-Vital Tooth
SYM_DEN_CYST_AT_NONVITAL_TOOTH_APEX.description: Radiographic examination reveals a well-defined, round or oval low-density area in the bone at the apex of a tooth with a necrotic pulp.

SYM_DEN_CYST_AROUND_UNERUPTED_TOOTH_CROW.name: Pericoronal Radiolucency Around Unerupted Tooth
SYM_DEN_CYST_AROUND_UNERUPTED_TOOTH_CROW.description: Radiographic examination shows that the crown of an unerupted tooth is completely surrounded by a well-defined, cystic low-density area.

SYM_DEN_ANTERIOR_CROSSBITE_AND_CONCAVE_P.name: Mandibular Prognathism With Anterior Crossbite and Concave Profile
SYM_DEN_ANTERIOR_CROSSBITE_AND_CONCAVE_P.description: The patient's mandible protrudes significantly, causing the lower anterior teeth to bite in front of the upper ones (anterior crossbite), with a "moon face" or concave profile in lateral view.

SYM_DEN_PROTRUDING_UPPER_TEETH_AND_CONVE.name: Maxillary Prognathism With Convex Profile
SYM_DEN_PROTRUDING_UPPER_TEETH_AND_CONVE.description: The patient's maxilla protrudes significantly, causing the upper anterior teeth to flare out (buck teeth), with a convex profile in lateral view.

SYM_DEN_RECEDING_CHIN_AND_SMALL_JAW.name: Micrognathia With Receding Chin
SYM_DEN_RECEDING_CHIN_AND_SMALL_JAW.description: The patient's mandible is underdeveloped, appearing as a short and significantly receded chin.

SYM_DEN_FACIAL_ASYMMETRY_AND_CHIN_DEVIAT.name: Facial Asymmetry With Chin Deviation
SYM_DEN_FACIAL_ASYMMETRY_AND_CHIN_DEVIAT.description: When viewed from the front, the patient's facial contours are asymmetrical, and the midpoint of the chin is noticeably deviated to one side.

SYM_DEN_TMJ_TRIAD.name: Joint Pain With Clicking and Abnormal Opening
