SYM_DEN_TMJ_TRIAD.description: The patient presents with pain in the temporomandibular joint area, joint sounds (such as clicking or crepitus) during mandibular movement, and abnormal opening or closing.

SYM_DEN_INABILITY_TO_CLOSE_MOUTH_AFTER_W.name: Inability to Close Mouth After Opening Wide
SYM_DEN_INABILITY_TO_CLOSE_MOUTH_AFTER_W.description: After opening the mouth wide (e.g., yawning), the patient's jaw gets stuck in the open position and cannot be closed voluntarily.

SYM_DEN_PAROXYSMAL_ELECTRIC_SHOCK_FACE_P.name: Electric Shock-Like Facial Pain
SYM_DEN_PAROXYSMAL_ELECTRIC_SHOCK_FACE_P.description: Brief, recurrent, severe pain like an electric shock or knife cut on one side of the face, which can be triggered by light touch or other actions.

SYM_DEN_LIP_INFLAMMATION_WITH_SCALING_AN.name: Cheilitis With Dryness and Scaling
SYM_DEN_LIP_INFLAMMATION_WITH_SCALING_AN.description: The lips are red and swollen, accompanied by significant dryness, peeling, cracking, and crusting.

SYM_DEN_TONGUE_INFLAMMATION_PAPILLAE_ATR.name: Glossitis With Smooth Tongue
SYM_DEN_TONGUE_INFLAMMATION_PAPILLAE_ATR.description: Inflammation of the tongue, with atrophy and disappearance of the lingual papillae on the dorsum, resulting in an abnormally smooth tongue surface that may be red or pale.

SYM_DEN_ATTACHMENT_LOSS_AND_BONE_LOSS.name: Periodontal Attachment Loss and Bone Resorption
SYM_DEN_ATTACHMENT_LOSS_AND_BONE_LOSS.description: Clinical or radiographic examination confirms destruction of the periodontal tissues connecting the tooth and alveolar bone, accompanied by resorption of the alveolar bone.

SYM_DEN_REVERSIBLE_GINGIVAL_INFLAMMATION.name: Gingivitis Without Attachment Loss
SYM_DEN_REVERSIBLE_GINGIVAL_INFLAMMATION.description: The gingiva shows signs of inflammation such as redness, swelling, and easy bleeding, but the deeper periodontal supporting tissues (like alveolar bone) are not yet destroyed.

SYM_DEN_RECURRENT_PAINFUL_OVAL_ORAL_ULCE.name: Recurrent Painful Oral Ulcers
SYM_DEN_RECURRENT_PAINFUL_OVAL_ORAL_ULCE.description: The oral mucosa repeatedly develops well-defined, centrally depressed, painful ulcers with a characteristic of periodic recurrence.

SYM_DEN_SICCA_SYNDROME_WITH_AUTOANTIBODI.name: Dry Mouth and Eyes With Positive Antibodies
SYM_DEN_SICCA_SYNDROME_WITH_AUTOANTIBODI.description: The patient experiences persistent dry mouth and dry eyes, and blood tests reveal autoantibodies associated with Sjögren's syndrome (e.g., SSA/SSB).

SYM_DEN_ENLARGED_TONGUE_IMPRINTS.name: Enlarged Tongue With Scalloped Borders
SYM_DEN_ENLARGED_TONGUE_IMPRINTS.description: The tongue is abnormally enlarged, causing its edges to be pressed against the teeth, forming distinct wavy indentations (scalloping).

SYM_DEN_SHORT_LINGUAL_FRENUM_RESTRICTING.name: Restricted Tongue Movement Due to Short Frenulum
SYM_DEN_SHORT_LINGUAL_FRENUM_RESTRICTING.description: The lingual frenulum connecting the underside of the tongue to the floor of the mouth is too short or tight, preventing normal protrusion or elevation of the tongue.

SYM_DEN_PROGRESSIVE_UNILATERAL_FACIAL_TI.name: Progressive Hemifacial Atrophy
SYM_DEN_PROGRESSIVE_UNILATERAL_FACIAL_TI.description: The soft and hard tissues of one side of the face progressively atrophy and thin over time, leading to severe facial asymmetry.

SYM_DEN_RADIOLUCENT_LESION_WITH_KERATINI.name: Histopathology Shows Keratinized Cyst Wall
SYM_DEN_RADIOLUCENT_LESION_WITH_KERATINI.description: Histopathological examination of a cystic lesion within the jawbone reveals its wall is lined with characteristic keratinized epithelium.

SYM_DEN_CYST_WITH_GHOST_CELLS_AND_CALCIF.name: Histopathology Shows Ghost Cells in Cyst
SYM_DEN_CYST_WITH_GHOST_CELLS_AND_CALCIF.description: Histopathological examination of a cystic lesion within the jawbone reveals the presence of characteristic "ghost cells" and calcifications.

SYM_DEN_CYST_WITH_GLANDULAR_STRUCTURES.name: Histopathology Shows Glandular Structures in Cyst
SYM_DEN_CYST_WITH_GLANDULAR_STRUCTURES.description: Histopathological examination of a cystic lesion within the jawbone reveals mucus cells and duct-like structures within its epithelium.

SYM_DEN_CYST_IN_PLACE_OF_MISSING_TOOTH.name: Cystic Radiolucency in Place of Missing Tooth
SYM_DEN_CYST_IN_PLACE_OF_MISSING_TOOTH.description: Radiographic examination shows a cystic low-density area in the bone at the location of a congenitally missing tooth.

SYM_DEN_CYST_LATERAL_TO_VITAL_TOOTH_ROOT.name: Cyst Lateral to Vital Tooth Root
SYM_DEN_CYST_LATERAL_TO_VITAL_TOOTH_ROOT.description: Radiographic examination reveals a cystic lesion on the lateral aspect of the root of a normal tooth with a vital pulp.

SYM_DEN_HEART_SHAPED_RADIOLUCENCY_ANTERI.name: Heart-Shaped Radiolucency in Maxillary Midline
SYM_DEN_HEART_SHAPED_RADIOLUCENCY_ANTERI.description: Radiographic examination shows a heart-shaped, well-defined low-density area in the bone at the midline of the anterior maxilla.

SYM_DEN_INVERTED_BETWEEN_MAXILLARY_INCIS.name: Inverted Pear-Shaped Radiolucency Between Maxillary Teeth
SYM_DEN_INVERTED_BETWEEN_MAXILLARY_INCIS.description: Radiographic examination shows an inverted pear-shaped, well-defined low-density area in the bone between the maxillary lateral incisor and canine.

SYM_DEN_MIDLINE_PALATAL_SWELLING_AND_RAD.name: Midline Palatal Swelling With Radiolucency
SYM_DEN_MIDLINE_PALATAL_SWELLING_AND_RAD.description: The patient presents with a swelling at the midline of the hard palate, and radiographic examination reveals an intraosseous low-density area at that site.

SYM_DEN_MIDLINE_MANDIBULAR_RADIOLUCENCY.name: Radiolucency Below Apices in Mandibular Midline
SYM_DEN_MIDLINE_MANDIBULAR_RADIOLUCENCY.description: Radiographic examination shows a low-density area in the bone at the midline position, below the apices of the mandibular central incisors.

SYM_DEN_DOUGHY_MIDLINE_CYST_IN_FLOOR_OF_.name: Doughy Midline Cyst in Floor of Mouth
SYM_DEN_DOUGHY_MIDLINE_CYST_IN_FLOOR_OF_.description: A slow-growing cystic mass appears in the midline of the floor of the mouth, which is soft and pliable to the touch, like dough.

SYM_DEN_PASTEY_CYST_IN_FLOOR_OF_MOUTH.name: Pasty Cyst in Floor of Mouth or Faceneck Region
SYM_DEN_PASTEY_CYST_IN_FLOOR_OF_MOUTH.description: A cystic mass appears in the floor of the mouth or the faceneck region, containing keratin that is white, pasty, or cheese-like.

SYM_DEN_LATERAL_NECK_SWELLING_ANTERIOR_T.name: Cyst Anterior to Sternocleidomastoid Muscle
SYM_DEN_LATERAL_NECK_SWELLING_ANTERIOR_T.description: A well-defined cystic mass appears on the lateral side of the neck, at the anterior border of the sternocleidomastoid muscle.

SYM_DEN_MIDLINE_NECK_CYST_MOVES_WITH_SWA.name: Midline Neck Cyst Moves With Swallowing
SYM_DEN_MIDLINE_NECK_CYST_MOVES_WITH_SWA.description: A cystic mass appears on the midline of the neck, which moves up and down when the patient swallows or protrudes the tongue.

SYM_DEN_TMJ_CLICKING_AND_LOCKING.name: Joint Clicking or Locking
SYM_DEN_TMJ_CLICKING_AND_LOCKING.description: The temporomandibular joint produces a distinct clicking sound or gets stuck during opening or closing, preventing smooth movement.

SYM_DEN_PROGRESSIVE_INABILITY_TO_OPEN_MO.name: Progressive Limitation of Mouth Opening
SYM_DEN_PROGRESSIVE_INABILITY_TO_OPEN_MO.description: The patient's ability to open their mouth progressively worsens over time, potentially leading to a complete inability to open the mouth.

SYM_DEN_MASTICATORY_MUSCLE_PAIN_WITH_TRI.name: Masticatory Muscle Pain With Trigger Points
SYM_DEN_MASTICATORY_MUSCLE_PAIN_WITH_TRI.description: Aching or dull pain occurs in the facial masticatory muscles. Pressing on the muscles can locate one or more intensely painful spots that may also refer pain elsewhere.

SYM_DER_COMEDONES.name: Open/closed Comedones (blackheads/whiteheads)
SYM_DER_COMEDONES.description: Comedones are the hallmark lesions of acne, consisting of hair follicles clogged with sebum and dead skin cells. Open comedones (blackheads) have a dark surface, while closed comedones (whiteheads) are skin-colored bumps.

SYM_DER_CENTROFACIAL_TELANGIECTATIC_FLUS.name: Persistent Centrofacial Flushing With Visible Telangiectasias
SYM_DER_CENTROFACIAL_TELANGIECTATIC_FLUS.description: Persistent redness in the central face, accompanied by fine, visible, dilated blood vessels (telangiectasias). This is a characteristic feature of erythematotelangiectatic rosacea.

SYM_DER_FLEXURAL_ECZEMA.name: Flexural Eczematous Lesions With Significant Pruritus and Lichenification
SYM_DER_FLEXURAL_ECZEMA.description: Itchy, inflamed skin patches located in the body's folds (e.g., behind the knees, inside the elbows). Chronic scratching leads to thickened, leathery skin (lichenification), a common presentation of atopic dermatitis.

SYM_DER_TRANSIENT_WHEALS_24H.name: Blanchable, Migratory Wheals
SYM_DER_TRANSIENT_WHEALS_24H.description: Raised, itchy, and swollen skin welts (wheals) that turn white when pressed. Individual lesions are transient, typically resolving within 24 hours and appearing in new locations, characteristic of urticaria (hives).

SYM_DER_AUSPITZ_SIGN.name: Pinpoint Bleeding After Scale Removal
SYM_DER_AUSPITZ_SIGN.description: Well-demarcated erythematous scaly plaques with pinpoint bleeding after scale removal, consistent with psoriasis features.

SYM_DER_WOOD_LAMP_DEPIGMENT.name: Depigmented Patches With Milky-White Fluorescence Under Wood's Lamp
SYM_DER_WOOD_LAMP_DEPIGMENT.description: Milky-white fluorescence under Wood's lamp suggests epidermal melanin loss (e.g., vitiligo).

SYM_DER_KOH_DERMATOPHYTE_POS.name: Hyaline Septate Hyphae on 10-20% KOH Prep of Skin Scrapings
SYM_DER_KOH_DERMATOPHYTE_POS.description: Presence of hyaline septate hyphae on KOH wet mount suggests dermatophyte infection.

SYM_DER_KOH_DERMATOPHYTE_POS_GROIN.name: Positive KOH Prep of Groin Skin Scrapings
SYM_DER_KOH_DERMATOPHYTE_POS_GROIN.description: Presence of hyaline septate hyphae on KOH wet mount suggests dermatophyte infection.

SYM_DER_BLACK_DOT_TCAP.name: Black Dot Alopecia (broken Hairs) or Positive KOH Hair Exam
SYM_DER_BLACK_DOT_TCAP.description: Black dot appearance left by broken hairs, common in endothrix tinea capitis.

SYM_DER_SPAGHETTI_MEATBALLS_KOH.name: Short Hyphae + Yeast Clusters on KOH Prep
SYM_DER_SPAGHETTI_MEATBALLS_KOH.description: Presence of short hyphae and clusters of yeast ('spaghetti and meatballs') on KOH prep, typical of tinea versicolor.

SYM_DER_THROMBOSED_CAPILLARIES.name: Black Dots/pinpoint Bleeding After Shaving
SYM_DER_THROMBOSED_CAPILLARIES.description: Pinpoint black dots/bleeding after shaving off the stratum corneum, caused by thrombosed capillaries within the wart.

SYM_DER_FLAT_WARTS_CLUSTERS.name: Multiple Small, Flat Papules in Clusters on Face/hands/legs
SYM_DER_FLAT_WARTS_CLUSTERS.description: Multiple small, flat papules in clusters, consistent with flat warts.

SYM_DER_HONEY_COLORED_CRUSTS.name: Honey-Colored Crusts
SYM_DER_HONEY_COLORED_CRUSTS.description: Golden honey-colored crusts, typical of impetigo.

SYM_DER_HERALD_PATCH.name: 'Herald Patch' Followed by Christmas Tree Distribution
SYM_DER_HERALD_PATCH.description: A larger oval scaly patch appears first, followed by a 'Christmas tree' distribution of secondary rashes a few days later.

SYM_DER_EXCLAMATION_MARK_HAIR.name: 'Exclamation Mark Hairs' at the Edge of Hair Loss
SYM_DER_EXCLAMATION_MARK_HAIR.description: Short broken hairs that are thinner at the proximal end and thicker at the distal end, resembling an 'exclamation mark', suggesting active alopecia areata.

SYM_DER_WICKHAM_STRIAE.name: Wickham's Striae on Lesion Surface
SYM_DER_WICKHAM_STRIAE.description: Fine, reticular white lines on the lesion surface, a characteristic sign of lichen planus.

SYM_DER_DH_IGA_DIF_POS.name: Granular IgA Deposits in Dermal Papillae on Direct Immunofluorescence
SYM_DER_DH_IGA_DIF_POS.description: Direct immunofluorescence showing granular IgA deposits in the dermal papillae supports the diagnosis of dermatitis herpetiformis.

SYM_DER_MELASMA_WOODLAMP_ENHANCE.name: Epidermal Pigmentation Enhanced Under Wood's Lamp
SYM_DER_MELASMA_WOODLAMP_ENHANCE.description: Enhanced border and color of pigmented spots under Wood's lamp, suggesting primarily epidermal pigmentation.

SYM_DER_HS_SINUS_TRACTS.name: Sinus Tracts and Scarring in Axilla/groin
SYM_DER_HS_SINUS_TRACTS.description: Subcutaneous sinus tracts and scarring formed after recurrent nodules and abscesses, a chronic change of hidradenitis suppurativa.

SYM_HYPO_PEN_LID.name: Pen Lid Found
SYM_HYPO_PEN_LID.description: X-ray imaging shows the patient has swallowed a pen lid.

SYM_HYPO_FISH.name: Fish Bone Found
SYM_HYPO_FISH.description: X-ray imaging shows the patient has swallowed a fish bone.

SYM_HYPO_COIN.name: Coin Found
SYM_HYPO_COIN.description: X-ray imaging shows the patient has swallowed a coin.

SYM_HYPO_NOSEBLEEDS.name: Nose Bleed
SYM_HYPO_NOSEBLEEDS.description: The patient is bleeding from the nose.

SYM_HYPO_STUBBED_TOE.name: Stubbed Toe
SYM_HYPO_STUBBED_TOE.description: X-ray imaging shows no damage to the toe.

SYM_HYPO_ALCOHOL.name: Alcohol Consumption
SYM_HYPO_ALCOHOL.description: The patient has previously consumed a large amount of alcohol.

SYM_HYPO_SUNBURN.name: Sunburn
SYM_HYPO_SUNBURN.description: Sunburn is red, hot and sore skin caused by too much sun.

SYM_HYPO_SORE_THROAT.name: Sore Throat
SYM_HYPO_SORE_THROAT.description: The patient has a sore throat.

SYM_HYPO_EARACHE.name: Earache
SYM_HYPO_EARACHE.description: Earache can be a sharp, dull or burning ear pain caused by a minor infection. One or both ears may be affected.

SYM_HYPO_OVEREATING.name: Overeating
SYM_HYPO_OVEREATING.description: Overeating is the excess food consumed in relation to the energy that an organism expends (or expels via excretion), leading to weight gaining and often obesity.

SYM_HYPO_SYNCOPE.name: Reflex Syncope
SYM_HYPO_SYNCOPE.description: The patient sufferes from brief losses of consciousness due to a neurologically induced drop in blood pressure.

SYM_HYPO_HICKEY.name: Hickey
SYM_HYPO_HICKEY.description: A hickey, hickie or love bite in British English, is a bruise or bruise-like mark caused by the kissing or sucking of the skin, usually on the neck or arm.

SYM_HYPO_ECCHYMOSIS.name: Ecchymosis
SYM_HYPO_ECCHYMOSIS.description: A discoloration of the skin resulting from bleeding underneath, typically caused by bruising.

SYM_HYPO_F_POISONING.name: Food Poisoning
SYM_HYPO_F_POISONING.description: Food poisoning is the result of eating contaminated, spoiled, or toxic food.

SYM_OPH_RETINAL_BREAK_DETACHMENT_SEEN.name: Retinal Break/Detachment Observed
SYM_OPH_RETINAL_BREAK_DETACHMENT_SEEN.description: Direct observation of a retinal tear or elevation during fundus examination.

SYM_OPH_ANTERIOR_CHAMBER_CELLS_FLARE.name: Anterior Chamber Cells/Flare Positive
SYM_OPH_ANTERIOR_CHAMBER_CELLS_FLARE.description: Slit-lamp examination reveals floating inflammatory cells and protein exudation.

SYM_OPH_LENS_OPACITY_SEEN.name: Lens Opacity Observed
SYM_OPH_LENS_OPACITY_SEEN.description: Direct observation of cortical, nuclear, or posterior capsular opacities of the lens via slit-lamp.

SYM_OPH_CENTRAL_VISION_LOSS.name: Central Vision Loss
SYM_OPH_CENTRAL_VISION_LOSS.description: Reduced visual acuity predominantly affecting the central fixation area.

SYM_OPH_AMD_OCT_FA_CONFIRM.name: Macular Neovascularization/Leakage
SYM_OPH_AMD_OCT_FA_CONFIRM.description: Imaging studies reveal macular edema, leakage, or neovascularization.

SYM_OPH_ORBITAL_CELLULITIS.name: Orbital Cellulitis
SYM_OPH_ORBITAL_CELLULITIS.description: Infection of the soft tissues within the orbit, often accompanied by swelling, tenderness, and fever.

SYM_OPH_PURULENT_LID_PUSTULE.name: Purulent Pustule on Eyelid Margin
SYM_OPH_PURULENT_LID_PUSTULE.description: A yellowish-white pus point visible at the base of the eyelashes or the orifice of a meibomian gland.

SYM_OPH_DR_FINDINGS_CONFIRMED.name: Diabetic Retinopathy Signs Confirmed
SYM_OPH_DR_FINDINGS_CONFIRMED.description: Fundus or imaging shows microaneurysms, hemorrhages, exudates, or neovascularization.

SYM_OPH_PTOSIS_LID_DROOP.name: Ptosis / Eyelid Drooping
SYM_OPH_PTOSIS_LID_DROOP.description: Abnormally low position of the upper eyelid, which may affect the visual field and appearance, common in neurological or muscular diseases.

SYM_OPH_MGD_ORIFICE_OBSTRUCTION.name: Meibomian Gland Orifice Obstruction
SYM_OPH_MGD_ORIFICE_OBSTRUCTION.description: Blockage of the meibomian gland orifices, leading to abnormal lipid secretion and potentially causing dry eye symptoms.

SYM_OPH_PTERYGIUM_GROWTH_SEEN.name: Fibrovascular Growth on Cornea Observed (Pterygium)
SYM_OPH_PTERYGIUM_GROWTH_SEEN.description: A wing-shaped pterygium is present on the conjunctiva, which may cause blurred vision or discomfort, requiring regular monitoring or surgical treatment.

SYM_OPH_ENTROPION_WITH_TRICHIASIS.name: Entropion With Trichiasis
SYM_OPH_ENTROPION_WITH_TRICHIASIS.description: Inward turning of the eyelid causes eyelashes to grow inward, potentially irritating the cornea and causing pain or discomfort.

SYM_OPH_ECTROPION_WITH_PUNCTAL_EVERSION.name: Ectropion With Punctal Eversion
SYM_OPH_ECTROPION_WITH_PUNCTAL_EVERSION.description: Outward turning of the eyelid causes exposure of the punctum, potentially leading to abnormal tear drainage or dry eye symptoms.

SYM_OPH_DENDRITIC_ULCER_STAIN_POSITIVE.name: Dendritic Corneal Ulcer
SYM_OPH_DENDRITIC_ULCER_STAIN_POSITIVE.description: Fluorescein staining reveals a branching, tree-like ulcer on the corneal epithelium, characteristic of herpes simplex virus infection.

SYM_OPH_ABNORMAL_MEIBUM_EXPRESSION.name: Abnormal Meibum Expression
SYM_OPH_ABNORMAL_MEIBUM_EXPRESSION.description: Abnormal secretions from the meibomian glands on the eyelids, which may lead to dry eye symptoms or discomfort.

SYM_OPH_LACRIMAL_SAC_REFLUX.name: Lacrimal Sac Reflux
SYM_OPH_LACRIMAL_SAC_REFLUX.description: Regurgitation of fluid from the puncta upon compression of the lacrimal sac, which may indicate nasolacrimal duct obstruction or infection.

SYM_OPH_MYOPIA_DEFINING.name: Decreased Distance Vision, Significantly Improved With Negative Spherical Lens Correction
SYM_OPH_MYOPIA_DEFINING.description: Distant objects are blurry, but vision improves significantly with a negative spherical lens; a defining feature of refractive myopia, distinguishing it from hyperopia/presbyopia.

SYM_OPH_SCISSORING_REFLEX.name: Scissoring Reflex on Retinoscopy
SYM_OPH_SCISSORING_REFLEX.description: During retinoscopy, the red reflex shows a splitting and crossing scissor-like movement, indicating irregular astigmatism, often seen in keratoconus.

SYM_OPH_WEISS_RING_SEEN.name: Weiss Ring Observed
SYM_OPH_WEISS_RING_SEEN.description: A ring-shaped opacity (Weiss ring) is visible anterior to the optic disc, often indicating posterior vitreous detachment; requires vigilance for associated retinal tears.

SYM_OPH_CHOROIDAL_LESIONS_ACTIVE.name: Active Choroidal Lesions
SYM_OPH_CHOROIDAL_LESIONS_ACTIVE.description: Imaging suggests lesions are in an active phase, often accompanied by exudation, fluorescein leakage, or an increase in adjacent vitreous cells.

SYM_OPH_VITRITIS_ACTIVE_CELLS.name: Vitreous Inflammatory Cells (+)
SYM_OPH_VITRITIS_ACTIVE_CELLS.description: Slit-lamp/fundus examination reveals suspended inflammatory cells and opacities in the vitreous cavity, often indicating active posterior uveitis.

SYM_OPH_RAPD_POSITIVE.name: Positive RAPD
SYM_OPH_RAPD_POSITIVE.description: Swinging flashlight test indicates a relative afferent pupillary defect, commonly seen in unilateral (or asymmetric) optic nerve disease.

SYM_OPH_RETINAL_MICROANEURYSMS.name: Retinal Microaneurysms
SYM_OPH_RETINAL_MICROANEURYSMS.description: Ophthalmoscopy reveals small, red, dot-like lesions (microaneurysms), mostly in the posterior pole; indicative of mild diabetic retinopathy, typically without neovascularization.

SYM_OPH_REDUCED_BCVA_WITH_NORMAL_EXAM.name: Amblyopia Detected
SYM_OPH_REDUCED_BCVA_WITH_NORMAL_EXAM.description: Reduced visual acuity that persists after ruling out organic lesions of the cornea, lens, vitreous, and retina, consistent with the diagnostic criteria for amblyopia.

SYM_OPH_LOCALIZED_LID_NODULE.name: Localized Eyelid Nodule
SYM_OPH_LOCALIZED_LID_NODULE.description: A red, swollen, and firm lump on the eyelid margin, which may gradually suppurate.

SYM_PLAS_R009.name: Complex Varicose Veins
SYM_PLAS_R009.description: The patient has varicose veins and unfortunately they appear to be rather complex and have the signs or symptoms of a more serious diagnosis. Varicose veins that cause symptoms should not be ignored and treated promptly to prevent patient discomfort and to help prevent future issues.

SYM_SH_LGV_NAAT_POS.name: Positive NAAT for Chlamydia Trachomatis L1–L3
SYM_SH_LGV_NAAT_POS.description: Positive nucleic acid amplification test (NAAT) for Chlamydia trachomatis L1–L3 supports the diagnosis of LGV.

SYM_SH_DONOVAN_BODIES.name: Donovan Bodies on Smear/biopsy
SYM_SH_DONOVAN_BODIES.description: Presence of Donovan bodies on smear/biopsy suggests granuloma inguinale (donovanosis).

SYM_SH_KOH_CANDIDA_POS.name: Budding Yeast/pseudohyphae on 10% KOH Prep of Vaginal Discharge
SYM_SH_KOH_CANDIDA_POS.description: Presence of budding yeast/pseudohyphae on KOH microscopy of vaginal discharge is consistent with Candida infection.

EXM_CRP_CCBT.name: Catecholamine Blood Sampling
EXM_CRP_CCBT.description: Catecholamine blood tests measure the amount of catecholamines in your body. “Catecholamines” is an umbrella term for the hormones dopamine, norepinephrine, and epinephrine. \n\nRequired Lab: Haematology

EXM_CRP_CCBT_TEST.name: Catecholamine Blood Test
EXM_CRP_CCBT_TEST.description: Catecholamine blood tests measure the amount of catecholamines in your body. “Catecholamines” is an umbrella term for the hormones dopamine, norepinephrine, and epinephrine.

EXM_CRP_CA.name: CA Blood Sampling
EXM_CRP_CA.description: A Cancer Antigen (CA) test is a blood test used to help diagnose and manage certain types of cancers. Tumour markers are substances, usually proteins, that are produced by the body in response to cancer growth or by the cancer tissue itself. Some tumour markers are specific for one type of cancer, while others are seen in several cancer types. Many of the well-known markers are seen in non-cancerous conditions as well as cancer. Consequently, they cannot be used to diagnose cancer. \n\nRequired Lab: Haematology

EXM_CRP_CA_TEST.name: CA Blood Test
EXM_CRP_CA_TEST.description: A Cancer Antigen (CA) test is a blood test used to help diagnose and manage certain types of cancers. Tumour markers are substances, usually proteins, that are produced by the body in response to cancer growth or by the cancer tissue itself. Some tumour markers are specific for one type of cancer, while others are seen in several cancer types. Many of the well-known markers are seen in non-cancerous conditions as well as cancer. Consequently, they cannot be used to diagnose cancer.

EXM_CRP_LIVER_FT_SAMPLING.name: Liver Function Test Sampling
EXM_CRP_LIVER_FT_SAMPLING.description: Liver function tests (also called LFTs) are blood tests that can provide information about how your liver is working. They test the levels of a number of proteins and enzymes that are either produced by liver cells or released into the blood when liver cells are damaged. \n\nRequired Lab: Haematology

EXM_CRP_LIVER_FT_TEST.name: Liver Function Test
EXM_CRP_LIVER_FT_TEST.description: Liver function tests (also called LFTs) are blood tests that can provide information about how your liver is working. They test the levels of a number of proteins and enzymes that are either produced by liver cells or released into the blood when liver cells are damaged.

EXM_CRP_MINERALT_SAMPLE.name: Comprehensive Mineral Panel Sampling
EXM_CRP_MINERALT_SAMPLE.description: The Comprehensive Mineral Panel includes testing the blood for five minerals and is used to detect deficiencies or overexposure (toxicity) across the mineral spectrum. This panel includes testing levels of the following five minerals: Calcium, Copper, Magnesium, Manganese and Zinc. \n\nRequired Lab: Haematology

EXM_CRP_MINERALT_TEST.name: Comprehensive Mineral Panel Test
EXM_CRP_MINERALT_TEST.description: The Comprehensive Mineral Panel includes testing the blood for five minerals and is used to detect deficiencies or overexposure (toxicity) across the mineral spectrum. This panel includes testing levels of the following five minerals: Calcium, Copper, Magnesium, Manganese and Zinc.

EXM_CRP_HGPO_SAMPLING.name: OGTT Sampling
EXM_CRP_HGPO_SAMPLING.description: The glucose tolerance test, also known as the oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT), measures your body's response to sugar (glucose).

EXM_CRP_HGPO_TESTING.name: OGTT Testing
EXM_CRP_HGPO_TESTING.description: The glucose tolerance test, also known as the oral glucose tolerance test (OGTT), measures your body's response to sugar (glucose).

EXM_CRP_BLOOD_SMEAR_SAMPLE.name: Blood Smear Sample
EXM_CRP_BLOOD_SMEAR_SAMPLE.description: A blood film—or peripheral blood smear—is a thin layer of blood smeared on a glass microscope slide and then stained in such a way as to allow the various blood cells to be examined microscopically. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_BLOOD_SMEAR_TEST.name: Blood Smear Test
EXM_CRP_BLOOD_SMEAR_TEST.description: A blood film—or peripheral blood smear—is a thin layer of blood smeared on a glass microscope slide and then stained in such a way as to allow the various blood cells to be examined microscopically.

EXM_CRP_EMICRO_SAMPLE.name: Electron Microscopy Sample
EXM_CRP_EMICRO_SAMPLE.description: Diagnostic Electron Microscopy is used as an important diagnostic tool to screen human tissues at high magnification (the ultrastructural level).  For diagnostic purposes solid tissues are prepared for TEM in the same way as other biological tissues, they are fixed in glutaraldehyde and osmium tetroxide then dehydrated and embedded in epoxy resin. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_EMICRO_TEST.name: Electron Microscopy Test
EXM_CRP_EMICRO_TEST.description: Diagnostic Electron Microscopy is used as an important diagnostic tool to screen human tissues at high magnification (the ultrastructural level).  For diagnostic purposes solid tissues are prepared for TEM in the same way as other biological tissues, they are fixed in glutaraldehyde and osmium tetroxide then dehydrated and embedded in epoxy resin.

EXM_CRP_COLONOSCOPY.name: Colonoscopy
EXM_CRP_COLONOSCOPY.description: A colonoscopy is a medical procedure that is done to look for cancer, colon polyps or other abnormalities inside your bowel. Colonoscopy is done using an instrument called a colonoscope (a type of endoscope) — a long, thin, flexible tube containing a camera and a light.

EXM_CRP_PELVIC_EXAM.name: Pelvic Exam
EXM_CRP_PELVIC_EXAM.description: A pelvic exam is a doctor's visual and physical examination of a woman's reproductive organs. During the exam, the doctor inspects the vagina, cervix, fallopian tubes, vulva, ovaries, and uterus.

EXM_CRP_DRE_EXAM.name: Digital Rectal Exam (DRE)
EXM_CRP_DRE_EXAM.description: A digital rectal exam (DRE) is a test for both men and women. It allows a doctor to check the lower rectum, pelvis, and lower belly for cancer and other health problems like prostate cancer.

EXM_CRP_SEIN.name: Breast Examination
EXM_CRP_SEIN.description: Visual inspection and palpation of breast.

EXM_CRP_UAS.name: Urine Antigen Screen Sampling
EXM_CRP_UAS.description: Urine test to check the amount of cancer based antigens in the urine. By capitalizing on the concentration of shed antigen from a variety of pathogens in the kidneys for excretion in the urine, urinary antigen testing can be used to obtain rapid test results. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_UAS_TEST.name: Urine Antigen Screen Test
EXM_CRP_UAS_TEST.description: Urine test to check the amount of cancer based antigens in the urine. By capitalizing on the concentration of shed antigen from a variety of pathogens in the kidneys for excretion in the urine, urinary antigen testing can be used to obtain rapid test results.

EXM_CRP_X_RAY_MAMMOGRAM.name: Mammogram
EXM_CRP_X_RAY_MAMMOGRAM.description: A mammogram is an X-ray of breast tissue that can find changes too small to be felt during a physical examination. \n\nRequired Room: X-Ray

EXM_CRP_SCINTIGRAPHIE.name: Scintigraphy
EXM_CRP_SCINTIGRAPHIE.description: Scintigraphy (from Latin scintilla, "spark"), also known as a gamma scan, is a diagnostic test in nuclear medicine, where radioisotopes attached to drugs that travel to a specific organ or tissue (radiopharmaceuticals) are taken internally and the emitted gamma radiation is captured by external detectors (gamma cameras) to form two-dimensional images in a similar process to the capture of x-ray images.

EXM_CRP_FLOWCYT.name: Flow Cytometry Sample
EXM_CRP_FLOWCYT.description: Flow cytometry is a technique used to detect and measure physical and chemical characteristics of a population of cells or particles. A sample containing cells or particles is suspended in a fluid and injected into the flow cytometer instrument. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_FLOWCYT_TEST.name: Flow Cytometry Test
EXM_CRP_FLOWCYT_TEST.description: Flow cytometry is a technique used to detect and measure physical and chemical characteristics of a population of cells or particles. A sample containing cells or particles is suspended in a fluid and injected into the flow cytometer instrument.

EXM_CRP_FISH_SAMPLE.name: F.I.S.H. Sampling
EXM_CRP_FISH_SAMPLE.description: Fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) is a molecular cytogenetic technique that uses fluorescent probes that bind to only those parts of a nucleic acid sequence with a high degree of sequence complementarity. It was developed by biomedical researchers in the early 1980s to detect and localize the presence or absence of specific DNA sequences on chromosomes. Fluorescence microscopy can be used to find out where the fluorescent probe is bound to the chromosomes. FISH is often used for finding specific features in DNA for use in genetic counseling, medicine, and species identification. FISH can also be used to detect and localize specific RNA targets (mRNA, lncRNA and miRNA) in cells, circulating tumor cells, and tissue samples. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_FISH_TEST.name: F.I.S.H. Test
EXM_CRP_FISH_TEST.description: Fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) is a molecular cytogenetic technique that uses fluorescent probes that bind to only those parts of a nucleic acid sequence with a high degree of sequence complementarity. It was developed by biomedical researchers in the early 1980s to detect and localize the presence or absence of specific DNA sequences on chromosomes. Fluorescence microscopy can be used to find out where the fluorescent probe is bound to the chromosomes. FISH is often used for finding specific features in DNA for use in genetic counseling, medicine, and species identification. FISH can also be used to detect and localize specific RNA targets (mRNA, lncRNA and miRNA) in cells, circulating tumor cells, and tissue samples.

EXM_CRP_IV_STAINING_SAMPLE.name: In Vitro Staining Sampling
EXM_CRP_IV_STAINING_SAMPLE.description: Staining is a technique used to enhance contrast in samples, generally at the microscopic level. Stains and dyes are frequently used in histology (the study of tissue under the microscope) and in the medical fields of histopathology, hematology, and cytopathology that focus on the study and diagnoses disease at a microscopic level. Stains may be used to define biological tissues (highlighting, for example, muscle fibers or connective tissue), cell populations (classifying different blood cells), or organelles within individual cells. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_IV_STAINING_TEST.name: In Vitro Staining Test
EXM_CRP_IV_STAINING_TEST.description: Staining is a technique used to enhance contrast in samples, generally at the microscopic level. Stains and dyes are frequently used in histology (the study of tissue under the microscope) and in the medical fields of histopathology, hematology, and cytopathology that focus on the study and diagnoses disease at a microscopic level. Stains may be used to define biological tissues (highlighting, for example, muscle fibers or connective tissue), cell populations (classifying different blood cells), or organelles within individual cells.

EXM_CRP_BONEBIOPSY_SAMPLE.name: Bone Marrow Biopsy Sample
EXM_CRP_BONEBIOPSY_SAMPLE.description: A bone marrow biopsy collects a cylindrical core sample that preserves the marrow's structure. The biopsy sample is evaluated by a specialist to determine cellularity, the volume of cells is compared to the volume of other components of the bone marrow, whether the different cell lineages are present in adequate numbers and if there are any abnormal infiltrates in the marrow. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_BONEBIOPSY_TEST.name: Bone Marrow Biopsy Test
EXM_CRP_BONEBIOPSY_TEST.description: A bone marrow biopsy collects a cylindrical core sample that preserves the marrow's structure. The biopsy sample is evaluated by a specialist to determine cellularity, the volume of cells is compared to the volume of other components of the bone marrow, whether the different cell lineages are present in adequate numbers and if there are any abnormal infiltrates in the marrow.

EXM_CRP_COLONOSCOPY_BIOPSY_SAMPLE.name: Colonoscopy Biopsy Sampling
EXM_CRP_COLONOSCOPY_BIOPSY_SAMPLE.description: A colonoscopy is a medical procedure that is done to look for cancer, colon polyps or other abnormalities inside your bowel. Colonoscopy is done using an instrument called a colonoscope (a type of endoscope) — a long, thin, flexible tube containing a camera and a light. During the colonoscopy, the doctor passes an instrument through the colonoscope to obtain a biopsy (a small sample of the colon lining) to be further analysed.

EXM_CRP_COLONOSCOPY_BIOPSY_TEST.name: Colonoscopy Biopsy Testing
EXM_CRP_COLONOSCOPY_BIOPSY_TEST.description: A colonoscopy is a medical procedure that is done to look for cancer, colon polyps or other abnormalities inside your bowel. Colonoscopy is done using an instrument called a colonoscope (a type of endoscope) — a long, thin, flexible tube containing a camera and a light. During the colonoscopy, the doctor passes an instrument through the colonoscope to obtain a biopsy (a small sample of the colon lining) to be further analysed.

EXM_CRP_FNA_SAMPLE.name: Fine Needle Aspiration Sample
EXM_CRP_FNA_SAMPLE.description: Fine needle aspiration is a quicker, less painful biopsy procedure to remove tissue, cell or fluid samples. In fine needle aspiration, a thin needle is inserted into an area of abnormal-appearing tissue or body fluid. As with other types of biopsies, the sample collected during fine needle aspiration can help make a diagnosis or rule out conditions such as cancer. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_FNA_TEST.name: Fine Needle Aspiration Test
EXM_CRP_FNA_TEST.description: Fine needle aspiration is a quicker, less painful biopsy procedure to remove tissue, cell or fluid samples. In fine needle aspiration, a thin needle is inserted into an area of abnormal-appearing tissue or body fluid. As with other types of biopsies, the sample collected during fine needle aspiration can be used to help support a diagnosis.

EXM_CRP_FCU_SAMPLING.name: Pap Smear Sampling
EXM_CRP_FCU_SAMPLING.description: A method for the early detection of cancer especially of the uterine cervix that involves the staining of exfoliated cells using a special technique which differentiates diseased tissue.

EXM_CRP_FCU_TESTING.name: Pap Smear Testing
EXM_CRP_FCU_TESTING.description: A method for the early detection of cancer especially of the uterine cervix that involves the staining of exfoliated cells using a special technique which differentiates diseased tissue.

EXM_CRP_LIVER_BIOPSY_SAMPLE.name: Liver Biopsy Sampling
EXM_CRP_LIVER_BIOPSY_SAMPLE.description: A liver biopsy is a procedure in which a small needle is inserted into the liver to collect a tissue sample. The tissue is then analysed in a laboratory to help doctors diagnose a variety of disorders and diseases in the liver. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_LIVER_BIOPSY_TEST.name: Liver Biopsy Testing
EXM_CRP_LIVER_BIOPSY_TEST.description: A liver biopsy is a procedure in which a small needle is inserted into the liver to collect a tissue sample. The tissue is then analysed in a laboratory to help doctors diagnose a variety of disorders and diseases in the liver.

EXM_CRP_OVARIAN_BIOPSY_SAMPLE.name: Ovarian Biopsy Sample
EXM_CRP_OVARIAN_BIOPSY_SAMPLE.description: There is currently no effective screening test for ovarian cancer. When a patient is suspected to have ovarian cancer the doctor will perform various tests to find any abnormalities that need to be followed up with a biopsy. The only way to confirm a diagnosis of ovarian cancer is by taking a tissue sample (biopsy) and looking at the cells under a microscope. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_OVARIAN_BIOPSY_TEST.name: Ovarian Biopsy Test
EXM_CRP_OVARIAN_BIOPSY_TEST.description: Histology technicians are currently working with the sample provided by the patient. They should return their results soon.

EXM_CRP_VAGINAL_BIOPSY_SAMPLE.name: Vaginal Biopsy Sample
EXM_CRP_VAGINAL_BIOPSY_SAMPLE.description: Certain signs and symptoms may strongly suggest vaginal cancer, but many of them can be caused by other problems. The only way to be certain that it's cancer is to do a biopsy. In this procedure, a small piece of tissue from the suspicious area is removed. A technician specializing in diagnosing diseases with lab tests (Histology Lab Technician) will then look at the tissue sample under a microscope to see if cancer or a pre-cancerous condition is present and, if so, what type it is. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_VAGINAL_BIOPSY_TEST.name: Vaginal Biopsy Test
EXM_CRP_VAGINAL_BIOPSY_TEST.description: Histology technicians are currently working with the sample provided by the patient. They should return their results soon.

EXM_CRP_SALIVARYGLAND_BIOPSY_SAMPLE.name: Salivary Gland Biopsy Sample
EXM_CRP_SALIVARYGLAND_BIOPSY_SAMPLE.description: A salivary gland biopsy involves the removal of cells or small pieces of tissue from one or more salivary glands in order to be examined in the laboratory. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_CRP_SALIVARYGLAND_BIOPSY_TEST.name: Salivary Gland Biopsy Test
EXM_CRP_SALIVARYGLAND_BIOPSY_TEST.description: Histology technicians are currently working with the sample provided by the patient. They should return their results soon.

EXM_OL_C1_BLOOD_SAMPLE.name: C1 Blood Sampling
EXM_OL_C1_BLOOD_SAMPLE.description: C1-inhibitor is a protease inhibitor belonging to the serpin superfamily. Its main function is the inhibition of the complement system to prevent spontaneous activation but also as the major regulator of the contact system. \n\nRequired Lab: Hematology

EXM_OL_C1_BLOOD_TEST.name: C1 Blood Testing
EXM_OL_C1_BLOOD_TEST.description: Hematology technicians are currently working with the sample provided by the patient. They should return their results soon.

EXM_OL_PORPHYRIA_URINE_SAMPLE.name: Porphyria Urine Sampling
EXM_OL_PORPHYRIA_URINE_SAMPLE.description: A raised urine PBG is the diagnostic hallmark of an acute attack of porphyria. \n\nRequired Lab: Histology

EXM_OL_PORPHYRIA_URINE_TEST.name: Porphyria Urine Testing
EXM_OL_PORPHYRIA_URINE_TEST.description: Microbiology technicians are currently working with the sample provided by the patient. They should return their results soon.

EXM_OL_MANOMETER.name: Manometer
EXM_OL_MANOMETER.description: Compartment pressures are often measured with a manometer, a device that detects intracompartmental pressure by measuring the resistance that is present when a saline solution is injected into the compartment. \n\nRequired room: Any ward / unit

EXM_OL_ESOPHAGEAL_MANOMETER.name: Esophageal Manometer
EXM_OL_ESOPHAGEAL_MANOMETER.description: This test involves placing a thin, pressure-sensitive tube through your nose into your esophagus to measure pressure as you swallow. \n\nRequired room: Any diagnostic unit

EXM_JM_OAE.name: Otoacoustic Emissions
EXM_JM_OAE.description: The OAE (Otoacoustic Emissions) test checks part of the inner ear's response to sound. The test is mostly done on infants and children who may not be able to respond to behavioral hearing tests because of their age. \n\nRequired room: ENT Office at ENT

EXM_JM_AUDIOGRAM.name: Audiogram
EXM_JM_AUDIOGRAM.description: An audiogram is a graph that shows the audible threshold for standardized frequencies as measured by an audiometer. The Y axis represents intensity measured in decibels and the X axis represents frequency measured in hertz. \n\nRequired room: Audiogram Unit at ENT

EXM_JM_OTOSCOPY.name: Otoscopy
EXM_JM_OTOSCOPY.description: Otoscopy is a clinical procedure used to examine structures of the ear, particularly the external auditory canal, tympanic membrane, and middle ear. Clinicians use the process during routine wellness physical exams, examination for any diagnostic clues and the evaluation of specific ear complaints. \n\nRequired room: Endoscopy Unit at ENT

EXM_JM_EAR_BIOPSY_SAMPLING.name: Ear Biopsy - Sampling
EXM_JM_EAR_BIOPSY_SAMPLING.description:
A ear biopsy is a surgical procedure, where a sample of the ear canal is extracted under surgical anesthesia. This is usually done to find out what a ear tumour is made of. 

Sampling: Any office / Any diagnostic unit 

Required room: ENT Lab at ENT 

Required equipment: Glassware + Microscope

EXM_JM_EAR_BIOPSY_TESTING.name: Ear Biopsy - Testing
EXM_JM_EAR_BIOPSY_TESTING.description:
A ear biopsy is a surgical procedure, where a sample of the ear canal is extracted under surgical anesthesia. This is usually done to find out what a ear tumour is made of. 

Sampling: Any office / Any diagnostic unit 

Required room: ENT Lab at ENT 

Required equipment: Glassware + Microscope

EXM_JM_RHINOSCOPY.name: Rhinoscopy
EXM_JM_RHINOSCOPY.description: Rhinoscopy is a method of looking into the nose and throat. While there are other methods of looking into the nose, these methods only allow the doctor to see about an inch inside the nasal cavity. A rhinoscopy allows the doctor to see almost all of the inside of the nose, throat, adenoids, and vocal cords. \n\nRequired room: Endoscopy Unit at ENT

EXM_JM_NOSE_BIOPSY_SAMPLING.name: Nose Biopsy - Sampling
EXM_JM_NOSE_BIOPSY_SAMPLING.description:
A nose biopsy is a surgical procedure, where a sample of the nasal cavity is extracted under surgical anesthesia. This is usually done to find out what a nose tumour is made of. 

Sampling: Any office / Any diagnostic unit 

Required room: ENT Lab at ENT 

Required equipment: Glassware + Microscope

EXM_JM_NOSE_BIOPSY_TESTING.name: Nose Biopsy - Test
EXM_JM_NOSE_BIOPSY_TESTING.description:
A nose biopsy is a surgical procedure, where a sample of the nasal cavity is extracted under surgical anesthesia. This is usually done to find out what a nose tumour is made of. 

Sampling: Any office / Any diagnostic unit 

Required room: ENT Lab at ENT 

Required equipment: Glassware + Microscope

EXM_JM_LARYNGOSCOPY.name: Advanced Laryngoscopy
EXM_JM_LARYNGOSCOPY.description: A procedure where a flexible tube (endoscope) with a tiny video camera and a light source in the end is inserted through the nose to examine the larynx, glottis, and vocal chords. Laryngoscopy is also used to facilitate tracheal intubation. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Trauma center / ICU

EXM_JM_THROAT_BIOPSY_SAMPLING.name: Throat Biopsy - Sampling
EXM_JM_THROAT_BIOPSY_SAMPLING.description:
A throat biopsy is a surgical procedure, where a sample of the throat is extracted under surgical anesthesia. This is usually done to find out what a throat tumour is made of. 

Sampling: Any office / Any diagnostic unit 

Required room: ENT Lab at ENT 

Required equipment: Glassware + Microscope

EXM_JM_THROAT_BIOPSY_TESTING.name: Throat Biopsy - Test
EXM_JM_THROAT_BIOPSY_TESTING.description:
A throat biopsy is a surgical procedure, where a sample of the throat is extracted under surgical anesthesia. This is usually done to find out what a throat tumour is made of. 

Sampling: Any office / Any diagnostic unit 

Required room: ENT Lab at ENT 

Required equipment: Glassware + Microscope

EXM_JM_RAPID_STREP_SAMPLING.name: Rapid Strep Test - Sampling
EXM_JM_RAPID_STREP_SAMPLING.description:
The rapid strep test (RST) is a rapid antigen detection test (RADT) that is widely used in clinics to assist in the diagnosis of bacterial pharyngitis caused by group A streptococci (GAS), sometimes termed strep throat. There are currently several types of rapid strep test in use, each employing a distinct technology. However, they all work by detecting the presence of GAS in the throat of a person by responding to GAS-specific antigens on a throat swab. 

Sampling: Any office / Any diagnostic unit 

Required room: ENT Lab at ENT 

Required equipment: Glassware + Fume hood + incubator + Microscope

EXM_JM_RAPID_STREP_TESTING.name: Rapid Strep Test - Testing
EXM_JM_RAPID_STREP_TESTING.description:
The rapid strep test (RST) is a rapid antigen detection test (RADT) that is widely used in clinics to assist in the diagnosis of bacterial pharyngitis caused by group A streptococci (GAS), sometimes termed strep throat. There are currently several types of rapid strep test in use, each employing a distinct technology. However, they all work by detecting the presence of GAS in the throat of a person by responding to GAS-specific antigens on a throat swab. 

Sampling: Any office / Any diagnostic unit 

Required room: ENT Lab at ENT 

Required equipment: Glassware + Fume hood + incubator + Microscope

EXM_JM_SPUTUM_CULTURE_SAMPLING.name: Sputum Culture - Sampling
EXM_JM_SPUTUM_CULTURE_SAMPLING.description:
A sputum culture is a test to detect and identify bacteria or fungi that infect the lungs or breathing passages. Sputum is a thick fluid produced in the lungs and in the adjacent airways. ... If no bacteria or fungi grow, the culture is negative. 

Sampling: Any office / Any diagnostic unit 

Required room: ENT Lab at ENT 

Required equipment: Glassware + Fume hood + incubator + Microscope

EXM_JM_SPUTUM_CULTURE_TESTING.name: Sputum Culture - Testing
EXM_JM_SPUTUM_CULTURE_TESTING.description:
A sputum culture is a test to detect and identify bacteria or fungi that infect the lungs or breathing passages. Sputum is a thick fluid produced in the lungs and in the adjacent airways. ... If no bacteria or fungi grow, the culture is negative. 

Sampling: Any office / Any diagnostic unit 

Required room: ENT Lab at ENT 

Required equipment: Glassware + Fume hood + incubator + Microscope

EXM_JM_THROAT_SWAB_SAMPLING.name: Throat Swab
EXM_JM_THROAT_SWAB_SAMPLING.description:
A throat swab culture, or throat culture, is a test commonly used to diagnose bacterial infections in the throat. These infections can include strep throat, pneumonia, diphtheria, tonsillitis, whooping cough, and meningitis. The purpose of a throat swab culture is to detect the presence of organisms in the throat that could cause infection. 

Sampling: Any office / Any diagnostic unit 

Required room: ENT Lab at ENT 

Required equipment: Glassware + Fume hood + incubator + Microscope

EXM_JM_THROAT_SWAB_TESTING.name: Throat Swab Culture
EXM_JM_THROAT_SWAB_TESTING.description:
A throat swab culture, or throat culture, is a test commonly used to diagnose bacterial infections in the throat. These infections can include strep throat, pneumonia, diphtheria, tonsillitis, whooping cough, and meningitis. The purpose of a throat swab culture is to detect the presence of organisms in the throat that could cause infection. 

Sampling: Any office / Any diagnostic unit 

Required room: ENT Lab at ENT 

Required equipment: Glassware + Fume hood + incubator + Microscope

EXM_JM_NASAL_SWAB_SAMPLING.name: Nasal Swab
EXM_JM_NASAL_SWAB_SAMPLING.description:
A nasal swab, is a test that checks for viruses and bacteria that cause respiratory infections. There are many types of respiratory infections. A nasal swab test can help your provider diagnose the type of infection you have and which treatment would be best for you. The test may be done by taking a sample of cells from your nostrils or from the nasopharynx. The nasopharynx is the uppermost part of your nose and throat. 

Sampling: Any office / Any diagnostic unit 

Required room: ENT Lab at ENT 

Required equipment: Glassware + Fume hood + incubator + Microscope

EXM_JM_NASAL_SWAB_TESTING.name: Nasal Swab Culture
EXM_JM_NASAL_SWAB_TESTING.description:
A nasal swab, is a test that checks for viruses and bacteria that cause respiratory infections. There are many types of respiratory infections. A nasal swab test can help your provider diagnose the type of infection you have and which treatment would be best for you. The test may be done by taking a sample of cells from your nostrils or from the nasopharynx. The nasopharynx is the uppermost part of your nose and throat. 

Sampling: Any office / Any diagnostic unit 

Required room: ENT Lab at ENT 

Required equipment: Glassware + Fume hood + incubator + Microscope

EXM_SH_GENITAL_SCREENING_SAMPLING.name: Genital Screening
EXM_SH_GENITAL_SCREENING_SAMPLING.description: A small sampler is used to gently take a small number of cells from the cervix or anus. The cells are sent to a lab to be tested.

EXM_SH_GENITAL_SWAB_SAMPLING.name: Genital Swab
EXM_SH_GENITAL_SWAB_SAMPLING.description: A swab is taken from either the vagina or penis.

EXM_SH_GENITAL_SWAB_TESTING.name: Genital Swab Analysis
EXM_SH_GENITAL_SWAB_TESTING.description: The swab culture is analysed to check for signs of infection.

EXM_SH_SEMEN_SAMPLE_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.name: Semen Collection
EXM_SH_SEMEN_SAMPLE_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.description: Semen collection refers to the process of obtaining semen from the patient, usually through masturbation, directing the sample into a clean cup.

EXM_SH_GROIN_EXAMINATION.name: Groin Examination
EXM_SH_GROIN_EXAMINATION.description: Examination of the patient's groin area.

EXM_KN_INORCHIECTOMY.name: Inguinal Orchiectomy
EXM_KN_INORCHIECTOMY.description: Inguinal orchiectomy is a specific method of orchiectomy whereby one or both testicles and the full spermatic cord are surgically removed through an incision in the lower lateral abdomen. The procedure is generally performed by a urologist, typically if testicular cancer is suspected.

EXM_KN_INORCHIECTOMY_TEST.name: Testing the Obtained Testicle in Inguinal Orchiectomy
EXM_KN_INORCHIECTOMY_TEST.description: Testing the obtained testicle in inguinal orchiectomy.

EXM_KN_TESTIS_PALP.name: Testis Palpation
EXM_KN_TESTIS_PALP.description: Palpation of the scrotum and its contents will reveal the presence, size, position, and shape of the testicles and their adnexa. The normal testicle is ovoid, smooth, firm, and mildly tender to palpation. The testicle is easily separated from the epididymis, which lies posterior and slightly lateral to the testicle.

EXM_NEW_PSY_OREXIN_IN_CEREBROSPINAL_FLUI.name: Orexin in Cerebrospinal Fluid Test
EXM_NEW_PSY_OREXIN_IN_CEREBROSPINAL_FLUI.description: By sampling cerebrospinal fluid, this test determines whether the concentration of orexin-1 in the cerebrospinal fluid is ≤100 pg/ml or lower than 1/3 of the normal value, used to diagnose typical episodes of narcolepsy with cataplexy. It is particularly beneficial for patients who are difficult to undergo MSLT due to cost or inability to cooperate with the examination, and who are using psychotropic drugs and are difficult to discontinue.

EXM_NEW_PSY_MSLT.name: Multiple Sleep Latency Test (MSLT)
EXM_NEW_PSY_MSLT.description: Requires advanced diagnosis. Multiple Sleep Latency Test (MSLT) is the most commonly used standard protocol for objectively evaluating daytime sleepiness in clinical practice and research, often used for narcolepsy or sleep-related breathing disorders.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSG.name: Polysomnography（PSG）
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSG.description: Requires advanced diagnosis. Polysomnography (PSG) system monitors breathing, arterial oxygen saturation, electroencephalogram (EEG), electrocardiogram (ECG), heart rate, and other indicators continuously during the night to detect sleep abnormalities and diagnose sleep disorders, especially sleep-related breathing disorders. Although PSG can reflect the condition of patients with chronic insomnia, the diagnosis of chronic insomnia mainly relies on the subjective report of patients. PSG is only used as a means to exclude other sleep disorders, rather than a routine examination for insomnia.

EXM_NEW_PSY_DIFFERENTIAL_DIAGNOSIS.name: Psychiatric Differential Diagnosis
EXM_NEW_PSY_DIFFERENTIAL_DIAGNOSIS.description: Special differential diagnosis conducted by a small number of highly experienced psychiatric experts.

EXM_NEW_PSY_INTERVIEW_SLEEP.name: Psychiatric Exam - Sleep
EXM_NEW_PSY_INTERVIEW_SLEEP.description: Understanding the patient's sleep through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_BDI.name: Beck's Depression Inventory (BDI)
EXM_NEW_PSY_BDI.description: Beck Depression Inventory (BDI) is a self-rating scale developed by the renowned American psychologist Beck in the 1960s. It is available in two versions: BDI-13 and BDI-21. Considering that severely depressed patients may have difficulty completing 21 items, Beck introduced BDI-13 in 1973, which is now widely used.

EXM_NEW_PSY_SAS.name: Anxiety Self-Rating Scale (SAS)
EXM_NEW_PSY_SAS.description: The Anxiety Self-Rating Scale was developed by Zung in 1971. Similar to the Self-Rating Depression Scale (SDS), it consists of 20 items used to assess the subjective feelings of anxious patients, with good validity.

EXM_NEW_PSY_AIS.name: Athens Insomnia Scale (AIS)
EXM_NEW_PSY_AIS.description: The Athens Insomnia Scale (AIS), is an internationally recognized self-assessment tool for measuring sleep quality, widely used.

EXM_NEW_PSY_MMSE.name: Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE)
EXM_NEW_PSY_MMSE.description: Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE) was developed by Folstein et al. in 1975. It is currently the most widely used and commonly employed cognitive screening tool in the world. It is simple to administer and has good reliability and validity.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_CONS.name: Psychiatric Exam - Consciousness
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_CONS.description: Understanding the patient's level of consciousness through psychiatric exam

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_THOU.name: Psychiatric Exam - Appetite
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_THOU.description: Understanding the patient's appetite through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_PERS.name: Psychiatric Exam - Personality
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_PERS.description: Understanding the patient's personality through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_MEMO.name: Psychiatric Exam - Memory
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_MEMO.description: Understanding the patient's memory through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_PERC.name: Psychiatric Exam - Perception
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_PERC.description: Understanding the patient's perception through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_EMOT.name: Psychiatric Exam - Emotion
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_EMOT.description: Understanding the patient's emotion through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_WILL.name: Psychiatric Exam - Will
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_WILL.description: Understanding the patient's will through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_MOVE.name: Psychiatric Exam - Movement
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_MOVE.description: Understanding the patient's movement through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_THOU.name: Psychiatric Exam - Thoughts
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_THOU.description: Understanding the patient's thoughts through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_ATTE.name: Psychiatric Exam - Attention
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_ATTE.description: Understanding the patient's attention through psychiatric exam.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_SUIC.name: Psychiatric Exam - Suicide and Self-Harm
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION_SUIC.description: Understanding whether patient has suicidal and self-harm behaviors through psychiatric exam

EXM_JM_MEDICAL_HISTORY.name: Family and Medical History
EXM_JM_MEDICAL_HISTORY.description: Medical history: In clinical medicine, the patient's past and present which may contain relevant information bearing on their health past, present, and future. The medical history, being an account of all medical events and problems a person has experienced is an important tool in the management of the patient..

EXM_MOREDG_FREIN_SAMPLING.name: Dexamethasone Suppression Test
EXM_MOREDG_FREIN_SAMPLING.description: The dexamethasone suppression test (DST) is used to assess adrenal gland function by measuring how cortisol levels change in response to an injection of dexamethasone. It is typically used to diagnose Cushing's syndrome.

EXM_MOREDG_FREIN_TESTING.name: Dexamethasone Suppression Test - Testing
EXM_MOREDG_FREIN_TESTING.description: The dexamethasone suppression test (DST) is used to assess adrenal gland function by measuring how cortisol levels change in response to an injection of dexamethasone. It is typically used to diagnose Cushing's syndrome.

EXM_MOREDG_SYNACTHENE_SAMPLING.name: ACTH Stimulation Test
EXM_MOREDG_SYNACTHENE_SAMPLING.description: The ACTH test (also called the cosyntropin, tetracosactide, or Synacthen test) is a medical test usually ordered and interpreted by endocrinologists to assess the functioning of the adrenal glands's stress response by measuring the adrenal response to adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH; corticotropin) or another corticotropic agent such as tetracosactide (cosyntropin, tetracosactrin; Synacthen) or alsactide (Synchrodyn). ACTH is a hormone produced in the anterior pituitary gland that stimulates the adrenal glands to release cortisol, dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA), dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate (DHEA-S), and aldosterone. During the test, a small amount of synthetic ACTH is injected, and the amount of cortisol (and sometimes aldosterone) that the adrenals produce in response is measured. This test may cause mild side effects in some individuals.

EXM_MOREDG_SYNACTHENE_TESTING.name: ACTH Stimulation Test - Testing
EXM_MOREDG_SYNACTHENE_TESTING.description: The ACTH test (also called the cosyntropin, tetracosactide, or Synacthen test) is a medical test usually ordered and interpreted by endocrinologists to assess the functioning of the adrenal glands's stress response by measuring the adrenal response to adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH; corticotropin) or another corticotropic agent such as tetracosactide (cosyntropin, tetracosactrin; Synacthen) or alsactide (Synchrodyn). ACTH is a hormone produced in the anterior pituitary gland that stimulates the adrenal glands to release cortisol, dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA), dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate (DHEA-S), and aldosterone. During the test, a small amount of synthetic ACTH is injected, and the amount of cortisol (and sometimes aldosterone) that the adrenals produce in response is measured. This test may cause mild side effects in some individuals.

EXM_MOREDG_TEST_COMBS_SAMPLING.name: Coombs Test
EXM_MOREDG_TEST_COMBS_SAMPLING.description: A Coombs test, also known as antiglobulin test (AGT) is either of two blood tests used in immunohematology. They are the direct and indirect Coombs tests. The direct Coombs test detects antibodies that are stuck to the surface of the red blood cells.

EXM_MOREDG_TEST_COMBS_TESTING.name: Coombs Test - Testing
EXM_MOREDG_TEST_COMBS_TESTING.description: A Coombs test, also known as antiglobulin test (AGT) is either of two blood tests used in immunohematology. They are the direct and indirect Coombs tests. The direct Coombs test detects antibodies that are stuck to the surface of the red blood cells.

EXM_WOOD_LAMP_EXAM.name: Wood's Lamp Examination
EXM_WOOD_LAMP_EXAM.description: A Wood's lamp examination is a non-invasive test that uses ultraviolet light (365nm) to observe skin pigment abnormalities and the fluorescence of specific pathogens. It is suitable for assessing hyper/hypopigmentation, vitiligo, and the extent of certain fungal infections.

EXM_DERMOSCOPY.name: Dermoscopy
EXM_DERMOSCOPY.description: Dermoscopy uses a dermatoscope for magnified and polarized light observation at the epidermal level, enhancing the identification of pigmented lesions, vascular patterns, and structures like scales and keratin plugs. It aids in the early detection of melanoma and the classification of inflammatory dermatoses.

EXM_KOH_WET_MOUNT_SAMPLING.name: KOH Wet Mount Sampling/preparation
EXM_KOH_WET_MOUNT_SAMPLING.description: KOH wet mount sampling/preparation involves scraping skin scales, hair, or nail clippings, treating them with 10-20% potassium hydroxide, and then examining them under a microscope to quickly detect dermatophyte hyphae or budding yeast.

EXM_KOH_WET_MOUNT_TESTING.name: KOH Wet Mount Examination
EXM_KOH_WET_MOUNT_TESTING.description: The KOH wet mount is examined under a microscope to detect signs of fungal infection.

EXM_DIF_SAMPLING.name: Direct Immunofluorescence Sampling
EXM_DIF_SAMPLING.description: Direct immunofluorescence sampling involves taking a skin biopsy from the edge of a lesion or next to a fresh blister for direct immunofluorescence to detect deposits of IgA/IgG/complement, aiding in the differentiation of various autoimmune blistering/inflammatory diseases.

EXM_DIF_TESTING.name: Direct Immunofluorescence Examination
EXM_DIF_TESTING.description: The skin sample is examined under a microscope to detect deposits of IgA/IgG/complement.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION.name: Psychiatric Examination
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHIATRIC_EXAMINATION.description: A psychiatric examination is a systematic evaluation of a patient's mental state conducted by a psychiatrist. It primarily includes the assessment of areas such as consciousness, attention, thought processes, mood and affect, volition, perception, and memory.

EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOMETRIC_SCALES.name: Psychometric Scales
EXM_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOMETRIC_SCALES.description: Psychometric scales are tools used by psychiatrists for the systematic psychological assessment of a patient. They primarily include measures such as the Self-Rating Depression Scale, Self-Rating Anxiety Scale, and the Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE).

EXM_HEMOGLOBIN_ADVANCED_BLOOD_TESTING.name: Advanced Blood Component Analysis
EXM_HEMOGLOBIN_ADVANCED_BLOOD_TESTING.description: Advanced blood component analysis includes tests for serum vitamin B12, folate, ferritin, coagulation factor activity, and von Willebrand factor antigen.

EXM_HEMOGLOBIN_ADVANCED_BLOOD_SAMPLING.name: Advanced Blood Component Analysis - Sampling
EXM_HEMOGLOBIN_ADVANCED_BLOOD_SAMPLING.description: Advanced blood component analysis includes tests for serum vitamin B12, folate, ferritin, coagulation factor activity, and von Willebrand factor antigen.

EXM_SLIT_LAMP_EXAM.name: Slit-Lamp Examination
EXM_SLIT_LAMP_EXAM.description: Uses a slit lamp to observe signs in anterior segment structures like the cornea, conjunctiva, anterior chamber, iris, and lens.

EXM_FLUORESCEIN_STAIN.name: Corneal Fluorescein Staining
EXM_FLUORESCEIN_STAIN.description: Uses fluorescein stain to observe corneal epithelial defects and measure tear film break-up time.

EXM_SCHIRMER_TEST.name: Schirmer's Test
EXM_SCHIRMER_TEST.description: Measures basal and reflex tear secretion using filter paper strips to evaluate dry eye.

EXM_AMSLER_GRID.name: Amsler Grid Test
EXM_AMSLER_GRID.description: Screens for macular dysfunction by checking for distortion or missing areas in a grid pattern.

EXM_OCT.name: Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT)
EXM_OCT.description: Non-contact tomographic imaging to assess macular edema, retinal nerve fiber layer thickness, etc.

EXM_FA.name: Fundus Fluorescein Angiography (FFA)
EXM_FA.description: Involves intravenous injection of fluorescein followed by fundus photography to assess for leakage, ischemia, and neovascularization.

EXM_DEN_LASER_FLUORESCENCE_DETECTION.name: Caries Fluorescence Detection
EXM_DEN_LASER_FLUORESCENCE_DETECTION.description: Using a specific wavelength of laser to illuminate the tooth surface to quantitatively analyze the mineral content of the tooth by detecting the fluorescence reaction, aiding in the diagnosis of early caries.

EXM_DEN_PULP_VITALITY_TEST.name: Pulp Vitality Test
EXM_DEN_PULP_VITALITY_TEST.description: Assessing the responsiveness of the pulp nerve to external stimuli by applying cold, hot, or electrical stimulation to the tooth to determine if the pulp is healthy, inflamed, or necrotic.

EXM_DEN_PERIODONTAL_PROBING.name: Periodontal Probing
EXM_DEN_PERIODONTAL_PROBING.description: Using a calibrated periodontal probe to measure the depth of the gingival sulcus or periodontal pocket and check for bleeding on probing to assess periodontal health and attachment loss.

EXM_DEN_SIALOGRAPHY.name: Sialography
EXM_DEN_SIALOGRAPHY.description: Injecting contrast medium into the salivary duct system followed by X-ray imaging. This examination clearly shows the morphology and structure of the ducts and any obstructions, used to diagnose sialoliths, strictures, or tumors.

EXM_DEN_TMJ_PALPATION.name: TMJ Palpation
EXM_DEN_TMJ_PALPATION.description: The doctor places fingers over the patient's TMJ area to feel and assess for tenderness, clicking, crepitus, or abnormal movements during jaw opening and closing.

EXM_DEN_MAX_MOUTH_OPENING_MEASUREMENT.name: Maximum Mouth Opening Measurement
EXM_DEN_MAX_MOUTH_OPENING_MEASUREMENT.description: Using a ruler or caliper to measure the vertical distance between the incisal edges of the upper and lower incisors when the patient opens their mouth as wide as possible, to objectively assess the range of mandibular motion.

EXM_DEN_MASTICATORY_MUSCLE_PALPATION.name: Masticatory Muscle Palpation
EXM_DEN_MASTICATORY_MUSCLE_PALPATION.description: The doctor systematically palpates the patient's masticatory muscles (e.g., masseter, temporalis) to locate tender points, trigger points, or taut bands, to diagnose conditions like myofascial pain.

TRT_CRP_IMIQUIMOD_CREAM.name: Imiquimod Cream
TRT_CRP_IMIQUIMOD_CREAM.description: Imiquimod is a prescription cream that acts as an immune response modifier that is used to treat superficial basal cell carcinoma and actinic keratosis. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_PHOTODYNAMIC_THERAPY.name: Photodynamic Therapy
TRT_CRP_PHOTODYNAMIC_THERAPY.description: Photodynamic therapy is a treatment that uses certain drugs called photosensitizing agents, along with a special light, to kill cancer cells. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_ESTRADIOL_CREAM.name: Estradiol Cream
TRT_CRP_ESTRADIOL_CREAM.description: Estradiol cream is a preparation of estrogen, a female hormone, for topical application to the vaginal area used to treat certain symptoms of menopause such as dryness, burning, and itching of the vaginal area and urgency or irritation with urination. \n\nRequired room: Any office.

TRT_CRP_ANTI_ACNEE.name: Anti-Acneic
TRT_CRP_ANTI_ACNEE.description: Medicine used to treat various types and forms of acne.

TRT_CRP_MOISTURIZER_CREAM.name: Moisturizer Cream
TRT_CRP_MOISTURIZER_CREAM.description: A cosmetic preparation used to prevent dryness in the skin.

TRT_CRP_IMMUNOTHERAPY_DRUGS.name: Immunotherapy
TRT_CRP_IMMUNOTHERAPY_DRUGS.description: Immunotherapy is a type of cancer treatment which assists the body's immune system to fight cancer. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_TARGETED_THERAPY.name: Targeted Therapy
TRT_CRP_TARGETED_THERAPY.description: Targeted therapies are drugs or other substances that block the growth and spread of cancer by interfering with specific molecules. Targeted therapies differ from standard chemotherapy because they are designed to act on specific molecular targets and attack cancer cells without harming healthy cells. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_MITOTANE.name: Mitotane
TRT_CRP_MITOTANE.description: Mitotane, sold under the brand name Lysodren, is a steroidogenesis inhibitor and cytostatic antineoplastic medication which is used in the treatment of adrenocortical carcinoma and Cushing's syndrome. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_SPIRONOLACTONE.name: Spironolactone
TRT_CRP_SPIRONOLACTONE.description: Spironolactone is a potassium-sparing diuretic (water pill) that prevents your body from absorbing too much salt and keeps your potassium levels from getting too low. Spironolactone is used to treat heart failure, high blood pressure (hypertension), or hypokalemia (low potassium levels in the blood). \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_BRAF_INH.name: BRAF Inhibitors
TRT_CRP_BRAF_INH.description: BRAF Inhibitors help block the BRAF-V600E gene mutation. In clinical trials, B-Raf increased metastatic melanoma patient chance of survival. In spite of the drug's high efficacy, 20% of tumors still develop resistance to the treatment. \n\nRequired room: Any office.

TRT_CRP_TAMOXIFEN.name: Tamoxifen
TRT_CRP_TAMOXIFEN.description: Tamoxifen is a hormone therapy for breast cancer in both women and men. It lowers the risk of early breast cancer coming back (recurring) after surgery or developing in the other breast. It can also control advanced breast cancer for some time. \n\nRequired room: Any office.

TRT_CRP_HORMONE_THERAPY.name: Hormone Therapy
TRT_CRP_HORMONE_THERAPY.description: Hormone therapy or hormonal therapy is the use of hormones in medical treatment. \n\nRequired room: Any office.

TRT_CRP_POCT.name: POC Combination Treatment
TRT_CRP_POCT.description: Pamidronate and Octreotide Combination Treatment. Isolated reports suggest that pamidronate and octreotide may help in relieving bone pain in some cases and assist in the treatment of hypertrophic pulmonary osteoarthropathy (HPOA). Treatment of the underlying problem is necessary to resolve the issue.  \n\nRequired room: Any office.

TRT_CRP_FEP.name: Dep
TRT_CRP_FEP.description: 5-Fluorouracil (5-FU), Cisplatin, and Etoposide (FEP) Combination Treatment. Chemotherapy has relatively poor curative efficacy in certain cancers such as Signet Ring Cell Carcinoma (SRCC) and patients and overall survival rates are lower compared to patients with more typical cancer pathology. These cancers are usually diagnosed during the late stages of the disease, so the tumors generally spread more aggressively than non-signet cancers, making treatment challenging but most active regimens include 5-Fluorouracil (5-FU), Cisplatin, and/or Etoposide. FEP combination chemotherapy seems to be an effective treatment regimen for gastric cancer as salvage chemotherapy. \n\nRequired room: Regular Hospitalisation at Oncology dpt.

TRT_CRP_OP.name: Estradiol-Progesterone Capsule
TRT_CRP_OP.description: This medication contains 2 female hormones: an estrogen and a progestin.

TRT_CRP_ANTI_ANDROGEN.name: Anti-Androgens
TRT_CRP_ANTI_ANDROGEN.description: Antiandrogen agents block androgen receptors, thereby inhibiting the effects of male sex hormones.

TRT_CRP_PROGESTATIF.name: Progestogen
TRT_CRP_PROGESTATIF.description: A naturally occurring or synthetic progestational steroid.

TRT_CRP_OESTROGENOTHERAPIE.name: Estrogen Replacement Therapy
TRT_CRP_OESTROGENOTHERAPIE.description: Hormone replacement therapy involving the administration of estrogen without progestin.

TRT_CRP_CHELATEUR_FER.name: Iron Chelators
TRT_CRP_CHELATEUR_FER.description: The goal of iron chelation therapy is to prevent iron-mediated injury to cells.

TRT_CRP_IMMUNOSUPRR.name: Immunosuppressive Drugs
TRT_CRP_IMMUNOSUPRR.description: Immunosuppressive drugs, also known as immunosuppressive agents, immunosuppressants and antirejection medications are drugs that inhibit or prevent activity of the immune system.

TRT_CRP_LACTULOSE.name: Lactulose
TRT_CRP_LACTULOSE.description: Lactulose is a prescription drug used by mouth or rectally to treat or prevent complications of liver disease (hepatic encephalopathy). It does not cure the problem, but may help to improve mental status. Lactulose is a colonic acidifier that works by decreasing the amount of ammonia in the blood. \n\nRequired room: Any office.

TRT_CRP_IMMUNOSUS_DRUGS.name: Immunosuppressive Drugs
TRT_CRP_IMMUNOSUS_DRUGS.description: Immunosuppressant drugs are a class of drugs that suppress, or reduce, the strength of the body’s immune system. Some of these drugs are used to make the body less likely to reject a transplanted organ, such as a liver, heart, or kidney. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

TRT_CRP_CORTICOSTEROID_NASAL_SPRAY.name: Corticosteroid Nasal Spray
TRT_CRP_CORTICOSTEROID_NASAL_SPRAY.description: Steroid nasal sprays, also called corticosteroid nasal sprays, are anti-inflammatory medicines that you spray into your nose.They can be used to treat a range of conditions, including hay fever, sinusitis, non-allergic rhinitis and nasal polyps.Some steroid nasal sprays are available to buy from pharmacies and shops, while others are only available on prescription. Steroid nasal drops are also available. \n\nRequired room: Otorhinolaryngology Diagnostic Unit / Otorhinolaryngology Doctor's Office / ICU / Observation Room

TRT_CRP_VOCAL_THERAPY.name: Vocal Therapy
TRT_CRP_VOCAL_THERAPY.description: Voice therapy is a program designed to reduce hoarseness through guided change in vocal behaviors and lifestyle changes. Voice therapy consists of a variety of tasks designed to eliminate harmful vocal behavior, shape healthy vocal behavior, and assist in vocal fold wound healing after surgery or injury.

TRT_CRP_INTUBATION.name: Intubation
TRT_CRP_INTUBATION.description: Intubation is the process of inserting a tube, called an endotracheal tube (ET), through the mouth and then into the airway. This is done so that a patient can be placed on a ventilator to assist with breathing during anesthesia, sedation, or severe illness.\n\nRequired room: Otorhinolaryngology Diagnostic Unit / Otorhinolaryngology Doctor's Office / ICU / Observation Room

TRT_CRP_CPAP.name: Continuous Positive Airway Pressure Machine
TRT_CRP_CPAP.description: CPAP is an effective treatment for moderate to severe obstructive sleep apnea.\n\nRequired room: Otorhinolaryngology Diagnostic Unit / Otorhinolaryngology Doctor's Office / ICU / Observation Room

TRT_CRP_HEARING_AIDS.name: Hearing Aids
TRT_CRP_HEARING_AIDS.description: A hearing aid is a device designed to improve hearing by making sound audible to a person with hearing loss. Hearing aids are classified as medical devices in most countries, and regulated by the respective regulations. Small audio amplifiers such as PSAPs or other plain sound reinforcing systems cannot be sold as "hearing aids". \n\nRequired room: Otorhinolaryngology Diagnostic Unit / Otorhinolaryngology Doctor's Office / ICU / Observation Room

TRT_CRP_MASSAGE.name: Massage
TRT_CRP_MASSAGE.description: Massage is the manipulation of the body's soft tissues. Massage techniques are commonly applied with hands, fingers, elbows, knees, forearms, feet, or a device. The purpose of massage is generally for the treatment of body stress or pain.\n\nRequired room: Otorhinolaryngology Diagnostic Unit / Otorhinolaryngology Doctor's Office / ICU / Observation Room

TRT_CRP_EPIPEN.name: Epinephrine Autoinjector
TRT_CRP_EPIPEN.description: An epinephrine autoinjector is a medical device for injecting a measured dose or doses of epinephrine by means of autoinjector technology. It is most often used for the treatment of anaphylaxis. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_OCTREOTIDE_INJECTION.name: Octreotide Injection
TRT_CRP_OCTREOTIDE_INJECTION.description: Octreotide is used for the treatment of growth hormone producing tumors (acromegaly and gigantism) and can assist in stabilising a patient suffering from a carcinoid crisis. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_AUTOGRAFT.name: Autograft
TRT_CRP_AUTOGRAFT.description: A graft of tissue from one point to another of the same individual's body.

TRT_CRP_ALLOGRAFT.name: Allograft
TRT_CRP_ALLOGRAFT.description: A tissue graft from a donor of the same species as the recipient but not genetically identical.

TRT_CRP_CHEMOTHERAPY.name: Chemotherapy
TRT_CRP_CHEMOTHERAPY.description: Chemotherapy is a type of cancer treatment that uses one or more anti-cancer drugs as part of a standardized chemotherapy regimen. Chemotherapy may be given with a curative intent, or it may aim to prolong life or to reduce symptoms. \n\nRequired room: Regular Hospitalisation at Oncology dpt.

TRT_CRP_ALEMTUZUMAB.name: Alemtuzumab IV
TRT_CRP_ALEMTUZUMAB.description: Alemtuzumab is a medication used to treat chronic lymphocytic leukemia and multiple sclerosis. In CLL, Alemtuzumab has been used as both a first line and second line treatment. In MS it is generally only recommended if other treatments have not worked. It is given by injection into a vein. \n\nRequired room: Regular Hospitalisation at Oncology dpt.

TRT_CRP_GEMCITABINE.name: Gemcitabine
TRT_CRP_GEMCITABINE.description: Gemcitabine, sold under the brand name Gemzar, among others, is a chemotherapy medication used to treat a number of types of cancer. These cancers include breast cancer, ovarian cancer, non-small cell lung cancer, pancreatic cancer, and bladder cancer. It is given by slow injection into a vein. \n\nRequired room: Regular Hospitalisation at Oncology dpt.

TRT_CRP_KCL.name: Potassium Salt
TRT_CRP_KCL.description: The most used is potassium chloride in micro-encapsulated form. It is an oral potassium supplement

TRT_CRP_HYDROCORTISONE.name: Hydrocortisone
TRT_CRP_HYDROCORTISONE.description: Hydrocortisone is the name for the hormone cortisol when supplied as a medication.

TRT_CRP_SCLEROTHERAPIE.name: Sclerotherapy
TRT_CRP_SCLEROTHERAPIE.description: It's the treatment of varicose blood vessels by the injection of an irritant which causes inflammation, coagulation of blood, and narrowing of the blood vessel wall.

TRT_CRP_IPP_IV.name: IV PPI
TRT_CRP_IPP_IV.description: Treatment with a high dose Proton Pump Inhibitor before gastroscopy decreases the need for endoscopic hemostasis.

TRT_CRP_INTRAVENOUS_IMMUNOGLOBULIN.name: IV Immunoglobulin
TRT_CRP_INTRAVENOUS_IMMUNOGLOBULIN.description: A purified blood product containing antibodies delivered by the intravenous (IV) route. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at Orthopaedy dpt / Neurology dpt / DID

TRT_CRP_AMMONUL.name: Ammonul
TRT_CRP_AMMONUL.description: Ammonul (sodium benzoate and sodium phenylacetate combination) is used to treat a condition caused by too much ammonia in the blood (hyperammonemia). \n\nRequired room: Regular Hospitalisation at Oncology dpt.

TRT_CRP_ACTIVE_MONITOR.name: Active Monitoring
TRT_CRP_ACTIVE_MONITOR.description: Active monitoring is when the oncologist recommends no treatment but monitors the patient's health with regular check-ups and blood tests. This is sometimes called “careful monitoring” or “watch and wait”. \n\nRequired room: Any Oncology office

TRT_CRP_SURVEILLANCE.name: Medical Care
