TRT_CRP_SURVEILLANCE.description: The provision of what is necessary for a person's health and well-being by a doctor, nurse, or other healthcare professional.

TRT_CRP_INCISION_AND_DRAINAGE.name: Incision and Drainage
TRT_CRP_INCISION_AND_DRAINAGE.description: Incision and drainage and clinical lancing are minor surgical procedures to release fuild or pressure built up under the skin. \n\nRequired room: Oncology Diagnostic Unit / Oncology Doctor's Office / ICU / Observation Room

TRT_CRP_RADIO_EMBOLISATION.name: Endovascular Embolization
TRT_CRP_RADIO_EMBOLISATION.description: In endovascular embolization, a catheter is inserts into a leg artery and threads it through blood vessels to injects an embolizing agent using X-ray imaging.

TRT_CRP_WATCH_WAIT.name: Watch and Wait
TRT_CRP_WATCH_WAIT.description: Watch and wait involves closely monitoring a patient's condition without giving any treatment until symptoms appear or change. Generally in these situations, letting the body naturally heal itself is the preffered approach as treatments may carry the risk of complications. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_COMPRESSION_GARMENT.name: Compression Garment
TRT_CRP_COMPRESSION_GARMENT.description: Wearing a compression garment after liposuction helps the body recover and increases the patients comfort during the healing process. A compression garment helps to correct overtime, baggy or irregular skin surface features caused by the surgery. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_INTRA_LIPIDEMULSION.name: Intravenous Lipid Emulsion
TRT_CRP_INTRA_LIPIDEMULSION.description: Lipid emulsion or fat emulsion refers to an emulsion of lipid for human intravenous use. It is often referred to by the brand name of the most commonly used version, Intralipid, which is an emulsion of soy bean oil, egg phospholipids and glycerin, and is available in 10%, 20% and 30% concentrations. Intravenous lipid emulsion is an established, effective treatment for local anesthetic-induced cardiovascular collapse. The predominant theory for its mechanism of action is that by creating an expanded, intravascular lipid phase, equilibria are established that drive the offending drug from target tissues into the newly formed 'lipid sink'. \n\nRequired room: Regular Hospitalisation at any dpt.

TRT_CRP_BOTOX.name: Botulinum Toxin
TRT_CRP_BOTOX.description: Botulinum toxin (Botox) is a neurotoxic protein produced by the bacterium Clostridium botulinum and related species. It prevents the release of the neurotransmitter acetylcholine from axon endings at the neuromuscular junction and thus causes flaccid paralysis. While useful for various Plastic Surgery procedures, Botulinum toxin is the most poisonous substance known. Intoxication can occur naturally as a result of either wound or intestinal infection or by ingesting preformed toxin in food. The estimated human lethal dose of type A toxin is 1.3–2.1 ng/kg intravenously or intramuscularly, 10–13 ng/kg when inhaled, or 1000 ng/kg when taken by mouth.  \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_ATROPINE_EYEDROP.name: Atropine Eye Drops
TRT_CRP_ATROPINE_EYEDROP.description: Atropine Eye Drops belongs to a class of medicines called antimuscarinic agents. It is used to dilate (enlarge) the pupil of the eye and to temporarily paralyse the lens so that your doctor can examine your eye(s). Putting cycloplegic drugs, such as atropine sulphate, into the healthy eye causes blurred vision and loss of ability to focus on different distances, forcing the lazy eye to compensate.  \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_EYE_PATCH.name: Eye Patch
TRT_CRP_EYE_PATCH.description: The eye patch works by blocking the vision of the patient's stronger eye. This forces the weaker, or "lazy", eye to work harder so that the vision in that eye can improve.  \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_CORTICOSTEROID_EYEDROPS.name: Corticosteroid Eye Drops
TRT_CRP_CORTICOSTEROID_EYEDROPS.description: Ophthalmic corticosteroids (cortisone-like medicines) are used to prevent permanent damage to the eye, which may occur with certain eye problems or due to surgery. They also provide relief from redness, irritation, and other discomfort. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_CHEMPEEL_L.name: Alpha Hydroxy Acid Peel
TRT_CRP_CHEMPEEL_L.description: Alpha Hydroxy Acids (AHAs) are usually derived from citric (citrus fruits), glycolic (sugar cane), lactic (fermented milk), malic (apples) and tartaric (grapes). Generally, glycolic and lactic acid are the two most commonly applied. AHAs are the mildest of all chemical peels. They are primarily beneficial for the treatment of very fine lines, dry and dehydrated skin, mild skin and pigment irregularities and acne. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_SCLEROTHERAPY.name: Sclerotherapy
TRT_CRP_SCLEROTHERAPY.description: Sclerotherapy is a treatment that involves the injection of a chemical solution into blood vessels, usually spider veins or varicose veins on the legs. Sclerotherapy irritates the vessel walls, making them stick together. This leads to fibrosis, or scarring, of the blood vessel. The unwanted veins injected with a sclerosing solution then dissolve over a period of weeks as the body naturally absorbs the treated vein. Sclerotherapy is a non-invasive procedure taking only about 10 minutes to perform. The downtime is minimal, in comparison to an invasive varicose vein surgery. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_SKIN_LT.name: Skin Laser Therapy
TRT_CRP_SKIN_LT.description: Laser Therapy or Laser Resurfacing is a procedure that uses a laser to improve the appearance of skin or treat minor facial flaws by removing layers of skin. The two most common types of resurfacing lasers are; Carbon dioxide (CO2), this type of laser is used to treat wrinkles, scars, warts and other conditions. The second type is Erbium, this type of laser is used to remove superficial and moderately deep lines and wrinkles on the face, hands, neck and chest. It causes fewer side effects than CO2 lasers.  \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_CHE_INJECT.name: Enzyme Injections (CCH)
TRT_CRP_CHE_INJECT.description: Injecting a type of enzyme, normally collagenase extracted from C histolyticum, into the taut cord of the patient's palm can soften and weaken it allowing the doctor to manipulate the hand in an attempt to break the cord and straighten the fingers. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_CORTICOSTEROID_INJECTIONS2.name: Corticosteroid Injections
TRT_CRP_CORTICOSTEROID_INJECTIONS2.description: Corticosteroids (also known as steroids) are medicines that can be used to treat inflammation. When corticosteroids are injected into or around a painful area (such as a joint or muscle) they can reduce the inflammation in that area, relieving pain, reducing tissue swelling, and improving function and mobility. A Doctor may inject a corticosteroid solution directly into a hypertrophic scar or keloid, which may help reduce its size. Steroids break the bonds between collagen fibers, which reduces the amount of scar tissue beneath the skin.  \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_CRYOSURGERY.name: Cryosurgery
TRT_CRP_CRYOSURGERY.description: Cryosurgery is a type of surgery that involves the use of extreme cold to destroy abnormal tissues, such as tumors. The surgery most often involves the use of liquid nitrogen, although carbon dioxide and argon may also be used. Cryosurgery works by taking advantage of the destructive force of freezing temperatures on cells. When their temperature sinks beyond a certain level ice crystals begin forming inside the cells and, because of their lower density, eventually tear apart those cells. Further harm to malignant growth will result once the blood vessels supplying the affected tissue begin to freeze.  \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_HYAACID_FILL.name: Hyaluronic Acid Filler
TRT_CRP_HYAACID_FILL.description: Injectable hyaluronic acid (HA) is a type of temporary dermal filler. Hyaluronic acid is found naturally through out the body, with the highest concentrations in the joints, eyes, and skin. In the skin hyaluronic acid attracts water, which allows the skin to become more hydrated. Injectable HA is used to reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, facial folds, and to create structure, framework, and volume to the face and lips. The effects of injectable hyaluronic acid are seen immediately. \n\nRequired room: Any office

SUR_SLP_WIDELOCALEXCISION.name: Wide Local Excision (WLE)
SUR_SLP_WIDELOCALEXCISION.description: A wide local excision (WLE) is a surgical procedure to remove a small area of diseased or problematic tissue with a margin of normal tissue. This procedure is commonly performed on the breast and to skin lesions, but can be used on any area of the body. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_LAPAROSCOPIC_SURGERY.name: Laparoscopic Surgery
SUR_SLP_LAPAROSCOPIC_SURGERY.description: Laparoscopic or “minimally invasive” surgery is a specialized technique for performing surgery. It's a low-risk, minimally invasive procedure that requires only small incisions. Laparoscopy uses an instrument called a laparoscope to look at the abdominal organs. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_LOBECTOMY_SURGERY.name: Pulmonary Lobectomy
SUR_SLP_LOBECTOMY_SURGERY.description: Pulmonary Lobectomy of the lung is a surgical operation where a lobe of the lung is removed. It is done to remove a portion of diseased lung, such as early stage lung cancer. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_ENDOVASCULAR_EMBOLISATION.name: Endovascular Embolization
SUR_SLP_ENDOVASCULAR_EMBOLISATION.description: Endovascular embolization (EE) is an invasive surgical procedure. It’s used to treat abnormal blood vessels found in your brain, as well as other areas of your body. This procedure is an alternative to open surgery. It blocks blood vessels to cut off blood flow to an affected area. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_ONCO_NERVE_DECOM_SURGERY.name: Nerve Reconstructive Surgery
SUR_SLP_ONCO_NERVE_DECOM_SURGERY.description: Nerve Reconstructive Surgery, or surgical nerve repair involves exploration of the injured nerve and removal of injured tissue or scar from the nerve endings. After that, a nerve can be directly reconnected if there is enough length on the ends to allow for a good quality repair without tension. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Neurology dpt.

SUR_SLP_POLYPECTOMY.name: Polypectomy
SUR_SLP_POLYPECTOMY.description: A polypectomy is a procedure used to remove polyps from the inside of the colon, also called the large intestine. A polyp is an abnormal collection of tissue. The procedure is relatively noninvasive. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_PARTIAL_COLECTOMY.name: Colectomy
SUR_SLP_PARTIAL_COLECTOMY.description: A Colectomy is bowel resection of the large bowel. It consists of the surgical removal of any extent of the colon, usually segmental resection. In extreme cases where the entire large intestine is removed. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_ADRENALECTOMY.name: Adrenalectomy
SUR_SLP_ADRENALECTOMY.description: Adrenalectomy is the surgical removal of one or both adrenal glands. It is usually advised for patients with tumors of the adrenal glands. The procedure can be performed using an open incision or laparoscopic technique. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_SEG_MASTECTOMY.name: Partial Mastectomy
SUR_SLP_SEG_MASTECTOMY.description: An operation to remove the cancer and some normal tissue around it, but not the breast itself. Some lymph nodes under the arm may be removed for biopsy. Part of the chest wall lining may also be removed if the cancer is near it. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_DEBULKING.name: Surgical Debulking
SUR_SLP_DEBULKING.description: Surgical debulking of tumors is a procedure whereby a surgically incurable malignant neoplasm is partially removed without curative intent in order to make subsequent therapy with drugs, radiation or other adjunctive measures more effective and, thereby, improve the length of survival. The doctor will need to book follow up treatment appointments with the patient in an attempt push the cancer into remission. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Neurology dpt.

SUR_SLP_GTR.name: Gross-Total Resection Craniotomy
SUR_SLP_GTR.description: Gross total resection is a complete removal of the magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) visible tumor tissue. Supratotal resection is performed as gross total resection with excising the MRI visible tumor tissue borders into the unaffected brain tissue. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Neurology dpt.

SUR_SLP_UNI_SALOOPH.name: Unilateral Salpingo-Oophorectomy
SUR_SLP_UNI_SALOOPH.description: A unilateral salpingo-oophorectomy is the surgical removal of one ovary and one fallopian tube, both of which are located on the same side of the body and share a common blood supply (in contrast, a bilateral procedure involves the removal of both ovaries and fallopian tubes). Unilateral salpingo-oophorectomy should be considered as the first choice of fertility-sparing treatment for women who wish to preserve fertility. Although the higher risk of local relapses is found, it is not associated with decreased overall survival. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_RAD_PROS.name: Radical Prostatectomy
SUR_SLP_RAD_PROS.description: Radical prostatectomy is an operation to remove the prostate gland and tissues surrounding it. This usually includes the seminal vesicles and some nearby lymph nodes. Radical prostatectomy can cure prostate cancer in men whose cancer is limited to the prostate. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_PARATHY.name: Parathyroidectomy
SUR_SLP_PARATHY.description: Parathyroidectomy is surgery to remove the parathyroid glands or parathyroid tumors. The parathyroid glands are right behind your thyroid gland in your neck. These glands help your body control the calcium level in the blood. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_SEGMENTECTOMY.name: Segmentectomy
SUR_SLP_SEGMENTECTOMY.description: Segmental resection is a surgical procedure to remove part of an organ or gland, as a sub-type of a resection, which might involve removing the whole body part. It may also be used to remove a tumor and normal tissue around it. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_HEPATECTOMY.name: Hepatectomy
SUR_SLP_HEPATECTOMY.description: Hepatectomy is the surgical resection (removal of all or part) of the liver. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_PAROTIDECTOMY.name: Parotidectomy
SUR_SLP_PAROTIDECTOMY.description: A parotidectomy is the surgical excision of the parotid gland, the major and largest of the salivary glands. The procedure is most typically performed due to neoplasms, which are growths of rapidly and abnormally dividing cells. Neoplasms can be benign or malignant. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_VAGINECTOMY.name: Vaginectomy
SUR_SLP_VAGINECTOMY.description: Vaginectomy is a surgery to remove all or part of the vagina. It is usually used as a treatment for vaginal cancer. If the uterus and ovaries are to remain intact, vaginectomy will leave a canal and opening suitable for draining menstrual discharge. Otherwise, a hysterectomy must be performed to avoid the danger of retaining menstrual discharge within the body. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

SUR_SLP_BRACHYTHERAPY.name: Brachytherapy
SUR_SLP_BRACHYTHERAPY.description: Brachytherapy is a procedure that involves placing radioactive material inside the patient's body. Brachytherapy is one type of radiation therapy that's used to treat cancer. Brachytherapy is sometimes called internal radiation. Brachytherapy allows doctors to deliver higher doses of radiation to more-specific areas of the body, compared with the conventional form of radiation therapy (external beam radiation) that projects radiation from a machine outside of your body. Brachytherapy may cause fewer side effects than does external beam radiation, and the overall treatment time is usually shorter. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Oncology dpt.

TRT_STANOZOLOL.name: Stanozolol
TRT_STANOZOLOL.description: Stanozolol may be used in the long-term treatment of patients with hereditary angioedema provided such patients are closely supervised with routine clinical, biochemical, and radiologic assessments. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_ZAVESCA.name: Zavesca
TRT_ZAVESCA.description: Zavesca is a medicine that is used to treat mild to moderate type 1 Gaucher disease in adults and it is also used to treat Niemann-Pick type C. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_BIOTIN.name: Biotin Capsules
TRT_BIOTIN.description: Biotin (vitamin B7) is a vitamin found in foods like eggs, milk, and bananas. Oral Biotin is offered as an over the counter supplement and is used in the treatment of Biotin deficiency. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_INTRAVENOUS_BERINERT.name: Berinert
TRT_INTRAVENOUS_BERINERT.description: Intravenous (IV) Infusion of Berinert(C1-INH concentrate). \n\nRequired room: Any ward / unit

TRT_INTRAVENOUS_PANHEMATIN.name: Panhematin
TRT_INTRAVENOUS_PANHEMATIN.description: Panhematin is a hemin for injection prescription medication used to relieve repeated attacks of acute intermittent porphyria. \n\nRequired room: Any ward / unit

SUR_OL_DISTAL_PANCREATECTOMY.name: Distal Pancreatectomy
SUR_OL_DISTAL_PANCREATECTOMY.description:
Distal pancreatectomy is a surgery to remove the distal (body-tail) part of
				your pancreas. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Department of Diagnostics.

SUR_OL_FASCIOTOMY.name: Fasciotomy
SUR_OL_FASCIOTOMY.description: Fasciotomy, a procedure in which the fascia is cut to relieve pressure in the muscle compartment, is used to treat people with acute or chronic compartment syndrome. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Department of Diagnostics.

SUR_OL_GASTRECTOMY.name: Gastrectomy
SUR_OL_GASTRECTOMY.description: A gastrectomy is a medical procedure where all or part of the stomach is surgically removed. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Department of Diagnostics.

SUR_OL_LIVER_TRANSPLANT.name: Liver Transplant
SUR_OL_LIVER_TRANSPLANT.description: A liver transplant is surgery to replace a diseased liver with a healthy liver from another person. A whole liver may be transplanted, or just part of one. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Department of Diagnostics.

SUR_OL_MYOTOMY.name: Myotomy
SUR_OL_MYOTOMY.description: Heller myotomy is a surgical procedure in which the muscles of the cardia are cut, allowing food and liquids to pass to the stomach. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Department of Diagnostics.

SUR_OL_LAPAROSCOPY.name: Laparoscopic Surgery
SUR_OL_LAPAROSCOPY.description: A minimally invasive surgical technique. The surgery is performed by cutting a series of small incisions through which surgical equipment is inserted. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Department of Diagnostics

SUR_OL_SPLENECTOMY.name: Splenectomy
SUR_OL_SPLENECTOMY.description: Splenectomy is a surgical procedure to remove your spleen. The spleen is an organ that sits under your rib cage on the upper left side of your abdomen. It helps fight infection and filters unneeded material, such as old or damaged blood cells, from your blood. The most common reason for splenectomy is to treat a ruptured spleen, which is often caused by an abdominal injury. Splenectomy may be used to treat other conditions, including an enlarged spleen that is causing discomfort (splenomegaly), some blood disorders, certain cancers, infection, and noncancerous cysts or tumors. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Department of Diagnostics

SUR_OL_AVM_RESECTION.name: AVM Resection
SUR_OL_AVM_RESECTION.description: AVM resection involves delicately separating the AVM from the surrounding tissue. Using a microscope, a neurosurgeon cuts off the blood supply to the AVM and removes it. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Neurology dpt

SUR_OL_HEMISPHERECTOMY.name: Anatomical Hemispherectomy
SUR_OL_HEMISPHERECTOMY.description: An anatomic hemispherectomy involves physically removing the diseased hemisphere of the brain including the frontal, parietal, temporal and occipital lobes. The deeper structures in the brain including the thalamus and basal ganglia are left in place. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Neurology dpt

TRT_JM_ANTACIDS.name: Antacids
TRT_JM_ANTACIDS.description: Antacids are substances that neutralize stomach acid, used to relieve heartburn, indigestion or reflux.\n\nRequired room: Otorhinolaryngology Diagnostic Unit / Otorhinolaryngology Doctor's Office / ICU / Observation Room

TRT_JM_CHEWING_GUM_OR_YAWN.name: Chewing Gum and Yawning
TRT_JM_CHEWING_GUM_OR_YAWN.description: Chewing Gum and Yawning can help open up the Eustachian tube to relieve pressure within the ears \n\nRequired room: Otorhinolaryngology Diagnostic Unit / Otorhinolaryngology Doctor's Office / ICU / Observation Room

TRT_JM_CDLS.name: Carbon Dioxide Laser Surgery
TRT_JM_CDLS.description: The carbon dioxide laser (CO2 laser) was one of the earliest gas lasers to be developed. It was invented by Kumar Patel of Bell Labs in 1964, and is still one of the most useful. Carbon dioxide lasers are the highest-power continuous wave lasers that are currently available. They are also quite efficient: the ratio of output power to pump power can be as large as 20%. The CO2 laser produces a beam of infrared light with the principal wavelength bands centering on 9.4 and 10.6 micrometers .

TRT_JM_PROTECTIVE_DRESSING.name: Protective Dressing
TRT_JM_PROTECTIVE_DRESSING.description: Bandage protective dressing. \n\nRequired room: Office/Diagnostic unit / Burn unit at Traumatology dpt. / ICU / Trauma center at Emergency

TRT_JM_DEBRIDEMENT.name: Debridement
TRT_JM_DEBRIDEMENT.description: Medical removal of dead tissue. \n\nRequired room: Office/Diagnostic unit / Burn unit at Traumatology dpt. / ICU / Trauma center at Emergency

TRT_PESSAIRE.name: Pessary
TRT_PESSAIRE.description: A device worn in the vagina to support the uterus, remedy a malposition, or prevent conception.

TRT_MYOLYSE.name: High-Intensity Focused Ultrasound
TRT_MYOLYSE.description: High-intensity focused ultrasound (HIFU) is a highly precise medical procedure used locally to heat and destroy diseased tissue through ablation.

TRT_OCYTOCINE.name: Oxytocin
TRT_OCYTOCINE.description: A hormone released by the pituitary gland that causes increased contraction of the uterus during labour and stimulates the ejection of milk into the ducts of the breasts.

TRT_ASPIRATION_ENDO_UTERINE.name: Vacuum Aspiration
TRT_ASPIRATION_ENDO_UTERINE.description: Vacuum or suction aspiration uses aspiration to remove uterine contents through the cervix.

TRT_OBGYN_REEDUCATION_PELV.name: Perineo-Sphincter Rehabilitation
TRT_OBGYN_REEDUCATION_PELV.description: Intravaginal manual work of the pelvic floor muscles, improves the control of urination.

TRT_OBGYN_CLOT_PREVENTING_DRUGS.name: Anticoagulants
TRT_OBGYN_CLOT_PREVENTING_DRUGS.description: Drugs preventing blood platelets to stick to each other and forming clots. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office / Diagnostic unit at Cardiology dpt

TRT_SALPINGOTOMIE.name: Salpingotomy
TRT_SALPINGOTOMIE.description: The operation of making an incision into a fallopian tube to remove an ectopic.

TRT_KYSTECTOMIE.name: Cystectomy
TRT_KYSTECTOMIE.description: The surgical excision of a cyst.

TRT_CESARIENNE.name: Cesarean Section
TRT_CESARIENNE.description: A surgical procedure involving incision of the walls of the abdomen and uterus for delivery of offspring.

TRT_OBGYN_ENDOSCOPIC_CAUTERIZATION.name: Endoscopic Cauterization
TRT_OBGYN_ENDOSCOPIC_CAUTERIZATION.description: Endoscopic cauterization is a surgical procedure where an electric current is used to burn/cautherize an area in order to open or close it off. It is commonly used to stop bleedings during surgeries.

TRT_EXERESE.name: Surgical Resection.
TRT_EXERESE.description: The surgical removal of part of an organ or structure..

TRT_HYSTERECTOMIE.name: Hysterectomy
TRT_HYSTERECTOMIE.description: A surgical operation to remove all or part of the uterus.

TRT_TUMORECTOMIE.name: Lumpectomy
TRT_TUMORECTOMIE.description: An operation to remove the cancer and some normal tissue around it, but not the breast itself.

TRT_OBGYN_LIG_TROMPES.name: Tubal Sterilization
TRT_OBGYN_LIG_TROMPES.description: Tubal ligation prevents an egg from traveling from the ovaries through the fallopian tubes and blocks sperm from traveling up the fallopian tubes to the egg.

TRT_OBGYN_MISE_PLAT_ABCES.name: Chirurgical Abscess Incision and Drainage
TRT_OBGYN_MISE_PLAT_ABCES.description: This consists of incising the vestibular mucosa vertically in the nymphohymeneal groove. One can also incise on the protruding dome of the gland. If the abscess is already fistulized, it is enough to widen the drainage hole sufficiently.

TRT_CRP_HOSPITALISEDMONTIOR_L3.name: Hospitalised Monitoring (Lengthy)
TRT_CRP_HOSPITALISEDMONTIOR_L3.description: Watch and wait monitoring of a patient's condition from the hospital. This allows for quicker response to issues and be necessary for high risk complications that arise from symptoms or treatments.

TRT_CRP_SHALLOWDEBRISREMOVAL.name: Shallow Debris Removal
TRT_CRP_SHALLOWDEBRISREMOVAL.description: Removal of shallow debris and foreign objects embedded in the patient's skin or face and disinfecting the effected areas.

SUR_SLP_AUGMENTATION_MAMMOPLASTY.name: Augmentation Mammoplasty
SUR_SLP_AUGMENTATION_MAMMOPLASTY.description: Breast augmentation is a cosmetic surgery technique using breast-implants and fat-graft mammoplasty techniques to increase the size, change the shape, and alter the texture of the breasts of a woman. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_CORRECTIVE_SURGERY.name: Corrective Surgery
SUR_SLP_CORRECTIVE_SURGERY.description: It is not uncommon for patients to occasionally require corrective surgery, this is carried out to address concerns or complications relating to a previous surgery. Addressing these concerns will help greatly with patient satisfaction at the expense of additional surgery time and further complication risks. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_SAL.name: Suction Assisted Lipectomy (SAL)
SUR_SLP_SAL.description: Liposuction is invasive surgery that aims to improve the body’s contours and proportion by removing localised and disproportional deposits of fat. Liposuction can remove up to 5 litres of fat with relative safety during one session. Suction Assisted Lipectomy (SAL) is where fat is removed from the body using a vacuum suction. SAL may be dry, wet or tumescent (Tumescent liposuction) depending on the volume of saline (salt solution) which is injected into the operation site before the procedure begins. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_RHINOPLASTY.name: Rhinoplasty
SUR_SLP_RHINOPLASTY.description: Rhinoplasty, commonly known as a nose job, is a plastic surgery procedure for correcting and reconstructing the nose. There are two types of plastic surgery used – reconstructive surgery that restores the form and functions of the nose and cosmetic surgery that improves the appearance of the nose.  \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_HAIRT.name: Follicular Unit Transplantation
SUR_SLP_HAIRT.description: Follicular unit extraction, sometimes referred to simply as FUE, is a method of hair transplant surgery that manually removes individual hair follicles from the patient's scalp. Once extracted, hairs are then implanted to bald or thinning areas around the head.  \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_MASTOPEXY.name: Mastopexy
SUR_SLP_MASTOPEXY.description: Mastopexy, or breast lift as it is commonly known, is a Cosmetic Surgery procedure designed to lift and re-contour sagging breasts or breasts that have lost volume. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_SEPTOPLASTY.name: Septoplasty
SUR_SLP_SEPTOPLASTY.description: Septoplasty, or alternatively submucous septal resection and septal reconstruction, is a corrective surgical procedure done to straighten a deviated nasal septum – the nasal septum being the partition between the two nasal cavities. Ideally, the septum should run down the centre of the nose. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_CHEEK_AUGMENTATION.name: Cheek Augmentation
SUR_SLP_CHEEK_AUGMENTATION.description: Cheek augmentation surgery, also known as cheek implant surgery is a procedure where your plastic surgeon inserts a solid implant over your cheekbone which enhances your facial contour by making your cheeks look fuller providing an overall better definition to your face. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_ABDOMINOPLASTY.name: Abdominoplasty
SUR_SLP_ABDOMINOPLASTY.description: Abdominoplasty or "tummy tuck" is a cosmetic surgery procedure used to make the abdomen thinner and more firm. The surgery involves the removal of excess skin and fat from the middle and lower abdomen in order to tighten the muscle and fascia of the abdominal wall.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_SCAR_REVISION_SURGERY.name: Scar Revision Surgery
SUR_SLP_PLAS_SCAR_REVISION_SURGERY.description: Scar revision is surgery to improve or reduce the appearance of scars. It also restores function, and corrects skin changes (disfigurement) caused by an injury, wound, poor healing, or previous surgery. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_IMPLANT_REMOVAL.name: Breast Implant Removal
SUR_SLP_PLAS_IMPLANT_REMOVAL.description: Breast implant infections are usually caused by Staphylococcus aureus and coagulase-negative staphylococci. Gram-negative bacilli are rarely reported to be involved in breast implant infections. Although rare, infections of this type generally require the implants to be removed. This surgery removes the breast implants and cleans the tissue around the implant site. Follow-up anti-biotic courses are normally required. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_CAPSULECTOMY.name: Capsulectomy
SUR_SLP_PLAS_CAPSULECTOMY.description: Capsulectomy is the surgical removal of scar tissue or capsule that has become thickened and hardened around a breast implant. The capsule is composed of fibroblasts, collagen, and blood vessels. Performing a capsulectomy should alleviate the discomfort and improve the appearance and feel of the patients breasts. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_EYEMSURGERY.name: Eye Muscle Surgery
SUR_SLP_PLAS_EYEMSURGERY.description: Eye muscle surgery is a surgery to correct strabismus (eye misalignment) or nystagmus (eye wiggling). The surgery involves moving one or more of the eye muscles to adjust the position of the eye or eyes.  \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_EMERG_REPAIR.name: Emergency Surgery (Plastic)
SUR_SLP_PLAS_EMERG_REPAIR.description:
Emergency Surgery can be defined as surgery that is required to deal with an acute threat to life, organ, limb or tissue caused by external trauma, acute disease process, acute exacerbation of a chronic disease process, or complication of a surgical or other interventional procedure. 

Importance: This surgery is considered critical and should be a priority. 

Required room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_REPLANT.name: Replantation
SUR_SLP_PLAS_REPLANT.description: Replantation refers to the surgical reattachment of a body part (such as a finger, hand, or toe) that has been completely cut from the body. The goal of replantation surgery is to reattach and restore function to as much of the injured part as possible.  \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_ENUCLEATION.name: Enucleation
SUR_SLP_PLAS_ENUCLEATION.description:
Enucleation is the removal of the eye that leaves the eye muscles and remaining orbital contents intact. This type of ocular surgery is indicated for a number of ocular tumors, in eyes that have suffered severe trauma, and in eyes that are otherwise blind and painful. A spherical implant (artificial eyeball) is used to replace the removed eyeball. 

Importance: This surgery is considered critical and should be a priority. 

Required room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_IMPLANT_REMOVAL_01.name: Cheek Implant Infection
SUR_SLP_PLAS_IMPLANT_REMOVAL_01.description: Cheek implant infections are usually caused by Staphylococcus aureus and coagulase-negative staphylococci. Although rare, infections of this type generally require the implants to be removed. This surgery removes the cheek implants and cleans the tissue around the implant site. Follow-up anti-biotic courses are normally required. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_ENDOVENOUS_LASER_AB.name: Endovenous Laser Ablation
SUR_SLP_PLAS_ENDOVENOUS_LASER_AB.description: Endovenous laser ablation, also known as endovenous laser therapy, is a procedure that uses heat from a laser to reduce varicose veins.

SUR_SLP_PLAS_SURGICAL_DEBRIDEMENT.name: Surgical Debridement
SUR_SLP_PLAS_SURGICAL_DEBRIDEMENT.description: Debridement is the medical removal of dead, damaged, or infected tissue to improve the healing potential of the remaining healthy tissue. Surgical or "sharp" debridement and laser debridement under anesthesia are the fastest methods of debridement. They are very selective, meaning that the person performing the debridement has complete control over which tissue is removed and which is left behind. Surgical debridement can be performed in the operating room or bedside, depending on the extent of the necrotic material and a patient's ability to tolerate the procedure. \n\nRequired room: Operating room at Plastic Surgery dpt.

TRT_SH_INSECTICIDE_CREAM.name: Insecticide Cream
TRT_SH_INSECTICIDE_CREAM.description: Kills insects (parasites) that live on humans, such as the scabies mite and crab (pubic) lice. \n\nRequired room: Sexual Health office

TRT_SH_ANTIRETROVIRAL.name: Antiretroviral Medication
TRT_SH_ANTIRETROVIRAL.description: Stops the virus replicating in the body, allowing the immune system to repair itself and preventing further damage. \n\nRequired room: Sexual Health office

TRT_SH_ANTIVIRAL_MEDICATION.name: Antiviral Medication
TRT_SH_ANTIVIRAL_MEDICATION.description: Used specifically for treating viral infections. \n\nRequired room: Sexual Health office

TRT_SH_CRYOTHERAPY.name: Cryotherapy
TRT_SH_CRYOTHERAPY.description: Cryotherapy involves freezing a wart using a very cold substance (usually liquid nitrogen). \n\nRequired room: Sexual Health office

TRT_SH_ANTIBIOTICS.name: Antibiotics
TRT_SH_ANTIBIOTICS.description: Drugs in various forms used to treat various bacterial infections. \n\nRequired room: Sexual Health office

TRT_SH_PREP.name: PrEP
TRT_SH_PREP.description: Pre-exposure prophylaxis (PrEP) is the use of drugs to prevent HIV. \n\nRequired room: Sexual Health office

TRT_SH_VIAGRA.name: Viagra
TRT_SH_VIAGRA.description: Viagra (Sildenafil) is a medication used to treat erectile dysfunction. \n\nRequired room: Sexual Health office

TRT_KN_SHOCK_WAVE_LITHOTRIPSY.name: Shock Wave Lithotripsy
TRT_KN_SHOCK_WAVE_LITHOTRIPSY.description:
A procedure commonly carried out to break stones lodged in the urethra (the tube connecting a kidney to the bladder). It is non-surgical treatment option using high energy ultrasound shock waves to broke stones into dust, allowing them to pass. 

Required room: Sonography unit at Urology dpt 

Required equipment: Therapy shockwave

TRT_KN_ALPHA_BLOCKERS.name: Selective Α1-Blockers
TRT_KN_ALPHA_BLOCKERS.description: Selective α1-blockers. They include alfuzosin, doxazosin, silodosin, tamsulosin, terazosin, and naftopidil

TRT_KN_5A_REDUCTASE_INHIBITORS.name: 5α-Reductase Inhibitors
TRT_KN_5A_REDUCTASE_INHIBITORS.description: The 5α-reductase inhibitors finasteride and dutasteride may also be used in men with BPH. These medications inhibit the 5α-reductase enzyme, which, in turn, inhibits production of DHT, a hormone responsible for enlarging the prostate.

TRT_KN_PDE5_INHIBITORS.name: PDE5 Inhibitors
TRT_KN_PDE5_INHIBITORS.description: PDE5 inhibitors sildenafil (Viagra), vardenafil (Levitra) and tadalafil (Cialis) are prescription drugs which are taken by mouth.

TRT_KN_IMMUNO_SUPRES.name: Immunosuppressiva
TRT_KN_IMMUNO_SUPRES.description: Immunosuppressiva are drugs that inhibit activity of the immune system, to make sure that the body doesn't reject the new organ.

TRT_KN_DIALYSIS.name: Dialysis
TRT_KN_DIALYSIS.description:
Is a process of removing the waste products from the cells metabolism from the bloodstream, when the kidneys are not able to maintain this function. 

Required room: Dialysis ward at Urology dpt 

Required equipment: Dialysis machine

TRT_KN_DIALYSIS_2.name: Dialysis
TRT_KN_DIALYSIS_2.description:
Is a process of removing the waste products from the cells metabolism from the bloodstream, when the kidneys are not able to maintain this function. 

Required room: Dialysis ward at Urology dpt 

Required equipment: Dialysis machine

TRT_KN_URN_CATH.name: Urinary Catheter
TRT_KN_URN_CATH.description: Urinary catheter: draining urine from the urinary bladder as in urinary catheterization.

TRT_KN_TRANS_REPAIR.name: Transvaginally Repair
TRT_KN_TRANS_REPAIR.description: Transvaginally repair: A procedure done through the vagina so no big incision is needed.

TRT_KN_ORCHIOPEXY.name: Orchiopexy
TRT_KN_ORCHIOPEXY.description: Orchiopexy is a surgery to move an undescended testicle into the scrotum and permanently fix it there. Orchiopexy typically also describes the surgery used to resolve testicular torsion.

TRT_KN_ORCHIECTOMY.name: Orchiectomy
TRT_KN_ORCHIECTOMY.description: Orchiectomy is a surgical procedure in which one or both testicles are removed (bilateral orchiectomy). The surgery is typically performed as treatment for testicular cancer, in some cases of testicular torsion, as a gender-affirming procedure for some trans women, and is sometimes used in the management of advanced prostate cancer.

TRT_KN_TURP.name: Transurethral Resection of the Prostate
TRT_KN_TURP.description: Transurethral resection of the prostate is thought to be the most effective approach for improving urinary symptoms and urinary flow, however, this surgical procedure may be associated with complications in up to 20% of men.

TRT_KIDN_TRANS.name: Kidney Transplantation
TRT_KIDN_TRANS.description: Kidney transplantation or renal transplantation is the organ transplant of a kidney into a patient with end-stage kidney disease. Kidney transplantation is typically classified as deceased-donor or living-donor transplantation depending on the source of the donor organ.

TRT_KN_VASECTOMY.name: Vasectomy
TRT_KN_VASECTOMY.description: Vasectomy is a surgical procedure for male sterilization or permanent contraception. During the procedure, the male vasa deferentia are cut and tied or sealed so as to prevent sperm from entering into the urethra and thereby prevent fertilization of a female through sexual intercourse.

TRT_KN_PARTNEPH.name: Partial Nephrectomy
TRT_KN_PARTNEPH.description: Nephrectomy is the surgical removal of a kidney. The procedure is done to treat kidney cancer as well as other kidney diseases and injuries. Nephrectomy is also done to remove a healthy kidney from a donor for transplantation. In partial nephrectomy, only the diseased or injured portion of the kidney is removed.

TRT_KN_RADNEPH.name: Radical Nephrectomy
TRT_KN_RADNEPH.description: Nephrectomy is the surgical removal of a kidney. The procedure is done to treat kidney cancer as well as other kidney diseases and injuries. Nephrectomy is also done to remove a healthy kidney from a donor for transplantation. Radical nephrectomy involves removing the entire kidney, along with a section of the tube leading to the bladder, the gland that sits atop the kidney , and the fatty tissue surrounding the kidney.

TRT_KN_RADNEPH_INFVECA.name: Radical Nephrectomy With Inferior Vena Cava Thrombectomy
TRT_KN_RADNEPH_INFVECA.description: Nephrectomy is the surgical removal of a kidney. The procedure is done to treat kidney cancer as well as other kidney diseases and injuries. Radical nephrectomy with inferior vena cava (IVC) thrombectomy is used, when the cancer has grown into nearby veins (as with some stage III cancers). Your surgeon may need to cut open these veins to remove all of the cancer. This may require putting you on bypass (a heart-lung machine), so that the heart can be stopped for a short time to remove the cancer from the large vein leading to the heart.

TRT_KN_BLAD.name: Bladder Surgery
TRT_KN_BLAD.description: Bladder surgery is done to resolve trauma to the bladder.

TRT_NMC_CARBAMAZEPINE.name: Carbamazepine
TRT_NMC_CARBAMAZEPINE.description: Carbamazepine is an anticonvulsant and mood stabilizer. In neurology, it is the first-line treatment for partial seizures, with superior efficacy for complex partial seizures compared to other antiepileptic drugs. It is also effective in treating secondary generalized tonic-clonic seizures but may exacerbate absence and myoclonic seizures. In psychiatry, carbamazepine is used for the treatment of acute manic episodes in bipolar disorder, particularly in patients who are unresponsive to lithium, or those experiencing rapid cycling or mixed episodes. Additionally, carbamazepine is employed in the management of certain types of neuropathic pain. It is important to note that carbamazepine is an inducer of various drug-metabolizing enzymes, which can lead to increased metabolism of concomitant medications, thereby reducing their plasma concentrations and potentially diminishing their therapeutic effects.

TRT_NEW_PSY_CALCIUM_CHANNEL_REGULATOR_PR.name: Pregabalin
TRT_NEW_PSY_CALCIUM_CHANNEL_REGULATOR_PR.description: Pregabalin is a calcium channel modulator and an anticonvulsant medication. It is commonly used to treat various types of neuropathic pain and chronic pain conditions. Additionally, it is considered a first-line treatment for generalized anxiety disorder (GAD).

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTICHOLINERGICS_TRIHEXYPHEN.name: Benztropine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTICHOLINERGICS_TRIHEXYPHEN.description: Benztropine is an anticholinergic medication that selectively blocks cholinergic neural pathways in the striatum while exerting minimal peripheral effects. This action helps restore the balance between dopamine and acetylcholine in the brain. It is used in the treatment of Parkinson's disease, Parkinsonism, and drug-induced extrapyramidal disorders. In other words, benztropine can counteract the extrapyramidal side effects caused by typical antipsychotic medications.

TRT_NEW_PSY_LEVODOPA_AND_BENSERAZIDE_HYD.name: Cabergoline
TRT_NEW_PSY_LEVODOPA_AND_BENSERAZIDE_HYD.description: Cabergoline is a combination formulation of levodopa and benserazide. Levodopa, a precursor in dopamine synthesis, can cross the blood-brain barrier and is used to increase dopamine levels in the brain, making it effective for treating Parkinson's disease, which is characterized by a deficiency of dopamine in the basal ganglia. Additionally, cabergoline can be used for treating restless legs syndrome, potentially due to its effect on normalizing dopaminergic abnormalities in the central nervous system. However, long-term use may exacerbate symptoms, so it should be administered only as needed. The levodopa component of the combination can cause gastrointestinal adverse effects, cardiovascular adverse effects, dyskinesias (involuntary movements), and psychiatric disorders.

TRT_NEW_PSY_PRAMIPEXOLE.name: Pramipexole
TRT_NEW_PSY_PRAMIPEXOLE.description: Pramipexole is a non-ergot, selective dopamine receptor agonist that specifically stimulates dopamine D2 receptors, with minimal action on D1 receptors and low affinity for adrenergic α2 receptors. It is commonly used in the treatment of Parkinson's disease. Due to its long half-life, pramipexole does not require nighttime dosing and is well-tolerated with minimal complications during long-term use. It effectively alleviates over 90% of symptoms in restless legs syndrome (RLS), making it a first-line treatment option for RLS, with pramipexole being the only approved medication for this condition. Initial treatment may be associated with orthostatic hypotension and gastrointestinal adverse effects.

TRT_NEW_PSY_VITAMIN_C.name: Vitamin C
TRT_NEW_PSY_VITAMIN_C.description: Vitamin C plays a crucial role in antibody and collagen formation, tissue repair (including certain redox reactions), metabolism of phenylalanine, tyrosine, and folate, utilization of iron and carbohydrates, synthesis of fats and proteins, and maintenance of immune function. It is also essential for the hydroxylation of serotonin, maintaining vascular integrity, and enhancing the absorption of non-heme iron. Vitamin C is commonly used for dietary supplementation, chronic dialysis, prevention and treatment of vitamin C deficiency, acidifying urine, and idiopathic erythrocytosis. Additionally, vitamin C can enhance iron absorption when used in conjunction with iron supplements.

TRT_NEW_PSY_DPD_ACETYLCYSTEINE.name: N-Acetylcysteine
TRT_NEW_PSY_DPD_ACETYLCYSTEINE.description: N-acetylcysteine (NAC) is primarily used as a mucolytic agent, facilitating the breakdown of mucus. In psychiatric settings, it has been noted, anecdotally, to be effective for treating symptoms of depersonalization, particularly emotional numbing. The proposed mechanism for this effect involves NAC’s potential to modulate the glutamatergic system. While ketamine, which is an NMDA receptor antagonist, can induce depersonalization-like symptoms, NAC acts as a glycine-NMDA receptor agonist. This agonistic action may theoretically help improve depersonalization by counteracting the NMDA receptor blockade induced by ketamine.

TRT_NEW_PSY_TCAs_CLOMIPRAMINE.name: Clomipramine
TRT_NEW_PSY_TCAs_CLOMIPRAMINE.description: Clomipramine, also known as Anafranil, is a traditional tricyclic antidepressant. Due to its broad spectrum of side effects, it is now infrequently used for depression and is primarily considered a second-line treatment option for obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD).

TRT_NEW_PSY_OTHER_NEW_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DR.name: Mirtazapine
TRT_NEW_PSY_OTHER_NEW_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DR.description: Mirtazapine is the only new antidepressant with strong antihistaminic effects, which helps in sedation and appetite stimulation. Similar to tricyclic antidepressants and SSRIs, mirtazapine is also a potent REM sleep suppressant, which can reduce nighttime awakenings and extend total sleep duration. The most common side effects of mirtazapine include excessive sedation, increased appetite, weight gain, and dry mouth.

TRT_NMC_AMITRIPTYLINE.name: Amitriptyline
TRT_NMC_AMITRIPTYLINE.description: Amitriptyline is a class of tricyclic antidepressants (TCAs) that can inhibit the reuptake of monoamine neurotransmitters and have strong sedative and anticholinergic effects. In the field of psychiatry, the tricyclic antidepressants to which amitriptyline belongs are now rarely used, but it has been found in clinical practice that amitriptyline is more effective in treating physical symptoms; In other specialized fields, amitriptyline is often used for the prevention and treatment of migraine and neuropathic pain, as well as for functional disorders such as irritable bowel syndrome and cardiac neurosis. In addition, the SNRI antidepressant drug duloxetine has been found to have good therapeutic effects on various physical discomforts and chronic pain.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_COMPOU.name: Fluphenazine-Melitracen Tablets
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_COMPOU.description: Fluphenazine-Melitracen Tablets is a combination medication that includes a typical antipsychotic (fluphenazine) and a tricyclic antidepressant (melitracen). It is approved for the treatment of mild to moderate depression and anxiety symptoms, including traditional neurotic disorders or neuroticism, and is used for various functional somatic symptoms or psychosomatic disorders, such as irritable bowel syndrome, functional dyspepsia, chronic gastritis, and cardiac neurosis. The medication is known for its relatively rapid onset of action. However, it is considered to have limited long-term efficacy and may cause serious adverse effects, such as tardive dyskinesia, and significant withdrawal reactions. Therefore, it is not recommended as a routine medication for depression, anxiety disorders, or similar conditions in major clinical guidelines.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SARIS_.name: Trazodone
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SARIS_.description: Trazodone is the first of the second-generation antidepressants. Its primary pharmacological effect is mediated through postsynaptic 5-HT2 receptors. Although it has weak effects on the 5-HT transporter, Ach receptors, and H1 receptors, these can be considered negligible. It does not have significant activity on NE and DA receptors but is an antagonist at peripheral α-adrenergic receptors, which can lead to side effects such as orthostatic hypotension, dizziness, dry mouth, and priapism. Trazodone is a choice for the acute treatment of depression, demonstrating efficacy comparable to that of tricyclic antidepressants (TCAs) and other new antidepressants. It also helps improve sleep, with its sedative effects potentially providing rapid relief for patients with severe insomnia or anxiety. The most common side effect of trazodone is drowsiness.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_AGOMEL.name: Agomelatine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_AGOMEL.description: In psychiatry, agomelatine is the first antidepressant that acts on melatonin receptors. Agomelatine likely exerts its antidepressant effects by activating the MT1 and MT2 receptors, which helps synchronize circadian rhythms. Additionally, agomelatine is an antagonist at the 5-HT2C receptor, which blocks the receptor's inhibitory effect on norepinephrine (NE) and dopamine (DA) release, thereby increasing their concentrations in the prefrontal cortex and contributing to its antidepressant properties. In the field of sleep medicine, agomelatine has the ability to regulate circadian rhythms and sleep cycles. It effectively improves sleep continuity and increases the proportion of slow-wave sleep, leading to a normalization of overnight slow-wave and delta wave distribution, thereby enhancing overall sleep quality without altering REM sleep. Overall, patients generally tolerate agomelatine well, with no significant withdrawal symptoms observed.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_NDRIS_.name: Bupropion
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_NDRIS_.description: Bupropion is a novel antidepressant whose pharmacological mechanism remains somewhat controversial. It can be classified as a norepinephrine and dopamine reuptake inhibitor (NDRI). Bupropion has antidepressant effects but lacks anxiolytic properties, making it commonly used for treating depression and smoking cessation.  However, it may lead to central nervous system activation symptoms, with insomnia being one of the most common adverse effects. Anxiety and depression may also occur.

TRT_NEW_PSY_VARENICLINE.name: Varenicline
TRT_NEW_PSY_VARENICLINE.description: Varenicline significantly increases smoking cessation rates and reduces cravings, urges, and the satisfaction derived from smoking among those who have relapsed. Varenicline has a high affinity and selectivity for the α4β2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptors in neurons, exhibiting both agonistic and antagonistic properties. It acts as an agonist by binding to these receptors, stimulating dopamine release, which helps alleviate cravings and withdrawal symptoms associated with smoking cessation. Simultaneously, its antagonistic effect prevents nicotine from binding to the receptors, reducing the pleasure of smoking and the urge to smoke, thereby lowering the likelihood of relapse.

TRT_NEW_PSY_NICOTINE_CHEWING_GUM.name: Nicotine Chewing Gum
TRT_NEW_PSY_NICOTINE_CHEWING_GUM.description: Nicotine Chewing Gum is a nicotine replacement therapy product that incorporates nicotine into chewing gum. When chewed, the gum gradually releases nicotine, which is then absorbed through the oral mucosa into the bloodstream. The specific usage involves chewing one piece of gum whenever the urge to smoke arises. This method helps alleviate withdrawal symptoms associated with quitting smoking, such as cravings, increased appetite, irritability, anxiety, and difficulties with concentration.

TRT_NEW_PSY_XIAOER_ZHILI_SYRUP.name: Xiao'er Zhili Syrup
TRT_NEW_PSY_XIAOER_ZHILI_SYRUP.description: Xiao'er Zhili Syrup is a traditional Chinese medicine composed of ingredients such as tortoise shell (Gui Jia), dragon bone (Long Gu), Polygala (Yuan Zhi), Acorus (Shi Chang Pu), and rooster comb (Xiong Ji). This formulation is used to enhance cognitive function, nourish the heart and kidneys, and calm the mind. It is specifically indicated for children with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) associated with heart and kidney deficiency and phlegm-damp obstruction.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIEPILEPTICS_TOPIRAMATE.name: Topiramate
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIEPILEPTICS_TOPIRAMATE.description: Topiramate, marketed under the brand name Topamax, is an anticonvulsant medication used primarily for the treatment of epilepsy. In pediatric psychiatry, it is utilized as a treatment option for refractory tic disorders.

TRT_NEW_PSY_SHAOMA_ZHIJING_GRANULES.name: Shaoma Zhijing Granules
TRT_NEW_PSY_SHAOMA_ZHIJING_GRANULES.description: Shaoma Zhijing Granules are composed of Bai Shao (white peony root), Tianma (Gastrodia elata), Shizi (spiny jujube), Gou Teng (Uncaria), Lingzhi (Reishi mushroom), He Shou Wu (Polygonum multiflorum), Suan Zao Ren (Sour Jujube Seed), Cu Wu Wei Zi (vine tea), Zhi Zi (Gardenia), Dan Nan Xing (Fritillaria), and Huang Qin (Scutellaria). This formulation is used for treating Tourette's syndrome from the perspective of traditional Chinese medicine, where it is believed to address conditions of liver wind stirring and internal phlegm-heat disturbance.

TRT_NEW_PSY_AWAKENING_PROMOTING_DRUGS_PI.name: Tilopidine
TRT_NEW_PSY_AWAKENING_PROMOTING_DRUGS_PI.description: Tilopidine is a recently approved selective histamine H3 receptor inverse agonist. By inhibiting the negative feedback mechanism, it increases histamine release and activates histaminergic neurons, resulting in an arousal effect. As a novel and potent wakefulness-promoting agent, tilopidine has not shown significant addiction potential to date.

TRT_NEW_PSY_NRIS_TOMOXETINE.name: Atomoxetine
TRT_NEW_PSY_NRIS_TOMOXETINE.description: Atomoxetine, marketed under the brand name Strattera, is a non-stimulant medication used to treat attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD). Specifically, it is a selective norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor (NRI). Atomoxetine is non-addictive and provides effects that are either slightly less potent than or comparable to those of stimulants like methylphenidate. It has demonstrated efficacy and good tolerance over a one-year follow-up period. Initially found to be effective in adults, Atomoxetine was later approved for use in children aged 6 years and older.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BRAIN_FUNCTION_IMPROVEMENT_D.name: Oxiracetam
TRT_NEW_PSY_BRAIN_FUNCTION_IMPROVEMENT_D.description: Oxiracetam is a nootropic drug classified as a new-generation cognitive enhancer. It works by promoting the synthesis of phosphatidylcholine and phosphatidylethanolamine, thereby increasing the ATP/ADP ratio in the brain. This action enhances protein and nucleic acid synthesis, which in turn improves brain metabolism. Oxiracetam is used to counteract brain function damage and memory impairment caused by physical and chemical factors. It has been shown to improve retrograde amnesia due to hypoxia and may enhance memory and learning functions in patients with various types of senile dementia and memory disorders.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BETA_BLOCKERS_PROPRANOLOL.name: Propranolol
TRT_NEW_PSY_BETA_BLOCKERS_PROPRANOLOL.description: Propranolol is a beta-blocker used to treat various arrhythmias, hypertension, and tremors. In the field of psychiatry, while propranolol has minimal direct effects on anxiety itself, it can alleviate the physical and physiological symptoms of anxiety, such as palpitations and tremors. As a result, it is sometimes used to manage performance anxiety in social phobia or social anxiety disorder, though its efficacy for generalized anxiety disorder remains a subject of debate.

TRT_NEW_PSY_MOOD_STABILIZER_SODIUM_VALPR.name: Sodium Valproate
TRT_NEW_PSY_MOOD_STABILIZER_SODIUM_VALPR.description: Sodium valproate (brand name: Divalproex) is an anticonvulsant and mood stabilizer used in the treatment of manic episodes in bipolar disorder, particularly effective for rapid cycling and mixed episodes. Its efficacy in preventing relapse of bipolar disorder is comparable to that of lithium, making it a suitable alternative to lithium. Additionally, sodium valproate is commonly used for managing and treating behavioral symptoms associated with dementia, such as euphoria that impacts daily functioning.

TRT_NEW_PSY_MOOD_STABILIZER_lAMOTRIGINE.name: Lamotrigine
TRT_NEW_PSY_MOOD_STABILIZER_lAMOTRIGINE.description: Lamotrigine is traditionally known as an antiepileptic drug, but it has also emerged as a mood stabilizer, particularly effective for the depressive phase of bipolar disorder. Additionally, there is anecdotal evidence suggesting that lamotrigine may be effective in treating depersonalization, potentially alleviating symptoms such as derealization, somatic depersonalization, and attentional disturbances associated with the condition.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_CLONAZEPAM_IV.name: Intravenous Infusion of Diazepam
TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_CLONAZEPAM_IV.description: Diazepam is a benzodiazepine medication. Intravenous injection can rapidly alleviate panic attacks and relieve anxiety states. However, it is crucial to administer it slowly to avoid the risk of respiratory depression.

TRT_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOSTIMUALNTS_METHYLPHENI.name: Methylphenidate
TRT_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOSTIMUALNTS_METHYLPHENI.description: Methylphenidate, also known as piperidine methyl ester and commercially available as Concerta (extended-release) and Ritalin (immediate-release), is a central nervous system stimulant. It acts directly on the brain to enhance cortical activity, improve mental alertness, alleviate fatigue, and boost overall mental energy. It is used to manage symptoms of attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and excessive daytime sleepiness related to narcolepsy.

TRT_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOSTIMUALNTS_MODAFINIL.name: Modafinil
TRT_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOSTIMUALNTS_MODAFINIL.description: Modafinil is a novel non-amphetamine central nervous system stimulant primarily used for the treatment of various types of excessive sleepiness disorders, such as narcolepsy, obstructive sleep apnea, and shift work sleep disorder. Compared to traditional stimulants, it generally has milder side effects. However, long-term use still carries a potential risk of drug dependence.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_DIAZEPAM.name: Diazepam
TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_DIAZEPAM.description: Diazepam, also known as Valium, is a long-acting, medium-potency benzodiazepine with the typical benzodiazepine properties of anxiolytic, sedative-hypnotic, and antiepileptic effects. Diazepam is valued for its rapid onset, safety, and effectiveness. However, like other benzodiazepines, it poses risks of dependence, addiction, and misuse with prolonged or improper use. Additionally, diazepam is frequently used in the management of alcohol withdrawal.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_LORAZEPAM.name: Lorazepam
TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_LORAZEPAM.description: Lorazepam, also known as Lora, is a medium-to-short-acting benzodiazepine with potent anxiolytic and hypnotic effects. It is recognized for having one of the strongest anxiolytic effects among benzodiazepines, and it also provides sedative benefits. Lorazepam offers several advantages, including rapid onset of action, safety, and efficacy. However, prolonged or improper use can lead to dependence, addiction, and misuse. In addition to its use in treating anxiety disorders, lorazepam is also commonly used for managing agitation and tension in conditions such as schizophrenia.

TRT_NEW_PSY_INTRAVENOUS_INFUSION_OF_SULP.name: Intravenous Infusion of Sulperazone
TRT_NEW_PSY_INTRAVENOUS_INFUSION_OF_SULP.description: Sulperidone is a benzamide-class antipsychotic medication that can be administered via intravenous infusion to help alleviate symptoms of acute psychosis. However, it is prone to recurrence, often requiring multiple doses to maintain its effects, and it is generally less effective compared to Modified Electroconvulsive Therapy (MECT).

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.name: Sertraline
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.description: Sertraline is a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI) with antidepressant, anxiolytic, and anti-obsessive properties. Due to its relatively low incidence of adverse effects, it is frequently used in children, adolescents, and older adults, particularly for symptoms associated with organic mental disorders.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.name: Fluvoxamine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.description: Fluvoxamine is a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI) that is approved for the treatment of depression and obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD). Clinically, among SSRIs, fluvoxamine is noted for its particularly effective treatment of OCD.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIHYPERTENSIVE_PRAZOSIN.name: Prazosin
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIHYPERTENSIVE_PRAZOSIN.description: Prazosin was originally developed as a traditional antihypertensive medication. However, in recent years, it has been discovered to have significant efficacy in treating nightmares, particularly those associated with post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD). Clinical practice and several research studies have demonstrated that doses of 2 to 6 mg of prazosin can effectively reduce the frequency and severity of nightmares in individuals with PTSD.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.name: Paroxetine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.description: Paroxetine, marketed under the brand name Paxil, is a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI). Clinically, it is used to treat depression, particularly major depressive disorder with anxiety, as well as generalized anxiety disorder, panic disorder, and obsessive-compulsive disorder.

TRT_NEW_PSY_MECT.name: MECT
TRT_NEW_PSY_MECT.description: Modified Electroconvulsive Therapy (MECT) is a treatment method that involves inducing a generalized seizure with a small amount of electrical current under general anesthesia and with the support of oxygenation. It is primarily used for the treatment of major depressive episodes, but it can also be employed for mood disorders, schizophrenia, and for the treatment of neuroleptic malignant syndrome or serotonin syndrome induced by antipsychotic or antidepressant medications.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_TIAPRIDE.name: Sulpride
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_TIAPRIDE.description: Sulpride, marketed as Thiapride, is a traditional benzamide antipsychotic that is not approved in Canada, the United States, or the United Kingdom but is more commonly used in Europe and China. It primarily acts as a dopamine antagonist, mainly targeting the midbrain and limbic system. Sulpride is used for conditions such as Tourette syndrome, chorea, and geriatric psychosis. Common side effects include drowsiness, gastrointestinal reactions, and dizziness or fatigue. It has a lower incidence of extrapyramidal side effects and may cause moderate, transient hyperprolactinemia. Notably, it does not affect cognitive functions or neurophysiological parameters in children, nor does it interfere with the hypothalamic-pituitary regulation of various hormones.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_RISPERIDO.name: Risperidone
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_RISPERIDO.description: Risperidone, a second-generation antipsychotic introduced after clozapine, is now available in a long-acting injectable formulation. It exhibits strong antagonistic effects on central 5-HT receptors, particularly 5-HT2A, and on D2 receptors. Its antagonistic effect on D2 receptors is similar to that of haloperidol. Additionally, risperidone has high affinity for α1 and α2 receptors but lower affinity for β receptors and muscarinic cholinergic receptors. Consequently, it shares similar efficacy with typical antipsychotics in treating positive symptoms of schizophrenia while also showing good effectiveness for negative symptoms. Risperidone has minimal sedative effects and lacks significant anticholinergic side effects.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_ARIPIPRAZ.name: Aripiprazole
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_ARIPIPRAZ.description: Aripiprazole is an atypical antipsychotic and is considered the only third-generation antipsychotic currently available. It functions as a unique serotonin-dopamine activity modulator. Aripiprazole exhibits partial agonistic effects on post-synaptic dopamine D2 receptors, and when dopamine (DA) activity is elevated, it helps reduce DA activity to alleviate positive symptoms of schizophrenia. Aripiprazole is indicated for the treatment of schizophrenia and has recently gained widespread recognition for its efficacy in treating Tourette's disorder, particularly Tourette's syndrome. The drug is noted for its effectiveness and relatively mild side effect profile, including minimal sedation and a low incidence of extrapyramidal symptoms or hyperprolactinemia.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_OLANZAPIN.name: Olanzapine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_OLANZAPIN.description: Olanzapine is a derivative of clozapine and is classified as a second-generation antipsychotic. It is a multi-receptor antagonist that blocks 5-HT2A, D2, D1, and D4 receptors, as well as muscarinic cholinergic receptors (M1), H1 histamine receptors, 5-HT3 receptors, and α1 adrenergic receptors. Its blockade of 5-HT2A receptors is approximately eight times more potent than its blockade of dopamine receptors. Although its pharmacological profile is similar to clozapine, olanzapine has a significantly lower risk of agranulocytosis. Common dose-related adverse effects include sedation, weight gain, and increased appetite, which are considered metabolic side effects.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_QUETIAPIN.name: Quetiapine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_QUETIAPIN.description: Quetiapine is a versatile second-generation antipsychotic with high affinity for 5-HT2, H1, 5-HT6, α1, and α2 receptors, moderate affinity for D2 receptors, and very low affinity for D1, M1, and D4 receptors. While its efficacy in treating positive symptoms of schizophrenia is relatively weak, it has a more pronounced effect on negative symptoms. Additionally, quetiapine is noted for its significant anxiolytic effects among atypical antipsychotics, with extensive research supporting its efficacy. It is also used in the treatment of refractory insomnia and can enhance the effects of antidepressants. It may have some benefit in alleviating agitation in individuals with intellectual disabilities.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_HALOPERID.name: Haloperidol
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIPSYCHOTIC_DRUG_HALOPERID.description: Haloperidol is a high-potency typical (first-generation) antipsychotic medication from the butyrophenone class, noted for its strong D2 receptor antagonism. It is highly effective in treating the positive symptoms of schizophrenia, including hallucinations, delusions, and agitation. Additionally, it is used for managing manic symptoms and behavioral disturbances, such as those associated with Tourette syndrome in children. Due to its side effect profile, particularly the risk of EPS and cardiac issues, haloperidol use has become less common in favor of newer antipsychotics with a more favorable side effect profile.

TRT_NEW_PSY_MOOD_STABILIZER_LITHIUM.name: Lithium Salts (lithium Carbonate)
TRT_NEW_PSY_MOOD_STABILIZER_LITHIUM.description: Lithium salts have various forms and exert complex physiological and pharmacological effects on the brain. The primary lithium compound used in clinical treatment is lithium carbonate, classified as a mood stabilizer. Lithium's broad and effective long-term benefits make it a widely used treatment option for bipolar disorder.

TRT_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOTHERAPY.name: Psychotherapy (Social Psychological Interventions)
TRT_NEW_PSY_PSYCHOTHERAPY.description: In the fields of psychiatry and clinical psychology, psychotherapy refers to a therapeutic approach based on talk therapy guided by psychological principles. It involves a process of interpersonal interaction where a socially recognized therapist helps an individual who is struggling with psychological distress or is unable to fully function.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.name: Escitalopram
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.description: Escitalopram, commonly known under the brand name Lexapro, is the oxalate salt form of escitalopram. It is the S-enantiomer of citalopram and exhibits a serotonin reuptake inhibition effect that is approximately 5 to 7 times stronger than that of citalopram. As a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI), escitalopram is primarily used in clinical practice for the treatment of depression.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.name: Fluoxetine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SSRIS_.description: Fluoxetine, commonly known by the brand name Prozac, is available in the form of fluoxetine hydrochloride capsules. It is an antidepressant that belongs to the class of selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs). Fluoxetine is widely recognized as the first SSRI to be marketed and is primarily used for the treatment of depression. Additionally, it is used as an adjunctive therapy for the treatment of bulimia nervosa and for various other mental disorders, including obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD).

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SNRIS_.name: Duloxetine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SNRIS_.description: Duloxetine is a phenylamine compound and a serotonin-norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor (SNRI). First synthesized in the 1980s, it is used in the treatment of depression, generalized anxiety disorder, and chronic musculoskeletal pain. It is contraindicated for use in combination with monoamine oxidase inhibitors (MAOIs), as well as with other SSRIs and SNRIs.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SNRIS_.name: Venlafaxine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_SNRIS_.description: Venlafaxine, classified as a bicyclic phenylethylamine compound, differs structurally from other antidepressants and anxiolytics. It is a serotonin and norepinephrine reuptake inhibitor (SNRI) used to treat major depressive disorder, generalized anxiety disorder, social anxiety disorder, and panic attacks. Venlafaxine's safety and tolerability are similar to those of SSRIs, though it commonly causes nausea, more frequently than SSRIs, and also has sexual dysfunction as a side effect. Venlafaxine is one of the most commonly reported antidepressants associated with discontinuation syndrome. Additionally, it is used in the preventive treatment of migraines.

TRT_NEW_PSY_SLEEPING_DRUGS_ZS_ZOLPIDEM.name: Zolpidem
TRT_NEW_PSY_SLEEPING_DRUGS_ZS_ZOLPIDEM.description: Zolpidem, marketed under the brand name Ambien, is an imidazopyridine class non-benzodiazepine sedative-hypnotic drug. It is a short-acting hypnotic agent with potent sedative effects, making it particularly effective for patients with difficulty falling asleep. Zolpidem significantly reduces sleep onset latency in individuals with insomnia. It works by selectively agonizing the ω-1 subtype of GABA_A receptors at the benzodiazepine (BZ) site, increasing chloride ion influx and enhancing the inhibitory effects of GABA.

TRT_NEW_PSY_SLEEPING_DRUGS_ZS_ZALEPLON.name: Zaleplon
TRT_NEW_PSY_SLEEPING_DRUGS_ZS_ZALEPLON.description: Zaleplon is a non-benzodiazepine sedative-hypnotic drug that selectively agonizes the ω-1 subtype of GABA_A receptors at the benzodiazepine (BZ) site, leading to an increase in chloride ion influx and potentiation of GABA's inhibitory effects. It has a rapid onset of action and is quickly absorbed when taken orally, effectively reducing sleep onset latency. However, due to its short duration of action, zaleplon does not significantly increase total sleep time or reduce the number of awakenings throughout the night. It is primarily used for the short-term treatment of insomnia characterized by difficulty falling asleep.

TRT_NEW_PSY_SLEEPING_DRUGS_ZS_ESZOPICLON.name: Eszopiclone
TRT_NEW_PSY_SLEEPING_DRUGS_ZS_ESZOPICLON.description: Eszopiclone is the right-handed isomer of zopiclone and is a non-benzodiazepine cyclopyrrolone-class sedative-hypnotic. It is the first medication approved for long-term use to improve both sleep onset and sleep maintenance difficulties. It demonstrates effective treatment for insomnia in both adults and elderly patients, with a rapid onset of action and efficacy lasting up to 6 hours. Eszopiclone is well-tolerated, with no rebound insomnia or withdrawal symptoms upon discontinuation. Possible side effects include dry mouth, dizziness, and drowsiness.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANXIOLYTIC_TANDOSPIRONE.name: Tandospirone
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANXIOLYTIC_TANDOSPIRONE.description: Tandospirone is an azapirone-class anxiolytic medication with a stronger serotonergic effect compared to buspirone. It is used to treat anxiety states associated with various neuroses, such as generalized anxiety disorder, and other conditions accompanied by anxiety. Unlike benzodiazepines or antipsychotics, tandospirone operates through a different mechanism of action, and does not produce sedative, muscle relaxant, or anticonvulsant effects. Its side effects are generally milder.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_CLONAZEPAM.name: Clonazepam
TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_CLONAZEPAM.description: Clonazepam is a potent benzodiazepine with anxiolytic, sedative, hypnotic, and anticonvulsant properties. Benzodiazepines are known for their rapid onset, reliability, and effectiveness; however, long-term or inappropriate use may lead to addiction, dependence, and abuse. Anecdotal evidence suggests that clonazepam may be effective for depersonalization, potentially by preventing sleep deprivation and promoting sleep. It may also address symptoms such as derealization, déjà vu, and reduced attention in depersonalization. Additionally, it is used in the treatment of refractory tic disorders.

TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_ALPRAZOLAM.name: Alprazolam
TRT_NEW_PSY_BZs_ALPRAZOLAM.description: Alprazolam is a benzodiazepine of the 3-benzodiazepine class, characterized by its medium duration and potency. It provides rapid and potent anxiolytic, sedative, and hypnotic effects. Research has also indicated that Alprazolam may possess some antidepressant properties. While benzodiazepines are known for their rapid onset, reliability, and efficacy, long-term or improper use can lead to addiction, dependence, and misuse.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEMENTIA_MEDICINE_MEMANT.name: Memantine
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEMENTIA_MEDICINE_MEMANT.description: Memantine, marketed under the brand name Ebixa, is a non-competitive NMDA receptor antagonist. It is used for the treatment of moderate to severe Alzheimer's disease-related dementia, where it may offer efficacy in managing symptoms.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEMENTIA_MEDICINE_DONEPE.name: Donepezil
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEMENTIA_MEDICINE_DONEPE.description: Donepezil (brand name: Aricept) is a cholinesterase inhibitor with potential cognitive-enhancing effects. It is commonly used in clinical practice for the treatment of Alzheimer's disease and various types of dementia.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_TCM_SH.name: Shu Gan Jie Yu Capsules
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_TCM_SH.description: Shu Gan Jie Yu capsules is an important compound preparation made from Hypericum perforatum and Eleutherococcus senticosus. It is used to soothe the liver, relieve depression, strengthen the spleen, and calm the mind. It is indicated for mild to moderate unipolar depression with symptoms of liver qi stagnation and spleen deficiency.。

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_TCM_WU.name: Wu Ling Capsules
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_TCM_WU.description: Wu Ling capsules are a pure traditional Chinese medicine formulation derived from the rare medicinal fungus Pachyma (Wu Ling Shen) using modern biotechnology. The capsules contain adenosine, polysaccharides, sterols, various amino acids, vitamins, and trace elements, and are effective in treating anxiety and depression. They have sedative and calming properties and are indicated for conditions such as insomnia, depressive and anxious states, migraines, and neuroses.

TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_TCM_BA.name: Bai Lemin Capsules
TRT_NEW_PSY_ANTIDEPRESSSANT_DRUGS_TCM_BA.description: Bai Lemin Capsules are a traditional Chinese medicine that has gained attention for its potential sleep-promoting effects based on clinical observations. The formulation includes ingredients such as Lily Bulb (Bai He), Eleuthero (Ciwujia), Fleeceflower Caulis (Shouwu Teng), Albizia Flower (Hehuan Hua), Mother-of-Pearl (Zhenzhumu), Gypsum (Shigao), Ziziphus Seed (Suanzaoren), Poria (Fuling), Polygala Root (Yuanzhi), Scrophularia Root (Xuanshen), Rehmannia Root (Dihuang), Ophiopogon Root (Maidong), Schisandra (Wuweizi), Juncus Pith (Dengxincao), and Salvia Root (Danshen). The capsules are believed to nourish Yin, clear heat, calm the mind, and are indicated for insomnia of the Yin deficiency with internal heat type.

TRT_JM_CHECKUP.name: Whole Body Examination
TRT_JM_CHECKUP.description: The doctor gives the patient a whole body examination or also known as a Checkup.

TRT_JM_LIFESTYLE_CHANGES.name: Lifestyle Changes
TRT_JM_LIFESTYLE_CHANGES.description: The doctor recommends that the patient changes their lifetyle to be more active and healthy.

TRT_JM_COURSE_OF_MEDICATIONS.name: A Course of Medication/Supplements
TRT_JM_COURSE_OF_MEDICATIONS.description: The doctor gives the patient a course of medication or supplements to treat existing diseases, minor problems or disorders.

TRT_JM_COUNSELING.name: Counseling
TRT_JM_COUNSELING.description: Counselling is a talking therapy that involves a trained therapist listening to you and helping you find ways to deal with emotional issues. Sometimes the term "counselling" is used to refer to talking therapies in general, but counselling is also a type of therapy in its own right.

TRT_JM_INSULIN_THERAPY.name: Insulin Therapy
TRT_JM_INSULIN_THERAPY.description: Medication that lowers the level of blood sugar. \n\nRequired room: Office / Diagnostic unit at Internal Medicine dpt.

TRT_JM_MMR_VACCINATION.name: MMR Vaccination
TRT_JM_MMR_VACCINATION.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Mumps, Measles and Rubella (MMR) vaccine will be administered prevent Mumps, Measles and Rubella.

TRT_JM_INFLU_VACCINATION.name: Influenza Vaccination
