DIA_062.description: Meniere's disease is an inner ear disorder that is characterized by a vertigo with unclear cause.

DIA_061.name: Nasal Polyp
DIA_061.description: Nasal polyp is a non-cancerous growth in the nasal cavity.

DIA_ANS.name: Glomerulonephritis
DIA_ANS.description: Glomerulonephritis refers to inflammation of the filtering unit of the kidneys. It can be caused by an infection or autoimmunity.

DIA_TIN.name: Interstitial Nephritis
DIA_TIN.description: Interstitial nephritis is an inflammation of the interstitial tissue of the kidneys. It can be caused by an infection or as an adverse reaction to medication.

DIA_PNS.name: Pyelonephritis
DIA_PNS.description: Pyelonephritis is inflammation of the kidney, usually due to ascending bacteria spreading up through the urinary tract.

DIA_ARF.name: Acute Renal Failure
DIA_ARF.description: Acute renal failure refers to an abrupt loss of kidney function, effectively seizing the ability to filter the blood and produce urine. The causes can be many, but are usually due to extensive tissue damage or decreased kidney blood flow.

DIA_CKD.name: Chronic Kidney Disease
DIA_CKD.description: Chronic kidney disease refers to a gradual loss of kidney function. The most common cause is untreated diabetes mellitus.

DIA_COK.name: Kidney Stones
DIA_COK.description: Kidney stones, also known as urolithiasis, are usually concretions of calcium. Even though the stones usually pass on their own through the urinary tract, it is an extremely painful condition.

DIA_047.name: HUS
DIA_047.description: Hemolytic uremic syndrome refers to the condition when small blood vessels in kidneys become damaged and inflamed. In most cases an infection of Escherichia coli is responsible.

TRM_KCB.name: Renal Contusion
TRM_KCB.description: Renal contusion refers to bruising of the kidney (latin: ren) after a direct impact to the upper lower back.

TRM_KLP.name: Renal Laceration
TRM_KLP.description: Renal laceration occurs when an object pierces the tissue of the body and tears the kidney.

DIA_COU.name: Calculus of Ureter
DIA_COU.description: Kidney stones, also known as urolithiasis, are usually concretions of calcium. Even though the stones usually pass on their own through the urinary tract, they can sometimes get stuck in the ureter, which is an extremely painful condition.

DIA_CIB.name: Calculus in Bladder
DIA_CIB.description: Bladder stones can be formed in the bladder due to a mineralization of concentrated urine. There will only be symptoms if the flow of urine is compromised.

DIA_ACS.name: Urinary Tract Infection
DIA_ACS.description: UTF, or cystitis, is an infection of the bladder usually by E. coli; a type of bacteria commonly found in the gastrointestinal (GI) tract. It is a condition a hundred times more common in women due to a shorter urinary tract.

DIA_INC.name: Interstitial Cystitis
DIA_INC.description: Interstitial cystitis is also called painful bladder syndrome.

SYM_BLOOD_LOSS.name: Blood Loss
SYM_BLOOD_LOSS.description: Also called hypovolemia, describes a significant loss of blood volume.

SYM_CEREBROSPINAL_FLUID_LEAKAGE.name: CSF Leak
SYM_CEREBROSPINAL_FLUID_LEAKAGE.description: The patient has cerebrospinal fluid leaking out of their nose or ear due to a defect in their skull.

SYM_GALLBLADDER_RUPTURE.name: Gallbladder Rupture
SYM_GALLBLADDER_RUPTURE.description: The gallbladder of the patient has ruptured!

SYM_HEART_FAILURE.name: Cardiac Arrest
SYM_HEART_FAILURE.description: Cardiac arrest is a sudden loss of blood flow resulting from the failure of the heart to pump effectively.

SYM_HEMORRHAGE.name: Hemorrhage
SYM_HEMORRHAGE.description: Hemorrhage, also known as bleeding, is the escape of blood from a ruptured blood vessel.

SYM_HYPERPYREXIA.name: High Fever
SYM_HYPERPYREXIA.description: The patient has a body temperature over 39.5 °C (103.1 °F).

SYM_INTRACRANIAL_HEMORRHAGE.name: Intracranial Hemorrhage
SYM_INTRACRANIAL_HEMORRHAGE.description: The patient is bleeding within the skull.

SYM_MULTIPLE_ARTERIES_DAMAGE.name: Multiple Arteries Damage
SYM_MULTIPLE_ARTERIES_DAMAGE.description: Damage to multiple smaller arteries due to a large wound.

SYM_SKULL_BASIS_FRACTURE.name: Skull Basis Fracture
SYM_SKULL_BASIS_FRACTURE.description: Fracture of a bone in the base of the skull.

SYM_SKULL_FRACTURE.name: Skull Fracture
SYM_SKULL_FRACTURE.description: Fracture of various bones in skull.

SYM_UNSTOPPABLE_BLEEDING.name: Unstoppable Bleeding
SYM_UNSTOPPABLE_BLEEDING.description: Also called hemorrhaging is a condition in which blood quickly escapes the circulatory system. Unstoppable bleeding can be due to a large wound or due to damage of multiple veins or arteries.

SYM_CARDIAC_TAMPONADE.name: Cardiac Tamponade
SYM_CARDIAC_TAMPONADE.description: A life-threatening post-surgical condition where the pericardium (a sac surrounding the heart) becomes filled with blood, making it very difficult for the heart to pump.

SYM_ALVEOLAR_COLLAPSE.name: Alveolar Collapse
SYM_ALVEOLAR_COLLAPSE.description: Collapse of the smallest parts of the lung tissue (the alveoli) following sedation and mechanical ventilation during surgery.

SYM_ABSCESS.name: Abscess
SYM_ABSCESS.description: Buildup of pus within the tissue surrounding a surgical wound.

SYM_SEPSIS.name: Sepsis
SYM_SEPSIS.description: Post-surgical complication and a life-threatening condition caused by the body's own response to an infection.

SYM_ATHEROSCLEROSIS.name: Atherosclerosis
SYM_ATHEROSCLEROSIS.description: A narrowing of arteries due to built up plaque compromising blood flow.

SYM_LDL_HIGH_LEVEL.name: Elevated LDL
SYM_LDL_HIGH_LEVEL.description: Raised LDL cholesterol blood levels above normal ranges.

SYM_PULMONARY_ARTERY_BLOCKAGE.name: Pulmonary Artery Blockage
SYM_PULMONARY_ARTERY_BLOCKAGE.description: Angiography has revealed blockage of patient's pulmonary artery by blood clots.

SYM_CARDIOMEGALY.name: Cardiomegaly
SYM_CARDIOMEGALY.description: An abnormal enlargement of the heart.

SYM_INTERNAL_BLEEDING_STOMACH.name: Bleeding to Stomach
SYM_INTERNAL_BLEEDING_STOMACH.description: Gastrocopy discovered a bleeding stomach ulcer; a wound in the stomach lining.

SYM_COMA.name: Coma
SYM_COMA.description: The patient is in a state of deep unconsciousness and cannot be woken up.

SYM_DEHYDRATION.name: Dehydration
SYM_DEHYDRATION.description: The patient has insufficient water in their body.

SYM_DIARRHEA.name: Diarrhea
SYM_DIARRHEA.description: The patient has frequent bowel discharges with faeces in liquid form.

SYM_DYSPNEA.name: Dyspnea
SYM_DYSPNEA.description: Sudden breathing difficulties, known also as shortness of breath.

SYM_EXHAUSTION.name: Severe Dehyrdation
SYM_EXHAUSTION.description: The patient has lost a very large volume of water and bodily fluids. A medical emergency requiring immediate and aggressive rehydration!

SYM_FAINTING.name: Fainting
SYM_FAINTING.description: Also called syncope, is a temporary loss of consciousness.

SYM_HYPERTENSION.name: Hypertension
SYM_HYPERTENSION.description: Hypertension, also known as high blood pressure, is a condition where the blood flows with a pressure above normal range.

SYM_IRREGULAR_HEARTBEAT.name: Irregular Heartbeat
SYM_IRREGULAR_HEARTBEAT.description: The patient has an irregular heart rate which is also known as arrhythmia.

SYM_TACHYCARDIA.name: Tachycardia
SYM_TACHYCARDIA.description: Tachycardia (elevated heart rate) is a common symptom mediated by the sympathetic nervous system's flight or fight response and occurs in various diseases or when one is exposed to dangers.

SYM_RESPIRATORY_FAILURE.name: Respiratory Failure
SYM_RESPIRATORY_FAILURE.description: Resulting from inadequate gas exchange by the respiratory system.

SYM_SEPTIC_SHOCK.name: Septic Shock
SYM_SEPTIC_SHOCK.description: Life-threatening complication of infections that can damage multiple organ systems, causing them to fail.

SYM_HEAD_TRAUMA.name: Head Trauma
SYM_HEAD_TRAUMA.description: The patient has suffered an injury to the head.

SYM_INJURY_TO_THE_HEAD.name: Head Injury
SYM_INJURY_TO_THE_HEAD.description: The patient has suffered an injury to the head.

SYM_UNSTABLE_BLOOD_PRESSURE.name: Unstable Blood Pressure
SYM_UNSTABLE_BLOOD_PRESSURE.description: Unstable blood pressure as a reaction to surgery.

SYM_HEMATURIA.name: Hematuria
SYM_HEMATURIA.description: Hematuria is the presence of blood in the urine.

SYM_NECROSIS.name: Necrosis
SYM_NECROSIS.description: Premature death of a tissue caused by its own enzymes. It occurs when too little blood flows to the tissue. This can be cause by an injury, radiation, or chemicals. Necrosis cannot be reversed.

SYM_BRAIN_HEMATOMAS.name: Brain Hematomas
SYM_BRAIN_HEMATOMAS.description: A CT scan has discovered both an epidural and an intracerebral hematoma.

SYM_HEART_ATTACK.name: Heart Attack
SYM_HEART_ATTACK.description: Electrocardiography has detected an ongoing heart attack.

SYM_HYPERTENSIVE_FINDINGS.name: Hypertensive Findings
SYM_HYPERTENSIVE_FINDINGS.description: Echocardiography discovered left ventricular hypertrophy and remodeling, diminished coronary flow reserve and silent myocardial ischemia.

SYM_OPEN_HEAD_WOUND.name: Open Head Wound
SYM_OPEN_HEAD_WOUND.description: The patient has suffered an open wound in the head.

SYM_PULMONARY_DAMAGE.name: Collapsed Lung
SYM_PULMONARY_DAMAGE.description: X-ray imaging has revealed pulmonary damage giving rise to a pneumothorax (collapsed lung) due to air escaping into the chest cavity from the damaged lung.(.

SYM_ARTERIAL_STENOSIS.name: Arterial Stenosis
SYM_ARTERIAL_STENOSIS.description: Coronary angiography has shown narrowing of the coronary arteries (the arteries supplying the heart with blood).

SYM_PLEURAL_TRANSUDATE.name: Pleural Transudate
SYM_PLEURAL_TRANSUDATE.description: MRI has detected the presence of pleural transudate.

SYM_NARROWED_ARTERIES.name: Narrowed Arteries
SYM_NARROWED_ARTERIES.description: Angiography has discovered narrowed arteries that need to be treated surgically.

SYM_AORTIC_ANEURYSM.name: Aortic Aneurysm
SYM_AORTIC_ANEURYSM.description: Echocardiography has discovered an aneurysm of the thoracic aorta.

SYM_B._ANTHRACIS_DETECTED.name: B. Anthracis Detected
SYM_B._ANTHRACIS_DETECTED.description: Technologists using polymerase chain reaction (PCR) have detected Bacillus anthracis in the patient's blood sample.

SYM_B._HENSELAE_DETECTED.name: B. Henselae Detected
SYM_B._HENSELAE_DETECTED.description: Technologists using polymerase chain reaction (PCR) have detected Bartonella henselae in the patient's blood sample.

SYM_BRONCHIAL_HYPERREACTIVITY.name: Bronchial Hyperreactivity
SYM_BRONCHIAL_HYPERREACTIVITY.description: A special bronchial test indicates that the patient suffers from asthma.

SYM_CARBOXYHEMOGLOBIN_HIGH_LEVEL.name: COHb High Level
SYM_CARBOXYHEMOGLOBIN_HIGH_LEVEL.description: A blood test has revealed high levels of carboxyhemoglobin in the patient's blood, suggestive of carbon monoxide poisoning.

SYM_CAROTID_ARTERIES_BLOCKED.name: Carotid Arteries Blocked
SYM_CAROTID_ARTERIES_BLOCKED.description: Ultrasonography with Doppler-techqniue has revealed a blockage of the carotid arteries due blood clots.

SYM_BRAIN_ARTERIES_BLOCKED.name: Brain Arteries Blocked
SYM_BRAIN_ARTERIES_BLOCKED.description: Diagnostic imaging has revealed the blockage of a vital blood vessel in the brain due to blood clots.

SYM_CEREBRAL_CONTUSION_FINDINGS.name: Cerebral Contusion
SYM_CEREBRAL_CONTUSION_FINDINGS.description: A CT scan of the patient's brain has revealed cerebral contusion.

SYM_CONCUSSION.name: Concussion
SYM_CONCUSSION.description: A CT scan of the patient's brain is suggestive of a concussion.

SYM_DIFFUSE_AXONAL_INJURY.name: Diffuse Axonal Injury
SYM_DIFFUSE_AXONAL_INJURY.description: MRI scan has revealed multiple findings that are suggestive of diffuse axonal injury.

SYM_E._HISTOLYTICA_DISCOVERED.name: E. Histolytica Discovered
SYM_E._HISTOLYTICA_DISCOVERED.description: Bacterial cultivation has detected Entamoeba histolytica in the patient's stool sample.

SYM_ENDOCARDITIS_DETECTED.name: Endocarditis Detected
SYM_ENDOCARDITIS_DETECTED.description: Echocardiography has discovered a vegatative growth on a valve in the heart, strongly suggestive of endocarditis.

SYM_F._TULARENSIS_DETECTED.name: F. Tularensis Detected
SYM_F._TULARENSIS_DETECTED.description: Technologists detected Francisella tularensis in a bacterial cultivation of the patient's sputum.

SYM_FLAIL_CHEST.name: Flail Chest
SYM_FLAIL_CHEST.description: The CT scan has revealed multiple broken ribs, isolating a part of the patient's chest from the remainder of the chest wall.

SYM_GIARDIA_LAMBLIA_DISCOVERED.name: Giardia Lamblia Detected
SYM_GIARDIA_LAMBLIA_DISCOVERED.description: Bacterial cultivation has detected Giardia lamblia in the patient's stool sample.

SYM_GRANULOMAS_HEART.name: Granulomas - Heart
SYM_GRANULOMAS_HEART.description: MRI has revealed granulomas affecting the heart.

SYM_H._PYLORI_DETECTED.name: H. Pylori Detected
SYM_H._PYLORI_DETECTED.description: Tests found specific antibodies related to Helicobacter pylori.

SYM_HEART_FAILING.name: Heart Failing
SYM_HEART_FAILING.description: Echocardiography has revealed regurgitation of the tricuspid valve.

SYM_INFLAMMED_MYOCARDIUM.name: Inflamed Myocardium
SYM_INFLAMMED_MYOCARDIUM.description: MRI scan has discovered signs of inflammation of the heart's muscle.

SYM_INFLAMMED_THYROID.name: Inflamed Thyroid
SYM_INFLAMMED_THYROID.description: Laboratory analysis of bioptical material from the thyroid discovered a large inflammation of the gland.

SYM_LUNG_TISSUE_FINDINGS.name: Lung Tissue Findings
SYM_LUNG_TISSUE_FINDINGS.description: CT scan has revealed serious bacterial infection in lungs and in bronchi.

SYM_MEDIAN_NERVE_COMPRESSION.name: Median Nerve Compression
SYM_MEDIAN_NERVE_COMPRESSION.description: A CT scan has discovered compression of the median nerve in the hand.

SYM_LEAD_LEVELS_HIGH.name: Lead Levels High
SYM_LEAD_LEVELS_HIGH.description: Very high levels of lead in blood.

SYM_LYME_DISEASE_DETECTED.name: Borrelia Antibodies Detected
SYM_LYME_DISEASE_DETECTED.description: Serology test has detected Borrelia antibodies.

SYM_M._TUBERCULOSIS_DETECTED.name: Tuberculosis Detected
SYM_M._TUBERCULOSIS_DETECTED.description: Mycobacterium tuberculosis has been detected by polymerase chain reaction.

SYM_MAC_DETECTED.name: MAC Detected
SYM_MAC_DETECTED.description: Mycobacterium avium complex has been detected by polymerase chain reaction.

SYM_MITRAL_STENOSIS.name: Mitral Stenosis
SYM_MITRAL_STENOSIS.description: Echocardiography has detected stenosis of heart's mitral valve.

SYM_OBSTRUCTED_AIRWAYS.name: Obstructed Airways
SYM_OBSTRUCTED_AIRWAYS.description: A spirometry test has proven that the patient suffers from an obstructive pulmonary disease.

SYM_P._MENINGITIS.name: Streptococcus Pneumoniae
SYM_P._MENINGITIS.description: Microbial cultivation of spinal fluid has detected the presence of Streptococcus pneumoniae, which is known to cause meningitis.

SYM_PANCREATIC_ELASTASE_LOW_LEVEL.name: Elastase - Low Level
SYM_PANCREATIC_ELASTASE_LOW_LEVEL.description: A fecal elastase test has shown low levels of pancreatic elastase in a stool sample.

SYM_PANCREATIC_TISSUE_INFLAMMED.name: Pancreatic Inflammation
SYM_PANCREATIC_TISSUE_INFLAMMED.description: Diagnostic imaging shows inflammation of the pancreas.

SYM_PSEUDOFRACTURES.name: Pseudofractures
SYM_PSEUDOFRACTURES.description: CT scan has revealed multiple pseudofractures in the patient's body.

SYM_SWOLLEN_PERICARDIUM.name: Swollen Pericardium
SYM_SWOLLEN_PERICARDIUM.description: CT scan has revealed a swollen and irritated pericardium (inflammation of the sac that the heart lies in).

SYM_CLAVICLE_FRACTURE.name: Clavicle Fracture
SYM_CLAVICLE_FRACTURE.description: A CT scan has shown a simple fracture of the clavicle.

SYM_CLAVICLE_SHAFT_FRACTURE.name: Clavicle Shaft Fracture
SYM_CLAVICLE_SHAFT_FRACTURE.description: A CT scan has shown a fracture of the shaft of the clavicle.

SYM_EMPHYSEMA_DISCOVERED.name: Emphysema Discovered
SYM_EMPHYSEMA_DISCOVERED.description: The CT scan has revealed emphysema in the lungs.

SYM_PNEUMONIC_LUNGS.name: Pneumonic Lungs
SYM_PNEUMONIC_LUNGS.description: An x-ray showing infiltrates in the patient's lungs together with examination of the patient has resulted in a diagnosis of pneumonia.

SYM_PORTAL_VEIN_BLOCKED.name: Portal Vein Blocked
SYM_PORTAL_VEIN_BLOCKED.description: Computed tomography (CT) with angiography (visualisation of the arteries or veins) has discovered a blood clot blocking the portal vein.

SYM_STOOL_CONTAINING_BACTERIAS.name: Campylobacter in Stool
SYM_STOOL_CONTAINING_BACTERIAS.description: Bacterial cultivation from a stool sample confirmed the presence of Campylobacter.

SYM_TBE_DETECTED.name: TBE Detected
SYM_TBE_DETECTED.description: CSF analysis has detected TBE (Tick Borne Encephalitis) virus.

SYM_TRYPANOSOMA_DETECTED.name: Trypanosoma Detected
SYM_TRYPANOSOMA_DETECTED.description: Serology testing has revealed Trypanosoma cruzi antibodies in a patient's blood sample.

SYM_TSH_LEVEL_LOW.name: TSH - Low Level
SYM_TSH_LEVEL_LOW.description: Low levels of TSH (thyroid stimulating hormone) in the blood indicates hyperthyroidism (overactive thyroid gland).

SYM_E_COLI_TOXIN_DETECTED.name: E. Coli Toxin Detected
SYM_E_COLI_TOXIN_DETECTED.description: Stool analysis has detected toxin produced by E. coli.

SYM_TIA_DETECTED.name: TIA Detected
SYM_TIA_DETECTED.description: MRI has detected elements of transient ischemic attack.

SYM_C_BOTULINUM_DETECTED.name: C. Botulinum Detected
SYM_C_BOTULINUM_DETECTED.description: ELISA has detected the presence of Clostridium botulinum.

SYM_ASBESTOSIS_DETECTED.name: Asbestosis Detected
SYM_ASBESTOSIS_DETECTED.description: CT examination discovered asbestosis in patient's lungs.

SYM_HEMOTHORAX_DISCOVERED.name: Hemothorax Discovered
SYM_HEMOTHORAX_DISCOVERED.description: Sonogaphic examination discovered a large amount of blood in patient's pleural cavity.

SYM_THROMBOCYTHEMIA.name: Thrombocythemia
SYM_THROMBOCYTHEMIA.description: Complete blood count revealed an abnormally high count of thrombocytes.

SYM_INFLAMMED_BILDUCT.name: Biliary Dilation Due to Obstruction
SYM_INFLAMMED_BILDUCT.description: Diagnostic imaging reveals an obstructed and inflamed bile duct. This finding with the overall clinical picture suggests a diagnosis of Acute Cholangitis.

SYM_CLOSED_ANGLE.name: Closed Angle
SYM_CLOSED_ANGLE.description: Gonioscopic examination has revealed a closed angle between the iris and cornea, suggestive of closed angle glaucoma.

SYM_PENETRATING_KIDNEY_LACERATION.name: Kidney Laceration
SYM_PENETRATING_KIDNEY_LACERATION.description: FAST (Focused Assessment with Sonography in Trauma) examination has revealed a laceration of the kidney resulting in an internal hemorrhage.

SYM_KIDNEY_TISSUE_DAMAGED.name: Kidney Tissue Damaged
SYM_KIDNEY_TISSUE_DAMAGED.description: MRI of kidney tissue has proven a certain level of damage.

SYM_KIDNEY_PROGRESSIVE_DAMAGE.name: Kidney Progressive Damage
SYM_KIDNEY_PROGRESSIVE_DAMAGE.description: CT examination has discovered progressive damage to the kidney tissue.

EXM_ORAL_CAVITY_INSPECTION.name: Oral Cavity Inspection
EXM_ORAL_CAVITY_INSPECTION.description: Examination and visual inspection of the patient's oral cavity. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit at all departments except Orthopaedy dpt and Neurology

EXM_INTERVIEW.name: Interview
EXM_INTERVIEW.description: Interviewing the patient to get as much information as possible about their medical condition. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit

EXM_URGENT_BLOOD_ANALYSIS.name: PS Blood Control
EXM_URGENT_BLOOD_ANALYSIS.description:
Post surgery blood analysis is used in post-operative care in patients that could suffer from sepsis. 

Required room: Cardiography unit 

Required equipment: Lab analyzer

EXM_LARYNGOSCOPY.name: Laryngoscopy
EXM_LARYNGOSCOPY.description: A procedure where a flexible tube (endoscope) with a tiny video camera and a light source in the end is inserted through the nose to examine the larynx, glottis, and vocal chords. Laryngoscopy is also used to facilitate tracheal intubation. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Trauma center / ICU / Department of Diagnostics

EXM_GASTROSCOPY.name: Gastroscopy
EXM_GASTROSCOPY.description: A procedure where a flexible tube (endoscope) with a tiny video camera and a light source in the end is inserted through the mouth in order to examine the upper part of digestive tract.\n\nRequired room: ICU / Office / Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Cardiology dpt / Department of Diagnostics

EXM_AUDIOMETRY.name: Audiometry
EXM_AUDIOMETRY.description: Audiometric testing determines the patient's level of hearing. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / ICU / Office / Diagnostic unit at Emergency / ICU / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Department of Diagnostics

EXM_URINE_SAMPLE_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.name: Urine Analysis - Sampling
EXM_URINE_SAMPLE_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.description:
Sampling of mid-stream urine from a patient, or from a patient's catheter. 

Required room: Histology Lab 

Sampling: Emergency dpt / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Cardiology dpt / ICU / DID / Traumatology dpt 

Required equipment: Glassware + Lab analyzer

EXM_CRP.name: CRP
EXM_CRP.description: C-reactive protein (CRP) is diagnostic test based on a quick blood test measuring the concentration of C-reactive protein that is indicative of inflammatory and infectious diseases. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit / Observation room / Trauma center / ICU at Any department except Neurology dpt

EXM_CBC_SAMPLING.name: CBC - Sampling
EXM_CBC_SAMPLING.description:
The sampling of blood for a Complete Blood Count (CBC) test. 

Required room: Hematology Lab 

Sampling: Emergency dpt / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Orthopaedy dpt / Neurology dpt / ICU / DID / Traumatology dpt 

Required equipment: Glassware + Hematoanalyzer

EXM_SPINAL_FLUID_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.name: CSF Sampling
EXM_SPINAL_FLUID_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.description:
Sampling of cerebrospinal fluid is performed by inserting a needle through the lower back into the spinal canal, draining a small amount of the fluid. 

Required room: Histology Lab 

Sampling: Neurology dpt / DID 

Required equipment: Glassware + Medical centrifuge + Lab analyzer

EXM_STOOL_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.name: Stool Collecting
EXM_STOOL_ANALYSIS_SAMPLING.description:
Procedure to collect a sample of patient's stool for further analysis. 

Required room: Histology Lab 

Sampling: Emergency dpt / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / ICU / DID 

Required equipment: Glassware + Stool analyzer

EXM_OPHTHALMOSCOPY.name: Ophthalmoscopy
EXM_OPHTHALMOSCOPY.description: A procedure where the fundus/retina is visualised through a funduscope/opthalmoscope. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Trauma center / ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at Emergency / ICU / Internal Medicine dpt / Neurology dpt / Traumatology dpt

EXM_PERIMETRY.name: Perimetry
EXM_PERIMETRY.description: Perimetry is ophtalmology test that systematically tests the complete field of vision. \n\nRequired room: Neuroexam Unit at Neurology dpt

EXM_TONOMETRY.name: Tonometry
EXM_TONOMETRY.description: Test that measures pressure inside the eye also known as intraocular pressure. \n\nRequired room: Neuroexam unit

EXM_GONIOSCOPY.name: Gonioscopy
EXM_GONIOSCOPY.description: Gonioscopy is an eye examination used to evaluate the drainage system of the eye. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office / Diagnostic unit at Neurology

EXM_BASIC_VISUAL_TEST.name: Basic Visual Test
EXM_BASIC_VISUAL_TEST.description:
Common visual test used to examine the patient's visual acuity. 

Required room: Any office at Emergency / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Cardiology / Neurology / DID 

Required equipment: Eye test

EXM_NEUROLOGICAL_TESTING.name: Neurological Testing
EXM_NEUROLOGICAL_TESTING.description: Different simple and specific questions and tests that provide information about the patient's central nervous system. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit at all departments except Orthopaedy dpt

EXM_SKIN_INJECTION_TEST.name: Skin Allergy Test
EXM_SKIN_INJECTION_TEST.description: Injection of a small amount of different allergen extracts into the skin usually on the arm to confirm or deny the allergic reactions to specific allergens. \n\nRequired room: Offices / Diagnostic units at Emergency dpt / ICU dpt / Neurology dpt

TRT_SALINE_NASAL_SPRAY.name: Saline Nasal Spray
TRT_SALINE_NASAL_SPRAY.description: A nasal spray that rinses nasal cavities and increases ventilation through the nose. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Office/Diagnostic unit at DID

TRT_VASODILATORS.name: Vasodilators
TRT_VASODILATORS.description: Medication that relaxes blood vessels. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at Cardiology dpt

TRT_ANTI_INFLAMMATORY_CREAM.name: Anti Inflammatory Cream
TRT_ANTI_INFLAMMATORY_CREAM.description: Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory cream used to treat various inflammations and also relieve from pain or swelling. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit at any department except Neurology dpt

TRT_COLD_TILING.name: Ice Wrap
TRT_COLD_TILING.description: Cold compression wraps are used to calm down painful areas of the body. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit / ICU

TRT_ANTICHOLINERGICS.name: Anticholinergics
TRT_ANTICHOLINERGICS.description: Most commonly used medication in the treatment of excessive sweating. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / ICU / Office / Diagnostic unit at Internal Medicine dpt / Cardiology dpt / Neurology dpt / DID / Traumatology dpt

TRT_MIEP.name: MIEP
TRT_MIEP.description:
Minimally-invasive endoscopic procedure is used to treat various otorhinolaryngological diseases. 

Required room: Special procedures unit 

Required equipment: ENT Workstation

TRT_EAR_DROPS.name: Ear Drops
TRT_EAR_DROPS.description: Medicine in liquid drops that treats specific symptoms in the human ear. Can contain various healing substances including antibiotics. Office / Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Neurology dpt

TRT_DESMOPRESSIN.name: Desmopressin
TRT_DESMOPRESSIN.description: Synthetic hormone that has an anti-diuretic effect, and can help stimulate factors responsible for the blood clotting process, for patient with genetic blood clotting disorders. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at Internal Medicine dpt

TRT_ANTITAPEWORM_PILLS.name: Antihelmintics
TRT_ANTITAPEWORM_PILLS.description: Pills that are used in the treatment of tapeworm infections. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room

TRT_ANTI_SEIZURES_MEDICINE.name: Anti Seizures Medicine
TRT_ANTI_SEIZURES_MEDICINE.description: Medicine used to treat various types and forms of seizures. \n\nRequired room: Any diagnostic unit / ICU

TRT_ANTI_ARRHYTMICALS.name: Antiarrhythmics
TRT_ANTI_ARRHYTMICALS.description: Drugs usually given intravenously or as pills to suppress abnormal rhythms of the heart. \n\nRequired room: ICU /  Any office / Diagnostic unit at Emergency dpt / General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Neurology dpt / Traumatology dpt

TRT_ANTIVIROTICS.name: Antivirals
TRT_ANTIVIROTICS.description: Medication that inhibits viral infections. Unlike antibiotics, antiviral drugs do not directly destroy the pathogen. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit ar General Surgery dpt / Neurology dpt / DID

TRT_ANTIDIARRHEALS.name: Antidiarrheals
TRT_ANTIDIARRHEALS.description: Drugs that stop diarrhea, relieve the symptoms, but don't eliminate the cause. They usually work by decreasing bowel movement. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Trauma center / ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / DID

TRT_TPA.name: IV TPA
TRT_TPA.description: Intravenous Tissue Plasminogen Activator is a medicine injected into the bloodstream that dissolves the blood clot blocking the blood vessel in the brain. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Trauma Center in Emergency Department / Observation Room in Emergency Department / Regular Ward / HDU / Neurology Department

TRT_THROMBOLYTICS.name: Thrombolytics
TRT_THROMBOLYTICS.description: Special medications that solves and destroy formed clots in blood stream, which reducing passage of the blood to the organs. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Trauma center Office / Diagnostic unit at Cardiology dpt

TRT_IRON_SUPPLEMENT.name: Iron Supplement
TRT_IRON_SUPPLEMENT.description: Supplement of iron. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room

TRT_INCISION_AND_DRAINAGE.name: Incision and Drainage
TRT_INCISION_AND_DRAINAGE.description: Incision and drainage of an abscesses. \n\nRequired room: Any Diagnostic unit / ICU / Observation room / Doctor's office

TRT_ANTIPSYCHOTICS.name: Antipsychotics
TRT_ANTIPSYCHOTICS.description: Medication used in the treatment of various psychiatric disorders. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at Internal Medicine dpt / Neurology dpt

TRT_CORTICOSTEROID_INJECTIONS.name: Corticosteroid Injections
TRT_CORTICOSTEROID_INJECTIONS.description: Synthetic steroid hormones is used to treat inflammation, and used on a broad spectrum of diseases. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at Orthopaedy dpt

TRT_BONE_STRENGTH_PILLS.name: Bone Strengthening Pills
TRT_BONE_STRENGTH_PILLS.description: Supportive drugs used to improve the strength of the bones. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Office/Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Orthopaedy dpt

TRT_BRONCHODILATORS.name: Bronchodilators
TRT_BRONCHODILATORS.description: Medication that dilates bronchi and bronchioles, decreasing the airways resistance, and therefore increasing the airflow to the lungs. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit / ICU except General Surgery dpt

TRT_ANXIOLYTICS.name: Anxiolytics
TRT_ANXIOLYTICS.description: Medication that inhibits anxiety. \n\nRequired room: Any office / Diagnostic unit at any department except Orthopaedy dpt

TRT_CLOT_PREVENTING_DRUGS.name: Anticoagulants
TRT_CLOT_PREVENTING_DRUGS.description: Drugs preventing blood platelets to stick to each other and forming clots. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office / Diagnostic unit at Cardiology dpt / General Surgery dpt / Department of Diagnostics

TRT_INTRAVENOUS_IMMUNOGLOBULIN.name: IV Immunoglobulin
TRT_INTRAVENOUS_IMMUNOGLOBULIN.description: A purified blood product containing antibodies delivered by the intravenous (IV) route. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at Orthopaedy dpt / Neurology dpt / Department of Diagnostics

TRT_DIURETICS.name: Diuretics
TRT_DIURETICS.description: Medications that lower the amount of sodium and water in the body. \n\nRequired room: ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Cardiology / Neurology / Department of Diagnostics

TRT_DEBRIDEMENT.name: Debridement
TRT_DEBRIDEMENT.description: Medical removal of death tissue. \n\nRequired room: Office/Diagnostic unit / Burn unit at Traumatology dpt. / ICU / Trauma center at Emergency / Department of Diagnostics

TRT_HYDROXYUREA.name: Hydroxyurea
TRT_HYDROXYUREA.description: Special medication that suppresses production of blood cells in bone marrow. \n\nRequired room: Diagnostic unit at Internal medicine dpt / ICU

TRT_MANNITOL_DOSES.name: Mannitol Doses
TRT_MANNITOL_DOSES.description: Mannitol doses are delivered to human body to fight intoxication by ciguatoxin. \n\nRequired room: Diagnostic unit at Internal medicine dpt / ICU

TRT_ANTIDEPRESSIVES.name: Antidepressants
TRT_ANTIDEPRESSIVES.description: Drugs used to treat depressive disorders. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Office/Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Neurology dpt

TRT_B-COMPLEX_SUPPLEMENT.name: B-Complex Supplement
TRT_B-COMPLEX_SUPPLEMENT.description: Supplement of B-vitamins. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Office/Diagnostic unit at Internal Medicine dpt

TRT_EYE_DROPS.name: Eye Drops
TRT_EYE_DROPS.description: Medicine in liquid state that treats specific symptoms or diseases in the eye. Either contains an antibacterial agent, anti-histamines or drugs to decrease the blood pressure inside the eye.\n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at any department except Orthopaedy dpt

TRT_ANTIBIOTIC_OINTMENT.name: Antibiotic Ointment
TRT_ANTIBIOTIC_OINTMENT.description: Special ointment applied to infected areas on the skin. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Office/Diagnostic unit at Neurology dpt

TRT_ANTIHYPERTENSIVES.name: Antihypertensive Medication
TRT_ANTIHYPERTENSIVES.description: Drugs used to treat hypertension - high blood pressure. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Cardiology dpt / Neurology dpt

TRT_ANTIHISTAMINES.name: Antihistamines
TRT_ANTIHISTAMINES.description: Allergy treating drugs, opposing the activity of histamine receptors, that are responsible for the symptoms of allergy. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / ICU / Office/Diagnostic unit at General Surgery dpt / Internal Medicine dpt / Neurology dpt / Traumatology dpt

TRT_BETA_BLOCKERS.name: Beta Blockers
TRT_BETA_BLOCKERS.description: Medication that is used to treat abnormal heart rhythm,high blood pressure, heart failure and tremor. \n\nRequired room: Any office / diagnostic unit at anz department except Orthopaedy dpt

TRT_INSULIN_THERAPY.name: Insulin Therapy
TRT_INSULIN_THERAPY.description: Medication that lowers the level of blood sugar. \n\nRequired room: Office / Diagnostic unit at Internal Medicine dpt.

TRT_NASAL_DROPS.name: Nasal Drops
TRT_NASAL_DROPS.description: Drugs that that clear the nasal cavity and increase ventilation through the nose. \n\nRequired room: Doctor's office / Observation room / Office/Diagnostic unit at Internal Medicine dpt / Cardiology dpt / DID
